PT P
PN CN111359625-A; CN111359625-B
TI Preparing carbon composite nano tricobalt tetroxide-based formaldehyde catalyst at room temperature, comprises adding cobalt salt to deionized water containing surfactant, stirring to obtain carbon dioxide solution.
AU HUANG Y
   LI R
   ZHU D
   CAO J
AE INST EARTH ENVIRONMENT CHINESE ACAD SCI (EART-Non-standard)
   INST EARTH ENVIRONMENT CHINESE ACAD SCI (EART-Non-standard)
GA 202063802M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing carbon composite nano tricobalt tetroxide-based formaldehyde catalyst at room temperature, comprises adding cobalt salt to deionized water containing surfactant, stirring to obtain carbon dioxide solution. Add to deionized water and stir to obtain complexing agent solution. Add the complexing agent solution dropwise to the carbon dioxide solution, stir, and dry to obtain the cobalt tetraoxide precursor, and crush to obtain the cobalt tetraoxide precursor powder. Mix Pt precursor and the cobalt tetroxide precursor powder uniformly, ball mill, and then calcined in an air atmosphere. Add the calcined powder to deionized water, ultrasonically disperse, add NaBH4 aqueous solution dropwise, stir or shake, and dry to obtain a carbon composite nano-cobalt tetraoxide-based formaldehyde catalyst at room temperature.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon composite nano tricobalt tetroxide-based formaldehyde catalyst at room temperature.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare carbon composite nano tricobalt tetroxide-based formaldehyde catalyst at room temperature, which has easily available and the preparation method is simple. The noble metal loading of the carbon composite nano cobalt tetroxide supported noble metal catalyst can be as low as 0.1 wt.%, and the removal rate of formaldehyde and carbon dioxide selectivity at room temperature are both greater than 90%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cobalt salt in step a is cobalt nitrate, cobalt chloride, cobalt acetate or cobalt sulfate. The agent is cetrimonium bromide, P123 or sdodecyl sulfate, and the complexing agent is citric acid, tartaric acid, ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid, diethylenetriaminepentaacetic acid, glucose or sodium alginate. The Pt precursor is chloroplatinic acid, platinum nitrate or dinitrosodiammine platinum. The molar ratio of cobalt in the Pt precursor and cobalt tetroxide precursor powder is 1:1500-1: 3000.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN CN110639488-A
TI Application method of biomass-based carbon material in electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide by dispersing biomass-based carbon material catalyst in ethanol, adding Nafion solution, coating to gas diffusion electrode and drying.
AU YUAN H
   QIAN X
   DENG L
   WANG L
   SHAN R
   LI D
AE GUANGZHOU ENERGY INST CONVERSION CHINESE (CAEC-C)
GA 202004131G
AB    NOVELTY - Application method of biomass-based carbon material in electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide comprises dispersing biomass-based carbon material catalyst in ethanol, adding Nafion (RTM: perfluoro-sulfonic acid polymer) solution, ultrasonically stirring to obtain mixed solution, coating mixed solution to gas diffusion electrode main body, drying to obtain gas diffusion electrode loaded with biomass-based carbon material, and performing electrochemical reduction conversion of carbon dioxide to obtain organic product with high added value. The biomass-based carbon material catalyst is prepared by hydrothermally reacting biomass raw material rich in carbon, oxygen and nitrogen at 160-200 degrees C for 18-24 hours, cooling, alternately washing with ethanol and water until solution is neutral, drying at 80-105 degrees C, carbonizing solid at 700-1000 degrees C for 2 hours under protection of inert gas, cooling, washing with water until neutral, drying at 100-110 degrees C, and grinding to 100-150 mesh.
   USE - The method is for application method of biomass-based carbon material in electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has low preparation cost, simple technique and mild reaction condition. The material has rich pore structure, large specific surface area and good stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gas diffusion electrode main body is made of glass carbon plate, carbon paper, carbon cloth or carbon felt. The biomass raw material is moss, or purple flower alfalfa.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN US2019227039-A1; WO2019142034-A1
TI System for managing fluid decompression between carbon dioxide-based chromatography column and detector, has detector located downstream of fluid connector, where detector receives fluid exiting cooled fluid connector.
AU TARAFDER A
AE WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
   WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
GA 2019642784
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a fluid connector located downstream of a chromatography column, where the fluid connector defines an interior bore, and fluid passes through the interior bore, and internal diameter of the interior bore at a downstream end is less than internal diameter of an upstream portion of the fluid connector. A cooling system (701) cools a portion of the fluid connector at or below a threshold temperature value associated with phase separation of the fluid. A detector is located downstream of the fluid connector, where the detector receives the fluid exiting the cooled fluid connector.
   USE - System for managing fluid decompression between a carbon dioxide-based chromatography column and a detector in gas chromatography.
   ADVANTAGE - The system avoids decompression of carbon dioxide-based mobile phase in phase separation of mobile phase in a mass spectrometry interface so as to prevent formation of distinct liquid and gas phases passing concurrently through the mass spectrometry interface.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for managing fluid decompression between a carbon dioxide-based chromatography column and a detector.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a capillary restrictor with a cooling system.
   Cooling system (701)
   Capillary restrictor (703)
   Capillary portion (705)
   Downstream portion (707)

PT P
PN IN201831048458-A; IN370791-B
TI Silica nanomaterial used as adsorbent for carbon dioxide comprises silica nanoflowers of assemblies of elongated spikes oriented outwards involving dandelion-like morphology and having excellent physical properties.
AU DAS S
   SAMANTA A
   JANA S
AE BOSE NAT CENT BASIC SCI S N (BOSE-C)
   BOSE NAT CENT BASIC SCI S N (BOSE-C)
GA 201910182P
AB    NOVELTY - Silica nanomaterial comprises silica nanoflowers of assemblies of elongated spikes oriented outwards involving dandelion-like morphology and having excellent physical properties selectively large specific surface area with diameter of 380-390 nm.
   USE - The silica nanomaterial is useful for desired tunable surface chemistry and related chemico-physical property and as adsorbent for carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The silica nanomaterial has tunable surface chemistry and related chemico-physical property having thermal and mechanical stability. The method: is environmental friendly, and cost-effective and is capable for large scale industrial production for the synthesis of monodispersed mesoporous silica nanoflowers with precise control over their size and accessible large surface area; and provides carbon dioxide capturing material with recyclability and long term durability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for manufacturing silica nanomaterial comprising providing flower like silica nanomaterial involving hydrolysis-polycondensation of silica precursor composition under light for 12-20 hours, preferably 15 hours and 80-100 degrees C preferably 90 degrees C and in the presence of a template molecule, a hydrolytic agent, solvent and co-solvent molecules. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the silica nanomaterial comprising surface functionalization with amines as adsorbent for carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The silica precursor composition comprises of tetraethyl orthosilicate. The template molecule is cetyltrimethylammonium bromide, cetyltrimethyl -ammonium chloride or similar surfactant moieties, the hydrolytic agent is urea, solvent is butyl ether, dioctyl ether, methyl phenyl ether (anisole), phenyl ether or dibenzyl ether and the co-solvent molecules is propanol, butanol, pentanol, hexanol, heptanol or octanol. Preferred Method: The silica nanomaterial is obtained of light driven hydrolysis route, uniform hierarchical flower-like morphology and with diameter of 380-390 nm, specific surface area 705-711 m2 g-1, mesoporous material, having average pore size of 3.44-3.45 nm, pore volume 0.996-1.021 cm3 g-1, spikes length of 190 plus minus 5 nm and diameter of 3-4 nm respectively. The hydrolysis of a silica precursor to produce silica nanoflowers is carried out at 80-100 degrees C. The step of providing the flower like silica nanomaterial is hydrolysis-polycondensation of tetraethyl orthosilicate using a light assisted method in presence of urea, cetyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTAB), methyl phenyl ether (anisole) or hexanol. The method comprises: dissolving in water the CTAB and urea; adding thereto the TEOS in the mixture of methyl phenyl ether and hexanol; carrying out hydrolysis-polycondensation of tetraethyl orthosilicate (TEOS) containing mix under visible light for 12-20 hours preferably 15 hours under stirring and reaction temperature of 80-100 degrees C preferably 90 degrees C, filtering and obtaining the flower like silica materials followed by preferably air drying and calcining. The method is carried out involving visible light irradiation preferably tungsten bulb at 80-100 degrees C and 12-20 hours, preferably 15 hours. The hydrolysis of a silica precursor to produce silica nanoflowers is carried out at 80-100 degrees C. The organic template molecule is removed from silica nanomaterial by calcination at 550-575 degrees C and 5-7 hours in air. The method comprises providing adsorbent silica nanomaterial by carrying out functionalization of the calcined silica nanoflowers with organosilanes and other amine containing moieties. The method comprises functionalization of the silica nanoflower through grafting reaction involving selectively organosilanes; (3-aminopropyl)triethoxysilane, N-(3-(trimethoxysilyl)-propyl)ethylenediamine or N1-(3-trimethoxysilylpropyl)- diethylenetriamine respectively.
   EXAMPLE - Flower like silica materials were synthesized via hydrolysis-polycondensation of tetraethyl orthosilicate (TEOS) using a light assisted method in presence of urea, cetyltrimethylammonium bromide (CTAB), methyl phenyl ether (anisole) and hexanol. First, 0.7 mmol CTAB and 2.5 mmol urea were taken together and dissolved in 5 ml of water. 0.5 ml TEOS was added in the mixture of 5 ml methyl phenyl ether and 0.25 ml hexanol and the solution was transferred into a glass vial fitted with cap and stirred vigorously to mix them properly. Then, the vial was placed under visible light for 15 hours under stirring condition and the reaction temperature was maintained at 90 degrees C. The resulting precipitate was collected by simple filtration and washed several times with millipore water. Thus obtained solid was air-dried, followed by calcination at 575 degrees C for 5 hours in air. The final yield of the reaction product was 98%.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

AU CHEN Z
AE MAOMING R & P PETROCHEMICAL ENG CO LTD (MAOM-Non-standard)
GA 201837026D
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide mixed gas conveying pipeline of the water-colleting drainage system, comprises a first seamless steel pipe, the top of the first seamless steel tube is connected with the welded branch pipe seat, the branch pipe on the welding seat is provided with a cap, the cap and the butt welding forming tee joint, the first seamless steel tube is provided with a valve, one side of the valve is provided with a maintenance bypass valve, one side of the valve is provided with a filter, the filter away from one end of the gate valve is provided with a bell type lyophobic valve. PT P
PN US2018072299-A1; US10556581-B2
TI Method for preventing infants, young children and animals from dying of heatstroke in vehicle, involves monitoring temperature inside vehicle through temperature sensor, and activating vehicle's air conditioning unit.
AU SOIFER S A
AE SOIFER S A (SOIF-Individual)
GA 201821155X
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves detecting whether an engine of the vehicle is off through a vehicle door sensor, and if all doors of the vehicle are closed. Changes in carbon dioxide levels in the vehicle are monitored through a carbon dioxide sensor to detect the presence of a breathing person or animal in the vehicle. A temperature inside the vehicle is monitored through a temperature sensor after the presence of a breathing person or animal in the vehicle is detected by the sensor. The vehicle's air conditioning unit is activated, where one of the vehicle's windows is lowered.
   USE - Method for preventing infants, young children and animals from dying of heatstroke in a vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables detecting presence of a human in the vehicle by detecting a critical level of carbon dioxide in the air within the vehicle, and by monitoring the interior vehicle temperature and taking corrective action to prevent the temperature from exceeding a preset value.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of a vehicular heatstroke prevention system.
   Vehicular heatstroke prevention system (100)
   Central controller (101)
   Vehicle door sensor (103)
   Engine sensor (105)
   Carbon dioxide sensor (107)

GA 201818627M
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a transport container (14) comprising a storage container for storing goods. A fluid line conducts sublimed dry ice discharged from dry ice containers i.e. replaceable battery packs (73a-73e) to the storage container. An expansion tank (74) is connected to a container (75) of liquidized carbon dioxide for receiving discharged liquidized carbon dioxide to the expansion tank. The expansion tank and the fluid line are connected by a quick-release coupling (76). The dry ice containers include sealed containers for dry ice enclosed within another sealed container.
   USE - System for cooling goods during transportation in a vehicle (claimed). Uses include but are not limited to a trailer, a lorry, a van and a ship.
   ADVANTAGE - The system enables coolant to efficiently cool down the container for dry ice when the coolant fed to the enclosure is sublimed to dry ice that is not used for cooling the storage container.

PT P
PN CN114574641-A; CN114574641-B
TI Method for smelting medium-low carbon            ferromanganese, comprises blast furnace smelting,            preparing converter raw material using coolant,            silicon-manganese alloy and liquid high-carbon            ferromanganese as raw material, converter smelting,            granulation or casting.
AU YANG J
   HAN Y
   LI C
   WANG H
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 2022788309
AB    NOVELTY - Method for smelting medium-low carbon                ferromanganese, involves (a) blast furnace smelting                by adding manganese ore, coke, and flux, to the                blast furnace in the order of coke-manganese ore                flux, and tapping the iron regularly according to                the iron casting cycle, (b) preparation of                converter raw materials by using coolant,                silicon-manganese alloy and the liquid high-carbon                ferromanganese obtained in step (a) for converter                smelting to carry out batching, (c) converter                smelting by weighing the liquid high-carbon                ferromanganese and mixing into the converter,                spraying a mixture of carbon dioxide and oxygen at                the top and bottom, and adding the coolant                configured in step (b), (d) adding the                silicon-manganese alloy configured in step (b) to                the converter, discharging the smelted liquid alloy                from the converter and injecting into the ladle to                obtain liquid medium-low carbon ferromanganese, and                (e) granulation or casting to produce the finished                medium-low carbon ferromanganese.
   USE - The method is useful for smelting middle-low                carbon ferromanganese, which is used in domestic                iron alloy enterprises in the converter                smeling-low-carbon ferromagenese test                production.
   ADVANTAGE - By setting the blast furnace-converter                process, the smelting cost of medium-low carbon                ferromanganese is further reduced, and through                different proportion of carbon dioxide and oxygen                in the converter process, mixing and blowing, so                that the corrosion of the device is reduced to the                lowest. The method reduces the volatilization loss                of the manganese, thus the quality of the final                product is ensured.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for smelting medium-low carbon                ferromanganese, involves (a) blast furnace smelting                by adding 76-78 pts. wt. manganese ore, 14-16 pts.                wt. coke, and 7-8 pts. wt. flux, to the blast                furnace in the order of coke-manganese ore flux,                setting the blast furnace air temperature to be                1000-1100degrees Celsius, where the iron casting                cycle is 90-120 minutes, and tapping the iron                regularly according to the iron casting cycle,                where the molten product obtained by the tapping                operation of the blast furnace is liquid                high-carbon ferromanganese, and its components are                60-82 wt.% manganese, 1-2 wt.% silicon, 2-8 wt.%                carbon, less than or equal to0.30 wt.% phosphorus,                and less than or equal to0.03 wt.% sulfur, and                remaining iron and inevitable impurities, (b)                preparation of converter raw materials by using                coolant, silicon-manganese alloy and the liquid                high-carbon ferromanganese obtained in step (a) as                raw materials for converter smelting to carry out                batching, where the ratio of liquid high-carbon                ferromanganese: coolant: silicon-manganese alloy is                (64-66): (20-22): (12-14), (c) converter smelting                by weighing the liquid high-carbon ferromanganese                obtained in step (a) according to the ratio set in                step (b) and then mixing into the converter, where                the mixing temperature is 1250-1350degrees Celsius,                and the converter adopts top-bottom composite                blowing way of training, spraying a mixture of                carbon dioxide and oxygen at the top and bottom,                where the volume ratio of carbon dioxide and oxygen                in the bottom-blown mixture is (5-15):(85-95), and                the volume ratio of carbon dioxide and oxygen in                the top-blown mixed gas is (5-35):(65-95) when the                furnace temperature rises to 1650-1850degrees                Celsius, adding the coolant configured in step (b),                and the converter blowing time is 32-92 minutes,                (d) converter discharge: after the converter                blowing time is up, adding the silicon-manganese                alloy configured in step (b) to the converter,                after the liquid alloy melt in the converter is                uniform and stable, discharging the smelted liquid                alloy from the converter and injecting into the                ladle to obtain liquid medium-low carbon                ferromanganese, where the components of the medium                and low carbon ferromanganese are: 60-85 wt.%                manganese, less than or equal to2.5 wt.% silicon,                1-2.9 wt.% carbon, less than or equal to0.40 wt.%                phosphorus, less than or equal to0.03 wt.% sulfur,                and remaining iron and inevitable impurities, and                (e) granulation or casting by adopting the liquid                medium and low carbon ferromanganese obtained in                step (d) as a raw material, and adopting the molten                iron granulation method to produce the middle-low                carbon ferromanganese finished product, or using                the liquid medium-low carbon ferromanganese                obtained in step (d) as a raw material, and                adopting a die casting method to produce the                finished medium-low carbon ferromanganese.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The coolant in step                  (b) is one or more of manganese dust obtained                  during the smelting process of lime, limestone,                  dried manganese ore or ferromanganese                  alloy.

TI 50MW level axial flow supercritical carbon dioxide turbine, has outer cylinder whose air inlet is set on lower part of side of outer cylinder, where air inlets are connected with air in-let valve.
AU GUO Q
   XU D
   ZHAO H
   WEI K
   PAN C

AE HARBIN TURBINE FACTORY CO LTD (HRBE-C)
GA 202275480T
AB    NOVELTY - The turbine has a front bearing housing, an inner cylinder, an outer cylinder, a rear bearing housing (10) and a base rack(16). The front bearing housing and the rear bearing housing are installed on both sides of the outer cylinder. Both the front bearing housing and the rear bearing housing are arranged on the base frame. The lower portion of the front end of the outer cylinder is connected with the front bearing box through the front centering beam structure (13). The lower portion of the rear end of the outer cylinder is connected with the rear bearing box through the rear centering beam structure and the exhaust port (6-1) of the outer cylinder is arranged on the top of outer cylinder. The air inlet of the outer cylinder is arranged at the lower part of the side of the outer cylinder. The intake port is connected to an intake valve. A forward vane mounted on the rotor (12) is provided between rear half of inner cylinder and the rotor.
   USE - 50MW grade axial flow supercritical carbon dioxide turbine.
   ADVANTAGE - The turbine adopts double-flow single exhaust design, thus saving the arrangement space, reducing the construction cost, and adopting double-split structure design. The air inlet loss is small, the circumferential pressure is uniform, the movable and static blades are geometrically symmetrical, and the axial thrust is balanced. The design is suitable for all kinds of heat source generating, such as thermal power generation, combined cycle, solar photo-thermal power generation and so on; set high rotating speed design, the design speed is 9000r/minute, obtaining higher cylinder efficiency; and the through-flow part is designed by double-shunt structure, compared with the single-flow structure, it avoids the air leakage at the air-inlet side, the full-cycle air-leakage matching split
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the structure of a lateral longitudinal section of a 50MW grade axial flow supercritical carbon dioxide turbine.

   Exhaust port (6-1)
   Rear bearing housing (10)

   Rear movable ring (14)
   Front moving ring (15)
   Base rack (16)

PN CN114349277-A
TI Treatment method of rural domestic sewage involves            sewage enters into aerobic conditions, adding aerobic            microorganisms and nitrifying bacteria, decomposing            organic matter in sewage into carbon dioxide and water,            converting ammonia nitrogen to nitrite nitrogen and            nitrate nitrogen.
AU WANG Z
   YUSUP E
   SHI X
   XU M
AE XINJIANG WATER TREATMENT ENG TECHNOLOGY (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 2022620803
AB    NOVELTY - Treatment method of rural domestic sewage                involves i. filtering domestic sewage to remove                large particles of suspended solids and sundries,                and then homogeneous adjustment is carried out; ii.                reacting conditioned sewage by adding                phosphorus-accumulating bacteria under anaerobic                conditions; iii. absorbing phosphorus in sewage and                converting into poly-beta-hydroxybutyric acid by                phosphorus-accumulating bacteria; iv. under the                induction of volatile fatty acids; v. sewage after                anaerobic reaction enters anoxic conditions and                adding facultative microorganisms to react,                converting organic nitrogen in the sewage into                ammonia nitrogen, and the nitrite nitrogen and                nitrate nitrogen are converted into nitrogen; vi.                the sewage after anoxic reaction enters into                aerobic conditions, adding aerobic microorganisms                and nitrifying bacteria for a synergistic reaction,                decomposing organic matter in sewage into carbon                dioxide and water, converting ammonia nitrogen to                nitrite nitrogen and nitrate nitrogen.
   USE - Treatment method of rural domestic                sewage
   ADVANTAGE - Treatment method of rural domestic sewage                greatly improves the treatment efficiency and                treatment effect of the domestic sewage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Treatment method of rural domestic sewage                involves i. filtering domestic sewage to remove                large particles of suspended solids and sundries,                and then homogeneous adjustment is carried out; ii.                reacting conditioned sewage by adding                phosphorus-accumulating bacteria under anaerobic                conditions; iii. absorbing phosphorus in sewage and                converting into poly-beta-hydroxybutyric acid by                phosphorus-accumulating bacteria; iv. under the                induction of volatile fatty acids, it is converted                into orthophosphate and released into sewage; v.                sewage after anaerobic reaction enters anoxic                conditions and adding facultative microorganisms to                react, converting organic nitrogen in the sewage                into ammonia nitrogen, and the nitrite nitrogen and                nitrate nitrogen are converted into nitrogen; vi.                the sewage after anoxic reaction enters into                aerobic conditions, adding aerobic microorganisms                and nitrifying bacteria for a synergistic reaction,                decomposing organic matter in sewage into carbon                dioxide and water, converting ammonia nitrogen to                nitrite nitrogen and nitrate nitrogen.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for treatment                device used in the method for treating rural                domestic sewage has grid connected in sequence for                filtering, conditioning tank for homogeneous and                homogeneous adjustment, anaerobic zone where the                reaction is carried out under anaerobic conditions,                anoxic zone where the reaction is carried out under                anoxic conditions, aerobic zone where the reaction                is carried out under aerobic conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition:                  Phosphorus-accumulating bacteria group is formed                  by mixing phosphorus-accumulating bacteria and                  aeromonas sobria in proportion, preferably, ratio                  of the phosphorus-accumulating bacteria to the                  aeromonas sobria in the phosphorus-accumulating                  bacteria group is (8-10):(1-3), preferably, the                  ratio of the phosphorus accumulating bacteria to                  the aeromonas sobria in the phosphorus                  accumulating bacteria group is 9:2. Facultative                  microorganism group is formed by mixing                  facultative microorganism and denitrifying                  bacteria in proportion, the ratio of the                  facultative microorganisms to the denitrifying                  bacteria in the facultative microflora is                  (8-10):(1-3), preferably, the ratio of the                  facultative microorganisms to the denitrifying                  bacteria in the facultative microflora is 9:2.                  Nitrifying bacteria group is that nitrifying                  bacteria and chemical oxygen demand degrading                  bacteria are mixed in proportion, ratio of the                  nitrifying bacteria to the chemical oxygen demand                  (COD) degrading bacteria in the nitrifying                  bacteria group is (7-9):(2-4). Ratio of the                  nitrifying bacteria to the COD degrading bacteria                  in the nitrifying bacteria group is 8:3.

PT P
PN US2022105461-A1
TI Absorber column apparatus for removing selected            component of gas, preferably carbon dioxide, has first            and third zones forming first domain through which            first fluid laden with select gaseous component to be            removed flows concurrently with second fluid, and            second zone formsing active packing zone.
AU STOLAROFF J K
   MOORE T C R
AE LAWRENCE LIVERMORE NAT SECURITY LLC (USGO-C)
GA 202248318G
AB    NOVELTY - The absorber column apparatus(10) has a first                zone (14a), a second zone (14b) and a third zone                (14c). The first and third zones form a first                domain through which a first fluid laden with a                select gaseous component to be removed flows                concurrently with a second fluid. The second fluid                operates to at least substantially remove the                select gaseous component from the first fluid to                create a third fluid. The first fluid leaves the                absorber column as a fourth fluid having had the                select gaseous component at least substantially                removed form them. The second zone forms an active                packing zone including a structure which forms an                independent second domain in thermal communication                with the first domain. The second zone operates to                receive a quantity of the third fluid and to                channel the quantity of the third fluid through the                second zone to help cool the at first fluid and the                second fluid. The second fluid comprises a                solvent.
   USE - Absorber column apparatus for removing a                selected component of a gas, preferably carbon                dioxide from power plants or industrial                sources.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus uses additively manufactured                structures to integrate heat exchange into a                gas/liquid exchange column to control the                temperature of carbon dioxide absorption reaction.                By applying the heat exchange strategy, the energy                efficiency of the process is improved and the                height of the column is reduced with less capital                equipment required. The apparatus is capable of                reducing the column height by at least about                10-20%, while simultaneously recovering waste heat                from the exothermic reactions occurring within the                absorption column. The structure of the heat                exchange packings allows for very large heat                exchange area between the two domains, typically on                the order of 250 m2/m3. This in turn allows the                apparatus to achieve highly efficient heat                removal.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for removing a selected component of a gas using an                absorber column.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a high level side view of                one embodiment of the absorber column                apparatus.

PN CN113738325-A; CN113738325-B
TI System for pyrolysis and carbon capture coupling rich oil coal, absorbs residual heat of coal seam into flue gas and then sends flue gas back to boiler, and boiler tail flue portion that is provided to extract heated coal seam and send fuel gas to boiler.
AU CHE D
   ZHAO L
   ZHOU L
   MAO Q
   WANG C
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2021F0245G
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an oxygen-enriched combustion boiler (1) of a power station, a compressor (2), an air separation device (3), a heat exchanger (7), a gas-liquid separation device (8) and a boiler air preheater (19). The power station oxygen-enriched combustion boiler burns to generate high-temperature carbondioxide (CO). The high-temperature CO generated by combustion in the flue gas is used for underground coal pyrolysis. A primary air (12) is sent into an air preheater (19) of a boiler to be continuously heated. The residual heat of the coal seam is absorbed into the flue gas and then the flue gas back is sent to the boiler. The boiler tail flue portion is extracted the heated coal seam and can be recycled back into the furnace, and the excess portion is subjected to a trapping carbon dioxide (15). The organic gas contains a methane and ethane small molecules.
   USE - System for pyrolysis and carbon capture coupling rich oil coal.
   ADVANTAGE - The system extracts the flue gas at the tail portion of the oxygen-enriched combustion power station boiler to heat the underground coal bed to prepare the oil gas so as to reduce the additional energy input, and the smoke heat energy of the boiler is fully used, which reduces the energy consumption of the whole system. The pure oxygen cold energy generated in the air separating device is used for freezing wall under the freezing coal layer, and generates the freezing wall, and reduces the pollution caused by the pyrolysis process to the underground. The system realizes the clean and efficient utilization of the rich oil coal, and pushes the target of energy carbon neutralization.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the oil-rich coal pyrolysis and carbon coupling system.
   Power station oxygen-enriched combustion boiler (1)
   Compressor (2)
   Air separating device (3)
   First coal layer block (4)
   Second coal layer block (5)
   Freezing wall (6)
   Heat exchanger (7)
   Gas-liquid separation device (8)
   Oil product (9)
   Circulating carbon dioxide (10)
   Pure oxygen (11)
   Primary air (12)
   Organic gas (13)
   Fuel (14)
   Trapping carbon dioxide (15)
   First valve (16)
   Second valve (17)
   Third valve (18)
   Boiler air pre-heater (19)

TI Apparatus for producing methane, comprises a first reactor in which a catalyst tube is provided inside supplied with source gas containing hydrogen and carbon dioxide to generate methane, catalyst for reaction for generating methane.
AU KIM S
   YOO Y D
   CHOI K S
AE INST ADVANCED ENG (ADVG-C)
GA 202193071M
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus comprises a first reactor (10) in which a catalyst tube (100) is provided inside which is supplied with a source gas containing hydrogen and carbon dioxide to generate methane, catalyst for a reaction for generating methane from the source gas, refrigerant supply tank connected to the first reactor to supply a refrigerant, flow rate control valve provided on a refrigerant supply line connecting the first reactor and the refrigerant supply tank, pressure control valve connected to the refrigerant supply tank and provided on a gas discharge line for discharging gas generated by evaporation of the refrigerant in the refrigerant supply tank to the outside of the refrigerant supply tank, temperature sensor provided at the rear end of the first reactor and measuring the temperature of the primary conversion gas including methane produced by reacting the raw material gas with the catalyst and control unit for controlling the flow rate control valve.
   USE - Apparatus used for producing methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus controls the reaction temperature in the exothermic reaction in real time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT Claim is included for a method for controlling a methane generating device, which inolves:
   (A) supplying a source gas containing hydrogen and carbon dioxide to the first reactor of the methane generating device;
   (B) measuring the temperature of the primary conversion gas containing methane produced by the reaction of the raw material gas with the catalyst in the first reactor; and
   (C) controlling an opening degree of a pressure control valve for regulating the pressure of a gas discharged from a refrigerant supply tank that supplies a refrigerant to the first reactor according to the temperature of the primary conversion gas, controlling the opening amount, when the temperature of the primary switching gas is higher than a preset temperature, controlling to increase the opening degree of the pressure control valve, when the temperature of the primary switching gas is lower than the preset temperature, the opening degree of the pressure control valve, controlling the amount to be reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an apparatus.
   Methane generating unit (1)
   First reactor (10)
   Catalyst tube (100)
   Upper diaphragm (110)
   Lower diaphragm (120)
   Upper chamber (130)
   Lower chamber (140)
   Cooling chamber (150)

   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN111203256-A
TI Preparing photocatalyst for reducing carbon            dioxide, by wrapping urea with tin foil, calcining,            acidifying, adding tin disulfide and graphitic carbon            nitride, centrifuging, adding gold tetrachloride,            irradiating UV rays, cooling and drying.
AU YIN S
   WANG H
   SUN L
   ZHOU Y
   LI X
   LI J
   SONG X
   HUO P
   YAN Y
AE UNIV JIANGSU (UYJS-C)
GA 2020498349
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing tin                disulfide/gold/graphitic carbon nitride composite                photocatalyst, involves (i) placing urea in a                crucible and wrapping with tin foil, placing above                crucible in a tube furnace for calcination,                acidifying product in nitric acid, reacting,                washing, and drying to obtain precursor of                graphitic carbon nitride, (ii) dissolving tin                chloride pentahydrate in mixed solution of ethylene                glycol and deionized water, adding L-cysteine and                sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate, and uniformly                stirring to prepare tin disulfide, adding graphitic                carbon nitride, adding mixed solution to reaction                kettle for hydrothermal reaction, washing,                centrifuging, and drying to obtain tin                disulfide/graphitic carbon nitride composite                photocatalyst, and (iii) dissolving tin                disulfide/graphitic carbon nitride composite                photocatalyst in deionized water, stirring,                ultrasonically processing, adding gold                tetrachloride, irradiating with UV lamp, naturally                cooling to room temperature, washing, and                drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing tin                disulfide/gold/graphitic carbon nitride composite                photocatalyst used for reduction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not cause waste of resources                and the formation of secondary pollution, is simple                to operate and environmentally-friendly, and                ensures the reduction of carbon dioxide by the                prepared catalyst to solve energy crisis and                environmental issues.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing tin                disulfide/gold/graphitic carbon nitride composite                photocatalyst, involves (i) placing urea in a                crucible and wrapping with tin foil, placing the                above crucible in a tube furnace for calcination,                acidifying the product in nitric acid having a pH                of 1, reacting under certain temperature                conditions, washing with deionized water and                ethanol several times, and drying by centrifugation                to obtain a solid, which is the precursor of                graphitic carbon nitride, (ii) dissolving tin                chloride pentahydrate in a mixed solution of                ethylene glycol and deionized water, adding                L-cysteine and sodium dodecylbenzenesulfonate,                uniformly stirring to prepare tin disulfide, adding                graphitic carbon nitride to form a mixed solution,                adding the mixed solution to a reaction kettle for                hydrothermal reaction, washing the product,                centrifuging, and drying to obtain a tin                disulfide/graphitic carbon nitride composite                photocatalyst, and (iii) dissolving tin disulfide/                graphitic carbon nitride composite photocatalyst                prepared in step (ii) in deionized water, stirring,                ultrasonically processing, adding gold                tetrachloride, irradiating with UV lamp, taking                out, naturally cooling to room temperature, washing                with deionized water and ethanol several times, and                drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of tin                  chloride pentahydrate, ethylene glycol, deionized                  water, L-cysteine and sodium                  dodecylbenzenesulfonate in step (ii) is 0.0877                  g:20 ml:10 ml:0.2423 g:0.5645 g. The mass ratio                  of tin disulfide and carbon nitride in step (ii)                  is 1:(0.25-4), preferably 1:4. The ratio of the                  amount of tin disulfide/ graphitic carbon                  nitride, deionized water and gold tetrachloride                  is 50 mg:50 ml:(1-7) ml. The mass ratio of tin                  disulfide/graphitic carbon nitride and gold in                  the tin disulfide/gold/graphitic carbon nitride                  composite photocatalyst in step (iii) is 1:49.                  Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the                  calcination is carried out at 550 degrees C for 4                  hours, the certain temperature is 80 degrees C,                  and the reaction is carried out for 8 hours. In                  step (ii), the hydrothermal reaction is carried                  out at 150-200 degrees C for 10 hours. In step                  (iii), the stirring is carried out for 20-30                  minutes, the ultrasonic processing is carried out                  for 40-60 minutes, and UV irradiation is carried                  out with power of 8 W for 10-15 minutes.

PN CN111039749-A; CN111039749-B
TI Electronic grade carbon tetrafluoride preparation system comprises e.g. fluorine gas source and reaction device, and fluorine gas source tightly connected to reaction device, fluorine gas source includes electrolytic cell.
AU ZENG X
   CHEN S
   LAI J
   LI J
   HUA X
   ZHANG C
   HUANG H
AE FUJIAN DEER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 202034631K
AB    NOVELTY - Electronic grade carbon tetrafluoride preparation system comprises a fluorine gas source and a reaction device, and a fluorine gas source is tightly connected to the reaction device. The fluorine gas source specifically includes an electrolytic cell, a low-temperature dust collector, a condenser and a fluorine gas buffer tank. The reaction device specifically includes a carbon reactor and a dust removal device.
   USE - Used as an electronic grade carbon tetrafluoride preparation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: adopts the use of fluorine gas of more than 98%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing electronic grade carbon tetrafluoride comprising (1) producing the fluorine gas automatically, passing through the low-temperature dust collector, the condenser, and reaches the fluorine gas buffer tank, (2) entering the fluorine gas in the buffer tank, carbon reactor (4), which combines with high-purity carbon particles and a small amount of coke, (3) using carbon tetrafluoride containing a small amount of impurities to remove solid matter through a dust collector, (4) passing the prepared carbon tetrafluoride gas through the pre-water washing tower to remove traces of unreacted fluorine gas, and controlling the acidity at 35-40%, replacing the liquid in the water washing tower, (5) removing substances easily soluble in water e.g. hydrogen fluoride, (6) entering the alkali washing tower of carbon tetrafluoride gas from the water washing tower for alkaline washing to remove traces of hydrogen fluoride, carbon dioxide and other acidic impurities, (7) entering the carbon tetrafluoride gas from the alkali scrubber , the pyrolysis tower to decompose micro-oxygen and other by-products in the gas, (8) entering the alkali washing tower to remove carbon dioxide from the gas, (9) entering the carbon tetrafluoride gas from the alkali scrubber through the gas-liquid separator (11) for gas-liquid separation, (10) entering the carbon tetrafluoride from the gas-liquid separator through the freezing water removal tower for water removal treatment, entering the low-pressure adsorption tower to remove trace moisture in the gas, entering the high-pressure adsorption tower to remove trace carbon dioxide and other impurity gases, (11) entering the carbon tetrafluoride gas from the high-pressure adsorption tower, the storage tank for storage, and enters the rectification tower for rectification, and (12) compressing the carbon tetrafluoride gas from the rectification tower by a high-pressure diaphragm compressor, and filling into a steel bottle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (2), the pressure in the fluorine gas buffer tank is 0.06-0.06 MPa, the ratio of high-purity carbon particles to coke in the carbon reactor is 100:1, the carbon particle size is 4-6 cm. Preferred Method: In the step (1), the temperature of the electrolytic cell is 80-95 degrees C, and the acidity is 40-42%. In the step (1), the temperature of the low-temperature dust collector is 0-4 degrees C. In the step (1), the temperature in the condenser is -70 to -60 degrees C.

ER

PT P
PN US2019144896-A1; US10894969-B2
TI Producing ethanol comprises e.g. obtaining methane sourced from biogas produced from anaerobic digestion, reforming gas comprising methane sourced from biogas to produce syngas gas, and converting at least one component of syngas to ethanol.
AU FOODY P J
AE IOGEN CORP (IOGE-Non-standard)
GA 201942209R
AB    NOVELTY - Process (p1) for producing ethanol comprises (i) obtaining methane sourced from biogas produced from anaerobic digestion, (ii) reforming a gas to produce syngas, where the gas comprises the methane sourced from biogas, (iii) converting at least one component of the syngas to ethanol, where the converting step comprises subjecting a gas substrate comprising the components to a gas fermentation, and the components comprises carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and/or hydrogen, (iv) using an aqueous stream comprising ethanol produced in the step (iii) in an ethanol production process that comprises fermenting carbohydrate to ethanol.
   USE - The process (p1) or (p2) are useful for producing ethanol.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: provides increased product yield and decreases fresh/treated water usage; allows a greater amount of the biogenic carbon from the feedstock to be converted to the final product, and thus the total energy of the biogenic carbon-based fuel or products that can be produced from the non-fossil organic material can be increased by at least 10%, 25% or 30% relative to a conventional biofuel production process without collecting carbon dioxide; produces the biogenic carbon-based fuel(s) which has(have) life cycle GHG emissions associated therewith that are at least 20%, 30% or 40% lower than a gasoline baseline, and thus the savings can be at least as much as 50% lower than a gasoline baseline, or even at least as much as 60%, 70%, 80% or 90% lower than a gasoline baseline; and is simple and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a process (p2) for producing ethanol comprising (i-1) treating a feedstock to obtain a fermentable carbohydrate, (ii-2) fermenting the fermentable carbohydrate to produce ethanol, (iii-3) feeding a portion of the treated feedstock not converted to ethanol to an anaerobic digester, (iv-4) collecting biogas from the anaerobic digester, (v-5) converting at least one component of the collected biogas to ethanol, where the converting step comprises fermenting a gas substrate comprising at least one component obtained or derived from the collected biogas and the components are carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide or hydrogen, and (vi-6) using an aqueous stream comprising ethanol produced in step (v-5) within the process so that ethanol produced in step (v-5) is copresent with fermentable carbohydrate for at least part of a step of fermenting the fermentable carbohydrate to produce ethanol.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The step (iv) comprises feeding the aqueous stream to a stage in the ethanol production process so that ethanol produced in the step (iii) is copresent with carbohydrate during at least part of a step of fermenting the carbohydrate to ethanol. The process (p1) comprises recovering ethanol produced in the step (iii) together with ethanol produced by fermenting the carbohydrate to ethanol. The gas substrate comprises carbon dioxide produced from fermenting the carbohydrate to ethanol. The ethanol production process is a cellulosic ethanol production process, where the biogas comprises biogas produced from anaerobic digestion of still bottoms produced in the cellulosic ethanol production process. The gas reformed in the step (ii) comprises fossil methane. The processes (p1) and (p2) comprise (a) converting fossil methane to carbon monoxide and hydrogen by reforming, (b) converting at least a portion of the carbon monoxide produced in (a) by a water-gas shift reaction to carbon dioxide for producing a stream comprising carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and (c) separating at least a portion of the hydrogen from the stream of step (b) from non-hydrogen components to produce a stream enriched in hydrogen and a stream comprising carbon dioxide, where the gas substrate comprises hydrogen from the stream enriched in hydrogen. The gas substrate comprises carbon dioxide produced from carbohydrate fermentation in the cellulosic ethanol production process. The gas substrate comprises hydrogen and carbon dioxide to provide a molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon dioxide of 2:1-4:1. The step of converting the components of the collected biogas to ethanol comprises: reforming a gas to produce syngas, where the gas comprises methane from the collected biogas, and the gas substrate comprises one or more components of the syngas; and subjecting a gas to a steam reforming process to produce syngas, where the gas comprises the collected biogas and carbon dioxide produced from fermenting the fermentable carbohydrate, and the gas substrate comprises at least one component of the syngas. The process (p2) comprises collecting carbon dioxide produced from fermenting the fermentable carbohydrate, where the gas substrate comprises the collected carbon dioxide. The process (p2) comprises recovering ethanol produced in step (v-5) and ethanol derived from the fermentable carbohydrate. The portion of treated feedstock not converted to ethanol and fed to anaerobic digestion comprises still bottoms. The gas substrate: is syngas produced by reforming the collected biogas; and comprises syngas produced by reforming the collected biogas, hydrogen derived from fossil methane and/or carbon dioxide collected from carbohydrate fermentation.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).);

PT P
PN EP3431537-A1
TI Chemical blowing agent used in foamable polymer composition for manufacturing foamed thermoplastic polymer e.g. PVC plastisol, comprises functionalized particulate bicarbonate comprising at least one additive comprising maltodextrin.
AU KABBABE MALAVE J A
   DETOISIEN T L A
   DUPONT C L A
AE SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
GA 2019074399
AB    NOVELTY - A chemical blowing agent comprises functionalized particulate bicarbonate comprising at least one additive comprising maltodextrin.
   USE - Chemical blowing agent used in foamable polymer composition for manufacturing foamed thermoplastic polymer e.g. PVC plastisol, polyurethane, polyolefin, and polyamide (all claimed), used in flooring applications, production of wallpaper foam-based, for artificial leather, for disposable packaging of fast-food, and for cushioning of furniture and insulation material. Can also be used for acrylonitrile butadiene styrene, acrylonitrile styrene acrylate, styrene acrylonitrile resin, polystyrene, engineering resin, polyphenylene oxide, polyimide, nitrile butadiene rubber, and chloroprene rubber.
   ADVANTAGE - The chemical blowing agent has excellent carbon dioxide release property and expansion property, and can be prepared economically. The functionalized particulate bicarbonate particles have low particle size. The gelation time of the polymer with the chemical blowing agent is less than the gelation time that would be obtained with a chemical blowing agent liberating nitrogen. The functionalization of bicarbonate particles enhances the protection of the bicarbonate particles with inactive barrier, which delays the thermal decomposition when having various bicarbonate particle sizes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) foamable polymer composition, which comprises a polymer, optionally a foaming stabilizer, and the chemical blowing agent;
   (2) foamable PVC plastisol composition, which comprises a PVC resin, optionally a foaming stabilizer, and the chemical blowing agent, and does not contain azodicarbonamide, benzenesulfonyl hydrazide and/or p-toluenesulfonyl hydrazide;
   (3) manufacture of polymer, which involves heating the foamable polymer composition at a temperature suitable for liberating carbon dioxide gas and melting the polymer during gelation time of less than 130 seconds at a temperature above the glass transition temperature and/or above the melting temperature, of the polymer; and
   (4) manufacture of PVC polymer, which involves heating the foamable PVC polymer composition at a temperature suitable for liberating carbon dioxide gas and melting the PVC polymer during gelation time of 90-120 seconds at a temperature of 190-210 degrees C, preferably 200-210 degrees C, to provide foamed PVC polymer. The foamable polymer composition is spread-coated on a surface before heating and melting. The foamed PVC polymer has an expansion ratio of at least 270, preferably at least 300 and/or a density of less than 0.6 g/cm3, preferably 0.5 g/cm3 or less.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The chemical blowing agent further comprises a compound (C1) which liberates carbon dioxide upon heating, and is chosen from carboxylic or polycarboxylic acid or its derivative (such as ester) or salt, and is optionally functionalized with the same additive in the functionalized particulate bicarbonate. The compound (C1) is preferably fumaric acid, tartaric acid, citric acid, citrate (such as sodium hydrogen citrate and disodium citrate), and/or esters of citric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The blowing agent does not contain an exothermic blowing agent, a compound that liberates nitrogen gas during heating, or a compound that liberates ammonia gas during heating. The functionalized particulate bicarbonate comprises at least one additional additive chosen from one or more polymers, one or more amino acids or their derivatives or salts, one or more inorganic salts, one or more oils, one or more fats, one or more resin acids or their derivatives or salts, one or more fatty acids or their derivatives or salts, carboxylic or polycarboxylic acid or its derivative (such as ester) or salt, one or more soaps, and one or more waxes. Preferred Composition: The functionalized particulate bicarbonate comprises 50-100 wt.%, preferably 65-100 wt.% (excluding 100) bicarbonate component, and 50-0.02 wt.%, preferably 35-0.02 wt.% additive. Preferred Property: The particles of the functionalized particulate bicarbonate have a particle size distribution (D50) of greater than 1-250 mu m, preferably greater than 1-25 mu m, or at most 1 mu m. Preferred Process: The functionalized particulate bicarbonate is obtained by spray-drying (atomization), where the additive is dissolved in the bicarbonate-containing solution, grinding or co-grinding (milling or co-milling) with the additive in emulsion or powder form, spray coating and granulating within fluidized bed, spray agglomeration within fluidized bed, spray chilling (e.g. spray cooling and spray freezing), roller compaction, and/or extrusion including simultaneous mixing/extrusion, optionally followed by subjecting the obtained functionalized particulate bicarbonate to milling to reduce its mean particle size.

PT P
PN CN109105810-A
TI Processing dehydrated vegetable product comprises e.g. selecting fresh vegetable, soaking in clean water, cutting into pieces, placing in vacuum container, introducing liquid carbon dioxide, refrigerating, drying, sealing and storing.
AU ZHANG X
AE JIESHOU WANHUACHAO BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIES-Non-standard)
GA 201904082M
AB    NOVELTY - Processing dehydrated vegetable product comprises (i) selecting fresh vegetable, removing the insect, rotting and wizened parts and soaking in saline solution; (ii) taking out soaked vegetables, draining water and cutting into pieces, filaments or strips; (iii) placing the cut vegetable in a vacuum container, introducing liquid carbon dioxide, carrying out insulating treatment, opening a pressure switch, relieving pressure to atmospheric pressure and discharging all gases; (iv) turning off pressure relief switch, passing emulsion into a container, turning on pressure relief switch, relieving pressure to atmospheric pressure and exhausting the remaining emulsion; (v) washing the surface dry water, rinsing the treated vegetable with clean water and performing low temperature refrigeration; (vi) taking out refrigerated vegetable and drying in a microwave vacuum; and (vii) cooling the dehydrated vegetables in a refrigerating chamber and storing.
   USE - The method is useful for processing dehydrated vegetable product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces product rich in nutrition and having bright color, good quality, easy storage and good rehydration property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing dehydrated vegetable product comprises (i) selecting fresh vegetable, removing the insect, rotting and wizened parts, washing and soaking with clean water at a concentration of 30-35 % of saline solution; (ii) taking out the soaked vegetables and draining the water and cutting into pieces, filaments or strips; (iii) placing the cut vegetable into a vacuum container, pressurizing at 2-3 MPa, introducing liquid carbon dioxide in a volume of 1/4-1/3 of the container, raising the temperature at 50-60 degrees C, carrying out insulating treatment for 3-5 minutes, opening the pressure switch, relieving pressure to atmospheric pressure and discharging all gases; (iv) turning off pressure relief switch, carrying secondary pressurization to 4-5 MPa, passing emulsion in a volume of 1/3-1/2 of the container, holding pressure for 4-6 minutes, turning on pressure relief switch, relieving pressure to atmospheric pressure and exhausting the remaining emulsion; (v) rinsing the treated vegetable with clean water after washing the surface dry water, performing low temperature refrigeration; (vi) taking out the refrigerated vegetable and drying in a microwave vacuum till the water content is 5-7%; and (vii) cooling dehydrated vegetables in a refrigerating chamber at 5-10 degrees C for 10-15 minutes, placing into packing bag, pumping out the air in the packaging bag for sealing and storing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: In the step (i) the ratio of solid-liquid vegetables to saline solution is 1 g/10-20 ml and the soaking time in the saline solution is 2-3 hours. Preferred Method: In the step (iv), preparing of emulsion comprises (i) adding 5-10 pts. wt. soluble starch into 2-3 pts. wt. distilled water, stirring and dissolving, mixing 3-4 pts. wt. sodium carboxy methylcellulose are, 2-3 pts. wt. polyethylene glycol, uniformly stirring and heating at 80-90 degrees C for 2-3 hours; and (ii) cooling at room temperature, mixing 2-3 pts. wt. glycolic acid, 1-2 pts. wt. lactic acid, uniformly stirring. In the step (v) refrigerating of low temperature is 0-3 degrees C and refrigerating time is 1-2 hours. In the step (vi) the process conditions for microwave vacuum drying pressure is 3- 5 MPa, the drying temperature is 45-50 degrees C, the microwave power is 15-20 kW and the drying time is 50-70 minutes.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PT P
PN CN108480577-A
TI Producing cast magnesium alloy profile comprises e.g. doping the magnesium alloy liquid with an inorganic reinforcing agent, blowing into the carbon dioxide for semi-solidification cooling treatment and starting the motor.
AU ZHU G
   JI Z
AE WENXI YUANHUA METALLURGICAL MATERIALS CO (WENX-Non-standard)
   ZHU G (ZHUG-Individual)
GA 201871785P
AB    NOVELTY - Producing cast magnesium alloy profile comprises (i) doping the magnesium alloy liquid with an inorganic reinforcing agent for enhanced treatment and blowing into the carbon dioxide for semi-solidification cooling treatment, (ii) starting the motor to drive the upper and lower rollers to rotate, (iii) adding the magnesium alloy liquid into the feeding nozzle through the infusion pump, sending into the cavity, cooling and condensing by the upper and lower rollers and sending out as a profile during the rotation process.
   USE - The method is useful for producing cast magnesium alloy profile.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing cast magnesium alloy profile comprises (i) doping the magnesium alloy liquid with an inorganic reinforcing agent for enhanced treatment and blowing into the carbon dioxide for semi-solidification cooling treatment, (ii) starting the motor to drive the upper and lower rollers to rotate, (iii) adding the magnesium alloy liquid into the feeding nozzle through the infusion pump, sending into the cavity, cooling and condensing by the upper and lower rollers and sending out as a profile during the rotation process, where the casting and rolling production device comprises a concrete base on which a caster frame is mounted by bolts. The caster frame is vertically mounted with a top roller and a lower roller through a bearing. The upper roller is having a cam edge in the middle and a concave edge on both sides. The middle of the lower roller is a wheel groove. The two sides are flange edges. The cam edge of the upper roller is located in the wheel groove of the lower roller and forms a cavity. The driving gear and a large pulley are mounted on one end of the axle of the upper roller. The driven gear is mounted on one end of the axle of the lower roller and the driving gear meshing with the driven gear. The large pulley is connected to a small pulley mounted on the output shaft of the motor via a belt. The feeding nozzle is disposed outside the cavity. The feeding nozzle is provided with a magnesium alloy liquid level control plate and the feeding nozzle is provided with electric heaters on both sides. The upper roller and the lower roller are provided with a spray paint pipe and a high pressure air cooling pipe.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic reinforcing agent is an inorganic fiber, an inorganic whisker, a carbide, a nitride or a boride.

PT P
PN CN107897355-A
TI Preparing fruit preservative involves dissolving Myrtle fruit seed extract in distilled water, adjusting pH value of the Myrtle fruit seed extract using sodium hydroxide solution, putting konjac polysaccharide in distilled water.
AU LUO Z
AE LIUZHOU FEISHENGPENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LIUZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018302177
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a fruit preservative involves dissolving Myrtle fruit seed extract in distilled water, and adjusting pH value of the Myrtle fruit seed extract to 10 to 11 using 1 to 2 mol/L sodium hydroxide solution. The temperature is 90-100 degrees C of water bath and stirring for 30-40min after cooling to 45-50 degrees C. The konjac polysaccharide is put in distilled water, acetic acid solution is added while stirring on the stirrer, until the konjac polysaccharide is completely dissolved. The acetic acid has a mass concentration of 1 to 5%.
   USE - Method for preparing fruit preservative.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare fruit preservative, which has good preservation effects for various fruits.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a fruit preservative involves dissolving Myrtle fruit seed extract in distilled water, and adjusting pH value of the Myrtle fruit seed extract to 10 to 11 using 1 to 2 mol/L sodium hydroxide solution. The temperature is 90-100 degrees C of water bath and stirring for 30-40min after cooling to 45-50 degrees C. The konjac polysaccharide is put in distilled water, acetic acid solution is added while stirring on the stirrer, until the konjac polysaccharide is completely dissolved. The acetic acid has a mass concentration of 1 to 5%. After the solution is mixed, the mixture of myristyl alcohol, Myrtle fruit peel and fig wine residue is added and mixed. The fruit preservative is obtained by applying ultrasonic-microwave irradiation for 20-30 minutes. The ultrasonic power is 200-400W, microwave power is 300-500W. The fig wine-making pomace extract is extracted by filtering fruit residue obtained by filtration in fig winemaking process and drying by microwave and extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction conditions are mixed solution of ethanol and acetone used as the entrainer, and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 20 to 30 L/min. Extraction pressure is 30-35MPa, extraction temperature is 32-42 degrees C, analytical temperature is 35-45 degrees C, extraction time is 1-3h. The extract is subjected to vacuum separation and extraction. The pressure of the separation tank is 20 to 25 MPa, and the temperature is 25 to 55 degrees C. The ficus extract of the fig wine is obtained by separation.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Process: The fig winemaking fruit residue is obtained by ishing the figs, peeling and crushing. The plant polysaccharides are added to adjust the pulp soluble solids content of 20 to 30%. Saccharomyces cerevisiaeis added to the blended fig pulp to obtain fig wine. The fig wine is filtered to obtain a clear fig fruit wine and the fig wine compote. The Myrtle fruit seed extract is obtained by crushing seeds of Myrtle fruit, and petroleum ether is added for stirring for 1 to 2 hours. The precipitate is left to stand, and the precipitate is separated from petroleum ether. The defatted Myrtle fruit seeds are placed in an alkaline solution with a pH of 9.5 to 11 and treated with ultrasound at 40-50 degrees C for 30-60 minutes. The supernatant is centrifuged at a speed of 3000-4000r/min for 20-30 min. 2mol/L HCl solution is added to adjust the pH of the supernatant to 4.5, and centrifuged at 20 to 30 minutes after the rotation speed is 3000 to 4000r/min. The precipitate is dissolved in distilled water, and the pH is adjusted to 9.5 to 11 with 2 mol/L sodium hydroxide solution. After the dialysis bag is used for desalting, the Myrtle fruit extract is obtained by freeze-drying at -15 to -20 degrees C. The ultrasonic power is 500-800W. The fruit liquid ratio of wick fruit seed and the alkaline solution is 1Kg: 10~20L. The dialysis bag has a pore size of 3500 to 4000 Da. The Myrtle fruit peel extract is obtained by drying rind peel, crushing through a 200-mesh sieve, and then placing in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction kettle and extracting with an ethanol solution having a 20% mass concentration as an entrainer for 10 to 30 minutes to obtain the Myrtle fruit peel extract. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction conditions are extraction temperature of 20 degrees C, extraction pressure of 35Mpa, carbon dioxide flow rate of 22L/min. The mass ratio of the ethanol solution and the rind peel is 10-15:100. Preferred Condition: The Myrtle fruit seed extract contained in Myrtle fruit seed extract is not less than 90 mg/100 g. During extraction process of the fig winemaking pomace extract, of ethanol and acetone and mass ratio is 1 33:5. Preferred Composition: The fruit preservative comprises 11 to 23 pts. wt. Myrtle fruit seed extract, 20 to 3 pts. wt. konjac polysaccharide, 5 to 10 pts. wt. myristyl alcohol, and 24 to 40 pts. wt. fig wine stalk extract.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)

PN CN107753878-A
TI Moxibustion patch comprises e.g. a protective layer, a moxibustion therapy layer, a heating layer and a backing layer, where backing layer is connected far away from one side of heat-generating layer bonded with transparent plastic film.
AU WANG Q
AE BEIJING CHAOTANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2018214856

The moxibustion therapy layer is filled with medicine, where the medicine includes 80-100 pts. wt. sodium alginate, 60-70 pts. wt. glycerin, 40-60 pts. wt. artemisinin extract, 20-28 pts. wt. poly(1,4- alpha -D-galacturonic acid). The flow rate of carbon dioxide fluid is 30-38 m2/hours during the extraction of the active ingredient of extract.

TI Heat pipe comprises two heat transfer sections and pipe or tube connecting heat transfer sections, with a filling of a heat transfer medium, and has acetone, water, hexane, methanol, pentane, ethanol, glycol and/or carbon dioxide.
AU ROEMER C
   EBERT H
   REIM M
AE BAYERISCHES ZENT ANGEWANDTE ENERGIEFORSC (BAYE-Non-standard)
GA 201812906H
AB    NOVELTY - Heat pipe comprises two heat transfer sections and at least one pipe or tube connecting heat transfer sections, with a filling of a heat transfer medium. The heat transfer medium has a working temperature of -20 degrees C to 50 degrees C and acetone, water, hexane, methanol, pentane, ethanol, glycol and/or carbon dioxide, preferably a water-glycol mixture having a glycol content of 30%. The heat pipe has a filling amount with the liquid heat transfer medium of 0.1-10% based on the void volume of the heat pipe.
   USE - The heat pipe is useful for the passive use of near-surface geothermal energy for tempering a multi-layered building facade (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The heat pipe improves thermal insulation of walls, facades and roofs with low thermal conductivity used with increasing thickness.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Heat pipe comprises two heat transfer sections and at least one pipe or tube connecting heat transfer sections, with a filling of a heat transfer medium. The heat transfer medium has a working temperature of -20 degrees C to 50 degrees C and acetone, water, hexane, methanol, pentane, ethanol, glycol and/or carbon dioxide, preferably a water-glycol mixture having a glycol content of 30%. The heat pipe has a filling amount with the liquid heat transfer medium of 0.1-10% based on the void volume of the heat pipe. The heat transfer section is in the ground and the other heat transfer section is in a functional layer of a building envelope. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) facade element for passive use of near-surface geothermal energy for tempering a multi-layered building facade, comprising at least one heat pipe preferably of a plurality of heat pipes connected in parallel, where at least one heat pipe is in direct thermal contact with a functional layer in the building envelope; and
   (2) building envelope comprising at least one heat pipe or at least one facade element.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a facade element with a heat pipe.
   Connecting pipe (1)
   Upper heat transfer section (2)
   Lower heat transfer section (3)
   Thermal insulation of the connecting pipe (4)
   Functional layer (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The two heat transfer sections are connected together by a capillary-active structure inside the heat pipe, preferably by a capillary-active filling material, such as sintered metal powder or metal mesh, or by an inner capillary-active, longitudinally grooved surface of the connecting tube, or by capillary-active filler material and/or capillary-active, longitudinally grooved surface. At least one of the two heat transfer sections comprise construction of a good heat-conducting material to increase the heat-absorbing or heat-emitting surfaces, preferably the lower heat transfer section is cast in a solid, preferably in concrete, preferably a well heat-conductive filling material in concrete. The upper heat transfer section is an enlarged area of a good heat conducting material. At least two heat pipes are connected in series one behind the other, where and the upper heat transfer section of the geometrically lower lying heat pipe with the lower heat transfer section of the geometrically higher heat pipe are thermally coupled by a good heat conducting material. At least two heat pipes are connected in parallel and the upper heat transfer sections and the lower heat transfer sections are gas-tight open together or separated gas-tight by a good heat conducting material, but are thermally coupled. At least one connecting tube of the heat transfer sections is thermally insulated by vacuum insulation, preferably at least one inner side of the vacuum envelope is subjected to a low-emitting coating. The functional layer comprises a good heat-conducting solid body and the surface is completely or partially incorporated on or in the thermal insulation of the building inner wall. The functional layer simultaneously represents the outer building envelope layer.
