PN CN107674859-A; CN107674859-B
TI Using small molecule composition to induce mouse fibroblasts into cartilage, by culturing mouse embryonic fibroblasts in chemically induced medium, transferring to cartilage induction medium and obtaining chondrocyte mass.
AU OUYANG H
   CHEN Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
   UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 201814496T
AB    NOVELTY - Method of using small molecule composition to induce mouse fibroblasts into cartilage, involves (a) taking mouse embryonic fibroblasts, slowly adding chemically induced medium containing small molecule composition, culturing, changing medium once every 2-3 days, and obtaining intermediate cells, and (b) transferring intermediate cells to cartilage induction medium, culturing at 37 degrees C under 15-25% oxygen, carbon dioxide, and remaining nitrogen environment, changing fresh cartilage induction medium once every 3-4 days, and obtaining chondrocyte mass.
   USE - The method of using small molecule composition is used for inducing mouse fibroblasts into cartilage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of using small molecule composition to induce mouse fibroblasts into cartilage, involves (a) taking mouse embryonic fibroblasts, slowly adding chemically induced medium containing small molecule composition, culturing at 37 degrees C under 3-8% oxygen, 3-8% carbon dioxide, and remaining nitrogen environment, changing medium once every 2-3 days, and after 4-12 days, obtaining intermediate cells, and (b) transferring intermediate cells to cartilage induction medium, culturing at 37 degrees C under 15-25% oxygen, 3-8% carbon dioxide, and remaining nitrogen environment, changing fresh cartilage induction medium once every 3-4 days, and after 14-28 days, obtaining chondrocyte mass. The small molecule composition comprises 0.3-0.8 mM histone deacetylase (HDAC) inhibitor, 2-4 mu M glycogen synthase kinase-3 (GSK-3) inhibitor and 0.5-2 mu M transforming growth factor (TGF)- beta signaling pathway inhibitor. The chemical-induced medium comprises KnockOut (RTM: Basal medium optimized for growth of undifferentiated of human embryonic and induced pluripotent stem cells)DMEM medium as a solvent, 10-20% serum replacement, 0.5-2% non-essential amino acid solution, 0.5-2% L-alanyl-L-glutamine, 0.5-2% sodium pyruvate, 0.05-0.2 mM beta -mercaptoethanol and 1000 U/ml recombinant human leukemia inhibitory factor. The non-essential amino acid solution comprises 10 mM glycine, 10 mM L-alanine, 10 mM asparagine, 10mM aspartic acid, 10 mM glutamate, 10 mM proline, 10 mM serine and remainder solvent. The cartilage induction medium comprises H-Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium (DMEM) as a solvent, 0.5-2% sodium pyruvate, 10-100 mu g/ml L-ascorbic acid 2-phosphate hydrate, 1-2x 10-7 M dexamethasone, 0.5-2% insulin-transferrin-selenium-ethanolamine, 5-40 ng/ml recombinant human transforming growth factor- beta 3, and natural pH.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The small molecule composition comprises 0.5 mM HDAC inhibitor, 3 mu M GSK-3 inhibitor and 1 mu M TGF- beta signaling pathway inhibitor. The small molecule composition further comprises 0.5-5 mu M, preferably 3 mu M retinoic acid receptor (RAR) agonist and 3-10 mu M, preferably 5 mu M cyclooxygenase-2 (COX-2) inhibitor. The cartilage induction medium further comprises 100 nM kartogenin. Preferred Components: The HDAC inhibitor is valproic acid (VPA). The GSK-3 inhibitor is CHIR-98014 or CHIR-99021. The TGF- beta signaling inhibitor is Repsox. The RAR agonist is 4-((E)-2-(5,6,7,8-tetrahydro-5,5,8,8-tetramethyl-2-naphthalenyl)-1-propenyl)benzoic acid (TTNPB). The COX-2 inhibitor is celecoxib. Preferred Method: In step (a), the culturing is performed by culturing at 37 degrees C under 5% oxygen, 5% carbon dioxide, and remaining nitrogen environment for 6-9 days, changing medium once every 2-3 days, and after 4-12 days, obtaining intermediate cells. In step (b), the culturing is performed by culturing at 37 degrees C under 21% oxygen, 5% carbon dioxide, and remaining nitrogen environment, changing fresh cartilage induction medium once every 3-4 days, and after 14-28 days, obtaining chondrocyte mass.

TI Carbon dioxide/calcium oxide responsive emulsifier            useful e.g. in preparing reversible emulsion and            reversible drilling fluid for cleaning oil-bearing            drill cuttings and oil-based filter cakes, and removing            low-density useless solid phases.
AU REN Y
   JIANG G
   LU Y
   XIE S
   YAO R
   XIE G
   YOU L
AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
   CNPC SAFETY ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY RES (CNPC-C)
GA 202175730X
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide/calcium oxide responsive                emulsifier is claimed. The emulsifier is product                obtained by contacting carbon dioxide donor with                amine compound (I); infrared characteristic peaks                of the emulsifier include: 3700-3200 cm-1,                3100-2700 cm-1, 2750-2200 cm-1, 1650-1350 cm-1,                1360-1000 cm-1, 630-570 cm-1 characteristic                absorption peaks; emulsifier can be contacted with                calcium oxide donor to obtain amine compound (I),                and (I) can be contacted with carbon dioxide donor                to reversibly obtain the emulsifier.
   USE - The emulsifier is useful in preparing                reversible emulsion and reversible drilling fluid                for cleaning oil-bearing drill cuttings and                oil-based filter cakes, removing low-density                useless solid phases, and recovery of waste                oil-containing liquid phase (all claimed); and in                oil-based mud cleaning including oil-containing                drill cuttings cleaning.
   ADVANTAGE - The emulsifier has cost reduction,                environmental protection, and prevention of                secondary pollution, and improves the economy and                environmental protection of drilling                projects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide/calcium oxide responsive                emulsifier is claimed. The emulsifier is product                obtained by contacting carbon dioxide donor with                amine compound of formula (I); infrared                characteristic peaks of the emulsifier include:                3700-3200 cm-1, 3100-2700 cm-1, 2750-2200 cm-1,                1650-1350 cm-1, 1360-1000 cm-1, 630-570 cm-1                characteristic absorption peaks; emulsifier can be                contacted with calcium oxide donor to obtain amine                compound (I), and (I) can be contacted with carbon                dioxide donor to reversibly obtain the                emulsifier.
   R = 12-22C linear saturated or unsaturated                hydrocarbon groups;
   R' and R'' = H or CH3;
   A' and A'' = O or NH; and
   m+n = less than or equal to 1-3.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing carbon dioxide/calcium oxide                responsive emulsifier comprising conducting first                contact reaction between carbon dioxide donor and                amine compound (I), where molar ratio of amount of                (I) to carbon dioxide donor is 1:0.55-1.5, method                of the first contact reaction is bubbling reaction                between carbon dioxide and the aqueous solution or                emulsion of (I), pressure of the first contact                reaction system is normal pressure or higher than                normal pressure, temperature of the first contact                reaction system is 5-200 degrees C;
   (2) reversible emulsion, comprising oil phase,                aqueous phase, and emulsifier, where emulsion can                be contacted with calcium oxide donor to obtain                water-in-oil emulsion containing (I), water-in-oil                emulsion can be contacted with a carbon dioxide                donor to reversibly obtain oil-in-water emulsion,                volume ratio of the oil phase to the aqueous phase                is 50:50-70:30, content of the emulsifier is 3-6 g                per 100 ml of the mixture of the oil phase and the                aqueous phase;
   (3) preparing reversible emulsion comprising                subjecting carbon dioxide donor and emulsion I                containing amine compound to a second contact                reaction under stirring conditions to obtain                reversible emulsion, where emulsion I also contains                oil phase and aqueous phase, based on (I) contained                in the emulsion I, molar ratio of the amount of                emulsion I to the carbon dioxide donor is                1:0.55-1.5, manner of the second contact reaction                is bubbling reaction between carbon dioxide and the                emulsion I, pressure of the second contact reaction                system is normal pressure or higher than normal                pressure, temperature of the second contact                reaction system is 5-200 degrees C, stirring speed                is 200-400 revolutions/minute, conditions of the                second contact reaction are controlled, obtained                emulsion at least satisfies: conductivity is above                1000 mu s/cm, the demulsification voltage is 6-10                V, and the pH value is 7-8;
   (4) reversible drilling fluid, comprising                oil-based stabilizer, organic soil, weighting                agent, oil phase, aqueous phase, and emulsifier,                where drilling fluid can be contacted with calcium                oxide donor to obtain water-in-oil drilling fluid                containing amine compound (I), water-in-oil                drilling fluid can be contacted with carbon dioxide                donor to reversibly obtain the oil-in-water                drilling fluid; and
   (5) preparing reversible drilling fluid                comprising subjecting carbon dioxide donor and                drilling fluid I containing amine compound to a                third contact reaction under stirring conditions to                obtain reversible drilling fluid, where drilling                fluid I also contains oil phase, aqueous phase,                oil-based stabilizer, organic soil and weighting                agent, based on amine compound contained in the                drilling fluid I, the molar ratio of the amount of                drilling fluid I to the carbon dioxide donor is                1:0.55-1.5, third contact reaction mode is carbon                dioxide bubbling reaction with the drilling fluid                I, pressure of the third contact reaction system is                normal pressure or higher than normal pressure,                temperature of the third contact reaction system is                5-200 degrees C, stirring speed is 200-400                revolutions/minute, conditions of the third contact                reaction are controlled so that the reversible                drilling fluid obtained after the third contact                reaction at least satisfies: conductivity is above                1000 mu s/cm, the demulsification voltage is 6-10                V, and the pH value is 7-8; and
   (6) use of reversible drilling fluid in                cleaning oil-bearing drill cuttings and oil-based                filter cakes, comprising cleaning oil-bearing drill                cuttings or oil-based filter cake include in the                presence of water, performing fourth contact                reaction between the carbon dioxide donor and the                oil-bearing drill cuttings or oil-based filter cake                with drilling fluid I attached, based on (I)                contained in the drilling fluid I, the molar ratio                of the amount of drilling fluid I to carbon dioxide                donor is 1:0.55-1.5, method of fourth contact                reaction is to bubble carbon dioxide into the                aqueous phase soaked with oil-containing drill                cuttings or soak the oil-based filter cake into the                carbonated aqueous solution, pressure of the fourth                contact reaction system is normal pressure or                higher than normal pressure, and temperature of the                fourth contact reaction system is 5-200 degrees                C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The volume ratio of                  oil phase to the aqueous phase is 50:50-70:30;                  based on every 100 ml of a mixture of oil and                  aqueous phases, content of the oil-based                  stabilizer is 1-2 g, and the content of the                  organic soil is 0.5-1 g, content of the weighting                  agent is such that the density of the reversible                  drilling fluid is 1.1-2 g/cm3, aqueous phase is a                  15-30 wt% calcium chloride aqueous solution, oil                  phase is at least one of No. 3 white oil, No.5                  white oil, diesel, and gas oil; weighting agent                  is at least one of calcium carbonate and barite.                  Preferred Method: The method comprises performing                  fifth contact reaction between carbon dioxide                  donor and the mixed system of the low-density                  useless solid phase and the drilling fluid I,                  where low-density useless solid phase is powder                  having average particle size of less than 75 mu                  m, based on (I) contained in drilling fluid I,                  molar ratio of amount of the drilling fluid I to                  the carbon dioxide donor is 1:0.15-0.35, manner                  of the fifth contact reaction is to bubble carbon                  dioxide into the mixed system containing the                  low-density useless solid phase and the drilling                  fluid I, pressure of the fifth contact reaction                  system is normal pressure, temperature of the                  fifth contact reaction system is 40-80 degrees C,                  conditions of the fifth contact reaction are                  controlled so that the drilling fluid system                  obtained after the fifth contact reaction at                  least satisfies: the demulsification voltage is                  300-400 V, the conductivity is 0-8 mu s/cm, and                  the pH value is 8.5-9. The method comprises                  performing sixth contact reaction between the                  calcium oxide donor and waste oil-containing                  liquid phase, and waste oil-containing liquid                  phase contains reversible drilling fluid, based                  on the emulsifier contained in the oil-containing                  liquid phase, the molar ratio of the calcium                  oxide donor to the oil-containing liquid phase is                  1-1.2:1, pressure of sixth contact reaction                  system is normal pressure, temperature of the                  sixth contact reaction system is 40-80 degrees C,                  mode of the sixth contact reaction is mixing and                  stirring, and the stirring speed is 300-450                  revolutions/minute, conditions of sixth contact                  reaction are controlled so that drilling fluid                  system obtained after the sixth contact reaction                  at least satisfies: demulsification voltage is                  600 V and above, electrical conductivity is less                  than 10 mu s/cm, and pH is 9.5-10.

PN CN109609436-A
TI Preparing madin-darby canine kidney cell suspension-free serum-free cell line without carbon dioxide environment, comprises e.g. resuscitating of madin-darby canine kidney adherent cells, culturing and incubating the culture.
AU AN F
   TAO S
   WU F
   TIAN B
   LI J
   GE Y
   CHAI L
   DONG W
   CHENG F
   LIU Q
   DING X
   LIU X
AE CHINESE AGRIC VET BIO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (CHAG-Non-standard)
GA 2019357907
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing Madin-Darby Canine Kidney (MDCK) cell suspension-free serum-free cell line without carbon dioxide environment, comprises e.g. resuscitating of MDCK adherent cells purchased from ATCC with good growth state, culturing in Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) culture medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS) after it grows to 85-90%, passaging 1:3 to the T25 square bottle (F10), adhering the adherent cells of the square bottle to the serum-free medium adherent culture of the MDCK cells by the stepwise adaptation method, acclimating to the cell growth rate, the cell state and the growth rate of the cells when the cell state is equivalent, using a MDCK cell suspension to serum-free medium, inserting the cell suspension into a 250 ml conical flask at a seeding density of 0.67-0.95x 106 cells/ml, culturing in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 72 hours before inoculation, incubating the culture further at a lower rotation speed for 2 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing madin-darby canine kidney cell suspension-free serum-free cell line without carbon dioxide environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for large-scale suspension culture of MDCK cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing Madin-Darby Canine Kidney (MDCK) cell suspension-free serum-free cell line without carbon dioxide environment, comprises (i) resuscitating of MDCK adherent cells purchased from ATCC with good growth state, culturing in Dulbecco's modified eagle medium (DMEM) culture medium containing 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS) after it grows to 85-90%, passaging 1:3 to the T25 square bottle (F10), (ii) adhering the adherent cells of the square bottle to the serum-free medium adherent culture of the MDCK cells by the stepwise adaptation method, acclimating to the cell growth rate, the cell state and the growth rate of the cells when the cell state is equivalent, using a MDCK cell suspension to serum-free medium, (iii) inserting the cell suspension into a 250 ml conical flask at a seeding density of 0.67-0.95x 106 cells/ml, culturing in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 72 hours before inoculation, incubating the culture further at a lower rotation speed for 2 hours, and culturing at a constant temperature of 70 revolutions/minutes, changing the every 72 hours but not divided, and culturing the cells after the growth rate of the cells became stable, diluting the cell density to 1.2-1.4x 106 cells/ml with fresh serum-free medium every 72 hours, increasing the rotation speed to 120 revolutions/minute, culturing subculture until MDCK cells at 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide constant temperature, the ability to lose the adhesion to the bottle wall in the box, the cell doubling rate, cell density and viability are basically stable, indicating that the cell line is basically adapted to suspension culture in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide incubator, and (iv) sterilizing the cell suspension by using a sterile venting plug and subjecting to the subculture culture, incubating in a 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide incubator for 48 hours, then transferring, incubating in a carbon dioxide-free incubator for 24 hours at a constant temperature, reducing the culture time in the carbon dioxide incubator every time the culture is divided, increasing the culture time in the carbon dioxide-free incubator, after passaging until the cells in the completely carbon dioxide-free thermostat, the doubling rate, cell density and viability are stable, and the cell morphology is good (round, full and translucent, uniform in size, no agglomeration), and indicating that the carbon dioxide-free environment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the serum-free medium means that no serum is added at all. In the step (iii), the 250 ml conical flask is subjected to a special treatment of dry baking for 50 minutes at 150 degrees C through a glass bottle of siliconizing agent before use. In the step (iii), firstly suspending and acclimating in the incubator of 37 degrees C, 5% carbon dioxide the operation step is static culture 30 minutes, 50 revolutions/minute constant temperature oscillation incubator 2 hours, 70 revolutions/minute constant temperature shaking culture at 72 hours. In the step (iii), the MDCK cells are cultured for at least 6 passages and cultured for at least 4 passages. In the step (iv), MDCK cells are subcultured for at least 6 generations, and a carbon dioxide-free environment, MDCK fully suspended serum-free cell line. The method comprises the invention selects an L9 (34) orthogonal table for testing, and selects key culture parameters are temperature (T), pH value, DO, stirring speed (Agit), and evaluates cell density and viability, the index determines the key process control parameters of MDCK cell-scale full-suspension serum-free culture to establish a large-scale production model of the cell line. The suspension culture key process parameters: the culturing temperature is 36.5-37 degrees C, the pH value is 6.8-7, the DO value is 55-60%, the stirring speed is 105-120 revolutions/minute. The invention establishes a 2L-10L-100L-500L linear step-by-step amplification of the cell line without a carbon dioxide environment scale production model.

TI Calculating carbon dioxide brine diffusion coefficient in combination with CT scan and PVT equation comprises e.g. using CT continuous scan to obtain steady-state and unsteady images under different temperature and pressure conditions.
AU SONG Y
   JIANG L
   LI X

   ZHAO Y
   YANG M
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
   UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201815344M
AB    NOVELTY - Calculating carbon dioxide brine diffusion coefficient in combination with CT scan and PVT equation comprises e.g. using CT continuous scan to obtain steady-state and unsteady images under different temperature and pressure conditions, filling the vacuum reactor with porous media, and injecting 1/4-1/3 volume of brine from the bottom of the reactor, using CT scan to obtain the image before carbon dioxide injection, injecting carbon dioxide into the reaction tank, maintaining stable target pressure, closing valve to form a closed system, the continuous CT scanning.
   USE - The method is useful for calculating carbon dioxide brine diffusion coefficient in combination with CT scan and PVT equation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Calculating carbon dioxide brine diffusion coefficient in combination with CT and PVT comprises (1) using CT continuous scan to obtain steady-state and unsteady images under different temperature and pressure conditions, filling the vacuum reactor with porous media, and injecting 1/4-1/3 volume of brine from the bottom of the reactor, using CT scan to obtain the image before carbon dioxide injection, injecting carbon dioxide into the reaction tank, maintaining stable target pressure, closing valve to form a closed system, the continuous CT scanning, the carbon dioxide diffuses into the dynamic image slice of the brine through the gas-liquid two-phase interface and the pressure data of the reactor is collected at the same time, (2) carrying out image processing and data processing, removing image slices taken from the CT scan for noise processing, performing three-dimensional reconstruction, and the principle of the relationship between gray levels and density of the CT imaging is combined to obtain CT images with different gray levels distribution, and extracting based on the difference in gas-liquid two-phase density and extracting the gas-liquid phase interface by using equation (1) as given in the specification, rho mix is the density of carbon dioxide-saline mixed phase, rho brine is the original density of brine, rho 0 is the density of two-phase interface, CT mix is the gray level of carbon dioxide-saline mixed phase, CT brine is the original gray of brine CT 0 is the gray level of the two-phase interface by using equation (2) as given in the specification, S nl is the area occupied by the liquid phase in each image slice, A is the cross-sectional area of the reactor, and S ng is the area occupied by the gas phase in each image slice, the volume of the CO2-bearing mixed phase obtained at different times is integrated, and the volume at different times is calculated using equation (3), (4) and (5) as given in the specification, V mix is the volume of the mixed phase at time t, h vox is the thickness of each slice, x is the number of slices containing the liquid phase for the dynamic diffusion process, x and S nl are both the diffusion time t and the initial injection pressure P 0 related variables h vox is related to the CT shooting resolution, FOVZ is the shooting distance in the vertical direction, N is the total number of slices, (3) calculating carbon dioxide brine diffusion coefficient, calculating mixed phase volume and the pressure-time data acquired during the diffusion process are brought into the PVT equation to obtain a dynamic distribution of the volume of the gas-liquid two-phase carbon dioxide material, the mass is plotted against the square root of the time, the slope of the curve is obtained by linear fitting and the diffusion coefficient of carbon dioxide is calculated based on the empirical formula of mass and time of mass transfer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises calculating the carbon dioxide brine diffusion coefficient includes calculating the mixed phase volume and combining with the pressure-time data, collecting during the diffusion process into the PVT equation to obtain the dynamic distribution of the amount of gas-liquid two-phase carbon dioxide material with equation (6) as given in the specification, Nt is the dissolved carbon dioxide molecular weight at time t, P 0 and P t are the gas pressures at the initial time and at the time t. V0 and Vt is the gas volumes at the initial time and t time respectively, the Z0 and Zt is the compression coefficient of the gas at the initial time and at time t, R is a constant, T is the temperature, where the equation (7) is as given in the specification, c in t is the interface concentration of gas-liquid two phases, Pe, Ve, and Ze is the equilibrium pressure, volume, and expansion coefficient, based on the empirical formula of mass transfer and time of mass transfer, the macroscopic diffusion coefficient of carbon dioxide to brine was calculated using equation (8), combining with the above formula, the formula for calculating the carbon dioxide diffusion coefficient using equation (9) as given in the specification, in order to simplify the calculation process, the slope of the quantity of the dissolved substance n t versus time is defined as k, and the calculation relationship between k and the diffusion coefficient D is equation (10) as given in the specification. The method specifically comprises calculating the carbon dioxide brine diffusion coefficient includes calculating the mixed phase volume and combining with the pressure-time data, collecting during the diffusion process into the PVT equation to obtain the dynamic distribution of the amount of gas-liquid two-phase carbon dioxide material with equation (6) as given in the specification, Nt is the dissolved carbon dioxide molecular weight at time t, P 0 and P t are the gas pressures at the initial time and at the time t. V0 and Vt is the gas volumes at the initial time and t time respectively, the Z0 and Zt is the compression coefficient of the gas at the initial time and at time t, R is a constant, T is the temperature, where the equation (7) is as given in the specification, c in t is the interface concentration of gas-liquid two phases, Pe, Ve, and Ze is the equilibrium pressure, volume, and expansion coefficient, based on the empirical formula of mass transfer and time of mass transfer, the macroscopic diffusion coefficient of carbon dioxide to brine was calculated using equation (8), combining with the above formula, the formula for calculating the carbon dioxide diffusion coefficient using equation (9) as given in the specification, in order to simplify the calculation process, the slope of the quantity of the dissolved substance n t versus time is defined as k, and the calculation relationship between k and the diffusion coefficient D is equation (10) as given in the specification.

TI Mixed separator used for carbon dioxide separation, comprises metal organic skeleton, and branched copolymer having preset particle size.
AU NOH D G
   ROH D K
AE KOREA INST CERAMIC ENG & TECHNOLOGY (KOCE-C)
GA 201962425U
AB    NOVELTY - A mixed separator comprises a metal organic skeleton, and a branched copolymer having a particle size of 100-800 nm.
   USE - Mixed separator used for separating carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The mixed separator has excellent adsorption performance, catalytic properties, and thermal stability. The branched copolymer has excellent hydrophobic property, affinity, and mechanical properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal precursor is chosen from titanium precursor, iron precursor, aluminum precursor, copper precursor and magnesium precursor. The titanium precursor is chosen from titanium butoxide, titanium isopropoxide, titanium chloride, and titanium propoxide. The iron precursor is chosen from tetrachloroferrite, ferric chloride, ferric citrate, ferric citrate hydrate, ferrous sulfate heptahydrate, iron oxalate dihydrate, iron acetylacetonate, ferric phosphate dihydrate, ferric hydroxide, ferric nitrate and iron acetate. The aluminum precursor is chosen from aluminum hydroxide, aluminum acetate, alumina trihydrate and aluminum chloride. The copper precursor is chosen from copper acetate, copper acetate hydrate, copper acetylacetonate, copper isobutyrate, copper carbonate, copper chloride, copper chloride hydrate, copper ethyl acetoacetate, copper 2-ethylhexanoate, copper fluoride, copper formate hydrate, copper gluconate, copper hexafluoroacetylacetonate, copper hexafluoroacetylacetonate hydrate, copper methoxide, copper neodecanoate, copper nitrate hydrate, copper nitrate, copper perchlorate hydrate, copper sulfate, copper sulfate hydrate, copper stannate hydrate, copper trifluoroacetylacetonate, copper trifluoromethanesulfonate, and tetraamine copper sulfate hydrate. The magnesium precursor is chosen from magnesium nitrate hexahydrate, magnesium sulfate, magnesium chloride and magnesium acetate. Preferred Properties: The carbon dioxide separation has permeability of 70-90 barrer, and permeation selectivity of 30-35.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic skeleton comprises organic linker, glycidyl compound, and metal precursor. The organic linker contains amine group in solvent is chosen from 2-aminoterephthalic acid, 4-aminophthalic acid, 4-aminoisophthalic acid, 5-aminoisophthalic acid, 2,5-diaminoterephthalic acid, 2,2'-diamino-4,4'-stilbendicarboxylic acid, 5-cyano-1,3-benzenedicarboxylic acid, 2-methylimidazole and 4,4,4"-s-triazine-2,4,6-triyl-tribenzoic acid. The solvent is chosen from methanol, ethanol, butanol, dimethylformamide, diethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, deionized water, N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone and dimethylacetamide. The organic skeleton comprises glycidyl compound chosen from glycerol diglycidyl ether, diglycidyl ether, ethylene glycol diglycidyl ether, diethylene glycol diglycidyl ether, propylene glycol diglycidyl ether, polyethylene glycol diglycidyl ether, poly (propylene glycol)diglycidyl ether, polyethylene glycol-epoxide, epoxy(2-hydroxy)propoxy-polyethylene glycol glycidyl ether and dodecyloxy-polyethylene glycol glycidyl ether. Preferred Composition: The mixed separator comprises 0.3-10 wt.%, preferably 0.3-1.0 wt.% organic linker in organic linker solution. The molar ratio of organic linker and glycidyl compound is 1:0.01-1.0, preferably 1:0.05-1.0. The molar ratio of organic linker and metal precursor is 40:60-90:10, preferably 60:40-40:60.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of metal organic skeleton and branched copolymer is 5.95-40.60. Preferred Components: The branched copolymer is copolymer obtained by grafting poly(ethylene glycol)methyl ether (meth)acrylate to polyvinyl chloride. The halogenated polymer compound comprises polyvinylidene fluoride-co-chlorotrifluoroethylene, polyvinyl chloride, polychlorotrifluoroethylene, polydichlorodifluoromethane, polyvinylidene dichloride, and/or their copolymers. The hydrophilic monomer is chosen from polyoxyethylene (meth)acrylate, poly(ethylene glycol)methyl ether (meth)acrylate, hydroxyethyl (meth)acrylate, hydrolyzed t-butyl (meth)acrylate, acrylamide, N-vinylpyrrolidone, aminostyrene, styrene sulfonic acid, methylpropene sulfonic acid, sulfopropyl (meth)acrylate, sulfoethyl (meth)acrylate and sulfobutyl (meth)acrylate.

C to 750?? C, or adding iron-containing                compound as a catalyst, then reducing the                temperature and introducing carbon dioxide and                water mixed gas to obtain sodium carbonate and                sodium bicarbonate, and using the obtained sodium                bilicarbonate for desulfurization, where the                gas-reducing agent is hydrogen, carbon monoxide or                mixed gas. PN CN114639805-A
TI Preparing porous structure nickel phosphide-carbon            anode electrode material, involves mixing nickel salt            solution with precipitate agent mixing and reacting,            and introducing carbon dioxide gas to control reaction            after finishing reaction, aging solid-liquid separation            to obtain precipitate.
AU LI C
   ZHANG X
   LI A
   YU H
   XIE Y
AE HUNAN BRUNP AUTOMOBILE CIRCULATION CO LT (NDCT-C)
   HUNAN BRUNP RECYCLING TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NDCT-C)
   GUANGDONG BRUNP RECYCLING TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202287396X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing porous structure nickel                phosphide-carbon anode electrode material involves                mixing the nickel salt solution with precipitate                agent mixing and reacting, and introducing carbon                dioxide gas to control the reaction pH to be                10.8-11.5, after finishing reaction, aging                solid-liquid separation to obtain precipitate, the                precipitator is sodium hydroxide, mixed solution of                sodium tetrahydroxy sodium aluminate and sodium                persulfate; placing the precipitate at the lower                air port of the tubular furnace, taking anhydrous                sodium hypophosphite to place at the upper air port                of the tubular furnace, the tubular furnace heating                a period of time, taking out the precipitate and                soaking in the sodium hydroxide solution,                separating solid and liquid to obtain the porous;                mixing the porous organic matter, carrying out                carbonization reaction under the isolation of                oxygen to obtain the porous structure nickel                phosphide-carbon negative electrode material.
   USE - Method for preparing porous structure nickel                phosphide-carbon anode electrode material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the contact area between                the electrodes and the electrolyte and has high                capacity, excellent cycle and rate                performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of                the preparation method in sodium ion battery or                lithium-ion battery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of                  the nickel salt solution is 1-2 mol/L in the                  precipitator, the concentration of the tetra                  hydroxy sodium aluminate is 0.05-0.2mol/L, the                  concentration of the sodium hydroxide is                  3-6mol/L, the concentration of the sodium                  persulfate is 1-2mol/L, the mixing way is                  parallel flow adding, the mol ratio of nickel and                  aluminum is 10:(1-2) to control the flow rate of                  the nickel salt solution and the precipitate                  agent. The nickel salt solution is nickel                  sulfate, nickel chloride or nickel nitrate                  solution. The solid-liquid separation to obtain                  the precipitate, further comprises the                  precipitate to wash and dry. The mass ratio of                  the anhydrous sodium hypophosphite to the                  precipitate is (8-15):1. The heating temperature                  of the tubular furnace is 300-400degrees Celsius;                  the heating time of the tubular furnace is                  120-180 min. The preparation method taking out                  the precipitate, it is first cooled to below                  10degrees Celsius, and the temperature of the                  sodium hydroxide solution is 2-8degrees Celsius.                  The concentration of the sodium hydroxide                  solution is 0.1-2 mol/L; the soaking time is                  10-25 min. The organic substance is sucrose,                  glucose or lactose.

PN CN114621488-A
TI Preparing flame retardant polyethylene            terephthalate foaming material comprises e.g.            functional flame-retardant chain-extended polymer,            mixing with polyethylene terephthalate pellets,            performing extrusion foaming using carbon dioxide as            foaming agent, and cooling.
AU LAI W
AE JIANGSU YUEKE NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202283206W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing flame retardant polyethylene                terephthalate (PET) foaming material comprises (1)                adding melamine and phosphate into four-neck                reaction flask, injecting solvent, stirring at low                speed at certain temperature, dissolving and fully                mixing the reaction system to obtain solution A;                adding olefin siloxane and epoxy compound to the                solvent to obtain solution B; pouring solution A                into solution B, adding initiator, mixing evenly,                and continuously reacting for many hours at certain                temperature to obtain functional flame-retardant                chain-extended polymer, (2) adding the functional                flame retardant chain-extended polymer into the PET                pellets in certain proportion, mixing evenly with                mechanical mixer, adding it into the hopper of the                extruder, and flowing into the extruder at uniform                speed in the extruding screw, selecting carbon                dioxide as foaming agent, and performing extrusion                foaming, and cooling to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing flame                retardant polyethylene terephthalate (PET) foaming                material.
   ADVANTAGE - The flame retardant polyethylene terephthalate                foaming material has high flame retardance and                improved mechanical property. The macromolecule                flame retardant chain extender not only has good                compatibility with the PET, and the epoxy                functional group can better react with the terminal                carboxy of the PET. The PET has obvious lifting in                the shearing and stretching aspect, and can be at a                higher level.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In step (i), melamine and                  phosphoric acid ester are firstly added to the                  four-necked reaction flask, and after being                  injected into the solvent, the mixture is stirred                  at a low speed at high temperature of                  60-150&#8451;. In step (i), solution A is                  poured into solution B, initiator is added, the                  mixture is evenly mixed, and the reaction is                  continued at a temperature of 30-60&#8451;                  for 4-8 hours. In step (ii): the temperature of                  single screw in the extrusion foaming process is                  set to 230-260&#8451;, and the temperature of                  the twin-screw is set to 260-290&#8451;; the                  added amount of CO2in PET extrusion foaming                  accounts for 0.3-0.8% of the total; the                  rotational speed of the single screw of the                  extruder is preferably 1-6 revolutions/minute,                  and the rotational speed of the twin-screw of the                  extruder is preferably 60-120                  revolutions/minute.Preferred Components: In step (ii), the                  functional flame-retardant chain-extended polymer                  is added to the PET at 0.5-3 %. In step (i), the                  functional flame-retardant chain-extended polymer                  is polymer compound of formula (I), olefin                  monomers containing X and Y functional groups are                  double bond compounds containing hydroxy groups.                  The hydroxy molar content of the hydroxyacrylic                  acid phosphate ester is greater than 0 and not                  greater than 15%; the functionalities m, n, z of                  the olefin melamine polymer, polyolefin siloxane                  and epoxy polyolefin are preferably 1-5.X =                  2,4,6-tris(dimethoxyamino)-1,3,5-triazine moiety                  of formula (II);R, R1, R2, Y = methyl, ethyl, phenyl,                  benzyl, phenethyl;                                                                                                                                                                                                
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the                  solvents are all tetrahydrofuran solvents. In                  step (i), the initiator is organic initiator                  compound includes benzoyl peroxide, tert-butyl                  benzoyl peroxide, methyl ethyl ketone peroxide,                  or azobisisobutyronitrile, azobisisoheptyl                  nitrile, and/or azoisobutylcyanoformamide.

PN CN114602498-A
TI Sea urchin-like In2O3 supported Cu-Ag bimetallic            alloy catalyst comprises the carrier is In2O3 with a            sea urchin-like structure, the active components are Cu            and Ag,.
AU FENG N
   WANG L
   DING J
   WAN H
   SONG S
   GUAN G
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 202283290L
AB    NOVELTY - Sea urchin-like In2O3 supported Cu-Ag                bimetallic alloy catalyst comprises the carrier is                In2O3 with a sea urchin-like structure, the active                components are Cu and Ag, the loading mass of                copper is 1-5% of the mass of the In2O3 carrier and                the loading mass of silver is 0.1-5% of the mass of                the In2O3 carrier.
   USE - The sea urchin-shaped In2O3-loaded Cu-Ag                bimetallic alloy catalyst is useful in the                production of acetic acid from methane and carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The sea urchin-shaped In2O3 load Cu-Ag                bimetallic alloy catalyst can catalyze and convert                methane and carbon dioxide to prepare acetic acid                and has good catalytic activity and                selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing sea urchin-shaped In2O3 loaded CuAg                bimetallic alloy catalyst comprising (1) adding the                indium salt and the bicarbonate radical salt                preparation water solution, then adding surface                active agent; (2) adding the alcohol and amine into                the water solution obtained in the step (1),                stirring, hydrothermal reaction, cooling,                centrifuging, washing, vacuum dry (3) preparing the                silver salt and copper salt into Cu-Ag aqueous                solution, then adding the solid obtained in the                step (2) into Cu-Ag aqueous solution, then water                bath aging, centrifuging and washing, vacuum, dry                the prepared solid under the H2-Ar mixed atmosphere                to obtain the sea urchin-shaped In2O3 load Cu-Ag                bimetallic alloy catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the                  indium salt is one of indium nitrate, indium                  chloride, indium chloride or indium sulphate;                  bicarbonate salt is one of sodium bicarbonate,                  calcium bicarbonate, ammonium bicarbonate or                  potassium bicarbonate; surface active agent is                  one of sodium dodecyl sulfate or sodium dodecyl                  sulfate. n the step (1), the mol ratio of the                  indium salt and bicarbonate salt is 1: (2 to 10),                  the mol ratio of indium salt to surfactant is 1:                  (1-9). The alcohol in the step (2) is ethanol,                  isopropanol, n-butyl alcohol, glycerol, glycol,                  glycol or 1, 2-ethylene glycol; amine is                  triethylene tetramine, triethylene diamine or                  trientine. The mol ratio of alcohol to amine in                  step (2) is (2-20): 1, the mol ratio of alcohol                  to indium salt is (6-200): 1; the temperature of                  the hydrothermal reaction is 120-260degrees                  Celsius, the hydrothermal reaction time is 6-24                  hours. The silver salt is silver nitrate, silver                  chloride, silver sulfate or silver sulfite. The                  copper salt is copper sulphate, copper nitrate,                  copper chloride, copper citrate, copper                  acetylacetonate or copper oxalate.Preferred Method: The temperature of the                  water bath in step (3) is 30-80degrees Celsius,                  the aging time is 1-8 hours, the vacuum drying                  temperature is 50-80degrees Celsius and dry time                  is 2-24 hours, the H2 in H2/Ar mixed gas is 5-95%                  of total volume of H2/Ar mixed gas, the reducing                  temperature is 400-600degrees Celsius and the                  reducing time is 0.5-8 hours. The reaction                  temperature is 300-700degrees Celsius, the                  reaction space velocity is 283-2200 h1 and the                  volume ratio of methane and carbon dioxide is 1:                  (0.7-1.2).

TI Oxidative post-processing of nitrided or nitrocarburized article, involves providing article in furnace, preparing fluid mixture containing water and carbon dioxide and nitrogen, and controlling water content of fluid mixture.
AU ASTROEM A
AE LINDE GMBH (LINM-C)
GA 202277152Q
AB    NOVELTY - Oxidative post-processing of a nitrided or nitrocarburized article, comprises providing the article in a furnace (120), preparing a fluid mixture containing water and carbon dioxide and/or nitrogen, controlling the water content, and preferably the dew point, of the fluid mixture, providing the mixture to the furnace, and heating the furnace to a predetermined temperature. The predetermined temperature is 400-600 degrees C, preferably 450-580 degrees C. The furnace is kept in a heated state for a period of time selected so that a thickness of an oxide layer on the article reaches or exceeds 1-3 mu m.
   USE - The method is useful for oxidative post-processing of nitrided or nitrocarburized article e.g. gun or rifle barrels, slides, bearings, drills, spindles, gears, dies and hydraulic pistons.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxidative post-treatment can be carried out in a controlled manner with high precision with less expected furnace wear and maintenance resulting in improved furnace lifetime. Post-treatment oxidation of the surface can further enhance corrosion resistance and the visual appearance of the object's surface.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for apparatus (100) for oxidative post-processing of a nitrided or nitrocarburized article.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a simplified block diagram of the apparatus.

AE ANHUI FUHUAI MINING SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO (ANHU-Non-standard)

PN CN114403207-A
TI Storing plum in controlled atmosphere at ice temperature comprises measuring freezing point temperature of plum, storing in ice-temperature air-conditioned fresh-keeping warehouse, cooling, adjusting volume of oxygen and carbon dioxide and carrying out ice temperature controlled atmosphere storage.
AU BATUER M
   MA W
   BASITI A
   SHEN X
   ZHU Z
   LIU J
   SUN L
   YANG Z
   YANG L
   CUI K
AE AGRIC MECHANIZATION RES INST XINJINAG AG (AGRI-Non-standard)
GA 202264421W
AB    NOVELTY - Storing plum in controlled atmosphere at ice temperature comprises (i) measuring the freezing point temperature of the plum to determine the storage temperature, where the storage temperature is -5 to 0degrees Celsius; (ii) storing the plums in an ice-temperature air-conditioned fresh-keeping warehouse, and cooling in steps until the internal temperature of the plum pulp cooled to the storage temperature; and (iii) adjusting the volume concentration of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the ice temperature controlled atmosphere fresh-keeping store-house to the target concentration, and carrying out the ice temperature controlled atmosphere storage.
   USE - The method is useful for storing plum in controlled atmosphere at ice temperature, preferably plum is Rosaceae plum and belongs to the European plum species.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: combines the air conditioning fresh-keeping and near ice temperature technology to determine the ice temperature belt of the plum thus reduces the metabolism tissue, and maintains the good original quality; provides always a suitable gas environment by delaying deterioration and preventing the occurrence of spoilage thus reaches the purpose of storing and refreshing; and maintains the quality of the plums during the storage period by delaying the quality deterioration of the plums e.g. softening, dehydration and spoilage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The temperature adjustment range of the ice-temperature controlled atmosphere fresh-keeping storehouse is -5 to 0degrees Celsius and the adjustment range of the oxygen volume concentration is 1-19%, and carbon dioxide volume concentration is 0-5%. The temperature fluctuation of the ice-temperature controlled atmosphere fresh-keeping storehouse is less than or equal to0.5degrees Celsius. The cooling rate of the step cooling is 8-10degrees Celsius/8 hours and the cooling are performed from room temperature. The storage time is 20-50 days. The method comprises heating plum at the end of storage in steps from the storage temperature to the cold chain temperature, where the gas in the ice-temperature air-conditioned fresh-keeping warehouse is replaced with air, filling ethylene gas to accelerate the ripening, and discharging the obtained plums from the warehouse. The temperature of the cold chain is 10-20degrees Celsiusand the time from the storage temperature to the temperature of the cold chain is 24 hours. The ripening time is 2-48 hours. The storage period is ended by means of integral warehousing and integral delivery.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The concentration of the ethylene gas in the ice-temperature controlled atmosphere fresh-keeping warehouse is 1-1000 ppm.

PN CN114390791-A
TI Printed circuit board (PCB) dry ice particle etching method involves spraying dry ice particle on PCB board vertically upward or downwardunder action of injection pressure and vertically penetrating etching liquid downward under extrusion of dry air particles.
AU XIA G
   WANG H
   LIAO R
   ZOU J
AE VICTORY GIANT TECHNOLOGY HUIZHOU CO LTD (VICT-Non-standard)
GA 2022613598
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves spraying dry ice particles to upper and lower sides of a PCB board in an etching groove spraying device. The dry ice particle is vertically upward or downward sprayed on the PCB board under the action of injection pressure. An etching liquid is vertically penetrated downward under the extrusion of the dry air particles. The dried ice particles dissolve into carbon dioxide gas in the etching solution to separate out, and diffusion from the middle of the board to two sides, so that the copper surface etching on the two side walls of the PCB is suppressed.
   USE - Printed circuit board dry ice particle etching method.
   ADVANTAGE - The good etching uniformity and low side etching degree are ensured by the method. The etching liquid in the etching is groove penetrated downward under the extrusion of the dry ice particles, so that etching of the PCB board is more uniform and the effect is better. The method allows carbon dioxide gas molecules to be diffused from the middle of the board to the two sides so that the copper surface etching on the two side walls of the PCB board is inhibited by an etching method.

AU HUANG T
   GAO S
AE UNIV ANHUI (UANH-C)
GA 202259492X
AB    NOVELTY - Composite material with uniform carbon-coated indium nitride nanoparticles, where the indium nitride nano-sheet particles are coated with a uniform carbon layer.
   USE - The nanoparticle is used as a catalyst for electrocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nanoparticle has broad application prospects in practical large-scale industrial applications.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing uniform carbon-coated indium nitride nanoparticles, comprising (1) taking indium chloride tetrahydrate and adding into deionized water, stirring at room temperature for 20-40 minutes, adding aqueous ammonia and stirring for 6-14 hours to form a white precipitate, washing the precipitate with deionized water and ethanol, and freeze drying for 5-12 hours to get white powder, (2) placing dried nanosheets in a quartz boat and placing in a tube furnace, calcining at 800 degrees Celsius for 1 hours to obtain a black-brown powder, and (3) adding polyvinylpyrrolidone to deionized water to form a transparent solution, and adding the dark brown powder obtained in step (2) above, stirring at room temperature for 7-13 hours to form a dark brown precipitate, washing the precipitate with deionized water and ethanol and vacuum drying for 5-12 hours, placing the dried black powder in a quartz boat and placing in a tube furnace, in an argon atmosphere, the temperature is programmed to 400-600degrees Celsius, and calcining at 400-600degrees Celsius for 0.5-2 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The thickness of the carbon layer is uniform, and the thickness value is 3-5 nm.Preferred Method: In step (1), under the premise of not changing the addition ratio of the three reactants, increasing the amount of reactants can achieve a large number of synthesis. When realizing a large amount of synthesis, the volume ratio of ammonia water and deionized water is 75-80:1, and the mass ratio of indium chloride tetrahydrate and deionized water is 1:100. In the step (2), the average heating rate of the ammonia atmosphere is 14degrees Celsius/minute. In the step (3), the average heating rate of the argon atmosphere is 15degrees Celsius/minute.

PN CN113831658-A
TI Preparing high flame retardant plastic extruded insulation plate comprises e.g. stirring each raw material, using oil-type mold temperature machine to heat raw materials to molten state, injecting carbon dioxide into molten raw material, adding foaming catalyst, and completing foaming reaction.
AU CHEN J
AE ANHUI DINGXIAN ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOGY (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 202235166P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high flame retardant plastic extruded insulation plate comprises (i) weighing each raw material, and stirring it; (ii) using an oil-type mold temperature machine to heat the above-mentioned raw materials for 2 hours to molten state for subsequent use; (iii) injecting carbon dioxide into the molten raw material, and adding foaming catalyst; (iv) after completing foaming reaction, adding the above-mentioned molten mixture to extruder, extruding for two times, extruding by second-stage extruder, guiding by leveling tractor, and intermittently transporting by unpowered conveyor belt to obtain whole insulation plate; and (v) levelling the edge on both sides of whole insulation plate by centering and trimming machine, and cutting whole insulation plate into fixed lengths by length cross-cutting machine to obtain the finished product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high flame retardant plastic extruded insulation plate.
   ADVANTAGE - The insulation plate: has moisture-proof and waterproof properties, which can reduce the thickness of the outer protective structure of the building, thus increasing the indoor use area; and has flame retardant property.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for the high flame retardant plastic extruded insulation plate is prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The insulation plate comprises 50-60 pts. wt. polystyrene, 15-20 pts. wt. flame retardant filler, 1-2 pts. wt. color masterbatch, 2-3 pts. wt. alumina fine powder, 1-2 pts. wt. anti-ultraviolet agent and, structural stabilizer. The used structural stabilizer is expanded graphite powder, and the amount structural stabilizer is 8-15 wt.% of polystyrene resin. The component of the anti-ultraviolet agent is ortho-hydroxybenzoate. The flame retardant filler is composed of calcined kaolin, nano-silicon dioxide, and magnesium hydroxide in mass ratio of 3:2:1. The particle size of the alumina fine powder is 10-15 mum. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), the heating temperature is 195-210degreesC, and the internal pressure of the oil-type mold temperature machine is 15-16 MPa. In the step (iii), the foaming catalyst is ethanol, and reaction pressure is controlled at 5-6 MPa.

PN CN113735143-A
TI Method for preparing high-purity lithium carbonate, involves introducing carbon dioxide into lithium hydroxide refined solution to perform carbonization operation, stirring and washing lithium carbonate wet material by pure water, heating and drying to obtain high-purity lithium carbonate product.
AU JIN Y
   LUO F
   CAI J
   YIN X
   LI Z
   QIAN P
   ZHANG Y
   XIAO J
   CHEN L
   CHEN C
   ZHOU F
   FU L
   AI G
   LI L
AE JIANGXI GANFENG LITHIUM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021E52856
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves putting raw material lithium hydroxide into a container, adding pure water, fully stirring and dissolving, filtering insoluble substances by using an ultrafiltration membrane, and collecting filtrate. The calcium, silicon, iron and sulfur oxide impurity ions are removed from the filtrate by a nanofiltration device. The lithium hydroxide refined solution by impurity ions is obtained. The carbon dioxide is introduced into the lithium hydroxide refined solution to perform carbonization operation, the pH value of the solution is controlled, and separated by a centrifuge to obtain a lithium carbonate wet material. The lithium carbonate wet material is stirred and washed by pure water, and heated and dried to obtain a high-purity lithium carbonate product.
   USE - Method for preparing high-purity lithium carbonate. Uses include but are not limited to magnetic material industry, atomic energy industry, electronic industry and optical instrument industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple in process, high in conversion rate, low in product impurity, and the device requirement is not high suitable for mass production. The high-purity lithium carbonate is produced with high conversion rate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for preparing high-purity lithium carbonate. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

PN CN113651992-A
TI Preparing shape memory aerogel comprises e.g. carrying out first reaction between mercapto reagent and olefin reagent in non-alcoholic organic solvent under action of benzoyl polymerization catalyst, aging, replacing solvent, and performing carbon dioxide supercritical drying.
AU PAN Y
   HE P
   CHENG X
AE UNIV CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UCST-C)
GA 2021D96915
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing shape memory aerogel comprises (i) carrying out the first reaction between mercapto reagent and olefin reagent in a non-alcoholic organic solvent under oxygen-free and stirring conditions and under the action of the benzoyl polymerization catalyst, (ii) carrying out the second reaction of the mixture obtained in the step (i) under the action of aromatic tertiary amine, and under oxygen-free and stirring conditions, (iii) carrying out the third reaction of reaction mixture obtained in step (iii) under the action of water and a phase transfer catalyst, aging the obtained reaction mixture, and replacing the solvent to obtain a gel, and (iv) performing carbon dioxide supercritical drying of the gel obtained in step (iii).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing shape memory aerogel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple flow, high controllability, high accuracy, and simple operation. The aerogel: has high porosity, low density, a pore diameter of 200-106 nm, a porosity of 80-99%, a high shape recovery rate of 95-100%, and a glass transition temperature of -60degreesC to 180degreesC.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a shape memory aerogel prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mercapto reagent comprises 1,6-hexanedithiol, isooctyl mercaptoacetate, mercaptopropyl methyldimethoxysilane, ethyl mercaptoacetate, (3-mercaptopropyl) trimethoxysilane, tetra (3-mercaptopropionic acid) pentaerythritol ester, and/or 3-mercapto-4-methyl-4H 1,2,4-triazole and pentaerythritol ester. The olefin reagent comprises triallyl isocyanurate, vinyl trimethylethoxysilane and/or hexamethylene diisocyanate trimer. The non-alcoholic organic solvent comprises acetone or acetonitrile. The benzoyl polymerization catalyst comprises hydrogen peroxide, benzoyl peroxide, potassium persulfate, tert-butanol peroxide, azobisisobutyronitrile and/or azobisisoheptonitrile. In the step (i), the molar ratio of the mercapto group in the mercapto reagent to the vinyl group in the olefin reagent is 1:1. The molar ratio of the non-alcoholic organic solvent to the total amount of the mercapto reagent and the olefin reagent is 1: (5-200). The molar ratio of the benzoyl polymerization catalyst to the mercapto group is 1: (1-100). The aromatic tertiary amine comprises N-methylbutylamine, succinimide, butylamine, butyramide, N-methylaniline, p-ethoxyaniline, N,N-dimethylbutylamine or N, N-dimethylaniline. In the step (ii), the volume ratio of the aromatic tertiary amine to the non-alcoholic organic solvent is 1:150-300. The phase transfer catalyst comprises butanamide, tetramethylammonium hydroxide solution, N-methylaniline, sodium hydroxide aqueous solution, or ammonia aqueous solution. In the step (iii), the volume ratio of the water and the aromatic tertiary amine is 1:0.1-10. The volume ratio of the phase transfer catalyst to the aromatic tertiary amine is 1:1-10. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the first reaction is carried out at room temperature under the protection of argon for 10-80 minutes. In the step (ii), the second reaction is carried out at room temperature under the protection of argon for 60-800 minutes. In the step (iii), the third reaction is carried out at room temperature for 10-200 minutes. The aging is carried out at 20-120degreesC for 1-10 days. The solvent replacement is carried out at by using an alcohol solvent or a ketone solvent as replacement solvent for 1-3 times for 1-5 days. The carbon dioxide supercritical drying is carried out under 18-22 MPa for 3-5 days.

TI Catalyst useful for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide comprises ternary composite oxide carrier including combinations of cerium, zinc, zirconium, aluminum and/or titanium, and metal active particles including copper, iron, cobalt, or nickel.
AU CHEN Y
   LIU X

AE INST URBAN ENVIRONMENT CHINESE ACAD SCI (CUBN-C)
GA 2021A2638Q
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises a ternary composite oxide carrier and metal active particles. The ternary in the ternary composite oxide carrier includes any three combinations of cerium, zinc, zirconium, aluminum and/or titanium. The ternary composite oxide carrier has a hierarchical pore structure in which macropores, mesopores and micropores exist simultaneously. The metal active particles include a combination of copper, iron, cobalt, or nickel.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for the hydrogenation of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: exerts the function of multiple oxides; and improves the catalyst conversion and selectivity in the catalytic reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for preparing catalyst comprising (1) mixing the soft template base material, citric acid, the first solvent for one time, adding precursor salt, mixing for the second time, soaking into the hard template assembly array to obtain the ternary composite oxide carrier, (2) soaking the ternary composite oxide carrier in the metal active particle salt solution to obtain the catalyst, where the metal element in the precursor salt is combinations of cerium, zinc, zirconium, aluminum and/or titanium, the metal active particle salt solution comprises copper salt solution, iron salt solution, cobalt salt solution and/or nickel salt solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The raw material comprises 30-50 wt.% metal active particles. The ternary compound oxide carrier is cerium, zinc and zirconium. In the step (1), the soft template substrate includes polyethylene oxide-polypropylene oxide-polyethylene oxide triblock copolymer. The concentration of the soft template substrate is 0.02-0.08 g/ml, preferably, the citric acid is 0.05-0.2 g/ml. The first solvent includes ethylene glycol and/or methanol. In the step (1), the hard template includes a polymer hard template and/or a silica hard template. The polymer hard template includes a polystyrene hard template and/or a polymethyl methacrylate hard template. Preferred Method: The preparation of the hard template assembly array includes mixing the hard template substrate, the second solvent and the initiator, heating and reacting, centrifugation once and centrifugation twice to obtain an orderly arranged hard template assembly array, preferably, the hard template substrate includes styrene, methyl methacrylate and/or silica. In the step (1), after the immersion of the hard template to assemble the array, sequentially performing drying and sintering, preferably, the drying temperature is 45-55 degrees C, preferably, the drying time is 18-28 hours, the sintering includes primary sintering and secondary sintering, preferably, the atmosphere for the primary sintering is a protective atmosphere, preferably the protective atmosphere includes a nitrogen atmosphere, an argon atmosphere, or a helium atmosphere, the heating rate in the primary sintering is 1-3 degrees C/minute, the temperature of the primary sintering is 300-350 degrees C, preferably the time for the primary sintering is 2-5 hours, preferably, performing the secondary sintering in an air atmosphere, the heating rate of the secondary sintering is 1-3 degrees C/minute, the temperature of the secondary sintering is 450-600 degrees C, preferably the time of the secondary sintering is 3-5 hours. In the step (2), the dipping time is 6-14 hours. In the step (2), after finishing the dipping, drying, preferably, the drying temperature is 80-120 degrees C. In the step (2), adding additives to the metal active particle salt solution, preferably, the additive includes rare earth salt solution, potassium salt or sodium salt.

The production technology of the production device provides maximum effect of intensification effect. The thick oil produced by the oil well is processed to anti-sticking treatment to light and injected into the well in hot state, exerting the function of heat oil displacement while exerting the function of diluting and displacing oil. The method enables collecting and utilizing the flue gas and carbon dioxide gas generated using the oil-displacing agent as gas-displacing oil.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a thickened oil viscosity-reducing integrated comprehensive oil displacement system. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

The reaction of the intermediate product is carried out at 110 degrees C for 24-72 hours. The cooling is slow cooling to room temperature for 2-8 hours. PT P
PN CN112410882-A
TI Preparing whitening calcium carbonate whisker by adding calcium hydroxide to water, adding magnesium salt, dispersing, pouring carbon dioxide into dispersed solution, adding tetrabutyl titanate and acetic acid solution and sintering.
AU CHEN Q
   ZENG J
   SI W
   LI J
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM ENG SCI & TECHNIC (XKLI-C)
GA 2021271532
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing whitening calcium carbonate whisker involves (i) adding calcium hydroxide to water to prepare a suspension, adding magnesium salt, stirring and dispersing at a high speed of 500-600 rpm for 1-3 hours to prepare solution (A), where the weight ratio of calcium hydroxide, water and magnesium salt is (10-15):(60-80):(1-2), (ii) mixing acetic acid, water and ethanol to prepare solution (B) with a pH of 4-5, where the weight ratio of the acetic acid, water and ethanol is (1-3):(6-8):(3-5), (iii) pouring carbon dioxide into the solution (A), stirring to gradually form calcium carbonate whisker under induction of magnesium salt, soaking, washing and drying to obtain calcium carbonate whisker, and (iv) adding the calcium carbonate whisker to a mixer for high-speed stirring and suspension, adding tetrabutyl titanate to solution (B), spraying into the mixer, hydrolyzing tetrabutyl titanate to coat on surface of whisker, and sintering at 400-500 degrees C to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing whitening calcium carbonate whisker for plastic reinforcement (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The obtained calcium carbonate whisker is white in color, achieves the purpose of both strength and whitening, and overcomes the defect of difficult to cover fibrous material by directly adding granular titanium dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In step (iii), the continuous introduction rate of carbon dioxide into the solution (A) is 30-60 ml/minute, and the stirring rate is 100-200 rpm. In step (iv), the stirring rate of the high-speed stirring is 800-1200 rpm, and the stirring time is 3-5 hours. Preferred Components: In step (i), the magnesium salt comprises magnesium chloride, magnesium hydroxide, magnesium sulfate, magnesium borate, magnesium oxide, and/or magnesium nitrate, preferably magnesium chloride. Preferred Composition: In step (i), the weight ratio of acetic acid, water and ethanol is 3:7:4. In step (i), the weight ratio of calcium hydroxide, water and magnesium salt 12:70:1.5. In step (iv), the mixing mass ratio of the tetrabutyl titanate and the solution (B) is 1:(2-4), preferably 1:3, and the spray amount of the tetrabutyl titanate after adding the solution (B) is 15-18 %mass, preferably 16 %mass.

PN CN109877418-A; CN109877418-B
TI Preparing surfacing of take-up side guide            comprises e.g. processing wear groove area of 718            material side guide of coiler into U-shaped groove by            carbon arc gouging and using argon and carbon dioxide            mixed gas shielded welding.
AU LI L
   TIAN M
   HAN L
   ZHU C
AE JIANGSU SHAGANG IRON & STEEL RES INST CO (JSSG-C)
   ZHANGJIAGANG HONGCHANG STEEL PLATE CO (JSSG-C)
   JIANGSU SHAGANG GROUP CO LTD (JSSG-C)
GA 201955667U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing surfacing of take-up side guide                comprises (i) processing wear groove area of 718                material side guide of the coiler into U-shaped                groove by carbon arc gouging, and the inner                carbonization layer of the groove and the sides of                the groove are 30-50 mm inside to reveal a metallic                luster and the inside of the groove is smooth and                smooth; (ii) using 80 wt.% argon and 20 wt.% carbon                dioxide mixed gas shielded welding, using                wear-resistant surfacing flux cored wire for                surfacing coiling side guide plate and each weld                seam is immediately hammered to the weld surface,                miscellaneous goods in the road, filling the arc                pit at the arc of the weld and weld layer by layer                until the weld is filled; and (iii) cooling the                weld to room temperature after the welding is                finished and polishing the weld bead to be flush                with surface of the take-up side guide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing surfacing                of take-up side guide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, has less surfacing                process and high repairing efficiency, multiple                repairs can be performed to reduce production cost                and realizes reeling of the take-up side                guides.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The U-shaped groove                  angle is 20 plus minus 5 degrees , the bottom                  edge of the groove is curved and the groove                  surface width is 10-28 mm and depth is 7-14 mm.                  The wear-resistant surfacing flux cored wire has                  a diameter of 1.6 mm, and the alloy system is a                  low carbon-chromium-molybdenum-nickel alloy, and                  the chemical components includes 0.1-0.2 wt.%                  carbon, 0.5-1 wt.% silicon, 0.5-1 wt.% manganese,                  9-10 wt.% chromium, 2-3 wt.% molybdenum, 1.5-2                  wt.% nickel, phosphorus less than or equal to                  0.01 wt.%, sulfur less than or equal to 0.01                  wt.%, and iron and unavoidable impurities                  (remaining amount). The microstructure of the                  weld overlay metal is martensite + retained                  austenite, and the hardness is HRC42-48.                  Preferred Method: The method before the welding                  is repaired, when the ambient temperature is                  greater than or equal to 5 degrees C, the 718                  material side guide plate is not preheated before                  welding, when the temperature is less than 5                  degrees C the 718 material side guide plate is                  preheated at 80-120 degrees C before welding. The                  welding current of the primer bead is 200-240 A,                  the arc voltage is 21-26 V, and the welding speed                  is 4.0-5.5 mm/second. The straight line does not                  swing when the bottom welding is performed. The                  welding current of the filling and cap welding is                  300-360 A, the arc voltage is 28-34 V, the                  welding speed is 3-5 mm/second, and the welding                  rod is welded, then subjecting to ontinuous                  welding, no heat treatment is required after                  welding, the arc end of the weld is filled with                  crater, the arc current is 200-220 A and the arc                  voltage is 20-24 V.

PN CN109841833-A
TI Negative electrode for lead-carbon battery,            comprises modified carbon fiber obtained by activating            carbon fiber with carbon dioxide, roasting and mixing            with hydrogen evolution inhibitor in inert            atmosphere.
AU YAN J
   ZHANG H
   XI Y
   LI X
   WANG Z
   HUO Y
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)

GA 2019523103
AB    NOVELTY - A lead-carbon battery negative electrode                comprises 0.1-50 wt.%, preferably 1-3 wt.% modified                carbon fiber. The modified carbon fiber is obtained                by activating the carbon fiber and reacting with                hydrogen evolution inhibitor. The modified carbon                fiber is obtained by activating carbon fiber in                carbon dioxide gas furnace, roasting at 600-900                degrees C for 30 minutes-24 hours, and mixing with                hydrogen evolution inhibitor in an inert atmosphere                at 100-500 degrees C for 10 minutes-24 hours. The                hydrogen evolution inhibitor is one or more of an                aqueous solution containing a soluble salt of a                metal lead, tin, antimony, indium, antimony or                bismuth, and the soluble salt is one of a nitrate,                and acetate. The concentration of the metal soluble                salt is 0.1 wt.%. The inert atmosphere is one or                more of nitrogen, argon, and ammonia.
   USE - Negative electrode for internal mixed lead                carbon batteries for starting or stopping energy                storage (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The negative electrode has conductive network                structure which reduces the internal resistance of                the battery, and effectively inhibits the sulfation                of the battery, reduces water loss of lead-carbon                battery cycle life during increased hydrogen                evolution over potential, increased reinforced                battery charging receiving ability and                low-temperature starting performance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The negative                  electrode is prepared by mixing 100 parts mass                  lead powder, 0.1-10 parts mass mixture of barium                  sulfate and lignin as additive, 0.1-50 parts mass                  modified carbon fiber, 10-21 parts mass deionized                  water and 4-100 parts mass 1.1-1.4 g/cm3 sulfuric                  acid.
