PN CN109717346-A; CN109717346-B
TI Preserving Cordyceps sinensis, by soaking fresh Cordyceps sinensis in preservative solution, drying, adding to packaging bag, filling with mixed gas of oxygen and carbon dioxide, irradiating with electron beam, and refrigerating.
AU BI H
   WEI L
   XIAO Y
   DU Y
   YANG H
AE NORTHWEST INST PLATEAU BIOLOGY CHINESE A (CAPB-C)
GA 201942956C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preserving Cordyceps sinensis, involves (i) soaking fresh C.sinensis in a preservative solution, drying to obtain soaked C.sinensis, where the preservative solution comprises 1-3 mmol/l sorbitol, 1-3 mmol/l vitamin C or isovitamin C, 6-9 mu mol/l phytic acid, 0.4-0.8 mg/kg L-cysteine, 30-100 mg/ml ethanol extract of C.sinensis fermented liquor, 50-200 mg/l chitosan oligosaccharide, and 100-200 mg/l antibacterial peptide, (ii) adding the soaked C.sinensis to a packaging bag, filling the bag with a mixed gas of oxygen and carbon dioxide for air-conditioning packaging to obtain an air-conditioned packaged C.sinensis, and (iii) irradiating the air-conditioned packaged C.sinensis with cobalt-60 gamma rays or 5-10 MeV electron beams, where the dose of irradiation is 0.1-1.5 kGy, and the non-uniformity is less than or equal to 1.25, and refrigerating the irradiated C.sinensis.
   USE - The method is useful for preserving C.sinensis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces weight loss rate, soluble protein content decline rate and polyphenol oxidase activity growth rate of C.sinensis, and preserves C.sinensis for at least 75 days without any damage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The step (i) involves ultrasonically cleaning fresh C.sinensis 2-5 times at a ultrasonic power of 250-1200 W, and ultrasonic frequency of 40-120 kHz, each time for 5-10 minutes, and soaking in the preservative solution. The ethanol extract of C.sinensis fermented liquor is prepared by (a) inoculating C.sinensis to a liquid fermentation culture medium, culturing for 6-8 days, and carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain C.sinensis fermented liquor, and (b) adding 70-80 vol.% ethanol to the C.sinensis fermented liquor, centrifuging to obtain supernatant, removing ethanol, and drying. Preferred Condition: In step (i), the drying is carried out at (-2)-10 degrees C. In step (iii), the refrigeration is carried out at 0-4 degrees C. Preferred Components: The liquid fermentation culture medium comprises potato, soybean, sucrose, glucose, peptone, yeast extract, mineral salt, and/or vitamin. The antibacterial peptide comprises Streptococcus mutans antibacterial peptide, Streptomyces antibacterial peptide and/or Pediococcus antibacterial peptide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: In step (ii), the volume ratio of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the mixed gas is (55-65):(35-45).

PT P
PN FR3072396-A1; WO2019073144-A1; FR3072396-B1
TI Method of treating electrofilter ash from boiler of liquor recovery circuit in Kraft pulp process, involves injecting carbon dioxide into one of aqueous streams while ensuring addition of water to solution stage.
AU CAMPO P
   ARAUJO O
   ALBAN B
   FONTES D V B
   FONTES DA VEIGA B
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 201936200Q
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves dissolving the ashes in an aqueous medium, in a mixing reactor (10), with stirring so as to form a saturated solution or a supersaturated solution. A liquid phase containing chloride and potassium ions, and a solid phase containing sodium ions, sulfides and carbonates are separated. All or portion of liquid phase at the dissolution stage, and a second portion of liquid phase being able to be purged are recycled. The pH of the solution is lowered to a pH value of between 8 and 10, and the carbon dioxide is injected into one of the aqueous streams while ensuring the addition of water to the solution stage.
   USE - Method of treating electrofilter ash from boiler of liquor recovery circuit in Kraft pulp process.
   ADVANTAGE - The process of treating electrofilter ash from boiler of liquor recovery circuit in Kraft pulp process is performed efficiently. The problem of increasing the sulphidity associated with the use of sulfuric acid and spent acids in large quantity is solved. The treatment method is easy to implement and is economical. The quality of the acidification is improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram illustrating the leaching of ash electrofilter.
   Supersaturated solution under pressure (7)
   Filtrate (9)
   Mixing reactor (10)
   Recycled portion (11)

TI Escape training method for expanded tobacco section, involves updating pheromone concentration of path according to influence of high temperature, toxic gas, carbon dioxide concentration and oxygen concentration on physical value of ant.
AU LIN S
   SHI D
   ZHAO Y
   DING N
   YAN J
   WANG H
   CHEN X
   WU J
   WANG Z
AE CHINA TOBACCO ZHEJIANG IND CORP (CNTA-C)
GA 201920215S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves establishing a three-dimensional scene map according to the actual scene of the expanded tobacco section. The door, the stairway, the corridor turn, and the cornice in the three-dimensional scene map are used as nodes to generate multiple escape paths to form an escape path set. The ant algorithm is introduced to treat the ant as the escaper and the pheromone concentration of the path is taken as the physical value of the escaper. The pheromone concentration of each path is updated according to the influence of high temperature, toxic gas, carbon dioxide concentration and oxygen concentration on the physical value of the ant. The transition probability is taken corresponding to the escape route less than the transition probability threshold and the pheromone concentration greater than 0 as the constraint condition such that the optimal calculation is obtained by using the formula in the optimization model of the optimal escape route represented by formulas.
   USE - Escape training method for expanded tobacco section.
   ADVANTAGE - The escape route has a strong guiding significance. The escape effect has certain guarantees. The escaper can be safely escaped from the scene of the accident according to the escape route.

PN CN109082911-A
TI Dyeing silk fabrics by using extraction liquid of            Dioscorea cirrhos involves adding certain amount of            soluble iron salt solution having mass concentration            and adjusting pH value to obtain dye solution for            dyeing.
AU LIU G
AE FOSHAN YIXIANG DISI CLOTHING DESIGN CO (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 201901563S
AB    NOVELTY - Dyeing silk fabrics by using extraction liquid                of Dioscorea cirrhos involves adding a certain                amount of a soluble iron salt solution having a                mass concentration of 10-15% and adjusting the pH                value to 5-6 to obtain a dye solution. The silk                fabric is placed in the dye solution obtained for                dyeing, then transferred the dyed silk fabric to a                reaction kettle, and then introduce carbon dioxide                into the reaction vessel, controlling the pressure                of the reaction vessel to 8-15 MPa, and the                temperature is 35-40 degrees C. The fluid in the                reaction kettle is a supercritical carbon dioxide                fluid, after the silk fabric is color-fixed in the                reaction kettle for 2-3 hours. The reaction kettle                is depressurized, and after the pressure relief is                completed, the reaction kettle is opened, and the                coloration and color fixation are taken out silk                fabric.
   USE - Method for dyeing silk fabrics by using                extraction liquid of Dioscorea cirrhos.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to dye silk fabrics by                using extraction liquid of Dioscorea cirrhos, which                has high extraction efficiency and is easy for                industrial production, and adopts supercritical                fluid to develop color and fix color, and the                dyeing quality is easy to control.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Process: The process of extraction                  liquid of Dioscorea cirrhos involves selecting a                  fresh Dioscorea cirrhos root, pulverizing, and                  add to pectinase corresponding to the weight of                  the root of the yam root is added by 0.1% to                  0.3%, and the hemicellulase corresponding to                  0.9-1.6 wt.% hemicellulase at 25-40 degrees C                  lower enzymolysis to obtain enzymolysis liquid.                  The raw material after enzymolysis liquid for                  inactivate enzyme protein by ultrahigh pressure                  process to obtain super-inactivation treatment,                  and then a certain amount of distilled water is                  added and stirred uniformly, and the                  high-pressure homogenization treatment is                  performed for 1-3 hours to get homogenate. The                  homogenate is treated under microwave conditions                  of 600W output for 6-10 minutes, extraction with                  ethanol-surfactant at a temperature of 70-90                  degrees C for 2- 5 times, stirred, centrifuged to                  collect the extract. The extract is sequentially                  filtered through a plate frame and filtered                  through an ultrafiltration membrane to collect a                  filtrate having a molecular weight cut off of                  more than 50,000 Da to obtain the desired                  product. Preferred Conditions: The pressure of                  the high pressure homogenization treatment was 60                  MPa, and the time is 1 hour. The                  ethanol-surfactant mixed extract is prepared by                  adding a surfactant having a total mass of the                  0.2-2 wt.% aqueous ethanol solution to an aqueous                  ethanol solution having an ethanol concentration                  of 50-80 vol.%. The mass ratio of the medium                  homogenate to the ethanol-surfactant mixed                  extract is 1:5-1:30. Preferred Components: The                  surfactant is selected from the group consisting                  of polyoxyethylene sorbitan monolaurate,                  polyoxyethylene sorbitan monopalmitate,                  polyoxyethylene sorbitan monostearate,                  polyoxyethylene sorbitol and mixture of any one                  or more of the alcohol anhydride                  monooleate.

PT P
PN US2018362880-A1; US10745643-B2
TI Producing oil involves treating distillers corn oil having high free fatty acid content with mixture of monohydric alcohol and alkali and separating oil from low-free fatty acid oily phase having impurities e.g. waxes by membrane filtration.
AU GALLOP C C
   DASARI M P
   WARREN H T
   KNOX K J
AE ICM INC (ICMI-Non-standard)
GA 2018A15147
AB    NOVELTY - Producing oil comprises: treating distillers corn oil having a high free fatty acid content with a mixture comprising at least 15 wt.% of a monohydric alcohol i.e. a solvent and optionally an alkali to form a low-free fatty acid oily phase comprising oil and at least one impurity, and an alcohol phase; and separating the oil and the impurities using membrane filtration, where the impurities comprise free fatty acid soaps, waxes (both preferred) and/or unsaponifiables.
   USE - The method is useful for producing oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple and highly effective in recovering free fatty acids while minimizing loss of valuable neutral oil (that is used for animal feed, industrial purposes including lubricants, biodiesel, polymers and paints, and potentially food) and emulsion formation; utilizes products and byproducts (e.g. corn oil, aqueous ethanol and carbon dioxide) of an ethanol production facility, and thus eliminates or reduces the release of carbon dioxide into the air, and is an environmentally friendly; and utilizes aqueous ethanol which provides cost savings as there is no need to process the aqueous ethanol prior to using same as a solvent, and effectively hydrates the phospholipids and separates them from the fats and oils.
TF EXAMPLE - Batch reactor was used to extract free fatty acids from distillers corn oil (DCO) containing 13.2% free fatty acids. Reaction was performed by adding 207.8 g of DCO (feedstock) to a 500 ml flask. The temperature of the corn oil was raised from ambient temperature to 65 degrees C. A solvent phase was then prepared by initially creating a solution of aqueous ethanol containing 40 wt.% ethanol, followed by addition of 3.9 g sodium hydroxide to 127.6 g of aqueous ethanol. In a separate flask, the solvent phase and alkali were mixed and heated from ambient temperature to 65 degrees C. The alkaline solvent was added to the feedstock and the mixture was then agitated for 1 minute, and then the mixture was allowed to separate, in a 65 degrees C environment, into two distinct phases. The top phase was collected and dried to yield 179.8 g of oil with free fatty acid content of 0.2%. 114.6 g Bottom solvent phase was collected into a separate beaker to which concentrated sulfuric acid was added until the pH of the mixture was 2. The mixture was then agitated for 1 minute, and then allowed to separate, in a 65 degrees C environment, into two distinct phases. The top phase was separated and dried to yield 27.3 g of fatty acids.

   USE - Electrochemical sensor for sensing materials such as carbon dioxide, alkali metal ion conductive material and acronym for sodium super ionic conductor (NASICON).
   ADVANTAGE - The sensor is provided with the arranged pore forming a 3D network and a 3D nano pattern electrode and forms the micro-pore on a surface of the 3D nano pattern electrode, so that a surface area of an electrochemical sensor electrode is maximized, mass transfer can be facilitated and perception ability of the electrochemical sensor can be improved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an exploded perspective view of an electrochemical sensor.
   Electrodes (12, 16)

AU ZHANG D
AE HEFEI JIENUO NON-WOVEN PROD CO LTD (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 201835340Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing medical non-woven cloth comprises (i) extracting each of traditional Chinese medicine raw material with using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method and drying, (ii) mixing all kinds of traditional Chinese medicine dry powder according to ratio, adding water and milling at high speed, concentrating, spraying and drying, (iii) mixing micron-level traditional Chinese medicine mixed dry powder with isopropanol, de-ionized water, polyethylene-vinyl alcohol and bamboo fiber and (iv) adopting high pressure electrostatic spinning method.
   USE - The cloth is useful for wound healing (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cloth has good antibacterial effect. The method is simple and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing medical non-woven cloth comprises (i) extracting each of traditional Chinese medicine raw material using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method and drying to obtain traditional Chinese medicine dry powder, (ii) mixing all kinds of traditional Chinese medicine dry powder according to ratio, adding water and milling at high speed, concentrating, spraying and drying to obtain micron-level traditional Chinese medicine mixed dry powder, (iii) mixing the micron-level traditional Chinese medicine mixed dry powder with isopropanol, de-ionized water, polyethylene-vinyl alcohol and bamboo fiber to obtain the static spinning solution, and (iv) adopting high pressure electrostatic spinning method to obtain medical non-woven cloth.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the mixed dry powder of micron level traditional Chinese medicine comprises 8-12 pts. wt. parasitic Loranthus, 8-12 pts. wt. In the step (ii), the particle size of the micron-level traditional Chinese medicine mixed dry powder is 10-80 mu m. In the step (iii), the electrostatic spinning solution comprises 2-8 wt.% micron-level traditional Chinese medicine mixed dry powder, 35-45 wt.% isopropanol, 8-12 wt.% polyethylene-vinyl alcohol, 20-35 wt.% bamboo fiber and de-ionized water (balance).
   ACTIVITY - Vulnerary.

AB    NOVELTY - Phosphating and saponificating zero discharge of high pressure closed circulation system comprises loading work-piece in a high-pressure kettle; then sealing to make the pressure range of the autoclave more than 20 MPa, degreasing carbon dioxide and rust removal, buffering carbon dioxide by a buffer tank and then adding to the autoclave through a hydraulic pump, and subjecting the workpiece to rust removal and degreasing treatment by spraying a high-pressure carbon dioxide gas stream through the venturi nozzle in the autoclave, adding supercritical carbon dioxide to autoclave for buffer cleaning by means of a buffer tank and a hydraulic pump until the autoclave is clean, and pushing the oil stain and solid residue into the separation kettle by supercritical carbon dioxide and injecting phosphating solution into the autoclave by a hydraulic pump.
   USE - Used as phosphating and saponificating zero discharge of high pressure closed circulation system.
   ADVANTAGE - The high pressure closed circulation system: has excellent working conditions; does not produce waste water and realizes zero discharge; and is environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Phosphating and saponificating zero discharge of high pressure closed circulation system comprises (i) loading work-piece in a high-pressure kettle; then sealing to make the pressure range of the autoclave more than 20 MPa, (ii) degreasing carbon dioxide and rust removal, buffering carbon dioxide by a buffer tank and then adding to the autoclave through a hydraulic pump, and subjecting the workpiece to rust removal and degreasing treatment by spraying a high-pressure carbon dioxide gas stream through the venturi nozzle in the autoclave, (iii) adding supercritical carbon dioxide to autoclave for buffer cleaning by means of a buffer tank and a hydraulic pump until the autoclave is clean, and pushing the oil stain and solid residue into the separation kettle by supercritical carbon dioxide, (iv) injecting phosphating solution into the autoclave by a hydraulic pump, mixing with the high pressure fluid in the autoclave under the action of the agitator of the autoclave; heating the autoclave to a reaction temperature, and phosphatizing the surface of the workpiece, then chemically reacting the phosphating solution on the surface of the workpiece to form a phosphate film; after the reaction is over, exporting the fluid in the autoclave to the separation vessel, and separating and collecting the phosphatized residue, (v) transferring the supercritical carbon dioxide into the autoclave by means of a buffer tank and a hydraulic pump for circulation cleaning until the inside of the autoclave is clean, (vi) injecting saponification into the autoclave by a hydraulic pump, mixing and diluting with the high-pressure fluid in the autoclave under the action of the agitator of the autoclave; heating the autoclave to the reaction temperature, and phosphatizing the surface of the workpiece, chemically reacting the phosphating solution on the surface of the workpiece to form a saponified film; after the reaction is completed, discharging the fluid in the autoclave to a separation vessel, and separating and collecting the residue after saponification, (vii) transporting the supercritical carbon dioxide into the autoclave by means of a buffer tank and a hydraulic pump for circulation cleaning until the autoclave is cleaned, and (viii) opening the high pressure kettle, ventilating and drying, where the circulation system comprises high pressure kettle, separating kettle, a buffer kettle, a hydraulic pump and a recycling barrel.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (v) and step (vii): cooling carbon dioxide separated from the separation tank, pressurizing, and then passing to an autoclave for circulating cleaning.

PT P
PN RU2745489-C1
TI Method for gas-cyclic injection of a mixture of carbon dioxide with associated petroleum gas under supercritical conditions into oil production well.
AU VOLKOV V A
   BELIKOVA V G
   PROKHOROV P E
   TURAPIN A N
   KEROSIROV V M
   AFANASEV S V
AE DELTA-PROM CO LTD (DELT-Non-standard)
GA 202139402L
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to methods for producing hard-to-recover oil reserves, including from low-permeability reservoirs. A method of gas-cyclic injection of a mixture of liquid carbon dioxide with associated petroleum gas under supercritical conditions into an oil production well is proposed, including the injection of two rims before and after injection of a mixture of carbon dioxide with APG into a production well. In this case, the injection of a mixture of carbon dioxide with APG is carried out under supercritical conditions, followed by a period of impregnation and initiation of oil production from the same production well. When implementing gas-cyclic injection, a mixture of carbon dioxide with associated petroleum gas is injected with a content of carbon dioxide and associated petroleum gas in the mixture of 75-95 vol.% and 5-25 vol.% correspondingly, under supercritical conditions: at the injection temperature at the wellhead Tinj and the injection pressure at the wellhead Pinj, exceeding the critical temperature Tcrit of the mixture and pressure Pcrit of the mixture of carbon dioxide with associated petroleum gas. Before the injection of a mixture of carbon dioxide with associated petroleum gas, the first rim is injected into the well in a volume of 5-20 m3 per meter of perforation of the treated reservoir, then a mixture of carbon dioxide with associated petroleum gas is injected under the above conditions and before the impregnation period, the second rim is injected in a volume of 5-20 m3 per meter of perforation of the treated reservoir, after which the well is closed for the period of impregnation, followed by oil production from the same production well. Moreover, the number of injection cycles in the specified sequence into the production well is at least 1. The first rim contains wt%: the composition "Delta ASPGO" 70-80 and the composition "ML-super" 20-30. The second slug contains the composition "Delta ASPGO" 60-70, the composition "ML-super" 10-20, and a gas phase permeability regulator, at least one of the oil-soluble corrosion inhibitors - foaming agents from the class of imidazolines: aminoethylalkylimidazoline or hydroxyethylalkylimidazoline 10-30. The pressure of injection of a mixture of carbon dioxide with associated petroleum gas at the bottom of the well Pbot.inj is in the range of more than the pressure of the critical mixture of carbon dioxide with associated petroleum gas Pcrit of the mixture and not more than the pressure P = 0.75fr, where Pfr is the fracture pressure. It is preferable that the injection pressure at the bottomhole Pbot.inj exceeds not only the critical pressure of the Pcrit mixture, but also the minimum miscibility pressure Pmmp of the mixture of carbon dioxide and associated petroleum gas with reservoir oil of a particular field.
   USE - Oil reserves.
   ADVANTAGE - Technical result is to increase oil recovery while ensuring the utilization of associated gas.
2 cl, 1 tbl

   YANASE M

AB    NOVELTY - A composition comprises at least one amine                compound (A) chosen from amine compounds (I)-(III),                and at least one amine compound (B) chosen from                amine compounds (IV) and (V).
   USE - Composition used for separating carbon dioxide                (claimed) from combustion exhaust gas generated in                thermal power plant, steel plant and cement                factory.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition can efficiently absorb and                separate large amount of carbon dioxide emitted in                a large-scale thermal power generation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A composition comprises at least one amine                compound (A) chosen from amine compounds of                formulae (I)-(III), and at least one amine compound                (B) chosen from amine compounds of formulae (IV)                and (V).
   R10-R14 = H, 1-4C alkyl, hydroxy,                hydroxymethyl, 2-hydroxyethyl, or 1-4C                alkoxy;
   a,b = 0 or 1;
   a+b = 1;
   R15 = H, 1-4C alkyl, methoxymethyl,                methoxyethoxymethyl, or 2-hydroxyethyl;

   R4 = 1-4C alkyl, hydroxymethyl,                2-hydroxyethyl, 3-4C aminoalkyl, or 3-4C                hydroxyalkyl;
   R7 = 1-4C alkylene;
   R4,R5 = 1-4C alkyl, hydroxymethyl,                2-hydroxyethyl, 3-4C aminoalkyl, 3-4C aminoalkyl or                hydroxyalkyl;
   R6 = H or R4;
   or R4+R6 = ring;and
   R7 = 1-4C alkylene.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                separation of carbon dioxide, which involves                bringing a gas containing carbon dioxide into                contact with the carbon dioxide separation                composition to absorb carbon dioxide in the mixed                gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The compound (I) is                  1,4-diazabicyclo(2.2.2)octane-2-methanol compound                  of formula (IA). The compound (II) is                  1-(2,3-dihydroxypropyl)-piperazine compound of                  formula (IIA). The compound (III) is                  N-methyldiethanolamine of formula (IIIA). The                  compound (IV) is N,N-dimethyl-1,3-diaminopropane                  or N,N-dimethyl-1,2-diaminoethane. The compound                  (V) is N-(2-aminoethyl)-2-aminoethanol or                  2-(3-aminopropyl)-2-aminoethanol. Preferred                  Composition: The weight ratio of the compound (A)                  with respect to the compound (B) is 5-300. The                  composition further comprises an amine compound                  (C) (excluding the compound (A) and the compound                  (B)) chosen from alkanolamine, propylenediamine,                  piperazine, piperidine, morpholine, pyrrolidine,                  azepane, and polyethylene polyamine, and 30-95                  wt.% water.
   EXAMPLE - 7 g 1,4-Diazabicyclo(2,2,2)octane-2-methanol,                32 g 1-(2,3-dihydroxypropyl)-piperazine, 6 g                N,N-dimethyl-1,3-diaminopropane and 55 g pure water                were mixed and stirred to obtain 100 g carbon                dioxide absorbent. The obtained absorbent was                placed in a 200 mL gas absorption bottle, and the                temperature was adjusted to 40 degrees C in a water                bath. A mixed gas of 140 mL/minute carbon dioxide                gas and 560 mL/minute nitrogen gas were blown into                the carbon dioxide absorption liquid for 1.5 hours,                and the amount of carbon dioxide gas absorbed was                measured using a gas flow meter and a carbon                dioxide concentration meter. The amount of carbon                dioxide gas absorbed was 5.65 L in terms of                standard state. 56.5 L Carbon dioxide was absorbed                in a standard state per 1 kg of the absorbing                liquid. The amount of carbon dioxide gas absorbed                per unit time for 30 minutes immediately after the                start of blowing the mixed gas was 1442 mL/minute                per 1 kg of the absorbing liquid. The gas                absorption bottle was placed in a water bath at 70                degrees C, a mixed gas of 140 mL/minute carbon                dioxide gas and 560 mL/minute nitrogen gas were                blown in for 0.5 hour, and a gas flow meter and a                carbon dioxide concentration meter were used. When                the amount of carbon dioxide gas emitted was                measured, the amount of carbon dioxide gas emitted                was 0.76 L in terms of standard state. 7.6 L Carbon                dioxide gas was emitted in a standard state per 1                kg of the absorbing liquid. The amount of carbon                dioxide gas emitted per unit time at this time was                253 mL/minute per kg of the absorbing liquid.

Calcium chloride is injected into the absorption liquid taken out from the first pressure container in the precipitation container. The solid-liquid separator separates and recovers calcium carbonate from the waste liquid of the absorption liquid. PN US2018134655-A1
TI New neutral bis(iminoguanidine) compound used for            removing oxyanion e.g. sulfate, nitrate, chromate,            selenate, phosphate, arsenate, carbonate, bicarbonate            and perchlorate from aqueous source, and removing            carbon dioxide from gaseous source.
AU CUSTELCEAN R
   SEIPP C A
   WILLIAMS N J
AE UT-BATTELLE LLC (UTBA-C)
GA 201838099J
AB    NOVELTY - A neutral bis(iminoguanidine) compound (I), is                new.
   USE - New neutral bis(iminoguanidine) compound is                used for removing oxyanion e.g. sulfate, nitrate,                chromate, selenate, phosphate, arsenate, carbonate,                bicarbonate and perchlorate from aqueous source,                and for removing carbon dioxide from gaseous source                (all claimed). The aqueous source includes                seawater, water from sewage treatment, and aqueous                effluent from industrial and commercial process.                Seawater is used in oil-field injection operation.                Uses include but are not limited to air, waste gas                from industrial or commercial process, flue gas                from power plant, exhaust from engine, and sewage                or landfill gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The compound (I) can be recycled and re-used,                and efficiently removes oxyanions from aqueous                solutions, and carbon dioxide from gaseous source                by a simple and an economical method without                requiring pressure, nanofiltration,                pre-concentration and organic solvents.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A neutral bis(iminoguanidine) compound of                formula (I) or its salts, is new.A =                ring-containing moiety.
   (i) In structure (I), H is optionally replaced                with methyl. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for                the following:
   (1) removal of oxyanion from aqueous source                comprising oxyanion, which involves dissolving an                oxyanion precipitating compound into an aqueous                source to precipitate an oxyanion salt of the                oxyanion precipitating compound, and removing the                oxyanion salt from the water-containing oxyanion to                provide water having reduced oxyanion content. The                oxyanion precipitating compound is a salt form of                bis(iminoguanidine) compound of formula (II);                and
   (2) removal of carbon dioxide from gaseous                source, which involves contacting the gaseous                source with an aqueous solution comprising a carbon                dioxide complexing compound to precipitate a                carbonate or bicarbonate salt of the carbon dioxide                complexing compound, and removing the carbonate or                bicarbonate salt from the aqueous solution. The                carbon dioxide complexing compound is the compound                (II).
   X'm = anionic group;
   m = 1 or more;
   n = 1 or more;and
   nx m = 2.
   (i) X'm is exchangeable with oxyanion in                aqueous source before contacting oxyanion                precipitating compound with the oxyanion and is                oxyanion in oxyanion salt of oxyanion precipitating                compound, and (ii) X'm is hydroxide in carbon                dioxide complexing compound before contacting                carbon dioxide complexing compound with carbon                dioxide and is carbonate or bicarbonate in                carbonate or bicarbonate salt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (disclosed): The compound (I) is                  obtained by reacting aminoguanidine,                  aminoguanidinium or their methylated derivatives                  of formula (III) with a ring-containing                  dialdehyde or diketone of formula (IV) under                  desired conditions to form an imine linkage                  between an amino group on the aminoguanidine or                  aminoguanidinium molecule and carbon of the                  aldehyde or ketone group.R = H, hydrocarbyl, preferably methyl,                  ethyl, n-propyl, isopropyl, n-butyl, isobutyl,                  secondary butyl, t-butyl, phenyl, or                  benzyl.
   Preferred Definitions:
   A = five-membered, six-membered or                seven-membered monocyclic ring, unsaturated                carbocyclic ring or ring system, unsaturated                heterocyclic ring or ring system comprising at                least one nitrogen ring atom and comprises benzene                ring and pyridine ring;and
   X'm = halide or nitrate, sulfato, or carbonate                or bicarbonate.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 4 neutral bis(iminoguanidine) compound are                  disclosed, e.g. 1,4-benzene-bis(iminoguanidinium)                  chloride of formula (Ia),                  1,4-benzene-bis(iminoguanidinium) sulfate,                  1,4-benzene-bis(iminoguanidinium) nitrate, and                  2,6-pyridine-bis(iminoguanidine).
   EXAMPLE - 4 g terephthalaldehyde and 7.26 g                aminoguanidinium chloride were added to 20 mL                ethanol in 50 mL round bottom flask. The solution                was heated to 60 degrees C using a hotplate, and                stirred with a magnetic stir bar for 2 hours. The                solution was cooled to 20 degrees C and left still                for three hours. The obtained solid was collected                and suspended in 20 mL ethanol and the suspension                was heated on a hotplate. The flask was allowed to                cool to room temperature, and then placed in 0                degrees C freezer overnight. The obtained solid was                collected and filtered to obtain                1,4-benzene-bis(iminoguanidinium) chloride. The                obtained 1,4-benzene-bis(iminoguanidinium) chloride                had a solubility of 6.3x 10-2 at 25 degrees C, and                exhibited high degree of separation of sulfate from                seawater.

Ratio of the butyric acid to                acetic acid concentration increases when the pH of                the fermentation broth decreases, and the ratio of                butyric acid to acetic acid concentration decreases                when the pH of the fermentation broth increases.                The acetogenic bacteria include a sodium                translocating ATPase that is active during                fermentation in the bioreactor. Sodium ions are                provided so that Na+ is maintained at 1000-11000                ppm (g/g) in culture broth.

PT P
PN WO2020165795-A1; CN113614255-A; EP3924525-A1; US2022136076-A1
TI Decarburizing steel involves contacting molten            iron-containing material with a sufficient amount of            carbon dioxide containing gas to decarburize at least a            portion of molten iron containing material.
AU AL-HARBI M
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 202079766S
AB    NOVELTY - Decarburizing steel involves contacting molten                iron-containing material with a sufficient amount                of carbon dioxide containing gas to decarburize at                least a portion of molten iron containing                material.
   USE - Method for decarburizing steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to decarburize steel, which                uses carbon dioxide as an oxygen source for                decarburization of steel during one or more stages                of steel making.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for decarbonizing iron-containing material, which                involves:
   (A) contacting an iron-containing material                with a sufficient amount of carbon dioxide                containing gas to at least partially decarbonize                the iron-containing material;
   (B) providing the at least partially                decarbonized iron-containing material to a                secondary steel process and contacting                iron-containing material with a sufficient amount                of carbon dioxide containing gas to produce a                decarbonized iron containing material having a                carbon content lower than decarbonized                iron-containing material; and
   (C) contacting decarbonized iron containing                material with a carbon dioxide containing gas under                a reduced pressure to produce a decarbonized steel                material having a carbon content lower than the                decarbonized iron containing material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Conditions: The contacting is                  performed at a temperature of 500-1000 degrees C.                  The carbon dioxide containing gas is provided                  during a primary steel processing step of melting                  direct reduced iron, pig iron, iron ore, scrap                  iron, or its combination. The method further                  involves providing carbon dioxide containing gas                  at a rate sufficient to agitate iron-containing                  material, and contacting the iron-containing                  material with an oxygen containing gas. The                  method further involves providing carbon dioxide                  containing gas in a direction opposite of flow                  oxygen containing gas. The contact of                  iron-containing material and carbon dioxide                  containing gas is a secondary steel processing                  step. The iron-containing material comprises                  molten slag on a top portion of a molten iron                  oxide material and method further involves                  providing carbon dioxide containing gas to a                  bottom portion of molten iron oxide material. The                  method further involves providing carbon dioxide                  containing gas at a rate of sufficient to agitate                  molten iron-containing material. The                  iron-containing material is molten iron oxide and                  method further involves contacting molten iron                  oxide material and carbon dioxide containing gas                  under a reduced pressure. The reduced pressure is                  a third steel processing step. The method further                  involves providing the carbon dioxide containing                  gas at a rate of sufficient to agitate the molten                  iron oxide material, and producing a steel                  material from the decarburized iron-containing                  material, where steel material has a carbon                  content of less than 0.5 wt.%, preferably less                  than 0.25 wt.% based on total weight of steel.                  The contacting of iron-containing material with                  carbon dioxide containing gas is performed in an                  arc furnace, step of providing partially                  decarbonized iron-containing material to a                  secondary steel process is performed in a ladle                  furnace, and step of contacting decarbonized iron                  containing material with a carbon dioxide                  containing gas is performed in a ladle vacuum                  degassing unit. The decarbonized iron-containing                  material is a steel material having a carbon                  content of less than 0.05 wt.%. The method                  further involves contacting iron-containing                  material with an oxygen containing gas,                  simultaneous or substantially simultaneous                  contacting of iron-containing material with the                  carbon dioxide containing gas and oxygen                  containing gas, and then providing carbon dioxide                  containing gas to a bottom portion of EAF, a                  bottom portion of ladle furnace, a bottom portion                  of ladle vacuum degassing unit, or its                  combination. Preferred Components: The carbon                  dioxide containing gas comprises nitrogen, argon,                  or both.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)
MC M24-B02D; M24-C06

PT P
PN US2022212927-A1
TI Autothermal reforming hydrocarbon involves            reacting hydrocarbon with carbon dioxide and oxygen via            reforming catalyst to obtain synthesis gas comprising            hydrogen and carbon monoxide, where reforming catalyst            comprises treated black powder comprising            hematite.
AU WEDHAYA A A
   ALBUALI M A
   FADHEL B A
   BAMAGAIN R
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202287848A
AB    NOVELTY - Autothermal reforming hydrocarbon involves                reacting the hydrocarbon with carbon dioxide and                oxygen via reforming catalyst to obtain synthesis                gas comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide, where                the reforming catalyst comprises treated black                powder comprising hematite.
   USE - Method for autothermal reforming                hydrocarbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The black powder catalyst in ATR may                facilitate use of greenhouse gases (methane (CH4)                and carbon dioxide (CO2)) and waste material (black                powder) to produce the valuable commodity syngas                (carbon monoxide (CO) and H2). The reforming                catalyst can reduce catalyst effectiveness and                lower conversion of the feed into syngas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. a method for preparing a reforming catalyst                for autothermal reforming methane, which involves:                receiving black powder, applying heat to the black                powder to remove carbon from the black powder, and                calcining the black powder in presence of air to                give calcined black powder as the reforming                catalyst for the autothermal reforming of methane,                where the calcined black powder comprises                hematite;
   2. a reforming catalyst for autothermal                reforming of methane with carbon dioxide and                oxygen, which comprising: 70 wt.% hematite, where                the hematite is both a support and active portion                of the reforming catalyst, and the hematite being                amphoteric advances both cracking of the methane                and dissociation of the carbon dioxide in the                autothermal reforming (ATR) of the methane;                and
   3. an autothermal reformer, which comprises:                an autothermal reformer vessel comprising at least                one inlet to receive methane, carbon dioxide (308),                and oxygen (309), a reforming catalyst comprising                calcined black powder to convert the methane,                carbon dioxide, and oxygen into syngas (310), and                an outlet to discharge the syngas, where the syngas                comprises hydrogen and carbon monoxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an                autothermal reformer.
   Autothermal reformer vessel (302)
   Hydrocarbon (306)
   Carbon dioxide (308)
   Oxygen (309)
   Syngas (310)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrocarbon (306)                  comprises methane. The treated black powder                  comprises calcined black powder.Preferred Composition: The hematite                  comprises 50 wt.% treated black powder.Preferred Process: The step of receiving the                  black powder involves receiving the black powder                  collected from a natural-gas pipeline system,                  applying heat to the black powder to remove                  carbon from the black powder in the presence of                  air at 500degrees Celsius for 3 hours obtain a                  heat-treated black powder, and calcining the                  obtained heat-treated black powder in the                  presence of air to obtain the calcined black                  powder as the reforming catalyst.Preferred Conditions: The reforming catalyst                  comprises calcined black powder comprising 50                  wt.% hematite, where the calcined black powder is                  black powder heat treated at a temperature of                  500degrees Celsius for 3 hours and calcined in                  presence of air at temperature of 775degrees                  Celsius for 4 hours, and the black powder from a                  natural gas pipeline.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN DE102020002755-A1; WO2021228428-A1
TI Carbon dioxide-neutral bioconverter plant comprises bioconverter for carbon dioxide-neutral, single-stage or multi-stage production of biogas by fermentation of biomass in fermentation liquid, and at least one ammonia supply unit.
AU NEFIGMANN S
AE NEFIGMANN S (NEFI-Individual)
GA 2021C5232J
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-neutral bioconverter plant comprehensively comprises at least one bioconverter (BK) for carbon dioxide-neutral, single-stage or multi-stage production of biogas (BG) by fermentation of biomass (BM) in a fermentation liquid (GF) moved with agitating agents in presence of elemental hydrogen, hydrogenotrophic and methanogenic archaea and activated coal masses; at least one ammonia supply unit with at least one ammonia line and at least one ammonia cracker (AC) to produce hydrogen and nitrogen through catalytically splitting ammonia with renewable energies; at least one line to introduce hydrogen or hydrogen-nitrogen mixture directly into at least one bioconverter; at least one device to treat activated coal masses with hydrogen at higher pressure with at least one pressure line and at least one spray lance to inject hydrogen-containing activated coal masses as a spray cone in fermentation liquid of at least one bioconverter.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide-neutral bioconverter plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The plant has high methane yield, and produces methane with high degree of purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) carbon dioxide-neutral, single-stage or multi-stage conversion process for production of biogas by fermentation of biomass in a fermentation liquid;
   (2) injecting hydrogen-containing, powdery activated coal masses into fermentation liquid of at least one bioconverter under pressure; and
   (3) use of the biomass or fermentation products converted in the carbon dioxide-neutral bioconverter plant and/or the activated carbon masses used in the carbon dioxide-neutral as fertilizers and for the production of terra preta.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the plant.
   Bioconverter (BK)
   Biogas (BG)
   Biomass (BM)
   Fermentation liquid (GF)
   Ammonia cracker (AC)

PT P
PN CN113521970-A
TI Membrane absorbing method for capturing and utilizing carbon dioxide, involves using gas flow adjustment system, gas pressure control and alkali solution flow adjustment system and alkali solution pressure adjustment system to maintain pressure difference between gas and liquid phases.
AU YANG J
   GU X
   RAN Y
   CAO J
   LIU Y
   HUANG A
   ZENG J
   LIU Q
AE XINZHONGTIAN ENVIRONMENTAL ENG CHONGQING (XINZ-Non-standard)
   UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 2021C4731M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves captures and absorbs carbon dioxide in the gas through the hydrophobic hollow fiber membrane module (4). The alkali solution is entered into the tube side or housing side of the hollow fiber membrane module through the alkali solution filtration system (7) and the cooling system (6) from the alkali solution pump (10) through the flow adjustment system for carbon dioxide absorption. The gas flow adjustment system (3), the gas pressure control and the alkali solution flow adjustment system (5) and the alkali solution pressure adjustment system are used to maintain a certain pressure difference between the gas and liquid phases for absorption. The two phases of gas and liquid are provided on the inner and outer sides of the hollow fiber membrane to achieve the capture of carbon dioxide in the gas without direct contact. The alkali solution after the capture and absorption comes out of the membrane absorber and enters the product preparation device.
   USE - Membrane absorbing method for capturing and utilizing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The hollow fiber membrane module has the characteristics of larger specific surface area, improves the removal rate of carbon dioxide, overcomes the problems of liquid entrainment and drift of the absorption tower and prepares soda and other products to form the utilization of carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the process flow for capturing carbon dioxide in gas by the alkaline solution membrane absorption method.
   Gas flow adjustment system (3)
   Hydrophobic hollow fiber membrane module (4)
   Alkali solution flow adjustment system (5)
   Alkali solution cooling system (6)
   Alkali solution filtration system (7)
   Alkali solution pump (10)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The main structure of the alkaline solution is sodium hydroxide and ammonium hydroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - The hollow fiber membrane material is polypropylene or polytetrafluoroethylene or polyvinylidene fluoride material.

PT P
PN WO2021151758-A1; SE202050076-A1; CN115003404-A
TI System for storing energy and capturing carbon            dioxide, has carbonator adapted to react calcium oxide            with carbon dioxide to produce calcium carbonate, and            calcium carbide storage container for receiving and            storing calcium carbate produced in carbonator.
AU CROON L
   BLACKMAN C
AE SALTX TECHNOLOGY AB (SALT-Non-standard)
GA 2021902783
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbonator (1) adapted to                react calcium oxide with carbon dioxide to produce                calcium carbonate. A calcium carbonate storage                container (2) receives and stores the calcium                carbonate produced in the carbonator. The calcium                carbonate storage container is configured to be                transportable such that the calcium carbonate is                supplied to a geographical location (3) remote from                the carbonator for carbon dioxide release. A                calciner (4) is located at geographical location                remote from the carbonator and adapted to heat the                calcium carbonate of the transportable calcium                carbonate storage container to a temperature where                carbon dioxide is released to produce calcium                oxide. A calcium oxide storage container (5)                receives and stores the calcium produced in the                calciner. The calcium oxide is coated with                particles comprising silica, having diameter in the                range 1-100 nm. The calcium oxide and calcium                carbonate are coated with particles comprising                silica.
   USE - System for energy storage and carbon dioxide                capture, used for minimizing long-term structural                changes and agglomeration in calcium oxide and                calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: utilizes the capacity of the                carbonator that can be adapted to the need at the                location where it is installed; and utilizes the                calciner which is more difficult to manufacture in                small scale and can be made large and serve many                carbonators, and thus can be operated at hours when                the energy cost is lower.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for storing energy and capturing carbon                dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                system for energy storage and carbon dioxide                capture.
   Carbonator (1)
   Calcium carbonate storage container                (2)
   Geographical location (3)
   Calciner (4)
   Calcium oxide storage container (5)
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. A third                alert is sent after a predetermined period of time                is elapsed after the second alert.

PN CN114991957-A
TI Method for co-generating cement heat and power and            producing chemical products to achieve zero-discharge,            involves setting gas turbine to burn fuel gas and pure            oxygen in combustion chamber, and supplying water for            electrolysis or other hydrogen and oxygen production            plants.
AU WANG Y
   WEI H
   ZHOU Y
   LV X
   ZHONG S
AE XI'AN CAMBRIDGE ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022B9966P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves setting a gas turbine to                burn fuel gas and pure oxygen in a combustion                chamber. The temperature in the combustion chamber                is controlled by circulating carbon dioxide. The                high temperature and high-pressure flue gas                generated by the combustion chamber enters the                turbine generator to generate electricity. Part of                an exhaust gas of gas turbine enters a building                material kiln for raw meal decomposition. The flue                gas from the upper part of building materials kiln                enters a steam waste heat boiler to generate steam.                Electrolyzed water or other hydrogen and oxygen                production devices are set to produce hydrogen and                oxygen. The oxygen is supplied to the combustion                chamber, and hydrogen is used as the raw material                for synthesizing chemical devices. Water for                electrolysis or other hydrogen and oxygen                production plants is supplied by water produced by                natural gas combustion and water produced by                chemical synthesis plants.
   USE - The method is useful for co-generating cement                heat and power and producing chemical products to                achieve zero-discharge.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: greatly improves the thermal                efficiency of system through the combustion of pure                oxygen natural gas, or through the electric                sintering technology; is no nitrogen oxides                production in the process, only carbon dioxide and                water in the flue gas, which greatly reduces the                carbon dioxide capture cost; utilizes the carbon                dioxide to produce chemical products, thus                achieving zero discharge of carbon dioxide and                harmful pollutants in the system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating                method for co-generating cement heat and power and                producing chemical products to achieve                zero-discharge (Drawing includes non-English                language text).

PN WO2022051586-A1; US2022077798-A1
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide system for use in            solar power plant, has active rectifier whose power            output is connected to direct current bus to combine            power from active rectifiers, and voltage controller            receiving speed of shaft.
AU KALRA C S
   CARLSON M D
AE HELIOGEN INC (HELI-Non-standard)
   HELIOGEN INC (HELI-Non-standard)
GA 202234635Q
AB    NOVELTY - The supercritical carbon dioxide system has a                set of turbine generator units (200A) comprising a                turbine with a supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2)                input and an output. A set of active rectifiers                comprises a power input and a power output. The                power input of each active rectifier is connected                to the power output of a generator of one of the                turbine generator unit. A voltage controller (280)                receives a speed from a speed sensor of each of the                generator units, and outputs a control signal to                the rectifiers. The controller monitors the speed                of each generator shaft and commands each rectifier                to vary a power electronic switching                frequency.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide system for                supercritical carbon dioxide power block used in                solar power plant. Can also be used in other types                of power plants.
   ADVANTAGE - The system provides high working fluid                density, compact wheel sizes at high operating                speeds, and capability to accommodate rather large                transition ducts at the end of the casing results                in very low pressure drop in the inter-stage piping                of less than 1 bar per stage. The system has high                efficiency aero-thermal design of the turbine                stages. The turbo-generator design lends itself to                a much simpler barrel type or other low-cost casing                designs and a simple, low cost, rotor manufacturing                process. Additionally, the single stage turbine                impellers can be over-hung on the shaft and not                require couplings or additional bearings. The                combination of turbine generator units provides an                axial thrust balanced solution to enable easier                startup transient control.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a functional block diagram                of a cascading turbine generator system.
   Multi-stage turbine generator system                (118)
   Turbine generator unit (200A)
   Voltage controller (280)

PN EP3964051-A1; US2022071111-A1; GB2598727-A; CA3129235-A1; AU2021225176-A1; JP2022044578-A
TI Vertical farming apparatus for farming e.g. fruit, has lower formation of gutters arranged in tiered configuration, and supply system arranged to supply gaseous carbon dioxide, air and mixtures of carbon dioxide and air to containers interchangeably.
AU MOORE D J
AE AGRIC INVESTMENTS LTD (AGRI-Non-standard)
   AGRIC INVESTMENTS LTD (AGRI-Non-standard)
   AGRIC INVESTMENTS LTD (AGRI-Non-standard)
   AGRIC INVESTMENTS LTD (AGRI-Non-standard)
GA 202234513V
AB    NOVELTY - The vertical farming apparatus (2) comprises a frame (4) that supports plant troughs, or pots. A base is set for supporting the frame. The array of gutters (6) are supported by the frame. The gutters contains an upper formation above a lower formation. A set of containers (8) houses plants, where each container is arranged to drain into a gutter in an array. The irrigation system (11) irrigates the containers. The supply system (17) supplies gaseous carbon dioxide, air, and mixtures of carbon dioxide, and air to the containers interchangeably. The supply system controls the carbon dioxide level around an array of containers.
   USE - Vertical farming apparatus for vertically farming crops, such as fruit, salads, and vegetable plants using vertical, hydroponic, and intensive growth farming systems.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus improves the farming and cultivation of fruits or vegetables by increasing growth of the produce, improving yield of produce, making the harvesting of produce easier and/or reducing the costs of produce by, for example, reducing the land usage or labor requirements. The apparatus allows for the production of certain vegetables, salads and fruits in climates and environments where it would be challenging to grow them economically using traditional or standard farming methods. The support elements are releasably attached to the U-shaped frame element such that their relative positions may be adjusted. This may be advantageous because plants generally increase in size as they grow, and require a greater spacing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a vertical farming method for vertically farming crops.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the vertical farming apparatus.

   Supply system (17)

TI Carbon dioxide compression heat pump integrated            heat supply system for use in coal burning boiler and            gas boiler, has cooler, absorber and condenser are            connected by heating pipeline to form heating circuit,            where another cooler and evaporator are connected by            another pipeline to form circuit.
AU LIU Y
   ZHANG J
AE BEIJING HUAYU ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202238865U
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a carbon dioxide compressing                heat pump provided with a compressor, a multi-stage                cooler, an expansion valve (5) and an evaporator                (6), where the compressor, the multi-stage cooler,                an expansion valve and an evaporator are                sequentially connected by a pipeline (7) to form a                first circuit. An absorption-type heat pump is                provided with a generator (8). A cooler, an                absorber (9) and a condenser (10) are sequentially                connected by a heating pipeline to form a heating                circuit. Another cooler and the evaporator are                connected by another pipeline (14) to form a second                circuit.
   USE - Carbon dioxide compression heat pump                integrated heat supply system for use in a coal                burning boiler and a gas boiler.
   ADVANTAGE - The system achieves effect of step-by-step                cooling through the multi-stage cooler, and solves                problem that temperature of carbon dioxide gas at                an outlet of the cooler is too high. The                multi-stage cooler cooperates with an absorption                heat pump to fully utilize heat released by                high-temperature gas, and ensures that absorption                heat pump can be driven by heat, and water in the                heating circuit can be reheated so that temperature                of the heating water at the outlet end is increased                to ensure better heating effect.

PT P
PN WO2021152614-A1; IN202021004464-A; GB2605087-A; DE112021000826-T5
TI Heterogeneous catalyst for selective carbon            dioxide methanation reaction comprises alkali promoter            metal and active metals and support for active metals            having combination of cerium oxide and gamma-aluminum            oxide.
AU YADAV G D
   SHEJALE A D
   MALKAR R S
   KATTI S
   PARVATALU D
AE INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (CHTE-Non-standard)
   ONGC ENERGY CENT TRUST (ONGC-Non-standard)
GA 202190274J
AB    NOVELTY - A heterogeneous catalyst for selective carbon                dioxide methanation reaction having high activity                and long-term stability ( greater than or equal to                1000 hours), comprises greater than or equal to 1                alkali promoter metal, and two active metals and                support for active metals having combination of                cerium oxide (CeO2)- gamma -aluminum oxide ( gamma                -Al2O3). Catalyst is synthesized in such a way                having surface area of 200-800 (m2/g), pore volume                of 0.5-2.5 (cm3/g) and average pore diameter of                5-30 nm.
   USE - A heterogeneous catalyst for selective carbon                dioxide methanation reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst provides a sustainable carbon                dioxide methanation process and totally eliminates                coke formation and catalyst regeneration or                reloading step due to its very long term stability.                The catalyst provides a high reactant conversion                and selectivity at preferred temperature, no coke                formation, resistance against metal sintering, and                high thermal stability for longer time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparing                heterogeneous catalyst for selective carbon dioxide                methanation reaction which involves (1) drop-wise                addition of nickel nitrate, iron nitrate and cesium                nitrate to aqueous solution mixture of cerium                nitrate and aluminum nitrate at continuous stirring                rate and temperature at 363 K, (2) dropwise                addition of sodium carbonate (Na2CO3) to step (1)                solution and maintaining alkaline pH to 9-10 by the                addition of 2 M sodium hydroxide (NaOH) solution,                (3) precipitating out the reaction mixture at 443 K                for 24 hours in Teflon (RTM: PTFE) lining bomb                reactor (pressure vessel) for hydrothermal                treatment, (4) cooling the solution to room                temperature and separation of precipitate by                filtration, (5) washing precipitate with de-ionized                water to attain neutral pH, (6) drying the                precipitate at 383 K for 12 hours, and (7)                calcining dried precipitate at 973 K for 5 hours in                air atmosphere.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali promoter                  metal is cesium. The concentration of alkali                  promoter metal is 1-5 wt.% on the basis of total                  weight of the support. The active metals are                  selected from nickel and/or iron. The                  concentration of active metals is 5-20 wt.% on                  basis of total weight of the support combination.                  Mole ratio of CeO2 and gamma -Al2O3 is 3:2.

PN DE102020200187-A1; WO2021140186-A1; DE112021000490-A5
TI Sensor arrangement for detecting carbon di-oxide            content in given environment, has amplifier that is            designed such that it generates output signal which is            representative of absolute carbon dioxide content in            predetermined environment.
AU KAESTLE H
AE OSRAM OPTO SEMICONDUCTORS GMBH (OSRA-C)
GA 2021796949
AB    NOVELTY - The sensor arrangement has a light source that                is provided for generating a light beam. An                absorption path which communicates with the                predetermined environment is arranged such that the                light beam passes through the absorption path and                has a length (L) between 5 mm and 20 mm. A light                detector is arranged in such a way that it detects                the light beam emerging from the absorption path                and is designed such that it generates an output                signal which is representative of a measured value                which is representative of the absolute carbon                dioxide content in the predetermined environment.                An amplifier is electrically coupled to the light                detector and is designed such that it generates an                output signal which is representative of the                absolute carbon dioxide content in the                predetermined environment.
   USE - Sensor arrangement for detecting carbon                di-oxide content in given environment such as in                the air of room or building and in the field of                building management, air quality monitoring and air                conditioning control.
   ADVANTAGE - The laser diodes are used to generate the                light beam and relatively long absorption paths in                the range of a few to several centimeters are used                to achieve a measurement result with sufficiently                good accuracy. The simple MIR-LED can be used as                the light source which is usually relatively                inexpensive. The sensor arrangement can be                manufactured particularly cost-effectively. The                absorption section can be provided in a                particularly simple manner which contributes to the                fact that the sensor arrangement can be                manufactured easily. The determination of the                carbon dioxide content is particularly                precise.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for determining carbon dioxide content in given                environment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graphical representation                of the sensor arrangement for detecting carbon                di-oxide content in given environment.

PN CN112457685-A; CN112457685-B
TI Extracting orange pigment of Monascus by separating Monascus fermentation broth, adding micro-powder of silica gel to Monascus wet mycelium, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and collecting orange pigment extract.
AU LI C
   WANG H
   XIE C
   YAN M
   SUN Y
AE GUANGDONG ZHAOQING STAR LAKE BIOSCIENCE (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202125300G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting orange pigment of Monascus involves (a) separating Monascus fermentation broth by tubular centrifugation at 10000-14000 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 5-15 minutes to obtain wet mycelium of Monascus, (b) passing obtained wet mycelium through a ball mill, adding micro-powder of silica gel when the degree of broken Monascus wet mycelium reaches to 50-70%, putting it into supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device to extract Monascus orange pigment for 60-150 minutes, and (c) discharging the material from separation kettle and collecting Monascus orange pigment extract. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device comprises an extraction kettle and a separation kettle.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting orange pigment of Monascus.
   ADVANTAGE - The method shortens extraction time, and exhibits high purity and high extraction rate of orange pigment of Monascus.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for extracting orange pigment of Monascus involves (a) separating Monascus fermentation broth by tubular centrifugation at 10000-14000 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 5-15 minutes to obtain wet mycelium of Monascus, (b) passing obtained wet mycelium through a ball mill, adding micro-powder of silica gel when the degree of broken Monascus wet mycelium reaches to 50-70%, putting it into supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device to extract Monascus orange pigment for 60-150 minutes, and (c) discharging the material from separation kettle and collecting Monascus orange pigment extract. The supercritical carbon dioxide extraction device comprises an extraction kettle and a separation kettle, where the extraction temperature of the extraction kettle is 45-65 degrees C, and the extraction pressure of the extraction kettle is 15-40 MPa, and the temperature of the separation kettle is 35-50 degrees C, and the pressure of the separation kettle is 5-15 MPa.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Component: The ratio of the added amount of micro-powdered silica gel in the step (b) to the mass of the wet mycelia of Monascus is 3:1-1:3. The entrainer is ethanol, where the amount of the entrainer is 1.5 ml/gram of Monascus mycelium. The volume fraction of ethanol is 70-100%, preferably 95%. Preferred Method: The step (b) further involves heating the extraction kettle and separation kettle to a set temperature respectively, pumping carbon dioxide into the extraction kettle and separating the kettle with a high-pressure pump, and pumping entrainer, where the dosage of the entrainer is 5 ml/g or less of Monascus mycelium. The step (b) involves maintaining extraction temperature of extraction kettle at 50 degrees C, extraction pressure of extraction kettle at 20 MPa, temperature of separating kettle at 45 degrees C, and pressure of separating kettle at 10 MPa. The method involves performing extraction for 120 minutes.

TI Implantable friction nano-generator useful for monitoring heart has friction layer arranged on pit surface of packaging layer, carbon dioxide gas produced by conductive hydrogel and provided with wires for electrical connection.
AU YI F
   ZHAO D
   YANG G
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 2020B7155C
AB    NOVELTY - Implantable friction nano-generator comprises a packaging layer (1) is provided with pits and a conductive hydrogel (4) is filled in the pits. A friction layer (2) is arranged on the pit surface of the packaging layer and forms a closed gap (3) with the pit. The gap is filled with carbon dioxide gas. The carbon dioxide gas is produced by the conductive hydrogel under heating and the conductive hydrogel is provided with wires for electrical connection.
   USE - The implantable friction nano-generator is useful for monitoring heart (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The nano-generator has good biocompatibility, stable performance, high sensitivity and realizes accurate monitoring of heart rate and heart movement. The method is simple; has high production efficiency, cheap manufacturing material; is easy to obtain and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing implantable friction nano generator comprises (a) making a mold, preparing a packaging layer and a friction layer by a mold, (b) mixing the polyvinyl alcohol and water, adding chloride electrolyte, fully dissolving to obtain the chloride salt hydrogel solution, adding bicarbonate powder into the solution, mixing uniformly, (c) placing the uniformly mixed hydrogel solution in the pit of the encapsulation layer, leading out the wires, sealing with the friction layer and (d) heating the device sealed in step (c), decomposing the bicarbonate to generate carbon dioxide under the air pressure of the carbon dioxide, making the friction layer outwardly generate arc-shaped bump and form a closed gap with the pit to obtain the implantable friction nano-generator.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the implantable friction nano-generator for monitoring heart.
   Packaging layer (1)
   Friction layer (2)
   Closed gap (3)
   Conductive hydrogel (4)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The materials of packaging layer and the friction layer comprise silicone rubber and polydimethylsiloxane. The material of the friction layer is also doped with silver nano wires. The conductive hydrogel contains bicarbonate. In the step (b) the chloride salt electrolyte comprises lithium chloride, sodium chloride and/or potassium chloride. In the step (b) the concentration of the chloride salt in the hydrogel solution is 0.5-1.5 mol/l. In the step (b) the concentration of bicarbonate in the hydrogel solution is 0.05-0.1 mg/ml.
