   USE - Crosslinked copolymer used for gas separation membrane which further used for carbon dioxide separation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The crosslinked copolymer show high carbon dioxide permeability and selectivity, including both a carbon dioxide soluble functional group and a carbon dioxide permeable functional group unit, and excellent anti-aging performance, plasticization prevention behavior and has thermal properties.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Crosslinked copolymer of 2-methyl-4-((Z)-2-(2-methyl-1,3-dioxo-6-vinyl-4,5,6,6a-tetrahydro-3aH-cyclopenta(c)pyrrol-4-yl)vinyl)-6-vinyl-4,5,6,6a-tetrahydro-3aH-cyclopenta(c)pyrrole-1,3-dione derivative of formula (I) is new.
   X=1-(2-(2-isobutoxypropoxy)ethoxy)-2-methyl-propane derivative of formula (II);
   Y'=siloxane derivative of formula (III);
   x and y=is x+y is1 in mole fraction;
   a and b=are a+b is1 in mole fraction;
   M1=is an integer of 0-50;
   l1 and n1=each independently an integer from 0-20;
   R1-R6=same as or different from each other, and are hydrogen or 1-3 carbons alkyl;
   p1=integer from 1-35; and
   q1 and r1=each independently an integer of 1-10.
   . An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing crosslinked copolymer of 2-methyl-4-((Z)-2-(2-methyl-1,3-dioxo-6-vinyl-4,5,6,6a-tetrahydro-3aH-cyclopenta(c)pyrrol-4-yl)vinyl)-6-vinyl-4,5,6,6a-tetrahydro-3aH-cyclopenta(c)pyrrole-1,3-dione derivative of formula (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The molecular weight of 1-(2-(2-isobutoxypropoxy)ethoxy)-2-methyl-propane derivative of formula (II) is 900-2900 g/mol, and the molecular weight of siloxane derivative of formula (III) is 1500-3500 g/mol. The crosslinked copolymer has a carbon dioxide permeability measured under a pressure of 10 atmospheric pressure compared to the carbon dioxide permeability measured under a pressure of 25 atmospheric pressure. The gas separation membrane has a carbon dioxide permeability of 380 barrels or more, and a carbon dioxide/nitrogen selectivity of 50 or more measured under conditions of 1 bar and 30 degrees C. The first norbornene-based monomer and the first crosslinking agent are reacted in a molar ratio of 1:1-1:5. The second norbornene-based monomer and the second crosslinking agent are reacted in a molar ratio of 1:1-1:5. The first macromonomer and the second macromonomer are mixed in a ratio of 6:1-12:1. Preparation (Claimed): The method of producing a crosslinked copolymer, which involves preparing a first macromonomer by reacting a first norbornene-based monomer and a first crosslinking agent, reacting a second norbornene-based monomer and a second crosslinking agent to prepare a second macromonomer, and preparing a crosslinked copolymer by mixing the first macromonomer and the second macromonomer. Preferred Components: The first norbornene-based monomer and the second norbornene-based monomer are norbornene dicarboxylic anhydride, exo-3,6-epoxy-1,2,3,6-tetrahydrophthalic anhydride (exo-3,6-epoxy1,2,3,6-tetrahydrophthalic anhydride), methyl-5-norbornene-2 ,3-dicarboxylic anhydride or 3-methyl-4-cyclohexene-1,2-dicarboxylic anhydride.

PN CN111059475-A; CN211976557-U
TI Low-pressure pre-warning protection system for mine liquid carbon dioxide pipelines includes low temperature stop valve connected to automatic opening and closing system connected to intrinsic safety control box.
AU ZHAO S
   ZHANG P
   GU X
   CHANG L
AE XIAN SENLAN TECHNOLOGY & TRADE CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
   XIAN SENLAN TECHNOLOGY & TRADE CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202036581X
AB    NOVELTY - A low-pressure pre-warning protection system includes pressure sensor (1) having monitoring terminal located in transmission pipeline (5), for sensing pressure value in transmission pipeline in real time; low temperature stop valve (3) provided on transmission pipeline, for controlling on-off of transmission pipeline; automatic opening and closing system (2) connected to low temperature stop valve by mine cable; and intrinsic safety control box (4) connected to automatic opening and closing system, and pressure sensor, for generating and sending control command to close or open low temperature stop valve to automatic opening and closing system. When pressure value is greater than preset pressure value, intrinsic safety control box generates control command to open low temperature stop valve. When pressure value is less than or equal to set pressure value, intrinsic safe control box generates control command to close low temperature stop valve.
   USE - The low-pressure pre-warning protection system is used for mine liquid carbon dioxide pipelines.
   ADVANTAGE - The protection system has automatic opening and closing system that controls opening or closing of low temperature stop valve through mining cable, thus realizing real-time monitoring and control of pressure value in carbon dioxide pipeline. When pressure value is lower than preset pressure value, low temperature stop valve can be effectively used to close pipeline, thus maintaining pressure in pipeline. To avoid problem that when pipeline pressure is lower than 1 MPa, the carbon dioxide of pipeline changes from liquid to solid and forms dry ice, which can cause carbon dioxide pipeline to be unusable for a long time.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the protection system.
   Pressure sensor (1)
   Automatic opening and closing system (2)
   Low temperature stop valve (3)
   Intrinsic safety control box (4)
   Transmission pipeline (5)

AE KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
GA 2021D6785M
AB    NOVELTY - Electrode catalyst comprises metal particles supported on a carbon-based carrier; and metal nanoparticles in the form of dendrites are disposed on the surface of the metal particles.
   USE - The electrode catalyst is useful in carbon dioxide reduction device (claimed) for carbon dioxide reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode catalyst has high energy efficiency and current density in a specific voltage range, having porous carbon-based carrier can increase the surface area to improve the current density, thus increasing the carbon dioxide reduction efficiency, having nanoporous structure can increase the carbon dioxide gas mass transfer of the electrode catalyst, and can produce carbon monoxide with high carbon monoxide selectivity and high current density in a system for electrochemically reducing gaseous carbon dioxide by increasing the Faraday efficiency of the carbon monoxide generating reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) carbon dioxide reduction device comprising electrode catalyst; and
   (2) producing electrode catalyst, comprising obtaining carbon-supported metal particles by drying a first solution including a carbon-based carrier and a first metal precursor dispersed in a first solvent and then heat-treating in a hydrogen atmosphere, and reducing the second solution in which the carbon-supported metal particles, the second metal precursor, and the dispersing agent are dispersed in a second solvent using an acid to grow the metal nanoparticles in the form of dendrites on the surface of the metal particles.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The electrode catalyst further comprises a metal oxide on the surface of the metal nanoparticles. Preferred Components: The metal particles comprise at least one of tungsten, platinum, iridium, molybdenum, tin, antimony, titanium, bismuth and their alloys. The metal nanoparticles include at least one of silver, gold, zinc, copper, indium and their alloys, and are grown in the form of dendrites on the surface of the metal particles using the metal particles as seeds, and uniformly cover the surface of the metal particles. The metal particles are in the form of nanoparticles of 10 nm or less, in the form of secondary particles in which the nanoparticles are aggregated, in the form of single particles having an average particle diameter of 0.01-2 mu m, or a mixture. The carbon-based carrier includes at least one of carbon black, carbon nanotubes, graphene, carbon nanofibers and graphitized carbon black. The content of the metal nanoparticles is 25-1000 pts. wt. based on 100 pts. wt. carbon-based carrier. The content of the metal particles is 2-100 pts. wt. based on 100 pts. wt. carbon-based carrier. Preferred Method: The first metal precursor includes at least one of tungsten chloride, ammonium tungstate, tungstic acid, tungsten acetate, tungsten sulfate and tungsten hydroxide. The second metal precursor is at least one of silver nitrate, silver perchlorate, silver chlorate, silver carbonate, silver sulfate, silver chloride, silver bromide, silver acetate and silver fluoride.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide reduction device includes a gas diffusion layer, a catalyst layer including the electrode catalyst, and a membrane electrode assembly including an electrolyte membrane.
   EXAMPLE - 100 mg of Carbon black (KB300J) and 215 mg tungsten chloride were dissolved in 20 ml ethanol, ultrasonicated for 20 minutes, and then dried at 80 degrees C using a hot plate. Then, hydrogen heat treatment was performed at 600 degrees C for about 1 hour to obtain carbon-supported tungsten. 50 mg carbon-supported tungsten was added with 234 mg silver nitrate and 200 mg sodium dodecyl sulfate (SDS) were added, dissolved in 200 ml of distilled water, and 234 mg ascorbic acid was dissolved in 200 ml distilled water was added. The solution was stirred at room temperature for about 2 hours, filtered and washed twice, and then dried in an oven at 70 degrees C to obtain a carbon-supported tungsten-silver electrode catalyst.

PN CN110669924-A
TI Desulfurizing lead paste based on carbon dioxide            recycling involves carrying out pre-desulfurization on            lead paste, decomposing desulfurized lead paste, and            recycling carbon dioxide and regenerating sodium            carbonate.
AU ZHANG J
   LUO L
   CAO J
   HUANG Y
   MA Y
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 202006165W
AB    NOVELTY - Desulfurizing a lead paste based on carbon                dioxide recycling involves: (1) carrying out                pre-desulfurization on lead paste by adding a waste                lead paste slurry made from waste lead storage                battery after mechanical crushing and sorting, and                a desulfurizing agent sodium carbonate to a                reaction tank to perform pre-desulfurization                reaction, and carrying out solid-liquid separation                to obtain a sodium sulfate filtrate and a filter                residue, namely a desulfurized lead paste; (2)                decomposing the desulfurized lead paste by                thermally decomposing the desulfurized lead paste,                converting lead carbonate into lead oxide and                releasing carbon dioxide flue gas; and (3)                recycling carbon dioxide and regenerating sodium                carbonate by introducing the sodium sulfate                filtrate into ammonia gas, then introducing in the                carbon dioxide flue gas to get sodium bicarbonate                crystals and ammonium sulfate, and thermally                decomposing the crystals to obtain sodium                carbonate, which is used in step (1).
   USE - The method is useful for desulfurizing a lead                paste based on carbon dioxide recycling.
   ADVANTAGE - The method overcomes the problem of ammonia                volatilization when ammonia is directly used in                lead paste desulfurization, avoids the high cost of                desulfurization of sodium lead paste, and recycles                CO2 to reduce carbon emissions. The desulfurization                agent regeneration process also utilizes the waste                heat generated by the thermal decomposition of the                desulfurized lead paste, and provides good                environmental and economic method for the                desulfurization of the lead paste in the                regeneration process of the waste lead                battery.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (3), the sodium                  sulfate filtrate obtained in step (1) is                  concentrated to a saturated sodium sulfate                  solution before passing into ammonia gas.                  Preferred Conditions: In step (2), the thermal                  decomposition temperature of the desulfurized                  lead paste is 300-500 degrees C. Preferred                  Components: In step (3), after the ammonia gas is                  dissolved, the ammonia concentration in the                  filtrate is 20-30 g/l.

TI Apparatus useful to electrochemically reduce carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, comprises oxidation electrode unit, reduction electrode unit, first chamber, second chamber, and power supplying unit.
AU YOON K
   LEE E
AE UNIV SOGANG RES FOUND (USOG-C)
GA 201819045N
AB    NOVELTY - Apparatus comprises oxidation electrode unit (120) provided with counter electrode (150), reduction electrode unit (110) provided with working electrode (140), first chamber (170) to supply carbon dioxide and/or reactant to reduction electrode unit, optional second chamber to supply carrier gas to oxidation electrode unit according to requirement, and power supplying unit to apply voltage and current to working electrode and counter electrode, respectively. The polarity of applied voltage and/or direction of the current are periodically switched in working and counter electrodes.
   USE - The apparatus is useful for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus obtains alcohols present as liquid at normal temperature as the product of the electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide using electrochemical reduction apparatus, comprising (a) supplying carbon dioxide and/or a reactant into the reduction electrode unit and selectively supplying a carrier gas into the oxidation electrode unit, and (b) periodically switching the polarity of voltage or direction of the current applied on the working electrode and the counter electrode, and reducing carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide supplied to the reduction electrode unit.

   Reduction electrode unit (110)
   Oxidation electrode unit (120)
   Working electrode (140)
   Counter electrode (150)
   First chamber (170)

TI Method for manufacturing steel product in two            steelmaking units, involves calculating expected level            of carbon dioxide emissions, and manufacturing product            into steelmaking unit, where calculated expected level            is lowest.

AE ARCELORMITTAL (ARCM-C)
GA 2022B2675C
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of a steel product (P) in at least                two steelmaking units (Si) involves calculating                (100) an expected level of carbon dioxide emissions                (Eexpi) to manufacture the product (P) in each                steelmaking unit (Si) and considering all carbon                dioxide contributions associated to raw materials,                energy sources and processes used for manufacturing                the product (P) in each steelmaking unit (Si) and                manufacturing (110) the product (P) into the                steelmaking unit (Si). The calculated expected                level of carbon dioxide emissions (Eexpi) is the                lowest.
   USE - Manufacture of steel product in steelmaking                unit used for production of pig iron and liquid                steel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables determining and reducing                the carbon dioxide footprint of steel products in                an effective manner.

TI Production of carbon material e.g. diamond,            involves electrolytically reducing carbon dioxide to            diamond in electrolytic reduction system having anode,            cathode, and electrolytic solution containing carbon            dioxide and ionic liquid.
AU GOTO T
   KOBAYASHI Y
   TCHORAP-YAMAMOTO S
   SUZUKI Y
AE UNIV DOSHISHA (UYDI-C)
GA 2022A6627J
AB    NOVELTY - Production of a carbon material involves                electrolytically reducing carbon dioxide to diamond                in an electrolytic reduction system having an                anode, a cathode, and an electrolytic solution (7)                containing carbon dioxide and an ionic                liquid.
   USE - Production of carbon material such as diamond,                glassy carbon, amorphous carbon, carbon nanotubes,                carbon nanohorns, and graphene used in                environmental applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently produces carbon                material by reducing carbon dioxide at low                cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                electrolytic reduction system, which comprises the                electrolytic solution containing ionic liquid which                is chosen from imidazolium-based ionic liquids,                aromatic-based ionic liquids, pyrrolidinium-based                ionic liquids, ammonium-based ionic liquids,                piperidinium-based ionic liquids, and quaternary                phosphonium-based ionic liquids.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                electrolytic reduction device.
   1Electrolytic cell
   2Carbon dioxide supply tube
   3Exhaust pipe
   4Lead wire
   7Electrolytic solution
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The anode is a                  platinum electrode, and the cathode is a silver                  electrode. The electrolytic solution contains a                  supporting electrolyte. The supporting                  electrolyte is potassium bicarbonate, potassium                  dihydrogen phosphate, lithium tetrafluoroborate,                  lithium hexafluorophosphate, lithium perchlorate,                  lithium hexafluoroarsenate, lithium triflate,                  lithium bis(trifluoromethane)sulfonamide, lithium                  bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide, potassium carbonate,                  lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate, or sodium                  bicarbonate, preferably lithium                  tetrafluoroborate, lithium hexafluorophosphate,                  lithium bis(trifluoromethane)sulfonamide, and                  lithium bis(fluorosulfonyl)imide. The                  electrolytic reduction device further comprises a                  reference electrode containing silver                  (Ag+)/silver electrode. Preferred Properties: The                  concentration of the supporting electrolyte in                  the ionic liquid is 0.01 mol/L to a saturated                  concentration. The cathode electrode has a                  potential of -5.0V to -0.5V.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ionic liquid is                  N,N-diethyl-N-methyl-N-(2-methoxyethyl)ammonium                  tetrafluoroborate,                  N,N-diethyl-N-methyl-N-(2-methoxyethyl)ammonium                  bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide,                  N-methyl-N-propylpiperidinium                  bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide,                  1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium                  bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide,                  1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate,                  triethylpentylphosphonium                  bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide,                  triethyloctylphosphonium                  bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide, or                  tributylmethylphosphonium                  bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide.

PN CN112619436-A
TI Preparation of mixed matrix film used for separating carbon dioxide/methane mixed gas, involves using raw material comprising polyether block amide and porous core-shell filler comprising zeolitic imidazolate framework-layered aminoclay.
AU LI X
   LV X
   DING S
   HUANG L
   ZHANG J
AE UNIV SHIHEZI (USHZ-C)
GA 2021404995
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of mixed matrix film involves using a raw material comprising 91-97 wt.% Pebax (RTM: polyether block amide) and 3-9 wt.% porous core-shell filler comprising zeolitic imidazolate framework-layered aminoclay.
   USE - Preparation of mixed matrix film used for separating carbon dioxide/methane mixed gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preparation of mixed matrix film using economical and easy-to-obtain raw materials under controllable reaction and mild conditions. The filler introduces unsaturated metal sites and amine-based carriers into mixed matrix film to improve synergistic promotion effect on carbon dioxide of the mixed matrix film, and builds a channel to promote carbon dioxide transfer to strengthen carbon dioxide separation performance of the mixed matrix film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The process involves preparing (i) zeolitic imidazolate framework-layered aminoclay by adding 0.84 g magnesium chloride hexahydrate to 20 mL ethanol at room temperature and ultrasonically-processing for 1 hour until it is completely dissolved to obtain a mixed solution, dripping 1.3 mL 3-aminopropyltriethoxysilane into the mixed solution and magnetically-stirring overnight, centrifuging, washing the obtained precipitate with ethanol three times, drying in a vacuum-drying oven at a drying temperature of 60 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain an aminoclay filler, dissolving 0.02 g aminoclay filler in 5 mL hydroalcoholic solution (60/40, volume/volume) at room temperature and adjusting dissolved pH to neutral to obtain a mixed solution, adding 0.117 g zinc nitrate hexahydrate to the mixed solution and ultrasonically-processing for 1 hour until it is completely dissolved to obtain a uniform mixed solution, adding 2.27 g 2-methylimidazole to 8 mL deionized water, ultrasonically-processing for 10 minutes until it is completely dissolved, adding to the uniform mixed solution and stirring overnight, centrifuging the mixed solution to collect a precipitate, washing with water and alcohol three times and drying in a vacuum-drying oven at a drying temperature of 40 degrees C for 48 hours, weighing (ii) Pebax (RTM: polyether block amide) particles and dissolving in a mixed solution of ethanol and water (mass ratio of 70/30) to obtain a mixed solution, heating and stirring in a water bath at 80 degrees C for 2 hours to dissolve Pebax (RTM: polyether block amide) to obtain 6 wt.% Pebax (RTM: polyether block amide) solution, and physically-blending (iii) 3 wt.%, 5 wt.%, 7 wt.% or 9 wt.% zeolitic imidazolate framework-layered aminoclay with 6 wt.% Pebax (RTM: polyether block amide) solution and stirring at room temperature for 4 hours to obtain a casting liquid, pouring the casting solution into a clean petri dish, casting and drying at room temperature (25 degrees C) for 48 hours, continuously vacuum-drying in a vacuum-drying oven at 40 degrees C to remove the residual solvent on the surface of the obtained mixed matrix film. The mixed matrix film is used to separate carbon dioxide/methane mixed gas under wet conditions of a carbon dioxide flux of 460-620 Barrer (1 Barrer = 10-10 cm3 cm/cm2 s cm mercury) and a carbon dioxide/methane selectivity of 27-40. Preferred Composition: The weight ratio of Pebax (RTM: polyether block amide) with respect to zeolitic imidazolate framework-layered aminoclay is 0.97:0.03, 0.95:0.05, 0.93:0.07 or 0.91:0.09. The amount of Pebax (RTM: polyether block amide)particles in the preparation of the Pebax (RTM: polyether block amide) solution is 0.54 g. Preferred Property: The mixed matrix film has a film thickness of 90-110 mu m.

PT P
PN KR2021036789-A; KR2319109-B1
TI Hybrid sodium-carbon dioxide secondary battery used in composite system, comprises negative electrode portion, positive electrode portion containing current collector, metal-carbon composite catalyst and electrolyte, and solid electrolyte.
AU LEEJINWOO
   JIO K
AE KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 202136726T
AB    NOVELTY - A hybrid sodium-carbon dioxide secondary battery comprises a negative electrode portion containing a current collector (A), sodium metal and an organic electrolyte, a positive electrode portion containing a current collector (B), a metal-carbon composite catalyst and an aqueous electrolyte, and a solid electrolyte positioned between the negative electrode portion and the positive electrode portion.
   USE - Hybrid sodium-carbon dioxide secondary battery used in composite system for producing carbon compounds (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The battery is efficient, and provides flexible electric energy. The system provides carbon compounds having improved yield and high quality during discharging, and efficiently uses energy by storing and conversion by using composite catalyst having excellent activity and selectivity for reduction reaction of carbon dioxide in the positive electrode.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of carbon compound of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The composite catalyst comprises metal nanoparticles bound on a carbon support in a weight ratio of 1:9-9:1. Preferred Components: The carbon support is at least one selected from carbon black, Ketjen black (RTM: electroconductive carbon black), acetylene black, graphite, carbon nanotubes, carbon nanofibers, graphene oxide and reduced graphene oxide. The metal nanoparticles are at least one selected from gold, silver, copper and tin. The aqueous electrolyte is at least one selected from sodium bicarbonate, sodium hydroxide, sodium sulfate, sodium nitrate, sodium chloride and sodium carbonate. The aqueous electrolyte is injected with carbon dioxide to operate the battery. Preferred Property: The metal nanoparticles have a diameter of 1-30 nm. Preferred Process: During charging, the battery undergoes a sodiation reaction which involves reacting sodium ion (Na+) and electron (e-) to obtain sodium at the negative electrode portion, and an oxygen evolution reaction (OER) which involves decomposition of four hydroxyl anion (4OH-) into oxygen, two molecules of water and four electrons (4e-) at the positive electrode portion. During discharging, the battery undergoes a desodiation reaction which involves ionizing sodium into sodium ion (Na+) and electron (e-) at the negative electrode portion, and at least one carbon dioxide reduction reaction at the positive electrode portion. The reduction reaction comprises reaction of carbon dioxide, two hydrogen ions (2H+) and two electrons (2e-) to obtain carbon monoxide and water, reaction of carbon dioxide, two hydrogen ions (2H+) and two electrons (2e-) to give formic acid, reaction of two molecules of carbon dioxide, twelve hydrogen ions (12H+) and twelve electrons (12e-) to give ethylene and four molecules of water, and/or reaction of two molecules of carbon dioxide, twelve hydrogen ions (12H+) and 12 electrons (12e-) to give ethanol and water. The compound generated is at least one carbon compound selected from carbon monoxide, formic acid, ethylene and ethanol.

AU YIN H
   FENG Y
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2020518740
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises 2,4,6-tris(dimethylaminomethyl)phenol, sodium polyacrylate and sodium chloride.
   USE - Composition for preparing carbon dioxide-responsive viscoelastic fluids (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition does not require complicated polymer structure design and synthesis, thus provides a simple, fast and low-cost viscoelastic fluid preparation method.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a polymer-based carbon dioxide responsive viscoelastic fluid, which comprises 2,4,6-tris(dimethylaminomethyl)phenol, sodium polyacrylate, sodium chloride, and water, the fluid has increased viscosity and elastic properties under carbon dioxide ventilation; and
   (2) a method for preparing a polymer-based carbon dioxide responsive viscoelastic fluid, which involves dissolving sodium polyacrylate and sodium chloride in water under stirring to obtain solution one, dissolving 2,4,6-tris(dimethylaminomethyl)phenol in water and stirring thoroughly to obtain solution two, mixing solution one and solution two and mixing well.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Condition: The molar ratio of 2,4,6-tris(dimethylaminomethyl)phenol to carboxyl groups in sodium polyacrylate is 1:(1-18), and the molar ratio of sodium chloride to carboxyl groups in polyacrylic acid is 1: (0.1-3). The mass concentration of the sodium polyacrylate is 1.0-5.0%. The viscosity and elastic properties of the fluid increase under the condition of passing carbon dioxide, and return to the initial rheological state after removing nitrogen through the introduction of nitrogen.

PN CN110940637-A
TI Polysilicon exogenous carbon detection method, involves removing other gasses mixed in carbon dioxide, where carbon dioxide is leaded into carbon detection cell for measuring content of exogenous carbon.
AU LIU R
   YUAN Z
   YOU S
   PENG Z
   HUANG S
AE INNER MONGOLIA TONGWEI HIGH PURITY CRYST (TWCO-C)
GA 202027488K
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves orderly putting pure iron particles, polycrystalline silicon samples, tin particles and tungsten particles on a crucible of an infrared carbon-sulfur analyzer, where the tin particles and the tungsten particles uniformly cover a surface of the polycrystalline silicon samples. The polycrystalline silicon samples are completely burned. Oxygen is passed into the infrared carbon-sulfur analyzer to oxidize carbon in the polysilicon samples to carbon dioxide. Other gasses mixed in the carbon dioxide are removed. The carbon dioxide is leaded into a carbon detection cell to measure content of exogenous carbon.
   USE - Polysilicon exogenous carbon detection method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables accurately and rapidly detecting the content of the exogenous carbon in polycrystalline silicon, ensuring high sensitivity and high disturbance resistivity.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a polysilicon exogenous carbon detection method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN CN110463883-A
TI Preparing juice by extracting aroma components of fresh flower based on supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology comprises e.g. using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology and using compound emulsifier.
AU DUAN H
AE SHANGHAI FANGQIANWUHOU INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019992408
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing juice by extracting aroma components of fresh flower based on supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology comprises (i) using supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology to extract the natural aroma components of flowers based on the properties of different flower aroma component extracts and fruit juice and performing complementary blending in proportion; and (ii) using compound emulsifier to fully emulsify the fat-soluble flower aroma component extract and the water-soluble fruit juice to prepare twelve fruit juices With the help of ultra-high pressure homogenization technology.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing juice by extracting aroma components of fresh flower based on supercritical carbon dioxide extraction technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The juice is rich in nutrition and has a floral-scented fruit juice which has a unique taste and flavor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction to prepare flower extracts comprises taking 1-10 kg flowers, vacuum drying at 30-50 degrees C to 15% moisture content, and then performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction under pressure 15-30 MPa at 35-50 degrees C, under carbon dioxide flow rate 2-6 l/hours for 2-5 hours, stopping pressure to remove carbon dioxide and to obtain flower component extract, where the flower is hibiscus, roselle, rose, lavender, jasmine flower, crape myrtle, Chrysanthemum, sweet-scented osmanthus, forget-me-not, carnation, violet and jasmine. The fruit juice is watermelon, orange, melon and apple juice obtained by squeezing out, and concentrating fruit juice made by vacuum concentration in the ratio 1: 5. The method for preparing juice according to the characteristics of different flowers and fruit juices comprises blending the flower extract and concentrated fruit juice, mixing watermelon juice is mixed with hibiscus flower, jasmine flower and forget-me-not, where the ratio of concentrated fruit juice and flower extract ratio is: 1: 0.01-0.1%. mixing apple juice mixed with roselle, crape myrtle and carnation flower extract in a weight ratio of 1: 0.01-0.1%, mixing melon juice with rose, Chrysanthemum and violet flower extracts in a ratio1: 0.01-0.1% and mixing sweet orange juice with sweet orange, lavender, sweet-scented, Osmanthus, jasmine flower extract in a ratio1: 0.01-0.1%. The weight ratio of watermelon juice and hibiscus flower is 1:0.05%, the weight ratio of apple juice and roselle flower 1: 0.1%, the weight ratio of cantaloupe juice and rose flower weight ratio is 1: 0.075% and weight ratio of sweet orange juice and lavender is 1: 0.1%. The ultra-high pressure homogenization process comprises adding adding 5 times purified water, using ultra-high pressure homogenization technology, using sucrose ester and monoglyceride is 1:1-4, compound emulsifier with different emulsification 0.5-1.5 g/kg at 30-50 degrees C, under pressure 40-80 MPa, mixing by an ultra-high pressure homogenizer, fullt emulsifying the fat-soluble flower component extract and the water-soluble fruit juice under ultra-high temperature sterilization at 135-140 degrees C for 5-15 seconds, where the aseptic filling is 150-2500 ml polyethylene terephthalate bottle.

PN KR2019067544-A
TI Hybrid hydrogen production equipment comprises a reformer for decomposing the compressed natural gas into hydrogen and carbon dioxide, a photovoltaic device to produce hydrogen with dc power produced by solar light, and a methanation unit.
AU JUNG S
AE OVERPLUS POWER CO LTD (OVER-Non-standard)
GA 201952647F
AB    NOVELTY - Hybrid hydrogen production equipment (100) comprises a reformer (110) for decomposing the compressed natural gas into hydrogen and carbon dioxide, a photovoltaic device (120) to produce hydrogen with dc power produced by solar light, and a methanation unit (130) for combining the carbon dioxide and the hydrogen to generate regenerated compressed natural gas and supplying the regenerated compressed natural gas into the reformer, where the reformer is supplied with the carbon dioxide decomposed from the reformer and receiving the hydrogen generated in the photovoltaic device.
   USE - Used as hybrid hydrogen production equipment.
   ADVANTAGE - The hybrid hydrogen production equipment prevents carbon dioxide emission.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the hybrid hydrogen production equipment.
   Hybrid hydrogen production equipment (100)
   Reformer (110)
   Photovoltaic device (120)
   Methanization unit (130)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The methanation unit includes a first pipe to supply the carbon dioxide, connected to the reformer, a second pipe to supply the regenerated compressed natural gas, connected to the reformer and a third pipe for supplying the hydrogen, connected to the photovoltaic device. The methanation unit supplies the hydrogen and the carbon dioxide into a reactor equipped with a catalyst, and forms plasmas on the catalyst to produce the regenerated compressed natural gas. The reactor is a catalyst-dielectric barrier discharge (DBD) plasma hybrid reactor. The methanation unit maintains the second pipe in a closed state when the hydrogen and the carbon dioxide are supplied and maintains the first and third pipes in a closed state when the regenerative compressed natural gas is supplied. The methanation unit checks whether any hydrogen is not used in the methanation reaction and transfers the surplus hydrogen to the hydrogen charging device that charges the hydrogen.

   NAT INST CLEAN & LOW CARBON ENERGY (CGUO-C)
GA 2021789493
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying carbon dioxide capturing amine                liquid involves using electrodialysis to purify                carbon dioxide capturing amine liquid. The water                replenishing of the electrodialysis dense chamber                is an amine absorbent solution with low                concentration. The concentration of the amine                absorbent in the amine absorbent solution is not                greater than 15 wt.%, preferably, 0.1-10                wt.%.
   USE - Method for purifying carbon dioxide capturing                amine liquid used to dilute the pure amine                absorbent to prepare the lean amine solution                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has uses the electrodialysis                concentrated water recycling low concentration                amine absorbent solution recovered by gas phase                absorbent water washing tower, not only the gas                entrained amine absorbent in the gas phase                absorbent water washing tower is used as the water                replenishing of the electrodialysis concentrated                water, inhibiting the concentration difference                diffusion of theamine liquid, and reducing the                amine loss of the purifying system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for online purification of carbon dioxide capture                amine solution, which involves performing online                purification of carbon dioxide capture amine                liquid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The water replenishing                  of the electrodialysis desalination chamber is                  the carbon dioxide capture amine solution to be                  purified. The carbon dioxide trapping amine                  solution to be purified is a lean amine solution                  containing thermally stable salt. Preferred                  Process: The method for online purification of                  carbon dioxide capture amine liquid involves                  contacting the lean amine liquid from the top of                  the absorption tower with carbon                  dioxide-containing flue gas from the bottom of                  the absorption tower, capturing the carbon                  dioxide in the flue gas, discharging the flue gas                  after the carbon dioxide is captured from the top                  of the absorption tower, making the rich amine                  liquid formed after the capture is discharged                  from the bottom of the absorption tower, making                  the trapped flue gas from the top of the                  absorption tower with carbon dioxide enters the                  gas-phase absorbent washing tower to recover the                  gas-phase amine absorbent entrained in the flue                  gas to obtain a low-concentration amine absorbent                  solution, discharging the rich amine liquid from                  the bottom of the absorption tower enters the                  regeneration tower after heat exchange to remove                  carbon dioxide in the rich amine liquid to obtain                  a regenerated lean amine liquid containing                  thermally stable salt, passing a portion of the                  obtained amine absorbent solution with a low                  concentration into the electrodialysis                  regeneration device as the replenishment water of                  the concentration chamber for circulation,                  discharging the waste liquid after the                  circulation is completed, regenerating the                  obtained lean amine solution containing thermally                  stable salt, refluxing to the top of the                  absorption tower after heat exchange, passing the                  other portion to the electrodialysis regeneration                  device as the make-up water of the desalination                  chamber for cyclic purification to obtain                  purified lean amine liquid and refluxing the                  obtained purified lean amine liquid to the top of                  the absorption tower. Preferred Conditions: The                  lean amine solution comprises 20-50 wt.% amine                  absorbent. The steam stripping is used to remove                  carbon dioxide from the rich amine liquid.

GA 2020B22564
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting tea essential oil, involves (a) adopting supercritical fluid extraction device, connecting carbon dioxide in steel cylinders, using Yinkou tea as raw material, making it reach the state of supercritical fluid through condensation, preheating and purifying carbon dioxide, and (b) placing tea leaves in a extraction device, using carbon dioxide fluid as solvent, setting pressure to 24 MPa and controlling the temperature at 30 degrees C and a carbon dioxide flow of 9 l/hour, and extracting for 4 hours, and separating many times to obtain the product.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting tea essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The material has no phase change process, which results in obvious energy saving, simple process flow and high extraction efficiency, no organic solvent residue, good product quality and no environmental pollution. The tea essential oil has strong aroma and high extraction value. The method retains original fragrance of the tea to a great extent, ensures large processing capacity, high extraction rate and high economic value, is environmentally friendly, and lays excellent foundation for the deep processing of tea.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The use of carbon dioxide as the extraction solvent has the characteristics of low critical temperature and critical pressure, and chemical inertness, and is suitable for the extraction and separation of volatile substances and substances containing heat-sensitive components. The stem content of raw tea leaves is controlled at 12%, and the water content of the tea pile is controlled within 30% to ensure the purity of the extracted essential oil. The method involves strictly controlling the extraction time at 4 hours, and sufficient extraction is ensured, and strictly controlling the extraction cost, and extraction efficiency is ensured. The three separation steps ensure that the essential oil obtained after extraction is pure and free of impurities.

PN WO2018164586-A1; NO201700348-A
TI Use of ultrasound for removing carbon dioxide from water originating from container/tank for breeding/cultivating aquatic organisms or animals or producing feed for aquatic animals from carbon dioxide emanating from aquatic animals.
AU MIKALSEN T E
AE TM HOLDING AS (TMHO-Non-standard)
GA 201871523A
AB    NOVELTY - Use of ultrasound is claimed, for removing carbon dioxide from water originating from a container/tank for breeding/cultivating aquatic organisms.
   USE - The ultrasound is useful for removing carbon dioxide from water for breeding/cultivating aquatic organisms or animals (claimed). The ultrasound is also used for producing feed for aquatic animals from carbon dioxide emanating from the aquatic animals.
   ADVANTAGE - The ultrasound removes carbon dioxide from water in a short period of time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system, which comprises at least one vessel/container/tank for water to accommodate the aquatic animals, where vessel/container/tank is connected to additional vessels/containers/tanks in the system by a number of conduits/pipes/tubes, where one of the vessels/containers/tanks is equipped with at least one ultrasound transducer for generating ultrasound to separate gases entrained in water from the breeding/cultivation container by subjecting the water to ultrasound waves, where the ultrasound-treated water is passed to a container/tank for introducing oxygen into the treated water before passing the water back to the breeding/cultivation vessel/container/tank.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred System: The container/tank is portion of a stationary fish breeding/cultivating system, or a land-based system for breeding/cultivating fish. The system comprises at least one container/tank including microorganisms, where the carbon dioxide separated from the water in the ultrasound-treating container/tank is passed to a container/tank including microorganisms using carbon dioxide as a source for carbon in their metabolism. The system comprises at least one filter for filtering water from the breeding/cultivation tank and/or from the ultrasound treatment container/tank. The water is treated with ultrasound before entering the filter, where the water is treated with ultrasound subsequently from its exit from the filter. The system is located with more than one ultrasound generator in connection with the treatment tank. The ultrasound generator(s) supply ultrasound at frequencies within 15000 Hz or more, preferably 15000-30000 Hz or more. The system comprises more than one ultrasound generator, where the ultrasound generators provide ultrasound at different frequencies.

PN WO2022186232-A1
TI Electrochemical reduction method involves            contacting solution at specific pH and carbon dioxide            with catalyst containing oxide containing titanium, and            electrochemically reducing carbon dioxide.
AU MIHO Y
   AKIHIKO A
AE UNIV KYUSHU NAT CORP (UKUY-C)
GA 2022B5927S
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical reduction method involves                contacting a solution of pH 5-15 and carbon dioxide                with a catalyst containing an oxide containing                titanium, and electrochemically reducing the carbon                dioxide.
   USE - As electrochemical reduction method.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrochemical reduction method improves                the selectivity of methane generation in the                electrochemical reduction reaction of carbon                dioxide, and has high industrial                applicability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   a catalyst containing an oxide containing                titanium and copper; and
   a method for producing the catalyst,                comprising mixing the raw materials and placing                them in a closed container and heating until the                surface temperature of the closed container reaches                the heating temperature, maintaining the heating                temperature for 10 minutes or more to prepare a                precursor, calcining the prepared precursor at a                calcination temperature for 1 minute or more to                produce a catalyst, where the raw materials are a                titanium raw material and a copper raw                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The step of heating is                  carried out at a temperature of 60-280&#176;                  C, The step of calcination is carried out at a                  temperature of 100-800&#176; C. The                  atmosphere when calcining the precursor is air.                  Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide is                  electrochemically reduced to produce methane. The                  content of the oxide containing titanium in the                  catalyst is 10 wt.% or more. The catalyst                  comprises copper. The copper content of the                  catalyst is 0.001-90 wt.%. The                  titanium-containing oxide is titanium dioxide.                  The titanium raw material is titanium(IV)                  tetrabutoxide. The copper raw material is copper                  (II) acetate. The weight ratio of titanium                  contained in the titanium raw material and copper                  contained in the copper raw material in the                  closed container is 1:99-99.99:0.01. The hydrogen                  concentration when calcining the precursor is 0.5                  vol.% or more. The hydrogen concentration is 100                  vol.% when calcining the precursor. Preferred                  Properties: At least part of the oxide containing                  titanium has a bandgap of 3 eV or less. In the                  X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy (XPS) spectrum                  of the catalyst measured by XPS, there is a peak                  between 931-933 eV, and the half width of the                  peak between 931-933 eV is 1.4-1.9 eV. In the                  UV/visible absorption spectrum of the catalyst,                  the ratio I 400/I 600 between the intensity I 400                  at a wavelength of 400 nm and the intensity I 600                  at a wavelength of 600 nm is 0.5-3. The XPS                  spectrum of the catalyst measured by XPS has a                  peak between 528-532 eV, and the half width of                  the peak between 528-532 eV is between 1.2-1.55                  eV. The XPS spectrum of the catalyst measured by                  XPS has a peak between 457-460 eV, and the half                  width of the peak between 457-460 eV is between                  1.1-1.5 eV.

   ADVANTAGE - The material exhibits excellent stability to                oxidative degradation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for separating carbon dioxide from a gas mixture,                which involves exposing a gas mixture to a porous                structure having supported therein an alkyl                diamine-substituted aryl compound and capturing the                carbon dioxide in the porous structure and                releasing the carbon dioxide from the porous                structure.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing illustrates nitrogen                physisorption isotherms (FIG. 1A and FIG. 1C) and                pore size distribution (FIG. 1B and FIG. 1D) for                Ph-3-ED/SBA-15 and Ph-6-ED/SBA-15 composites of                various organic loadings (0%-60%) recorded at                77K.

Preferred Components:                  The alkyl diamine-substituted aryl compound is                  physically impregnated in the porous structure.                  The alkyl diamine-substituted aryl compound is                  covalently bonded to the porous structure. The                  alkyl diamine-substituted aryl compound is an                  ethyl group, propyl group or butyl group. The                  alkyl diamine-substituted aryl compound includes                  3 to 6 alkyl diamine moieties. The alkyl                  diamine-substituted aryl compound includes 3                  alkyl diamine moieties. The 3 alkyl diamine                  moieties are in the 1, 3, and 5 positions on the                  alkyl diamine-substituted aryl compound. 2                  position, 4 position, 6 position, or any                  combination of the aryl group each independently                  have a moiety selected from H or an alkyl. The                  alkyl of the alkyl diamine-substituted aryl                  compound is an ethyl group, propyl group or butyl                  group, where the alkyl diamine-substituted aryl                  compound includes 3 or 6 alkyl diamine moieties.                  The alkyl diamine-substituted aryl compound                  includes 6 alkyl diamine moieties. The porous                  structure is mesoporous. The orous structure is                  selected from the group consist of silica,                  alumina, aluminosilicates, zirconia, germania,                  magnesia, titania, hafnia, mixed oxides composed                  of those elements, a combination or the porous                  structure is selected from the group consist of a                  porous metal organic framework, a porous polymer                  structure, and a porous carbon structure.

TI Regenerating lithium precursor by putting            lithium-containing waste mixture into reactor,            replacing inside of reactor with carbon dioxide, and            performing temperature raising treatment on            lithium-containing waste mixture and carbon            dioxide.
AU LA Y H
   HA D W
   LA YEON H
   LA Y
   LUO L
   HE D
AE SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
   SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
   SK INNOVATION CO LTD (SKSK-C)
GA 202095783B
AB    NOVELTY - A lithium precursor is regenerated by putting                a lithium-containing waste mixture into a reactor;                replacing an inside of the reactor with carbon                dioxide; and performing temperature raising                treatment on the lithium-containing waste mixture                and the carbon dioxide to produce lithium carbonate                and a transition metal-containing mixture.
   USE - Regeneration of a lithium precursor.
   ADVANTAGE - Lithium precursor can be regenerated from a                lithium-containing waste mixture with high purity,                high yield, and high efficiency. The lithium                precursor may be prepared through dry mixing with                carbon dioxide at a high temperature, allowing to                prevent generation of waste liquid during wet                processing, and to regenerate the lithium precursor                with high purity under a non-acidic condition. In                addition, it is possible to recover the lithium                contained in the waste liquid which is                discarded.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: Reactor has a                  non-oxidizing atmosphere. Temperature raising                  treatment is performed at 600-1000 degrees C,                  preferably 500-800 degrees C. Preferred                  Processes: Temperature raising treatment                  comprises supplying additional carbon dioxide.                  Temperature raising treatment comprises supplying                  an inert gas or a reductive material. Temperature                  raising treatment comprises 1st treatment raising                  a temperature inside the reactor to 600 degrees C                  and maintaining for 30-120 minutes; 2nd treatment                  raising the temperature inside the reactor to 700                  degrees C after 1st treatment and maintaining for                  30-120 minutes; and 3rd treatment raising the                  temperature inside the reactor to 800 degrees C                  after the second treatment and maintaining for                  30-120 minutes. Preferred Components: Inert gas                  comprises nitrogen or argon. Reductive material                  comprises hydrogen, carbon monoxide, and/or                  carbon-based solid material. Lithium-containing                  waste mixture comprises a compound of formula                  LixM1aM2bM3cOy (1). Transition metal-containing                  mixture comprises a transition metal or its                  oxide. Transition metal comprises nickel, cobalt,                  and/or manganese. Lithium precursor consists of                  lithium carbonate. Lithium-containing waste                  mixture comprises a cathode active material                  mixture obtained from a waste lithium secondary                  battery.M1, M2, M3=Ni, Co, Mn, Na, Mg, Ca, Ti, V,                  Cr, Cu, Zn, Ge, Sr, Ag, Ba, Zr, Nb, Mo, Al, Ga,                  and/or B;x=0-1.1;y=2-2.02;a, b, c=0-1; anda+b+c=0-1.Preferred Reactor: Reactor comprises a                  fluidized bed reactor.

TI Method for treating natural gas stream for removal of water and carbon dioxide from natural gas, involves sending gas stream through multi-layered adsorbent bed, and adsorbent preferentially adsorbing hydrocarbons and aromatics.

   XU Q
AE UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
   UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 201983238S
AB    NOVELTY - A natural gas stream treating method involves sending gas stream through multi-layered adsorbent bed, where the multi-layered adsorbent bed comprises layer of an adsorbent, preferentially adsorbing C8+ hydrocarbons and aromatics over other impurities, layer of a zeolite preferentially adsorbing carbon dioxide over other impurities and layer of a zeolite preferentially removing C7- hydrocarbons over other impurities to produce a gas stream comprising methane.
   USE - Method for treating a natural gas stream for removal of water and carbon dioxide from natural gas to prevent freezing in liquefied natural gas production.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables treating a natural gas stream with strong competitive adsorption between carbon dioxide and hydrocarbons on 13X zeolite, best performance for carbon dioxide removal, and strong competitive adsorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for treating natural gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The layer of an adsorbent preferentially adsorbing C8+ hydrocarbons and aromatics comprises silica gel, zeolite, 4A zeolite, X zeolite, 13X zeolite. Preferred Method: The method further involves sensing parameter of the process and generating a signal from the sensing, sensing parameter of the process and generating data from the sensing, generating and transmitting a signal, generating and transmitting data. Preferred Process: The 4A zeolite layer removes water and bulk amounts of carbon dioxide. The 13X zeolite layer removes C7- hydrocarbons and carbon dioxide allowing methane to join gas stream comprising methane.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aromatic hydrocarbons comprises benzene, toluene and xylene.

TI Modification of biochar using supercritical carbon            dioxide e.g. in soil improvement, water treatment            and/or atmospheric treatment, involves putting biochar            into supercritical carbon dioxide to carry out            reaction.
AU XU G
   ZHANG B
   XUE B
   YANG Z
   ZHANG H
   HUANG G
   LU Y
   TENG D
AE GUIZHOU MATERIALS IND TECHNOLOGY RES INS (GUIZ-Non-standard)
   GUIZHOU EUROPER ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (GUIZ-Non-standard)
   CHINESE ACAD SCI GEOCHEM INST (CHSC-Non-standard)
   GUIZHOU MATERIALS IND TECHNOLOGY RES (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2019735899
AB    NOVELTY - Modifying biochar using supercritical carbon                dioxide involves putting biochar into supercritical                carbon dioxide to carry out reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for modifying biochar                using supercritical carbon dioxide, which is useful                in soil improvement, water treatment and/or                atmospheric treatment and for removing heavy metal                ions in a water component, where the heavy metal                ions are mercury ions, cadmium ions and/or lead                ions (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple,                environmentally-friendly, and low in cost. The                modified biochar has large specific surface area,                high pore volume, and rich surface functional                group, which can effectively remove the heavy metal                ion in the aqueous solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) the modified biochar produced by the above                method; and
   (2) use of the modified biochar in soil                improvement, water treatment and/or atmospheric                treatment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reaction                  temperature is 31.3-70 degrees C, preferably                  40-50 degrees C. The stirring speed of the                  reaction is 100-200 rpm, preferably 170-180 rpm.                  The reaction pressure of the reaction is 7.4-30                  MPa, preferably 10-15 MPa. The reaction time of                  the reaction is 1-5 hours, preferably 1-2 hours.                  Preferred Components: The biochar is chosen from                  one or more of tobacco stem biochar (preferred),                  straw biochar, shell biochar, wood biochar, pine                  cone biochar and rice husk biochar. The increase                  rate of the saturated adsorption amount of the                  modified biochar to mercury ions is greater than                  or equal to 0.67%, preferably greater than or                  equal to 3.68%. The increase rate of the                  saturated adsorption amount of cadmium ions is                  greater than or equal to 1.69%, preferably                  greater than or equal to 6.40%. The increase rate                  of the saturated adsorption amount of lead ions                  is greater than or equal to 1.63%, preferably                  greater than or equal to 20.81%. The biochar is                  tobacco rod biochar. The saturated adsorption                  capacity of modified tobacco rod biochar to                  mercury ions is greater than or equal to 414.15                  mg/g, preferably greater than or equal to 422.47                  mg/g. The saturated adsorption capacity of                  cadmium ions is greater than or equal to 210.18                  mg/g, preferably greater than or equal to 217.18                  mg/g. The saturated adsorption amount of lead                  ions is greater than or equal to 102.74 mg/g,                  preferably greater than or equal to 119.17                  mg/g.

TI Electrolysis of carbon dioxide involves providing electrolytic cell comprising ion exchange membrane containing anion exchanger and/or anion transporter, separator membrane, and salt bridge comprising liquid and/or dissolved acid.
AU SCHMID B
   RELLER C
   SCHMID G
AE SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
   SIEMENS AG (SIEI-C)
GA 2019060342
AB    NOVELTY - Electrolysis of carbon dioxide involves providing an electrolytic cell comprising an ion exchange membrane containing an anion exchanger and/or anion transporter adjacent to the cathode compartment, such that the cathode is in direct contact with the membrane, an anode compartment comprising an anode, a separator membrane, a salt bridge space arranged between the ion exchange membrane and separator membrane, and a cathode compartment comprising a cathode in which carbon dioxide is reduced. The salt bridge space comprises a liquid and/or dissolved acid.
   USE - Electrolysis of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient electrolysis of carbon dioxide into economically reusable products without mobile cations and with suppressed generation of hydrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) electrolysis plant comprising electrolytic cell; and
   (2) electrolysis system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Property: The liquid and/or dissolved acid has a pKa of 6 or less, preferably 0 or less. Preferred Composition: The liquid and/or dissolved acid is chosen from dilute or undiluted sulfuric acid, phosphoric acid solution, hydrofluoric acid solution, hydroiodic acid solution, hydrobromic acid solution, diluted or undiluted formic acid, diluted or undiluted acetic acid, sulfamic acid solution, diluted or undiluted perchloric acid, diluted or undiluted trifluoroacetic acid, diluted or undiluted trifluoromethanesulfonic acid and bis((trifluoromethyl)sulfonyl)imide solution. The anode compartment comprises an anolyte comprising liquid and/or dissolved acid. The anolyte and/or acid in salt bridge space do not comprise mobile cations excluding protons and/or deuterons. Preferred Condition: The electrolysis is carried out at a current density of more than 50 mA.cm-2, preferably 400 mA.cm-2 or more, or 600 mA.cm-2 or more.

PN CN108906049-A
TI Preparation of flower-shaped copper oxide for            photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, involves            hydrothermally reacting copper in solution obtained by            adding oxidant, shape control agent, hydrophilic            surfactant, and alkali to water.
AU XU J
   WANG Y
   ZHANG F
   TONG B
   LUAN B
AE SHANDONG CHAMBROAD PETROCHEMICALS CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 201898558N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of flower-shaped copper oxide,                involves adding an oxidant, a shape control agent,                a hydrophilic surfactant, and an alkali to water to                obtain an initial solution, and hydrothermally                reacting cleaned copper in the initial solution.                The shape control agent is sodium tungstate,                potassium tungstate, sodium molybdate, urea, or                ethylenediamine.
   USE - Preparation of flower-shaped copper oxide used                as electrode for photocatalytic reduction of carbon                dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides flower-shaped copper                oxide, which does not require additional molding                treatment and can be directly used as electrode for                photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, which                involves catalytically reducing carbon dioxide in                an electrolyte containing bicarbonate, using the                flower-like copper oxide as a working electrode,                under photoelectric conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxidant is                  ammonium persulfate, sodium persulfate, potassium                  persulfate, potassium dichromate, or potassium                  iodate. The alkali is sodium hydroxide or                  potassium hydroxide. The initial solution                  comprises 0.03-0.5 mol/L oxidant, 0.01-0.15 mol/L                  structure control agent, 1-15 mol/L alkali, and                  0.01-0.15 mol/L hydrophilic surfactant. Copper is                  copper plate or copper sheet. The electrolyte is                  potassium bicarbonate or sodium bicarbonate.                  Preferred Conditions: Hydrothermal reaction is                  carried out for 12-36 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrophilic                  surfactant is sodium dodecyl sulfate,                  hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide, or sodium                  dodecylsulfonate.

AU ZHONG H
   ZHANG H
   QIU Y
   LI X
   ZHANG T
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 201825317L
AB    NOVELTY - An electrode has a base layer, carbon dioxide                adsorption layer made of amine compound, and outer                catalyst layer, which are superimposed one upon                another. The catalytic layer consists of a catalyst                and a proton conductive agent. The loading amount                of catalyst in the catalyst layer is 0.5-5 mg/cm2.                The catalyst is heteroatom-doped carbon nanotube                grafted with metal macrocyclic compound. The base                layer has thickness of 100-400 mu m. The carbon                dioxide adsorption layer is made of porous carbon                material. The amine compound is composed of binder,                and has thickness of 20-100 mu m.
   USE - Electrode is used as cathode for                electrochemical reduction of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrode is simple and easy to implement,                and has reduced mass transfer resistance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The heteroatom is                  nitrogen, phosphorus, fluorine and/or boron. The                  catalytic layer comprises catalyst and proton                  conductive agent in the weight ratio of 1:3-3:1.                  The content of micropore is 20-50% with respect                  to total pore volume of porous carbon. The                  content of mesopores is 40-70% with respect to                  total pore volume of porous carbons. Preferred                  Properties: The nanotubes have diameter of 10-80                  nm and length of 40 nm to 5 mu m. The total pore                  volume of porous carbon is 0.5-3 cm3/g. The                  porous carbon has pore size of 2-0.5 nm and                  mesoporous pore size of 3-30 nm.

The                  heteroatom-doped carbon nanotubes have heteroatom                  mass content of 0.1-10%. The mass ratio of                  heteroatom-doped carbon nanotubes with respect to                  metal macrocyclic compound is 1:1-10:1. The                  binder in carbon dioxide adsorption layer is                  20-40% with respect to weight of adsorbent layer                  on which it is located. The amine compound is                  aniline, ethylenediamine, n-hexylamine and/or                  trimethylamine. The content of amine compound is                  1-10% with respect to total mass of porous carbon                  materials and amines. The pores of large pores                  are staggered through mesopores and micropores.                  The pore size of macropores is 0.5-5 mu m. The                  content of macropores is 10-20% with respect to                  total pore volume of porous carbon. The porous                  carbon material is KB600 having particle size of                  10-100 nm, KB300, BP2000, XC-72, acetylene black,                  acetylene activated carbon, carbon aerogels or                  carbon xerogels, carbon nanotubes, carbon                  nanofibers, and/or graphene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The binder is                  polytetrafluoroethylene or polyvinylidene                  difluoride. The proton conductive agent is                  perfluorosulfonic acid resin, sulfonated                  polyether sulfone resin, sulfonated                  polyetheretherketone resin, sulfonated                  polybenzimidazole, and/or sulfonated polyimide                  resin.

TI Preparing organic carbonate at room temperature and atmospheric pressure involves mixing imidazole ionic liquid containing carbon dioxide with alcohols and halogenated hydrocarbons, and performing addition-substitution reaction.
AU QI Z
   HU X
   CHEN L
   CHENG H
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 202063645X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing organic carbonate at room temperature and atmospheric pressure involves: (1) passing carbon dioxide into an imidazole ionic liquid to obtain an ionic liquid containing carbon dioxide; and (2) mixing the ionic liquid containing carbon dioxide obtained in step (1) with alcohols and halogenated hydrocarbons, and performing addition-substitution reaction. The steps (1) and (2) are carried out under room temperature and atmospheric conditions.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing organic carbonate at room temperature and atmospheric pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol in step (2) is monohydric alcohol and/or dihydric alcohol. The structure of the cation in the imidazole ionic liquid in step (1) is of formula (I) or formula (II). The anion of the imidazole ionic liquid in step (1) includes bicarbonate ion, acetate ion, propionate ion, butyrate ion, isobutyrate ion, trimethylacetate ion, levulinate ion or 1,2,4-triazole ion. The imidazole ionic liquid includes 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium bicarbonate, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium bicarbonate, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium acetate, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium acetate, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium levulinate, 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium levulinate, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium triazole and/or 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolim triazole. The alcohol in step (2) includes methanol, ethanol, n-propanol, isopropanol, n-butanol, isobutanol, n-pentanol, 2-pentanol, 3-pentanol, 2-methyl-1-butanol, 2-methyl-2-butanol, 3-methyl-2-butanol, n-hexanol, 2-hexanol, 3-hexanol, n-heptanol, 2-heptanol, 3-heptanol, n-octanol, 2-octanol, 3-octanol, 4-octanol, ethylene glycol, 1,2-propanediol and/or 1,2-butanediol. The halogenated hydrocarbon in step (2) is a halogenated hydrocarbon compound of formula: R5-X1 (III), a halogenated hydrocarbon compound of formula: X2-R6-X3 (IV), and halogenated hydrocarbon compounds of formula (V) and (VI). The halogenated hydrocarbon includes chloromethane, methyl bromide, iodomethane, methylene chloride, dibromomethane, diiodomethane, chloroform, bromoform, iodoform, carbon tetrachloride, carbon tetrabromide, carbon tetraiodide, chloroethane, bromoethane, iodoethane, 1,2-dichloroethane, 1,2-dibromoethane, 1,2-diiodoethane, 1-chloropropane, 1-bromopropane, 1-iodopropane, 2-chloropropane, 2-bromopropane, 2-iodopropane, 1,2-dichloropropane, 1,2-dibromopropane, 1,2-diiodopropane, 1-iodobutane, 2-iodobutane, 1-iodopentane, 1-iodohexane, 1-iodoheptane and/or 1-iodooctane. The mass ratio of the amount of imidazole ionic liquid and alcohol is 1:(0.1-10). PN CN110685089-A
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing and post-treatment process involves pre-cleaning printed and dyed material, drying, adding disperse dyes and additives, passing carbon dioxide supercritical fluid, dyeing, washing and drying.
AU HUANG X
AE JIANGSU GAOKE PHARM EQUIP CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202008658T
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing and post-treatment process involves (i) adding printed and dyed material to clean water for pre-cleaning, adding the obtained material into a cleaning solution, cleaning for 50-60 minutes, and removing and drying the obtained printed material, (ii) placing the obtained printed and dyed materials on a setting device, controlling the temperature to 200-220 degrees C for 30-50 seconds and cooling, (iii) feeding the treated printed and dyed material into an autoclave, adding 2-4 %mass disperse dyes and additives to the autoclave, passing a carbon dioxide supercritical fluid into the autoclave, controlling the temperature inside the autoclave to 120-140 degrees C and the pressure of 18-28 MPa, stirring, dyeing, and removing printed material, and (iv) adding the obtained material to clean water, washing, drying, adding stabilizer and softening agent to the water, soaking the printed material in water and removing the printed material and washing and drying.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing and post-treatment process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of improving coloring effect of the textile, and reducing fading of printed material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical carbon dioxide dyeing and post-treatment process involves (i) adding printed and dyed material to clean water for pre-cleaning at a temperature of 40-50 degrees C for 10-15 minutes, adding the obtained material into a cleaning solution, controlling the temperature to 70-85 degrees C, cleaning for 50-60 minutes, and removing and drying the obtained printed material, (ii) placing the obtained printed and dyed materials on a setting device for cleaning, preheating in the preheating zone for 1-2 minutes and controlling the preheating temperature to 60-80 degrees C, further controlling the temperature to 200-220 degrees C for 30-50 seconds and cooling the obtained material in the environment of 45 degrees C, (iii) feeding the treated printed and dyed material into an autoclave, adding 2-4 %mass disperse dyes and additives to the autoclave, passing a carbon dioxide supercritical fluid into the autoclave at a flow rate of 15-20 L/hour, controlling the temperature inside the autoclave to 120-140 degrees C and the pressure of 18-28 MPa, stirring for 30-50 minutes, dyeing, and removing printed material when the autoclave reaches normal temperature and pressure, and (iv) adding the obtained material to clean water, repeatedly washing for 3-5 minutes each time for 2-4 times, drying, adding stabilizer and softening agent to the water, soaking the printed material in water for 5-10 minutes and removing the printed material and washing for 2-3 times and drying at 30-40 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Arrangement: The setting device is a needle-chain heat setting machine or a dual-purpose needle plate cloth setting machine, preferably a dual-purpose needle plate cloth setting machine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The additive comprises 2-4 parts mass, preferably 4 parts mass co-solvent, 3-5 parts mass, preferably 5 parts mass dispersing agent, 6-8 parts mass, preferably 8 parts mass glacial acetic acid, 4-6 parts mass, preferably 6 parts mass surfactant and 2.5-4.5 parts mass, preferably 4.5 parts mass fixing agent. The usage amount of disperse dye is 30-50 parts mass.
