PT P
PN US2019382808-A1
TI Capturing and conversion of e.g. carbon dioxide into biomass, by fixing carbon contained in feedstock into organic compounds within environment via chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction utilizing chemoautotrophic microorganisms.
AU REED J S
AE KIVERDI INC (KIVE-Non-standard)
GA 2019A5864E
AB    NOVELTY - Biological and chemical process for capture and conversion of carbon dioxide and/or other sources of inorganic carbon, into biomass, involves introducing a feedstock into an environment suitable for maintaining chemoautotrophic organisms and/or chemoautotrophic cell extracts, where the feedstock does not contain any fixed carbon, and the carbon contained in the feedstock is selected from carbon dioxide gas; and fixing the carbon contained in the feedstock into organic compounds within the environment via at least one chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction utilizing one or more chemoautotrophic microorganisms selected from Ralstonia sp., Alcaligenes sp., and Hydrogenomonas sp. The chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction is driven by chemical and/or electrochemical energy provided by electron donors and electron acceptors that have been generated chemically and/or electrochemically.
   USE - The biological and chemical process is useful for capturing and conversion of carbon dioxide and/or other sources of inorganic carbon, into biomass (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can enable the effective capture of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere or from a point source of carbon dioxide emissions for the production of liquid transportation fuel and/or other organic chemical products, which can help address greenhouse gas induced climate change and contribute to the domestic production of renewable liquid transportation fuels without any dependence upon agriculture. It is designed to couple the efficient production of high value organic compounds such as liquid hydrocarbon fuel with the capture of carbon dioxide (CO2) emissions, making carbon capture a revenue generating process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Biological and chemical process for capture and conversion of carbon dioxide and/or other sources of inorganic carbon, into biomass, involves introducing a feedstock into an environment suitable for maintaining chemoautotrophic organisms and/or chemoautotrophic cell extracts, where the feedstock does not contain any fixed carbon, and the carbon contained in the feedstock is selected from carbon dioxide gas; and fixing the carbon contained in the feedstock into organic compounds within the environment via at least one chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction utilizing one or more chemoautotrophic microorganisms selected from Ralstonia sp., Alcaligenes sp., and Hydrogenomonas sp. The chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction is driven by chemical and/or electrochemical energy provided by electron donors and electron acceptors that have been generated chemically and/or electrochemically and/or are introduced into the environment from at least one source external to the environment. The electron acceptor comprises oxygen that is introduced into the environment in the form of bubbles that are not greater than 7.5 mm average diameter. The concentrations of the electron donors, electron acceptors, nitrogen, and phosphorus are maintained at levels for maximum chemoautotrophic growth. Biomass is produced by the at least one chemosynthetic reaction. It is separated from the environment and is processed into a product comprising an animal feed, a fertilizer, a soil additive, a soil stabilizer, a carbon source for large scale fermentations, and/or a nutrient source for the growth of other microbes or organisms.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Process: The biomass and/or biochemicals are processed into a carbon source for a large scale fermentation and/or a nutrient source for the growth of other microbes or organisms, where the large scale fermentation and/or the other microbes or organisms produce commercial enzymes, antibiotics, amino acids, vitamins, and bioplastics. The biomass and/or biochemicals are processed into a nutrient source for the growth of fish. Hydrogen is generated through electrolysis of water and/or thermochemical splitting of water. Electrolysis of water comprises at least one of: proton exchange membranes (PEM); a liquid electrolyte; high-pressure electrolysis; and high temperature electrolysis of steam (HTES). Hydrogen is generated through one or more of: electrolysis of hydrogen sulfide; thermochemical splitting of hydrogen sulfide; and the half-cell reduction of H+ to H2 accompanied by the half-cell oxidization of electron sources comprising ferrous iron (Fe2+) oxidized to ferric iron (Fe3+) and/or the oxidation of sulfur compounds, where the oxidized iron or sulfur is recycled to back to a reduced state through additional chemical reactions with minerals comprising at least one of a metal sulfide, hydrogen sulfide, and a hydrocarbon. Thermochemical splitting of water comprises at least one of: iron oxide cycle; cerium(IV) oxide-cerium(III) oxide cycle; zinc-zinc oxide cycle; sulfur-iodine cycle; copper-chlorine cycle; calcium-bromine-iron cycle; and hybrid sulfur cycle. Hydrogen is generated through an electrochemical or thermochemical process known to produce hydrogen with low- or no-carbon dioxide emissions, comprising at least one of: carbon capture and sequestration enabled methane reforming; carbon capture and sequestration enabled coal gasification; the Kvaerner process; a process that generates a carbon-black product; and carbon capture and sequestration enabled gasification or pyrolysis of biomass. The electron donors and/or electron acceptors are generated or recycled using renewable, alternative, or conventional sources of power that are low in greenhouse gas emissions, and where the sources of power comprise at least one of: photovoltaic, solar thermal, wind, hydroelectric, nuclear, geothermal, enhanced geothermal, ocean thermal, ocean wave, and tidal power sources. The electron donor is generated within or recycled to the environment through non- or low-carbon dioxide emitting chemical reactions with hydrocarbons, comprising one or more of: thermochemical reduction of sulfate reaction (TSR); the Muller-Kuhne reaction for the production of hydrogen sulfide or reduced sulfur; and methane reforming-like reactions utilizing metal oxides in place of water, where the metal oxides comprise one or more of: iron oxide, calcium oxide, and magnesium oxide, and where the hydrocarbon is reacted to form solid carbonate with little or no emission of carbon dioxide gas along with hydrogen electron donor product. Fixing step is followed by one or more process steps in which unused nutrients and/or process water left after removal of chemoautotrophic cell mass and/or chemical co-products of chemosynthesis and/or waste products or contaminants of the process stream produced during the fixing step are recycled back into a reactor system in which the chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction is performed to. Chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction is performed by chemoautotrophic microorganisms that have been improved, optimized or engineered for the fixation of carbon dioxide and/or other forms of inorganic carbon and the production of organic compounds. Delivery of reducing equivalents from the electron donor to the chemoautotrophic microorganisms for the chemosynthetic reaction during the fixing step is kinetically and/or thermodynamically enhanced by one or more of: introduction of hydrogen storage materials into the environment in the form of a solid support media for microbial growth that facilitates bringing absorbed or adsorbed hydrogen electron donors into close proximity with the chemoautotrophic organisms; introduction of electron mediators comprising cytochromes, formate methyl-viologen, NAD+/NADH, neutral red (NR), and quinones to help transfer reducing power from a poorly soluble electron donor comprising H2 gas or electrons in solid state electrode materials into chemoautotrophic culture media in the environment; and introduction of electrode materials in the form of a solid growth support media directly into the environment to facilitate bringing solid state electrons into close proximity with the chemoautotrophic microorganisms. The environment comprises a bioreactor, and the microorganisms are maintained in a culture medium in the bioreactor. The process further involves, prior to the fixing step, a step of reacting carbon dioxide with minerals to form a carbonate or bicarbonate product, which is then used in the fixing step. It also involves fixing the carbon dioxide and/or inorganic carbon into the organic compounds via at least one chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction within a reactor system, where the electron donor utilized in the chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction is produced via a non-biological process in the reactor system. The environment suitable for maintaining chemoautotrophic organisms and/or chemoautotroph cell extracts is maintained using continuous influx and removal of nutrient medium and/or biomass. The chemosynthetic carbon fixing reaction is aerobic. Preferred Components: The animal feed product is a feed for cattle, sheep, chickens, pigs, and/or fish. TI Carbon dioxide gas separation device comprises a gas source, a flow distributor, a gas flow meter, a venturi injection unit provided with two liquid suction ports and a tubular hydrate reaction unit.
AU FAN Q
AE JIANGYIN CHENGYUN MACHINERY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201892702J
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide gas separation device comprises a gas source, a flow distributor, a gas flow meter, a venturi injection unit provided with two liquid suction ports, a tubular hydrate reaction unit, and a gas-liquid-solid three-phase separation a unit, a first mud pump having a hydrate decomposition unit of a first pressure maintaining valve at the top. The second mud pump and a solution saturating tank having a third safety valve at a top. The third safety valve is sequentially connected. The first mud pump further comprises a chemical absorption tower, a second corrosion resistant pump, a heat exchanger a regeneration tower, a third corrosion-resistant pump and a reservoir containing a carbon dioxide chemical absorbent, where the reservoir and the upper chemical absorption tower of the first corrosion-resistant pump are cycled by the first corrosion-resistant pump.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide gas separation device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide gas separation device comprises a gas source, a flow distributor, a gas flow meter, a venturi injection unit provided with two liquid suction ports, a tubular hydrate reaction unit, and a gas-liquid-solid three-phase separation a unit, a first mud pump having a hydrate decomposition unit of a first pressure maintaining valve at the top. The second mud pump and a solution saturating tank having a third safety valve at a top. The third safety valve is sequentially connected. The first mud pump further comprises a chemical absorption tower, a second corrosion resistant pump, a heat exchanger a regeneration tower, a third corrosion-resistant pump and a reservoir containing a carbon dioxide chemical absorbent, where the reservoir and the upper chemical absorption tower of the first corrosion-resistant pump are cycled by the first corrosion-resistant pump. The flow distributor is in communication with the bottom inlet of the solution saturation tank and the bottom outlet of the solution saturation tank is sequentially passed through the liquid phase mass flow meter and the two liquid suction port communication valves of the ninth station and the venturi injection unit. The second safety valve is arranged on the top of the gas-liquid-solid three-phase separation unit, and the gas-liquid-solid three-phase separation unit sequentially passes through the third one-way valve, the second pressure-retaining valve and the fourth one-way valve and is in communication with the lower part of the chemical absorption tower. The upper part of the chemical absorption tower is connected to the hydrogen collecting tank provided with the first safety valve through the fifth one-way valve. The regeneration tower is further connected to the hydrate decomposition unit, and the regenerated carbon dioxide gas is led to the hydrate decomposition unit where it will be mixed with the carbon dioxide generated during the dissociation process and then subjected to subsequent treatment.

PT P
PN WO2022101275-A1
TI Preparing cyclic carbonate by continuously reacting gaseous mixture of epoxide and carbon dioxide in presence of heterogeneous catalyst to liquid cyclic carbonate product and gaseous effluent stream having unreacted epoxide and the dioxide.
AU VERGUNST F
   VELDER P
   VAN DER HORST N T
AE NEW GREEN WORLD BV (NEWG-Non-standard)
GA 202268916P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic carbonate comprises continuously reacting a gaseous mixture of an epoxide compound and carbon dioxide in the presence of a heterogeneous catalyst at a pressure of 0.1-0.4 MPa in at least one reactor to a liquid cyclic carbonate product and a gaseous effluent stream comprising unreacted epoxide compound and carbon dioxide, where part of the gaseous effluent is purged from the process and another part of the gaseous effluent is fed to an ejector and the gaseous effluent mixes with a gaseous mixture of epoxide compound and carbon dioxide having a pressure which is at least more than 0.3 MPa higher than the pressure of the gaseous effluent to obtain an ejector effluent which ejector effluent is fed to the reactor.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: is simple and consumes less energy, the loss in carbon dioxide caused by operating the stripper at higher pressure in the process has been found to be low and fully compensated by the beneficial of not having to use complex compressor and by lower energy requirement; utilizes higher pressure mixture may beneficially obtained by evaporating liquid epoxide at elevated pressure and by evaporating liquid carbon dioxide having elevated pressure stored or provided and mixing the evaporated gaseous components, tin this way use is made of high storage pressures of carbon dioxide to arrive at a process which does not require a compressor or does not require a large capacity compressor; free of special measures, e.g. compressors or blowers, to create a flow of the intermediate gaseous effluent from an upstream reactor to a downstream reactor; and utilizes reactors are aligned in series is beneficial because it allows one to position a reactor having a more active catalyst as a downstream reactor, preferably as the downstream reactor, this will enhance overall conversion to cyclic carbonate and lower the amount of the epoxide compound in the gaseous effluent and in turn is beneficial because this will result in that less of the valuable epoxide compound is lost via the purge.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The gaseous effluent is increased in pressure by means of a blower before mixing the gaseous effluent in the injector. The gaseous mixture of epoxide compound and carbon dioxide as supplied to the ejector is obtained by mixing gaseous epoxide obtained by evaporating liquid epoxide and gaseous carbon dioxide obtained by evaporating liquid carbon dioxide having a pressure of 1.4-4 MPa. The liquid cyclic carbonate product is discharged from the reactor and any epoxide compound present in the discharged liquid cyclic carbonate product is stripped out by contacting the liquid cyclic carbonate product with the gaseous carbon dioxide, where a cleaned product stream is obtained. The reactors in series comprises upstream reactor and a downstream reactor and optional intermediate reactors, where to the upstream reactor the ejector effluent is fed, a liquid cyclic carbonate product is discharged from every reactor, an intermediate gaseous effluent comprising unreacted epoxide compound and carbon dioxide is routed from an upstream reactor to the next downstream reactor in the series of reactors and from the downstream reactor of the series the gaseous effluent stream comprising unreacted epoxide compound and carbon dioxide is discharged. The catalyst of the upstream reactor is regenerated by taking this reactor off line such the second reactor in the series becomes the upstream reactor of the series of reactors, where a new reactor comprises regenerated catalyst is connected to the series of reactors as the downstream reactor. The heterogenous catalyst is present in the at least two reactors in series as a slurry, where the temperature in the reactors is 20-150 degrees C and below the boiling temperature of the cyclic carbonate product at the chosen pressure. The heterogeneous catalyst is a supported dimeric aluminum salen complex and the activating compound is a halide compound. The support (S1) is composed of particles having an average diameter of 10-2000 mu m and particle comprising silica, alumina, titania, siliceous mobil composition of matter 41 (MCM-41) or siliceous MCM-48. Preferred Components: The pressure of the gaseous carbon dioxide is 0.5-0.8 MPa. The heterogeneous catalyst comprises an organic compound containing at least one nucleophilic group. The nucleophilic group is a quaternary nitrogen halide. The solid support is supported dimeric aluminum salen complex of formula (I). The halide compound is benzyl halide. The benzyl halide is benzyl bromide. The epoxide compound is ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, butylene oxide or pentene oxide.S1 = solid support connected to the nitrogen atom via an alkylene group, where the supported dimeric aluminum salen complex is activated by a halide compound;X1 = tertiary butyl;X2 = H; andEt1 = 1-10C alkyl.
   EXAMPLE - Gaseous epoxide was fed at 4.5 kg/second the fresh carbon dioxide was fed at 3.5 kg/second and the recycle flow was set at 2 kg/second. In the energy calculations the energy input for heating the epoxide feedstock of 16-110 degrees C (at 100 degrees C, feedstock A vapor pressure was 5 barg) and an additional super heating to 110 degrees C to prevent unwanted condensation in downstream piping was taken into energy input for carbon dioxide feedstock pressurization (will be supplied and stored at 10+ barg) was taken into account. In static ejector stream was pressurized to a resulting discharge pressure. The discharge pressure was calculated based on the pre-defined ratio between flows and provided by the supplier of static ejector equipment. The remaining required compression duty for a compressor/blower between elector and upstream reactor was calculated to achieve the same pressure to obtain cyclic carbonate.

GA 202245917C
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a dry ice storage tank (1), a rectifying tank (2) and a collecting tank (3). The inner wall of the dry ice storage tank is fixedly connected with baffle plate. The top unit of the dry ice storage tank is provided with a driving motor. The output end of the driving motor is fixedly connected with stirring shaft. The stirring shaft extends to the inner unit of the dry ice storage tank. The outer wall of the stirring shaft is fixedly connected with connecting rod. The first end of the connecting rod is fixedly connected with ring-shaped plate.
   USE - Device for recycling and using carbon dioxide in dry ice preparation process for dry ice heating, dry ice heat absorption sublimation and temperature of water.
   ADVANTAGE - The recovery rate and recycling efficiency of the carbon dioxide are improved. The carbon dioxide recovery efficiency and recovery rate are improved, the whole structure of the device is simple, it is suitable for mass production. The dry ice is driven to keep the loose motion state, the gaseous carbon dioxide remained on the surface of the dry ice can flow, so as to collect the gas. The connecting spring returns the reverse elastic force to the two ends of the elastic connecting plate, and the connecting spring is stretched and compressed force, and thus the swinging frequency of the connecting plate is accelerated, prolonging the swinging time of the plate, better applying force to dry ice, and keeping the ice to move to promote the flow of the gas carbon dioxide.

PT P
PN CN113402061-A
TI Method for accelerating capture of carbon dioxide            using nanofiltration seawater, involves adding            polyaluminum chloride, anionic polyacrylamide, and            sodium methylene naphthalenesulfonate to wastewater            containing seawater until calcium carbonate and            magnesium carbonate are formed in wastewater.
AU WANG R
   WANG Z
   CAO S
   LIU Y
AE HUBEI FORBON TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2021B0833C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for accelerating capture of carbon                dioxide using nanofiltration seawater involves                adjusting pH of the wastewater containing                nanofiltration seawater to 7-9, adding polyaluminum                chloride, anionic polyacrylamide, and sodium                methylene naphthalenesulfonate until large                flocculant calcium carbonate and magnesium                carbonate are formed in product wastewater                containing nanofiltration seawater to capture                carbon dioxide, leaving still, discharging settled                calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate                wastewater to concentration tank, drying to obtain                block calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate,                mechanically cutting block calcium carbonate and                magnesium carbonate, pulverizing to break block                into small pieces of magnesium carbonate and                calcium carbonate, passing pulverized material to                thermal infrared drying heating chamber through the                material transmission system, performing thermal                infrared drying to obtain calcium carbonate and                magnesium carbonate as products of nanofiltration                seawater.
   USE - Method for accelerating capture of carbon                dioxide using nanofiltration seawater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively produces calcium                carbonate and magnesium carbonate product with                multiple voids and low moisture content, consumes                less energy, and is suitable for large-scale                production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for accelerating capture of carbon                dioxide using nanofiltration seawater involves                adding sodium carbonate or potassium carbonate to                adjust the pH value of the wastewater containing                nanofiltration seawater to 7-9, continuously adding                0.05-2 %mass polyaluminum chloride, and 0.5-100 ppm                anionic polyacrylamide, 0.05-0.1 %mass sodium                methylene naphthalenesulfonate until large                flocculant calcium carbonate and magnesium                carbonate are formed in product wastewater                containing nanofiltration seawater to capture                carbon dioxide, lowering temperature of product                wastewater to 5-10 degrees C, leaving still for                sedimentation of calcium carbonate and magnesium                carbonate, discharging settled calcium carbonate                and magnesium carbonate wastewater to concentration                tank through the pipeline, drying calcium carbonate                and magnesium carbonate using filter press to                obtain block calcium carbonate and magnesium                carbonate, mechanically cutting block calcium                carbonate and magnesium carbonate, pulverizing to                break block into small pieces of magnesium                carbonate and calcium carbonate, passing pulverized                material to thermal infrared drying heating chamber                through the material transmission system, and                performing thermal infrared drying to obtain dried                calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate as                products of nanofiltration seawater trapping carbon                dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The thermal infrared                  drying time is 0.1-0.5 hour. Preferred Property:                  The dried calcium carbonate and magnesium                  carbonate have moisture content of 0.5-5%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Property: The anionic                  polyacrylamide has molecular weight of                  10000000-18000000.

PN US2021213409-A1; WO2021146756-A1; CN114980998-A
TI Zero emission nested-loop reforming system            comprises a reactor comprising inlet and an outlet, a            regenerator having an inlet, first outlet and a second            outlet, and photocatalytic reformer having at least one            inlet and at least one outlet.
AU KELLEY K P
   SIDITSKY M E
   SIDITSKY M
   KELLEY K
AE PURE SUSTAINABLE TECHNOLOGIES LLC (PURE-Non-standard)
   PURE SUSTAINABLE TECHNOLOGIES LLC (PURE-Non-standard)
GA 202179985J
AB    NOVELTY - Zero emission nested-loop (ZEN) reforming                system comprises a reactor comprising an inlet and                an outlet, a regenerator having an inlet, a first                outlet and a second outlet, and a photocatalytic                reformer having at least one inlet and at least one                outlet.
   USE - Zero emission nested-loop reforming                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The zero emission nested-loop reforming system                provides a scalable, eco-friendly process to                produce high quality hydrogen at a relatively low                operating cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for for Zero Emission Nested-loop                reforming for hydrogen production, which involves                supplying a steam methane reforming catalyst to a                reactor, receiving the steam methane reforming                catalyst adsorbed with carbon dioxide into a                regenerator, desorbing carbon dioxide from the                steam methane reforming catalyst in the                regenerator, receiving the steam methane reforming                catalyst into the reactor, then receiving carbon                dioxide into a photocatalytic reformer, receiving a                mixture of oxygen and methane into a gas separator,                and receiving methane into the reactor;
   (2) a method for converting carbon dioxide to                methane and oxygen, which involves transporting                carbon dioxide from a regenerator to a                photocatalytic reformer, adding water to the                photocatalytic reformer, and discharging a mixture                of methane and oxygen.
   (3) a regenerator, which includes a vessel,                where the vessel has a multiple of chambers and a                multiple of valves, where a first of the first                multiple of valves separates the first of the                multiple of chambers from the second of the                multiple of vessel, where the second of the first                multiple of valves separates the second of the                plurality of chambers from the third multiple of                chambers; and
   (4) a photocatalytic reformer comprises a                vessel, where the vessel has at least one inlet and                an outlet, where carbon dioxide enters the vessel                via the at least one inlet, and where methane and                oxygen exit the vessel via the outlet.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of                system for Zero Emission Nested-loop reforming for                hydrogen production.
   Carbon dioxide to methane and oxygen system.                (11.)
   Photocatalytic methanation system.                (12.)
   Reactor. (13.)
   Regenerator. (14.)
   Gas separator. (16.)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRICAL POWER AND ENERGY - Preferred Conditions: The reactor is                  configured to receive a gas mixture via the                  inlet, and where the reactor is configured to                  output hydrogen and catalyst adsorbed with carbon                  dioxide via the outlet. The methane from outside                  of the system is received into the inlet of the                  reactor. Preferred Components: The gas mixture is                  selected from a group consisting of: methane,                  steam, and hydrogen.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN112876449-A; CN112876449-B
TI Production of cyclic carbonate involves adding alkylene oxide, carbon dioxide to reactor, maintaining supply of carbon dioxide for reaction, and subjecting material from negative pressure flash evaporation to thin-film evaporation.
AU DONG Z
   ZHU J
   LIU Z
   ZHANG H
   SHI D
   WANG G
   YU H
AE JIANGSU OXIRAN CHEM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
   LIAONING OXIRANCHEM INC (LIAO-Non-standard)
GA 202164020T
AB    NOVELTY - Production of cyclic carbonate involves continuously adding alkylene oxide, carbon dioxide and mother liquor containing catalyst and cyclic carbonate to the reactor (R1) to perform one-stage reaction, continuously feeding the material from the reactor to the reactor (R2), maintaining the supply of carbon dioxide in the reactor (R2) for two-stage reaction, subjecting the materials from the reactor (R2) to low pressure flash evaporation and negative pressure flash evaporation to discharge excess carbon dioxide and unreacted alkylene oxide, and subjecting the material from negative pressure flash evaporation to thin-film evaporation to obtain cyclic carbonate product and residual liquid containing catalyst. The reactor (R1) is circulating loop reactor. The method involves feeding residual liquid, excess carbon dioxide and unreacted alkylene oxide into reactor (R1) and/or reactor (R2) for recycling.
   USE - Production of cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces cyclic carbonate with high production efficiency, and is continuous and stable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for continuous production system, which consists of reactor (R1) for performing one-stage reaction of alkylene oxide and carbon dioxide and is circulating loop reactor, reactor (R2) which is connected to the reactor (R1) and for performing two-stage reaction on the materials from the reactor (R1), low pressure flashing device connected to the reactor (R12) and for performing low pressure flashing of the material from reactor (R2), negative pressure flashing device connected to the low pressure flashing device and for performing negative pressure flashing on the low pressure flashing material, thin film evaporation device connected to the negative pressure flash evaporation device and for evaporating negative pressure flash evaporation material under reduced pressure to obtain cyclic carbonate product, and tail gas storage device. The gas inlet of the tail gas storage device is connected to the low pressure flashing device and negative pressure flashing device. The gas outlet is connected to the first reactor and/or the second reactor. The tail gas storage device is provided to collect the flashing tail gas of the low pressure flashing device and negative pressure flashing device, in which the flashing tail gas enters the reactor (R1) and/or reactor (R2) for recycling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Properties: The reactors (R1) and (R2) have a reaction pressure of 0.4-3 MPa, and reaction temperature of 100-150 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves feeding the material from reactor (R1) to reactor (R2), when the material level of the reactor (R1) is 80-90%, and subjecting the material from reactor (R2) to low-pressure flash distillation and the negative-pressure flash distillation, when the alkylene oxide conversion rate in the reactor (R2) is 99.9% or more. Preferred Properties: The mass ratio of the alkylene oxide to catalyst is (50:1)-(5000:1). The mass ratio of the alkylene oxide to mother liquor containing catalyst is 1:10-10:1. Preferred Conditions: The low pressure flash evaporation is carried out at a pressure of 0.1-0.15 MPa. The negative pressure flash evaporation is carried out at a pressure of 2-50 KPa. The thin film evaporation is carried out at a pressure of 0.1-10 KPa. Preferred Components: The catalyst is a complex catalyst comprising zinc-halide-ferrocyanide-ligand of formula: (ZnX)((Fe(CN)4)mL)(ZnX2)n (I). The complex catalyst comprises double metal cyano active group fixed by diamine bridging group of formula (II).m = 1 or 1/2;n = 1-40;X = F, Cl, Br or I;andL = group chosen from 13 groups e.g. groups of formulae (a)-(e), preferably diamine ligand coordinated with iron.

PT P
PN US10947486-B1; WO2021102212-A1; CA3162234-A1; EP4048429-A1; CN114980997-A; IN202217034339-A
TI Producing biochemicals from biomass feedstock by            cooking biomass feedstock in heated cooking solution,            to release saccharides, fermenting saccharides,            hydrogenating carbon dioxide, feeding biochemical in            aqueous solution and compressing.
AU CRAWFORD L A
   SCHAFER W B
   CRAWFORD L
   SCHAFER W
AE ENERGY INTEGRATION INC (ENER-Non-standard)
   ENERGY INTEGRATION INC (ENER-Non-standard)
   ENERGY INTEGRATION INC (ENER-Non-standard)
GA 2021268385
AB    NOVELTY - Process for producing biochemicals from a                biomass feedstock involves (a) cooking a biomass                feedstock in a heated cooking solution, to release                saccharides from the biomass feedstock; (b)                fermenting the saccharides to generate a first                biochemical in aqueous solution, and carbon                dioxide; (c) hydrogenating the carbon dioxide with                a hydrogen source to generate a second biochemical;                (d) feeding the first biochemical in the aqueous                solution to a first distillation column, to                generate a purified first biochemical; (e) feeding                the second biochemical to the first distillation                column, or to a second distillation column, to                generate a purified second biochemical; and (f)                compressing vapors from the first distillation                column, using mechanical vapor recompression and/or                thermal vapor recompression, to recover heat of                distillation of the first biochemical.
   USE - The process is useful for producing                biochemicals from a biomass feedstock.
   ADVANTAGE - The process can optimize the process energy                required for the conversion of carbon dioxide to                biochemicals through vapor compression and adopts                improved processes and systems that are desired                commercially for converting carbon dioxide into                useful chemicals and materials, where such                processes and systems may be implemented within                biorefineries designed for conversion of biomass                into high-value biofuels and biochemicals.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system                for producing biochemicals from a biomass feedstock                comprising a cooking stage configured for cooking a                biomass feedstock in a heated cooking solution, to                release saccharides from the biomass feedstock; a                fermentation stage configured for fermenting the                saccharides to generate a first biochemical in                aqueous solution, and carbon dioxide; a catalytic                or reactor vessel configured for hydrogenating the                carbon dioxide with a hydrogen source to generate a                second biochemical; a first distillation column                configured for separating a purified first                biochemical from the aqueous solution; optionally,                a second distillation column configured for                purifying the second biochemical; a vapor                recompression unit configured for mechanical vapor                recompression and/or a thermal vapor recompression,                where the vapor recompression unit is arranged in                vapor-flow communication with the first                distillation column, and where the vapor                recompression unit is configured to recover heat of                distillation from the first distillation                column.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The process further                  involves mechanically treating the biomass                  feedstock prior to step (b), where the first                  biochemical in aqueous solution is preheated via                  heat exchange with the heated cooking solution,                  prior to feeding the first biochemical in the                  aqueous solution to the first distillation                  column. In the process, the heat of distillation                  from step (f) is utilized, at least in part, for                  heating the heated cooking solution in step (a).                  The process further involves dehydration of the                  purified first biochemical, to generate a highly                  purified first biochemical, where the heat of                  distillation from step (f) is utilized, at least                  in part, for the dehydration of the purified                  first biochemical. The process further involves                  evaporation and/or drying of a bottoms stream                  from the first distillation column, to generate a                  stillage product, where the heat of distillation                  from step (f) is utilized, at least in part, for                  the evaporation and/or drying of the bottoms                  stream from the first distillation column. In the                  process, where the heat of distillation from step                  (f) is utilized, at least in part, for directly                  or indirectly providing heat in step (c). In the                  step (f), the vapors are compressed using the                  mechanical vapor recompression, where the                  mechanical vapor recompression is driven by an                  electrical source.Preferred Components: In the step (c), the                  hydrogen source is water, where the water is                  derived from the aqueous solution and the water                  is recycled steam condensate. The second                  biochemical is fed to the first distillation                  column. The second biochemical is fed to second                  distillation column, and where vapors from the                  second column are compressed, using mechanical                  vapor recompression and/or thermal vapor                  recompression, to recover heat of distillation of                  the second biochemical.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the process, the carbon                  dioxide passes directly to step (c), where all                  carbon dioxide hydrogenated in step (c) is from                  the carbon dioxide generated in step (b).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The system further                  comprises a dehydration unit configured for                  removing additional water from the purified first                  biochemical. The system is configured to utilize                  at least some of the heat of distillation in the                  dehydration unit. The system is configured for                  evaporation and/or drying of a bottoms stream                  from the first distillation column, to generate a                  stillage product. The system is configured to                  utilize at least some of the heat of distillation                  in generating the stillage product. The vapor                  recompression unit is a mechanical vapor                  recompression unit. The mechanical vapor                  recompression unit is driven by an electrical                  source. The second distillation column is present                  in the system.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)
MC J01-A01; J01-A02B; J01-A02A2; J01-A03; D05-C; D05-A04A; H09-F03; E11-F03; E11-F02B; E10-E04E; E11-D02

PN CN210543484-U; CN112295258-A
TI Supercritical extraction system, has collecting chamber whose inner side is equipped with collecting container, and condensing device connected to upper part of collecting container, where condensing device is provided with outlet and collecting pipe.
AU YANG J
AE SEASON FARM BIO-ELECTROMECHANICAL CO LTD (SEAS-Non-standard)
   SEASON FARM BIO-ELECTROMECHANICAL CO LTD (SEAS-Non-standard)
GA 202046081T
AB    (CN210543484-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a supercritical extraction system, comprising an extracting device, which is provided for accommodating the raw material placer wants to extract, while the outside of the extraction device is connected with the supercritical device and a collecting device; the supercritical device connected with a storage component extracting carbon dioxide enters the extraction device by the pressurizing assembly, and the is connected with collecting pipe collecting device is connected with the extraction device, the collecting pipe is provided with a preventing check valve of carbon dioxide reflux extraction device; the collecting device has: collecting room and the condensing device, the collection chamber is placed with the collecting container, and the condensing device is corresponding to the collecting container is provided with a outlet. The supercritical extraction system of the utility model uses the carbon dioxide in a supercritical state to extract extraction, which effectively avoids the extract of active ingredient of damage and the problem of residual solvent.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of a Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction system.
EA    NOVELTY - The system has an extracting device whose inner side is provided with a raw material placing device. The extracting device is provided with a valve port. The valve port is provided with a reciprocating valve for opening and closing the valve port. An outer side of the extracting device is provided with a raw material conveying device that is connected with the valve port. The raw material conveying device is provided with a reciprocating carrier for carrying the raw material placing device from the valve port. A condensing device is connected with a collecting pipe of the extracting device. The condensing device is provided with condensed drop outlet at upper side of collecting container.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system extracts supercritical carbon dioxide, avoids damage of active component in the extract and problem of solvent residue.

PT P
PN CN109668592-A
TI Parking lot environment monitoring system compares obtained carbon dioxide concentration information of underground parking lot with preset concentration, and opens exhaust fan so that exhaust fan is in underground parking lot.
AU REN Q
AE REN Q (RENQ-Individual)
GA 201939903C
AB    NOVELTY - The system obtains (101) temperature information, humidity information, and carbon dioxide concentration information of the underground parking lot. The obtained temperature information of the underground parking lot is compared (102) with a preset temperature, and the fan is turned (103) on so that the fan cools the underground parking lot. The obtained humidity information of the underground parking lot is compared (104) with the preset humidity, and the dryer is turned (105) on when the humidity information of the underground parking lot is determined to be greater than the preset humidity information. The obtained carbon dioxide concentration information of the underground parking lot is compared (106) with a preset concentration, and the exhaust fan is opened (107) when determining that the carbon dioxide concentration information of the underground parking lot is greater than a preset concentration.
   USE - Parking lot environment monitoring system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes automatic monitoring of the underground parking lot environment, saves electric energy, and has high monitoring efficiency.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the parking lot environment monitoring process. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Step for obtaining temperature information, humidity information, and carbon dioxide concentration information of underground parking lot (101)
   Step for comparing obtained temperature information of the underground parking lot with preset temperature (102)
   Step for turning on fan so that fan cools underground parking lot when determining that temperature information of underground parking lot is greater than preset temperature information (103)
   Step for comparing obtained humidity information of underground parking lot with preset humidity (104)
   Step for turning on dryer when humidity information of underground parking lot is determined to be greater than preset humidity information in order to make the dryer heat air temperature of underground parking lot to prevent underground parking lot from generating odor due to mildew on ground (105)
   Step for comparing obtained carbon dioxide concentration information of the underground parking lot with preset concentration (106)
   Step for opening exhaust fan when determining that carbon dioxide concentration information of underground parking lot is greater than preset concentration in order to make exhaust fan exchange air with outside air in underground parking lot (107)

PT P
PN CN109555569-A; CN109555569-B
TI Supercritical carbon dioxide circulation cold waste heat recycling power generating system, has regenerator whose outlet is connected to inlet of heat collector, and super-heater whose outlet is connected with inlet of turbine.
AU LIU M
   ZHANG X
   YAN J
   CHONG D
   XING Q
   LIU J
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 201932441W
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a secondary air preheater (13) connected with a pre-cooler (14), a main compressor (1), and a low-temperature regenerator (2). An exhaust outlet of a low-pressure turbine (11) is connected to a high temperature regenerator (3) and an inlet of the low temperature regenerator. An air outlet of the secondary air preheater is connected with an air inlet of an air pre-heater (15). An outlet of a re-compressor (12) is connected with an inlet of the high temperature regenerator. A cold working medium outlet of the low temperature regenerator is connected to a working medium inlet of a heat collector (16). A working medium outlet of a high temperature super-heater (6) is connected with an inlet of a high pressure turbine (7).
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide circulation cold waste heat recycling power generating system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system utilizes hearth radiation heat load shared by two radiant heat receiving surfaces of a hot air wall and reheating gas wall in a furnace so as to prevent overheating of supercritical carbon dioxide. The system diverts part of low-temperature supercritical carbon dioxide working fluid from the outlet of the re-compressor into the super-heater of a boiler tail so as to absorb heat of low-temperature flue gas, thus reducing exhaust temperature of the boiler and improving efficiency of the boiler. The system reduces cold source loss by using cold source supercritical carbon dioxide working medium to preheat cold air in the secondary air preheater, thus improving cycle efficiency and power generation efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a supercritical carbon dioxide circulation cold waste heat recycling power generating system operating method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a supercritical carbon dioxide circulation cold waste heat recycling power generating system. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
   Main compressor (1)
   Low-temperature regenerator (2)
   High temperature regenerator (3)
   High temperature super-heater (6)
   High pressure turbine (7)
   Low-pressure turbine (11)
   Re-compressor (12)
   Secondary air preheater (13)
   Pre-cooler (14)
   Air pre-heater (15)
   Heat collector (16)
DC Q72 (Steam generation (F22)); Q73 (Combustion equipment/processes (F23)); X15 (Non-Fossil Fuel Power Generating Systems)
MC Q72-A01; Q72-A02; Q72-A04; Q72-T; Q72-U16; Q73-T02; Q73-T07; Q73-U20; X15-H

PT P
PN JP2018199103-A; JP6890320-B2
TI Catalyst used for hydrogenating carbon dioxide and storing hydrogen for producing produce formic acid and/or formate, comprises metal complex coordinated by nitrogen with bidentate ligand, or its isomer or salt as active ingredient.
AU HIMETA Y
   ONISHI N
   KANEGA R
   MURATA K
AE NAT INST ADVANCED IND SCI & TECHNOLOGY (NIIT-C)
GA 2018A2174D
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises a metal complex coordinated by nitrogen with a bidentate ligand, or its isomer, or salt as an active ingredient.
   USE - Catalyst is used for hydrogenating carbon dioxide and storing hydrogen (all claimed) for producing produce formic acid e.g. methanol and/or formate used as liquid fuel for transportation/storage.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is capable of hydrogenating carbon dioxide with high efficiency. The catalyst structure is simple and can be prepared by an economical process. The catalyst structure can be changed to various derivatives. The catalyst provides formic acid and/or formate which are easy to store and transport. The catalyst is easy to incorporate into a carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction apparatus.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A catalyst comprises a metal complex of formula (I) coordinated by element group (X1) and nitrogen with a bidentate ligand, or its isomer or salt as an active ingredient.
   M = Ir, Rh, Ru, Co, Os, Ni, Fe, Pd or Pt;
   L = aryl anionic ligand or aryl ligand (both optionally substituted with one or more substituent);
   J = arbitrary ligand or absent;
   Xi = N, O, S or C;
   i = 1 or 2;
   Qi = H, alkyl, hydroxy, alkoxy, oxyanion, oxo, nitro, halo, sulfonyl, acyl, carboxylic acid or its derivative, amino, alkylamino, amido, or phenyl;
   Q1+Q2 = ring;
   Z = O or S;
   T = H or arbitrary substituent;and
   n = positive or negative integer.
   (i) When Xi is N, it optionally comprises Qi as substituent bonded by a single bond; (ii) When Xi is C, it optionally comprises a Qi as substituent bonded by a single bond or a double bond; (iii) X1 and X2 are bonded by a single bond or double bond; and (iv) When Qi bonds with Xi with a double bond, Qi is O, S, N or C. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) hydrogenation method of carbon dioxide, which involves reacting hydrogen and carbon dioxide in the presence of the catalyst; and
   (2) storage method of hydrogen, which involves reacting hydrogen and carbon dioxide in the presence of the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The metal complex is preferably chosen from complexes of formulae (1a)-(1c) and comprises a bidentate ligand coordinating with an amide nitrogen and nitrogen or carbon on a 6-membered ring, its isomer or salt as an active ingredient.Y1 = N, O, S or C;Y2 = N or C;A1-A4 = O, S, N or C;Y1+Y2 = 6-membered ring;R1-R7 = H, or alkyl, hydroxy, alkoxy, oxyanion, oxo, nitro, halo, sulfonyl, acyl, carboxylic acid or its derivative, amino, alkylamino, amido or phenyl (all optionally substituted);andT = H atom or optional substituent.
   Preferred Definitions:
   M = Ir;and
   L = pentamethylcyclopentadienyl ligand.

PN CN115234200-A
TI Non-conventional natural gas reservoir methane            fixed point explosion fracturing method involves using            liquid carbon dioxide to perform forced convection heat            exchange on rock in high-temperature area, so as to            achieve purpose of quickly reducing rock temperature of            reservoir.
AU FENG Y
   GAO F
   GAO Y
   YANG Y
   DI C
   ZHOU Y
   ZOU Z
   TAO Z
   WANG B
   CAI C
AE UNIV CHINA MINING & TECHNOLOGY BEIJING (UYMB-C)
GA 2022D5495G
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining perforation                position and a fracture target well section                according to a production requirement of an                unconventional natural gas well. A high-speed                oxygen jet is formed in the wellbore by using the                throttling action of the lateral nozzle and the                forward nozzle of a high-pressure spray gun. A                liquid carbon dioxide (13) is quickly pumped into a                perforation hole (2) and an impact crack (10) after                methane combustion is finished. The liquid carbon                dioxide is used to perform forced convection heat                exchange on the rock in the high-temperature area,                so as to achieve the purpose of quickly reducing                the rock temperature of the reservoir. The                secondary modification is provided within the                existing impact fracture to increase the complexity                of the original fracture and fracture volume by                continuous alternating combustant placement,                long-time combustion of methane and cold impact                fracturing with liquid carbon dioxide.
   USE - Non-conventional natural gas reservoir methane                fixed point explosion fracturing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple in steps and low in                implementation cost, and can solve the problems of                short blasting time, fast explosion pressure                attenuation, and strong impact of explosion                pressure on a wellbore in the existing blasting                fracturing technology, and significantly improves                the fracture degree and the modification volume of                the reservoir, which is beneficial to greatly                improving the yield of a single well. The method is                good in environmental protection performance, high                in development efficiency and suitable for                large-area popularization and application.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view                illustrating cracking effect of liquid carbon                dioxide cold shock.
   2Perforation hole
   10Impact crack
   13Liquid carbon dioxide
   14And thermal crack

PN CN114887613-A
TI Magnesium-based photothermal catalytic material            used for carbon dioxide hydrogenation reduction            reaction, and reducing carbon dioxide to carbon            monoxide and/or methane, which is obtained by uniformly            mixing eucryptite lithium aluminum silicate powder and            magnesium powder, and sintering.
AU JIANG B
   DONG Z
   SONG J
   LI Y
   ZHOU D
   HUANG Y
   WANG Y
   WU S
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 2022A7810U
AB    NOVELTY - Magnesium-based photothermal catalytic                material which is obtained by uniformly mixing                eucryptite lithium aluminum silicate powder and                magnesium powder, and then hot pressing and                sintering, is claimed. The content of the                eucryptite in the magnesium-based photothermal                catalytic material is 3-10 wt.%, preferably 3-7                wt.%.
   USE - The magnesium-based photothermal catalytic                material is used for carbon dioxide hydrogenation                reduction reaction, and reducing carbon dioxide to                carbon monoxide and/or methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the solid-phase reaction                method to prepare the magnesium-based                photocatalytic material, is simple to operate, the                obtained material has excellent performance and                strong selectivity, can be used for industrial                production in batches, the magnesium-based                photocatalytic material can realize carbon dioxide                reduction in a solar heat collector tube, the                composite material can be used under atmospheric                pressure, and the device for reduction reaction is                simple, economical and easy to popularize, explores                the promising application of magnesium alloys, and                provides novel idea for designing a high-efficiency                catalyst for solar-driven thermal catalysis of                carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the magnesium-based photothermal                catalytic material, comprising (S1) crushing the                eucryptite into powder, and then fully mixing with                magnesium powder to obtain a mixed powder, and (S2)                placing the mixed powder into a hot-pressing                sintering furnace, evacuating, raising the mixed                powder from room temperature to 500-600&#176;C                at a heating rate of 5-25&#176;C/minutes, at                the same time, controlling the environmental                pressure to 23-27 MPa, maintaining the heat                preservation and pressure for 50-70 minutes, and                then cooling to room temperature with the furnace                to obtain the magnesium-based alloy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (S1), the                  eucryptite is crushed to 100-300 mesh, preferably                  200 mesh and mixed with magnesium powder by ball                  milling, the ball-to-material ratio of the ball                  mill is 8-12:1, preferably 10:1, the rotational                  speed is 70-90 revolutions/minutes, preferably 80                  revolutions/minutes, and the ball-milling time is                  2-3 hours, preferably 2.5 hours. In the step                  (S2), the heating rate is 20&#176;C/minutes,                  the heating end point is 550&#176;C, the                  ambient pressure is 25 MPa, and the heat                  preservation and pressure holding time is 60                  minutes. In the step (S2), the mixed powder is                  placed into a graphite mold, and then placing in                  a hot pressing sintering furnace. The                  magnesium-based photothermal catalytic material                  reduces carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide and/or                  methane through a hydrogenation reduction                  reaction under sunlight or simulated sunlight.                  The method includes the following steps: placing                  the magnesium-based photothermal catalytic                  material in a solar heat collecting reactor,                  feeding hydrogen and carbon dioxide gas into the                  reactor as raw material gas to make the hydrogen:                  carbon dioxide gas is mixed according to the                  volume ratio of 2-6:1, sealing the reactor, using                  a simulated light source to illuminate the solar                  collector reactor, heating the magnesium-based                  photothermal catalytic material to                  280-420&#176;C, preferably hydrogen and                  carbon dioxide gas is mixed in a volume ratio of                  4:1.Preferred Components: The solar heat                  collecting reactor is a quartz reaction tube                  coated with a selective light-absorbing material                  coating, the illumination intensity of the                  simulated light source is 1-3 kW/m2, preferably                  1-2.5 kW/m2 or 1.5-2.5 kW/m2 or 2-2.5 kW/m2,                  preferably the simulated light source is a xenon                  lamp or a solar concentrator, preferably the                  magnesium-based photothermal catalytic material                  is subjected to surface deoxidation treatment and                  then placed in a solar collector reactor,                  preferably the surface deoxidation treatment                  refers to soaking the magnesium-based                  photothermal catalytic material in nitrous                  alcohol and then cleaning it with alcohol,                  preferably the selective light absorbing material                  is graded aluminum nitride-aluminum,                  tungsten-aluminum nitride, and                  molybdenum-alumina.

A combustion unit (30) burns the by-product gas after separating nitrogen. An oxygen production unit (40) produces the oxygen supplied to the combustion unit. A gas turbine (50) obtains motive power by expanding the combustion gas from the combustion unit. An exhaust heat recovery boiler (60) collects the heat of the waste gas from the gas turbine, and generates vapor with the heat. A steam turbine (70) obtains motive power by expanding the vapor generated in the exhaust heat recovery boiler. A carbon-dioxide collection unit (80) collects the carbon dioxides contained in the waste gas discharged from the exhaust heat recovery boiler.
   USE - Gas turbine hybrid power system for use in iron industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The electric power is generated efficiently using by product gas, while low motive power collects the carbon dioxide from combustion exhaust gas of by product gas by-produced in the iron mill efficiently. The motive power for compressing oxygen at an oxygen production process is reduced. The combustion efficiency of the by-product gas in a combustion process by using highly purified oxygen is improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for gas turbine combined cycle power generation method.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The molar ratio between (II) and (IV) is 1:1-5, and the concentration of (IV) in the mixed solution is 0.1-0.5 mol/l. The alkali is sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, cesium carbonate, lithium carbonate, potassium phosphate, triethylamine, 1,4-diazabicyclo(2.2.2)octane or 1,8-diazabicyclo( 5.4.0)undec-7-ene. The molar ratio between alkali and (II) is 0-4:1. The eluent used for column chromatography is a mixed solvent of petroleum ether and ethyl acetate in a volume ratio of 5-30:1. Preferred Method: The pressure of introducing carbon dioxide into the reaction container is 0.1-4 MPa. The heating and reacting temperature is 60-100 degrees C, and reacting time is 1-10 hours. The separating and purifying method comprises cooling the reaction liquid to room temperature, washing using water, extracting using ethyl acetate, drying over anhydrous sodium sulfate, filtering, concentrating under reduced pressure to obtain crude product, and purified the crude product by column chromatography using eluent to obtain (I).

   JIN X
   LI Q
   WANG X
   LIN P
AE GREE ELECTRIC APPLIANCES INC (GREZ-C)
GA 2022B78832
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining indoor first air                component data. The indoor first carbon dioxide                concentration and the first oxygen concentration                are determined. A first ratio between the indoor                first CO2 concentration is determined. The first                ratio is greater than a pre-stored first threshold                value. A prompt message is sent under the condition                that the first ratio value is larger than a preset                threshold value, where the first prompt message                prompts a user to open a window.

PN WO2021155357-A1
TI Preparing quaternary ammonium-functionalized poly(arylene ether sulfone) copolymer for moisture-swing carbon dioxide capture, by reacting diallyl bisphenol A with bisphenol A and 4,4-difluorodiphenyl sulfone, modifying copolymer and converting tertiary amine of copolymer to quaternary ammonium.
AU GREEN M
   YANG Y
   SHOKROLLAHZADEH BEHBAHANI H
AE ARIZONA BOARD OF REGENTS (UYAZ-C)
GA 2021903115
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing quaternary ammonium-functionalized poly(arylene ether sulfone) copolymer for moisture-swing carbon dioxide capture involves (i) reacting diallyl bisphenol A (DABA) with bisphenol A (BPA) and 4,4-difluorodiphenyl sulfone (DFDPS) to form an allyl-modified poly(arylene ether sulfone) (PAES-co- APAES) copolymer, (ii) modifying the PAES-co-APAES copolymer to convert the allyl functionalities to tertiary amines, forming tertiary amine-modified PAES (PAES-co-TAPAES) copolymer and (iii) converting the tertiary amine of the PAES-co-TAPAES copolymer to quaternary ammonium and forming quaternary ammonium-modified PAES.
   USE - The quaternary ammonium-functionalized poly(arylene ether sulfone) copolymer is used for moisture-swing carbon dioxide capture from sources such as fossil fuel plants, cement plants and oil refineries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables sorption and release of carbon dioxide via the moisture swing phenomenon, has excellent mechanical properties and high selectivity, is simple and economical, and does not need high thermal energy input.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the quaternary ammonium-functionalized poly(arylene ether sulfone) copolymer for moisture swing carbon dioxide capture, which comprises a polysulfone copolymer having a copolymerization unit based on diallyl bisphenal A (DABA) and comprising quaternary ammonium functionalities.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method further involves exchanging halide counterions of the quaternary ammonium-modified PAES with one of hydroxide, carbonate, chloride, bromide, fluoride, and bicarbonate ions, where converting the tertiary amine of the PAES-co-TAPAES copolymer to quaternary ammonium comprises the addition of an alkyl halide to the PAES-CO-TAPAES copolymer. The method further involves processing the quaternary ammonium-modified PAES into one of a membrane, a film, and a hollow fiber. The method involves performing the process of the quaternary ammonium-modified PAES before any counterion exchange. The method involves modifying the PAES-co-APAES copolymer through a thiol-ene click reaction. Preferred Components: The alkyl halide is methyl iodide. The quaternary ammonium-modified PAES is processed into the hollow fiber, where the hollow fiber having a wall thickness between 50-500 pm. The copolymer further comprises another copolymerization unit based on bisphenol A (BPA). The copolymer further comprises counter ions such as hydroxide, carbonate, chloride, bromide, fluoride and/or bicarbonate. Preferred Composition: The ratio of copolymerization units based on BPA to copolymerization units based on DABA is at most 1.

   (2) use of catalyst in three-component coupling reaction of methanol, carbon dioxide and 2-cyanopyridine, where the reaction conditions are: mixing and dissolving methanol and 2 cyanopyridine in a molar ratio of 1:5-5:1 and then feeding in liquid form, feeding the carbon dioxide in gaseous form, the liquid material is 10-50 vol.% of the reactor and the reaction temperature is 80-160 degrees C and charging carbon dioxide to 0.1-6 MPa.

TI Catalyst composite used to convert carbon dioxide            to carbon monoxide, comprises perovskite-oxide, and            oxide support having formula different from the            perovskite-oxide.
AU KUHN J
   HARE B

   DAZA Y
   BHETHANABOTLA V
AE UNIV SOUTH FLORIDA (UYSF-C)

GA 201896901L
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst composite comprises: a                perovskite-oxide (I); and an oxide support having a                formula different from the perovskite-oxide.
   USE - The catalyst composite is useful for                converting carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composite catalyst: exhibits high rates of                conversion of CO2 to CO; increases long-term                stability for repeated catalytic                oxidation-reduction cycles; is stable; and allows                repetitive cycles of the of conversion of CO2 to                CO, thus generating large quantities of CO, which                can be subsequently converted to hydrocarbons via                Fischer-Tropsch synthesis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalyst composite comprises: a                perovskite-oxide of formula (A1B1O3) (I); and an                oxide support having a formula different from the                perovskite-oxide.
   A1 = alkaline earth element, rare-earth                element, alkali metal element and/or metal element;                and
   B1 = transitional metal element and/or metal                element.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing (M1) the catalyst composite,                comprising (a) mixing (I) with the oxide support                having a formula different from the                perovskite-oxide to form a mixture, and (b) heating                the mixture to a temperature of 600-1300 degrees C;                and
   (2) converting carbon dioxide to carbon                monoxide comprising (a1) contacting hydrogen with                the catalyst composite, where the perovskite-oxide                is reduced, and hydrogen is oxidized to produce                water, and (b1) contacting carbon dioxide with the                catalyst composite, where the reduced                perovskite-oxide is oxidized, and the carbon                dioxide is reduced to produce carbon                monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxide support                  increases the perovskite-oxide surface area and                  the perovskite-oxide surface area is 1-1000 m2/g.                  The perovskite-oxide crystallite size is 5-100                  nm. The perovskite-oxide is                  lanthanoid0.75strantium0.25ferrous oxide. The                  oxide support is cesium oxide, zirconium oxide,                  alumina, silica and/or titanium oxide. The weight                  ratio of the perovskite-oxide to the oxide                  support is 10:90-90:10. The oxide support                  comprises M2, where M2 is an element other than                  O, and the perovskite-oxide and the oxide support                  form a secondary phase having formula of                  (M1pM2qOv) (II).M1 = the A1 or B1 component of (I);p, q = 1-3; andv = 2-7.Preferred Method: The step (a1) and step                  (b1) is carried out isothermally at a temperature                  of 450-800 degrees C. The temperature of step                  (a1) is 400-850 degrees C, or the temperature of                  step (b1) is 450-900 degrees C. The carbon                  monoxide is produced at a rate of 0.5 to 1 mmol                  carbon monoxide/g perovskite-oxide/minutes. The                  method (M2, further comprises repeating step (a1)                  and step (b1) in succession.
   Preferred Definitions:
   A1 = A1axA1by;
   A1a, A1b = Pb, Ca, Mg, Be, Sr, Ba, La, K or                Na;
   x = 0-1;
   y = 0-1; and
   x+y = 1; and
   B1 = Fe, Ti, Y, Mn, Al, Cr, Co, Ta, Ni and/or                Ce.

In step (i), the concentration of hydrochloric acid solution is 1.5-6 mol/l. In step (ii), the mass ratio between active powder obtained in (i), ethanol, hexadecyltrimethylammonium bromide and water is 0.6-1.8:0.8-3.2:1.2-3.5:15-50. In step (ii), the pH is 10.5-11.5. Preferred Method: The temperature is raised to a pressure of 0.5-5 MPa and the temperature is 60-150 degrees C. The method further comprises filtering the solution treated by the step (iii), washing the residue and drying. The dried treated filter residue is again used for catalytic ring-opening addition reaction of ethylene oxide or its derivatives and carbon dioxide.

AU MIYAMOTO K
   UCHIYAMA M
   OKADA M
   IMAMURA S
AE NIPPON LIGHT METAL CO (NIMI-C)
GA 202276410L
AB    NOVELTY - Production of 2-iodoxybenzoic acid (I) involves reacting 2-iodobenzoic acid (II) with hypochlorite or an aqueous solution of hypochlorite in the presence of 4.0% or more carbon dioxide.
   USE - Production of 2-iodoxybenzoic acid used for producing Dess-Martin periodinane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is safe, environmentally-friendly, easy, simple, and enables production of 2-iodoxybenzoic acid with high yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of 2-iodoxybenzoic acid of formula (I) involves reacting 2-iodobenzoic acid of formula (II) with hypochlorite or an aqueous solution of hypochlorite in the presence of 4.0% or more carbon dioxide. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of Dess-Martin periodinane of formula (III), which involves reacting the iodobenzoic acid (II) with hypochlorite or the aqueous solution of hypochlorite in the presence of carbon dioxide to prepare the compound (I), and adding acetic anhydride, heating and reacting the resultant product with the acetic anhydride.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hypochlorite or the aqueous solution of hypochlorite is sodium hypochlorite hydrate or the aqueous solution of sodium hypochlorite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The production of Dess-Martin periodinane is a one-pot reaction.
   EXAMPLE - 724 mg Sodium hypochlorite pentahydrate, 1.3 mL water and 496 mg 2-iodobenzoic acid were added in a glass reaction vessel, and filled with carbon dioxide. A balloon was attached and the mixture was reacted at room temperature for 12 hours. 1.0 M Hydrochloric acid was added to the obtained reaction mixture to adjust the final pH to 1-2, and the resulting white precipitate was collected by filtration through a glass filter. It was washed for 4 times with 5 mL water, and then washed 3 times with 5 mL acetone. The white solid was dried under reduced pressure for 2 hours to obtain 493 mg 2-iodoxybenzoic acid (IBX) crystals with yield of 88%.
