TI Carbonic anhydrase-metal-organic framework            material useful as catalyst for aqueous phase            electrocatalytic capture of carbon dioxide.
AU GONG D
   LV Y
   TU X
   PI H
   GUO J
   GUO S
   REN L
AE UNIV CHINA THREE GORGES (UYCR-C)
GA 202220159U
AB    NOVELTY - Carbonic anhydrase-metal-organic framework                material, having structural formula                Zn(ZnO2COH)4(BBP)3, where BBP is                2,6-bis(2-benzimidazolyl)pyridine.
   USE - The carbonic anhydrase-metal-organic framework                material is useful as catalyst for aqueous phase                electrocatalytic capture of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can continuously catalyze carbon                dioxide hydration reaction similar to carbonic                anhydrase catalysis to generate HCO3- and improves                the unit time collecting amount of carbon dioxide                by 150 % at the highest.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing the carbonic anhydrase-metal-organic                framework material comprising (i) electroplating                the surface of the electrode to generate a layer of                zinc oxide and (ii) reacting the electrode plated                with resultant zinc oxide with a mixed solution of                an acetic acid aqueous solution and a methanol                solution of 2,6-bis (2-benzimidazolyl) pyridine and                then reacting with sodium bicarbonate solution and                soaking with anhydrous methanol and vacuum drying                to remove water and in-situ generating MOF material                imitating carbonic anhydrase on the electrode                surface.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In the step (i) the                  electrode comprises a standard electrode formed                  by wrapping gold, inert material which is not                  easy to undergo redox reaction of glassy carbon,                  and polytetrafluoroethylene, where the steps of                  generating zinc oxide on the surface of the                  electrode by electroplating comprises placing a                  zinc sheet as an anode and an electrode as a                  cathode into a single electrolytic cell and (ii)                  filling electroplating solution consisting of                  zinc sulfate, boric acid and polyethylene glycol                  into the electrolytic cell, introducing oxygen                  into one side of the electroplating solution                  close to the cathode at the speed of 5-8                  ml/minutes, applying constant current between the                  anode and cathode for electrifying                  electroplating, washing the surface of the                  electrode electroplated with zinc oxide by using                  deionized water and drying by using nitrogen gas                  to realize the generation of zinc oxide by                  electroplating the surface of the electrode,                  where the constant current is 0.001-0.005 A and                  the electricity is electrified for 100-200 s. In                  the step (ii), the mixed solution of the aqueous                  acetic acid solution and methanol solution of                  2,6-bis (2-benzimidazolyl) pyridine the                  concentration of the aqueous acetic acid solution                  is 75-90 g/l and the concentration of the                  methanol solution is 2,6-bis (2-benzimidazolyl)                  pyridine is 3.5-5.5 g/l. The volume ratio of the                  acetic acid aqueous solution to the methanol                  solution of the 2, 6-bis (2-benzimidazolyl)                  pyridine is 1:10-18. The concentration of the                  sodium bicarbonate aqueous solution is 6-10 g/l.                  In the preparation method a mixed solution of an                  acetic acid aqueous solution and a methanol                  solution of 2, 6-bis (2-benzimidazolyl) pyridine                  is placed in a bottle and is turned over on the                  electrode with the zinc oxide plated on the                  surface, which is prepared in the step (i) and                  after the reaction is carried out for 20-30                  minutes at normal temperature, the surface of the                  electrode is washed clean by deionized water and                  dried by nitrogen, then sodium bicarbonate is                  added and placing the aqueous solution on the                  dried electrode which is turned upside down again                  in the bottle, reacting for 25-35 minutes at                  normal temperature, washing the surface of the                  electrode with deionized water and drying with                  nitrogen, the anhydrous methanol was filled into                  a new bottle and inverted to sodium bicarbonate                  soaking the electrode for 0.8-1 hours at normal                  temperature after reaction, washing the surface                  of the electrode with anhydrous methanol and                  vacuum drying to obtain Zn(ZnO2COH)4(BBP)3. The                  method further comprises covering the surface of                  the Zn(ZnO2COH)4(BBP)3 electrode with a                  polyacrylic resin film. The Paraloid B48N                  polyacrylic resin was dissolved in acetone to                  prepare a polyacrylic resin-acetone solution with                  a concentration of 8-15 g/l, which was                  drop-coated on the surface of the prepared                  Zn(ZnO2COH)4(BBP)3 electrode. The surface of the                  Zn(ZnO2COH)4(BBP)3 electrode can be prepared by                  vacuum drying and covered with a polyacrylic                  resin film. In the electrocatalytic process,                  Zn(ZnO2COH)4(BBP)3 or coated Zn(ZnO2COH)4(BBP)3                  is used as an anode and placed in an anode                  chamber and a mixed gas is introduced into the                  anode chamber and apply a constant working                  voltage for electrolysis, where the ventilation                  flow of the mixed gas is 5-8 ml/min. The constant                  working voltage is 0.05-0.1 V.

TI New mononuclear transition metal complex for            photocatalyst used for reducing carbon dioxide to            formic acid.
AU KIM J
   KIM J H
   SEOK S
AE UNIV EWHA IND COLLABORATION FOUND (EWHA-C)
   KYTECBIO CO LTD (KYTE-Non-standard)
   KYTECBIO CO LTD (KYTE-Non-standard)
   KEYTECHBIO CO LTD (KEYT-Non-standard)
GA 2021C1023X
AB    NOVELTY - A mononuclear transition metal complex (I), is                new.
   USE - Mononuclear transition metal complex for                photocatalyst used for reducing carbon dioxide to                formic acid (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mononuclear transition metal complex                provides photocatalyst having excellent catalytic                efficiency and selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A mononuclear transition metal complex of                formula: L1-M-(L2)2 (I), is new.
   M = mononuclear transition metal of nickel,                iron, manganese, or cobalt;
   L1 = group chosen from groups of formulae                (i)-(iv);
   L2 = group of formula (iv);
   X = N or P;
   Y' = -CH, -N-, -NH, -S- or -O-;
   Z = -O-, -S- or -NH;and
   W' = -P- or -N-.
   Aryl and heteroaryl of L1 and L2 are                optionally substituted by linear or branched 1-6C                alkyl, 3-6C cycloalkyl, 2-6C heterocycloalkyl,                linear or branched 1-6C alkoxy, halo, amino, or                linear or branched 1-6C alkylamino. INDEPENDENT                CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) photocatalyst for carbon dioxide                reduction, which comprises mononuclear transition                metal complex; and
   (2) method for reducing carbon dioxide to                formic acid, which involves using the                photocatalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The transition metal of                  the mononuclear transition metal complex is                  6-coordinated in a distorted octahedral                  structure.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The photocatalyst                  further contains a promoter chosen from eosin Y,                  ruthenium compound (Ru(bpy)3), carbon nitride                  (C3N4), cadmium sulfide, cadmium selenide and                  triethanolamine. Preferred Properties: The                  photocatalyst is produced at rate of 2000-5000                  ton/hour, and has selectivity of 90% or more. The                  conversion of carbon dioxide to formic acid is                  carried out at pH of 8-14 and room temperature in                  a solvent chosen from a mixed solvent containing                  water and alcohol. PT P
PN US2020239913-A1; KR2020092641-A; KR2174090-B1; US11193100-B2
TI Carbon resource utilization by supplying carbon dioxide to medium to form bicarbonate ions, inoculating microalgal species into medium followed by photo-culture, and supplying calcium ions to medium to produce calcite-containing biomass.
AU SIM S J
   YU B S
   HONG M E
   SEON Y B
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
   UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 202071188Q
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon resource utilization involves: (a) supplying carbon dioxide to a medium to form bicarbonate ions (HCO3-), (b) inoculating one or more microalgal species into the medium, followed by photo-culture, and (c) supplying calcium ions (Ca2+) to the medium, where the microalgal species are photo-cultured, to produce calcite (CaCO3)-containing biomass.
   USE - The method is useful for carbon resource utilization (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The use of microalgae enables the production of biomass while reducing a large amount of carbon dioxide, and at the same time, hard calcite is produced through calcium ion-mediated mineralization in an induction stage for high lipid accumulation so that the reduction rate of carbon dioxide can be markedly increased and concomitantly the content of lipids in biomass can be significantly increased. The separated biomass can be easily collected without the need for additional energy consumption.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method further involves supplying a basic solution to the medium to maintain the pH of the medium at 7.0-8.5 between steps (a) and (b). It also involves primarily supplying a basic solution to maintain the pH of the medium at 7.5-8.5 such that biomineralization is induced, after supply of the calcium ions in step (c). The method further involves stopping the supply of the carbon dioxide to flocculate and collect the biomass after step (c). The calcium ions are supplied by adding an aqueous calcium chloride (CaCl2) solution to the medium. The method also involves secondarily supplying another basic solution to induce both biomineralization and chemical mineralization after the primary supply of the basic solution. Step (c) is carried out when the microalgal species are photo-cultured to a density of greater than or equal to 1.0 g/L. Preferred Components The calcium ions are bound to the cell walls of the microalgal cells and react with the bicarbonate ions to produce calcite, and the microalgal cells adhere to one another through nonpolar covalent bonds between the calcite particles to produce biomass. The microalgal species are selected from Chlorella vulgaris, Chlorella sorokiniana, Spirurlina sp., Dunaliella sp., Haematococcus pluvialis, Schizochytrium sp., Crypthecodinium sp., Chlamydomonas sp., Neochloris sp., Aphanizomenon sp., and Cyanobacterium sp..
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The basic solution is 8-10 mM aqueous potassium hydroxide (KOH) solution. The aqueous calcium chloride solution has a concentration of 0.03-0.06 M. The primarily supplied basic solution is an aqueous KOH solution having a concentration of not greater than 20 mM. The secondarily supplied basic solution is an aqueous KOH solution having a concentration of not greater than 40 mM. Preferred Components: The basic solution is a solution of at least one base selected from sodium hydroxide (NaOH), KOH, ammonia (NH4OH), calcium hydroxide (Ca(OH)2), and magnesium hydroxide (Mg(OH)2). The calcite is in the form of nanoparticles.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

PT P
PN CN110563007-A; CN110563007-B
TI Conversion of sodium sulfate into sodium            bicarbonate using calcium oxide and carbon dioxide,            involves mixing sulfate, calcium oxide, and water, ball            milling mixture, and separating sodium bicarbonate            solution from mixed slurry.
AU YAN N
   QU Z
   LIU W
   WANG J
   LI X
   NING P
AE UNIV SHANGHAI JIAOTONG (USJT-C)
GA 2019A6677S
AB    NOVELTY - Conversion of sodium sulfate into sodium                bicarbonate involves (A) mixing and uniformly                spraying the sodium sulfate, calcium oxide, and                water to obtain granules or powdery mixture, (B)                adding obtained mixture to ball mill with a sealing                function for sufficient grinding, and at the same                time, passing carbon dioxide gas into the ball                mill, (C) removing powdery mixture from the ball                mill into the reactor, adding water to fully mixed                to obtain a slurry, passing carbon dioxide gas into                the reactor, performing high-speed stirring to                obtained in the liquid phase, and (D) reducing the                stirring speed, adding mixed slurry, separating                mixed slurry by solid-liquid separation device to                obtain clear liquid is sodium hydrogen carbonate                solution, and the solids are calcium sulfate, and                calcium carbonate.
   USE - Conversion of sodium sulfate into sodium                bicarbonate using calcium oxide and carbon                dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables easy and economical                conversion of sodium sulfate into sodium                bicarbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of                  sodium sulfate to calcium oxide is 1-1.5:1, and                  addition of water in mixed material is 4-8%.                  Preferred Components: The calcium oxide is one or                  more mixture of calcium oxide (CaO) or calcium                  hydroxide, or calcium carbide slag rich in                  calcium hydroxide, and sodium sulfate is                  anhydrous sodium sulfate, sodium                  sulfate-containing crystal water, or aqueous                  solution of sodium sulfate. Preferred Process:                  The method further involves introducing carbon                  dioxide at 0.2-0.5 MPa during the ball milling                  process, using carbon dioxide as industrial grade                  product or industrial tail gas with volume                  concentration of more than 50%, diluting the                  water into the mixed material with solid content                  of 10-30% of slurry, and recovering solid by                  crystallizing the sodium hydrogen carbonate                  solution, or adding solution directly returned to                  the desulfurization system as supplement of                  sodium base, and using calcium sulfate solid as                  mineral smelting auxiliary material for                  recycling, and small amount of calcium carbonate                  does not affect the use quality. The grinding                  step is performed in the ball mill for 0.5-3                  hours, and the average grinding particle size of                  0.5-5 microns. The reactor is performed at                  high-speed stirring of 100-200 rpm for 0.5-2                  hours.

PT P
PN CN110448661-A; CN110448661-B
TI Preparing Curcuma zedoaria oil mixed micelles by dispersing C.zedoaria oil and surfactant, adding lecithin, mixing mixture by magnetic stirring, introducing carbon dioxide, carrying out pressurized reaction.
AU ZHAO L
   WU H
   ZHANG Y
   WANG Y
AE HEFEI FUTURE DRUG DEV CO LTD (HEFE-Non-standard)
GA 201999248S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing Curcuma zedoaria oil mixed micelles involves dispersing C.zedoaria oil and surfactant in deionized water, adding lecithin, mixing uniformly, placing in a supercritical reaction kettle, adding a stirring magnet particle to a supercritical reaction kettle, turning on the magnetic stirring device, introducing a carbon dioxide from a steel cylinder using high pressure pump to pressurize with carbon dioxide into the kettle, controlling the reaction temperature and pressure to carry out a pressurized reaction, releasing carbon dioxide gas until the pressure in the supercritical reaction kettle drops to normal pressure to obtain the product (per 100 ml) comprises 0.05-1 mg turmeric oil, 0.5-4 mg surfactant and 0.1-0.4 mg lecithin.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing C.zedoaria oil mixed micelles.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple in operation, adopts short preparation time, does not affect biological activity of C.zedoaria oil during process, and adopts large-scale production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surfactant comprises at least one of Pluronic P-123 (RTM: Symmetric triblock copolymer comprising polyethylene oxide and polypropylene oxide), polyethylene oxide-polypropylene oxide-polyethylene oxide triblock copolymer, caprylocaproyl polyoxylglycerides, polyvinyl caprolactam-polyvinyl acetate-polyethylene glycol graft copolymer, distearoylphosphatidyl ethanolamine-polyethylene glycol 2000, polyethoxylated castor oil-35, vitamin E polyethylene glycol succinate and polyethylene glycol 15-hydroxystearate. Preferred Method: The step of pressurized reaction is carried out at 35-55 degrees C for 15-60 minutes with a pressure of 9-25 MPa. The step of magnetic stirring is carried out at 200-800 rpm. The method involves releasing the carbon dioxide gas at a pressure relief rate of 1-4 MPa/minute. Preferred Properties: The C.zedoaria oil mixed micelles has a diameter of 100-300 nm and a dissolution resistance of 25-200 times.

GA 2019751179

PT P
PN CN109908906-A; CN109908906-B
TI Methane carbon dioxide reforming synthesis gas catalyst comprises active component (comprised of nickel or cobalt)and complex carrier (comprised of alkaline earth metal aluminate and petroleum coke-based activated carbon).
AU SUN X
   ZHANG S
   ZHANG Q
   SONG Y
   LIU J
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2019577363
AB    NOVELTY - Methane carbon dioxide reforming synthesis gas catalyst comprises 5-30 wt% active component and 10-20 wt% complex carrier, the active component is nickel or cobalt, preferably nickel, the carrier is a complex material of 5-20 wt%, preferably 10-15 wt% alkaline earth metal aluminate and 40-85 wt%, preferably 60-75 wt% petroleum coke-based activated carbon.
   USE - Used as methane carbon dioxide reforming synthesis gas catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst has good dispersion of active metals, high reaction activity, anti-sintering, non-carbon deposition. The method is simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the methane carbon dioxide reforming synthesis gas catalyst, comprising (i) mixing petroleum coke, alkaline earth metal aluminate and activator, mixing and activating; (ii) washing and drying the sample; and (iii) introducing the active component into the solid sample, drying and calcining.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline earth metal aluminate is calcium aluminate and magnesium aluminate. The specific surface of the catalyst is from 200-1000 m2/g, preferably from 300-600 m2/g. In the catalyst pore size distribution, less than 2 nm of micropores is greater than 60%, preferably greater than 80%. In the step (i), the alkaline earth metal aluminate is calcium aluminate and/or magnesium aluminate. In the step (i), the activator is potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydrogen carbonate and/or sodium hydrogen carbonate, preferably potassium hydroxide. In the step (i), the mass ratio of the petroleum coke, the alkaline earth metal aluminate, and the activator is 1:0.025-0.15:1-5, preferably 1:0.05-0.1:2-4. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the activation process is as follows: (a) grinding petroleum coke and alkaline earth metal aluminate to powder form, respectively, and uniformly mixing with the activator, and (b) heating to the activation temperature, where the temperature is lowered to room temperature for subsequent treatment. The activation temperature is 600-1000 degrees C, preferably 700-900 degrees C, and the activation time is 5-240 minutes, preferably 10-120 minutes. The activation process is carried out under microwave irradiation conditions of 2450 MHz or 915 MHz, the microwave power is 1-10 kw, preferably 2-4 kw per kg of petroleum coke. In the step (b), activating in two stages, when activated under microwave irradiation conditions, and activating the first stage under vacuum conditions at 400-600 degrees C for 10-60 minutes under constant temperature conditions, introducing an inert gas or nitrogen gas to normal pressure, and increasing the temperature to 700-900 degrees C under microwave irradiation for 10-30 minutes. In the step (ii), the washing includes two processes of pickling and water washing, mixing firstly the sample with an acid solution, preferably, grinding the sample into a powder and mixing with an acid solution after mixing uniformly, carrying out solid-liquid separation, washing the obtained solid with water until the pH of the filtrate is neutral. In the step (i), the acid solution is a formic acid solution or an acetic acid solution, further preferably acetic acid solution, acid solution concentration is 10-50 wt%, preferably 20-40 wt%, and the mass ratio of the sample obtained to the acid solution is 1:5-1:30, preferably 1:10-1:20. In the step (ii), the drying temperature is 80-200?OC, preferably the drying temperature is 120-180?OC, the drying time is 2-10 hours, and the drying time is preferably 4-8 hours. In the step (ii) and (iii), introducing the active component to the solid by impregnating with a soluble salt solution containing the active component cation, where the soluble salt containing the active component cation is hydrochloride, nitrate, sulfate, preferably nitrate. In the step (iii), the drying temperature is 60-160 degrees C, preferably the drying temperature is 80-120 degrees C, the drying time is 2-10 hours, and the drying time is preferably 4-8 hours. In the step (iii), the calcination is carried out under an inert atmosphere or a nitrogen atmosphere at a calcination temperature is 300-700 degrees C, preferably a calcination temperature is 400-600 degrees C, a calcination time is 2-10 hours, and preferably a calcination time is 4-8 hours.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Defect-regulating graphite phase carbonitride carbon useful photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprises ultra-thin nanosheet structure doped with potassium, and there are nitrogen vacancies and oxygen substitution.
AU CAO S
   GUAN H
   YU J
AE UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
   UNIV WUHAN TECHNOLOGY (UYWL-C)
GA 201942388S
AB    NOVELTY - Defect-regulating graphite phase carbonitride carbon comprises an ultra-thin nanosheet structure doped with potassium, and there are nitrogen (N) vacancies and oxygen (O) substitutions.
   USE - The defect-regulating graphite phase carbonitride carbon is useful photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The graphite phase carbonitride carbon: has high activity; and is environmentally-friendly and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the defect-regulated graphite phase carbon nitride, comprising (a) mixing urea and potassium persulfate, grinding, and collecting the pre-preparation and processing the sample, (b) calcining the pretreated sample in step (a) to obtain graphite phase carbon nitride, collecting the calcined sample into the mortar for full grinding, and(c) washing the graphite phase carbonitride in water and drying; and
   (2) processing photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide, comprising carrying out the photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction by using the defect-controlled graphite phase carbonitride carbon.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The defect-regulated graphite phase carbon nitride has a size of micron and an average thickness of 1.5-2 nm. The defect-controlled graphite phase carbonitride has a potassium doping amount of 8.5-10 wt.%. The band gap width of the defect and graphite phase carbon nitride is 2.2-2.5 eV. In the step (a), the potassium persulfate is 5-10 wt.% urea. Preferred Method: In the step (b), the calcination temperature is 545-555 degrees C. In the step (b), the calcination temperature is at 550 degrees C at a heating rate of 5 degrees C/min under an air atmosphere for 2 hours. he step (c) comprises dispersing the graphite phase carbon nitride obtained in the step (b) in deionized water at 80 degrees C, heating for 12 hours, removing the supernatant allowing to stand, and then placing the sample in an oven at 80 degrees C overnight and drying.

PN CN109621891-A; CN109621891-B
TI Preparing lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises e.g. selecting wollastonite as a raw material, crushing silicon hydroxyapatite, adding wollastonite particles into lithium hydroxide monohydrate solution, stirring and calcining.
AU YANG Y
   LIU W
   XU M
   TONG X
   LI Q
   ZHANG S
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2019380086
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent comprises (i) selecting wollastonite as a raw material and crushing and grinding the silicon hydroxyapatite to obtain wollastonite particles; (ii) adding the wollastonite particles into lithium hydroxide monohydrate solution and stirring until the water is evaporated to dryness, to obtain solid substance containing a silicon calcium compound and lithium hydroxide; and (iii) calcining the solid matter, reacting the silicon calcium compound with lithium hydroxide.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and the adsorbent has better adsorption performance and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for adsorbent comprising the total mass fraction of calcium oxide and lithium orthosilicate in the lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent is 75-95%,the mass fraction of the impurity phase is 5-25% and the mass ratio of the calcium oxide and lithium orthosilicate is 1:1-3.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i),the particle diameter of the wollastonite particles 50-200 mu m. In the step (ii) the ratio of silicon and lithium in the lithium hydroxide monohydrate solution is 1:4-4.5. The impurity phase is lithium calcium silicate and an oxide, the oxide is phosphorus pentoxide, magnesium oxide, aluminum oxide or ferric oxide. The particle diameter of lithium-based carbon dioxide adsorbent is less than 300 mu m. Preferred Method: In the step (ii) the stirring temperature is 85-95 degrees C. In the step (iii), the calcination temperature is 700-1100 degrees C and the calcination time 2-5 hours.

PN CN107783417-A
TI Burning process controlling method, involves determining air flow at inlet of cracking furnace pipe based on concentration difference value so as to control burning process with better tracking effect of desired burning path.
AU SUN K
   LI Q
   WU J
   LV Y
   LI H
   WU X
AE SINOPEC CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC BEIJING RES INST CHEM IND (SNPC-C)
GA 201820834A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves receiving a carbon dioxide concentration value at an outlet of a cracking furnace pipe during burning process. The carbon dioxide concentration value is compared with expected carbon dioxide concentration curve. A concentration difference value is calculated. Air flow is determined at an inlet of the cracking furnace pipe based on the concentration difference value so as to control the burning process with better tracking effect of a desired burning path. The carbon dioxide concentration value of the outlet of the cracking furnace pipe is detected in real time.
   USE - Burning process controlling method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables increasing producing efficiency, reducing burning time and realizing burning process optimizing operation.

PN CN113154409-A; CN113154409-B
TI Method of extracting helium from boil-off gas and energy utilization for liquefied natural gas plant, involves passing oxygen and boil-off gas into pure oxygen combustion chamber and steam generating unit, processing and removing impurities.
AU ZHANG D
   XU P
   ZHAO G
AE BEIJING FULLCRYO CRYOGENIC TECHNOLOGY CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202191367G
AB    NOVELTY - Method of extracting helium from boil-off gas and energy utilization involves passing oxygen and boil-off gas into pure oxygen combustion chamber for pure oxygen combustion, removing methane and hydrogen in boil-off gas, passing into steam generating unit, producing low-pressure superheated steam, cooling to form a low-temperature mixed gas, passing into an air cooler for condensation adsorption and drying and dehydration to release all water, passing the mixed gas after removing water into the carbon dioxide removal and liquefaction unit for carbon dioxide removal treatment, purifying and liquefying the carbon dioxide removed, obtaining high-purity liquid carbon dioxide, passing the mixed gas after removing carbon dioxide into cold box (A), performing stepped cooling treatment to remove oxygen and nitrogen, obtaining crude helium, passing the crude helium into the cold box (B), removing impurities by cooling and purifying, and obtaining pure helium.
   USE - Method of extracting helium from boil-off gas and energy utilization for liquefied natural gas plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method generates heat energy by pure oxygen combustion boil-off gas, which can meet the production heat of liquefied natural gas plant and the energy consumption demand of this system for helium extraction and the electricity consumption demand of liquefied natural gas plant. The system is simple in structure and consumes less energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of extracting helium from boil-off gas and energy utilization involves (1) passing oxygen and boil-off gas into pure oxygen combustion chamber for pure oxygen combustion, removing methane and hydrogen in boil-off gas, (2) passing the mixed gas after pure oxygen combustion into the steam generating unit for processing, producing low-pressure superheated steam, cooling the low-pressure superheated steam to form a low-temperature mixed gas and removing portion of water, passing the low-temperature mixed gas into an air cooler for condensation adsorption and drying and dehydration to release all water, (3) passing the mixed gas after removing water into the carbon dioxide removal and liquefaction unit for carbon dioxide removal treatment, purifying and liquefying the carbon dioxide removed, obtaining high-purity liquid carbon dioxide, (4) passing the mixed gas after removing carbon dioxide into cold box (B), performing stepped cooling treatment to remove oxygen and nitrogen, obtaining crude helium, (5) passing the crude helium into cold box (B), removing impurities by cooling and purifying, and obtaining pure helium. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system for extracting helium from boil-off gas and energy utilization, which comprises pure oxygen combustion chamber, heat exchanger, a steam generator, production unit, air cooler, carbon dioxide removal and liquefaction unit, cold box (A), and cold box (B).

PT P
PN CN113005155-A; GB2590586-A
TI Adjusting pH of decarboxylation process using carbonic anhydrase comprises adding carbonic anhydrase to capture carbon dioxide released during decarboxylation reaction catalyzed by decarboxylase.
AU CHEN K
   XU S
   WANG X
   OUYANG P
   LIU F
   CHEN C
   TAN W
   MA R
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
   UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 202175960H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for adjusting the pH of a decarboxylation process in situ using carbonic anhydrase comprises adding carbonic anhydrase to capture the carbon dioxide released during decarboxylation reaction catalyzed by decarboxylase.
   USE - The method is used for adjusting the pH of the decarboxylation process in situ using carbonic anhydrase.
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes the dissolution of carbon dioxide, alleviates the dramatic changes in pH during the reaction, effectively stabilizes the pH of the decarboxylase catalytic process and increases the catalytic activity of L-lysine decarboxylase by 2 times.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The decarboxylase comprises L-lysine decarboxylase (preferred), ornithine acid decarboxylase, arginine acid decarboxylase, glutamic acid decarboxylase and/or histidine decarboxylase. Preferred Method: The preparation of carbonic anhydrase comprises (i) cutting and connecting a nucleotide sequence comprising a 669 base pair sequence (SEQ ID No.1), fully defined in the specification and a carrier pETdute-1 enzyme to obtain a recombinant plasmid pETdute-SazCA, (ii) introducing the recombinant plasmid pETdute-SazCA into Escherichia coli to obtain a recombinant bacteria, (iii) culturing the recombinant bacteria, collecting bacterial solution, centrifuging and collecting precipitate, and (iv) re-suspending the precipitate in buffer solution, ultrasonically processing, centrifuging and collecting supernatant which is the crude enzyme solution of carbonic anhydrase. The L-lysine decarboxylase catalyzes the decarboxylation reaction of L-lysine or its salt to prepare pentane diamine in the system and carbonic anhydrase is added to capture the carbon dioxide released by the decarboxylation reaction to adjust the pH of the decarboxylation process in situ. Preferred Composition: The enzyme activity ratio of decarboxylase and carbonic anhydrase is 1:(0.5-2). The dosage of decarboxylase is 150-250 U/g substrate. The L-lysine or its salt is added in the form of a solution. The concentration of L-lysine or its salt in the solution is 150-500 g/L. Preferred Conditions: The decarboxylation reaction is carried out at an initial pH of 5.0-6.0, temperature of 25-65 degrees C and rotating speed of 50-500 rpm.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-A02D; D05-H99

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): The compound (I) is adding phenylphosphine reactant of formula (IA) or (IB) with solvent, stirring in glove box to obtain white suspension, dissolving iron salt in solvent, pouring to white suspension at ratio of 1:5-1:10, mixing for 0.5-1 hour, and reacting.    TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The conjugated manganese porphyrin bipyridine microporous polymer material is prepared by using Scholl coupling reaction with manganese tetraphenylporphyrin and 2,2-dipyridyl as monomer, and anhydrous aluminum chloride as catalyst. The conjugated-manganese porphyrin pyrrole microcellular polymeric material is prepared by using Scholl coupling reaction with manganese tetraphenyl porphyrin and pyrrole as monomer, and anhydrous aluminum chloride as catalyst.

   TARDY A
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
   LINDE AG (LINM-C)
GA 2019742233
AB    NOVELTY - Producing gas product containing predominantly or exclusively hydrogen, comprises forming a first feed mixture containing at least one hydrocarbon and water, subjecting first feed mixture to steam reforming, steam reforming a first product mixture comprising at least water, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide containing at least one hydrocarbon and hydrogen, where at least a portion of the first product mixture is used, a second feed mixture comprising at least water, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide containing at least one hydrocarbon and hydrogen. The second feed mixture is subjected to a water gas shift (3). The water gas shift comprises a second product mixture comprising at least water, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide. The at least one hydrocarbon and hydrogen and is enriched in carbon dioxide and hydrogen over the first product mixture and depleted in carbon monoxide and water.
   USE - The method is useful for producing gas product containing predominantly and exclusively hydrogen.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing gas product containing predominantly or exclusively hydrogen, comprises forming a first feed mixture containing at least one hydrocarbon and water, subjecting first feed mixture to steam reforming, steam reforming a first product mixture comprising at least water, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide containing at least one hydrocarbon and hydrogen, where at least a portion of the first product mixture is used, a second feed mixture comprising at least water, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide containing at least one hydrocarbon and hydrogen. The second feed mixture is subjected to a water gas shift (3). The water gas shift comprises a second product mixture comprising at least water, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide. The at least one hydrocarbon and hydrogen and is enriched in carbon dioxide and hydrogen over the first product mixture and depleted in carbon monoxide and water. At least part of the second product mixture is subjected to hydrogen separation (4) after at least partial removal of water by hydrogen separation the gas product and the residual mixture comprising at least carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide containing at least one hydrocarbon and hydrogen. The second feed mixture is formed using a part of the remainder mixture. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for plant for producing gas product containing hydrogen, comprising an unit to form a first feed mixture containing hydrocarbon and/or water, an unit for steam reforming the first feed mixture by to form a first product mixture comprising water, carbon dioxide and/or carbon monoxide containing hydrocarbon and/or hydrogen using at least a portion of the first product mixture, a second feed mixture containing at least water, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide containing at least one hydrocarbon and hydrogen.

   Steam reforming (2)
   Water gas shift (3)
   Hydrogen separation (4)

PT P
PN CN109320412-A; CN109320412-B
TI Preparing 2,4,6-trimethylbenzoic acid comprises            reacting mesitylene and carbon dioxide under a catalyst            and obtaining 2,4,6-trimethylbenzoic acid.
AU WANG H
   HU L
   FENG G
   GAN L
   SHEN X
   WANG Z
AE GUIZHOU XINTIANXIN CHEM CO LTD (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2019172834
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 2,4,6-trimethylbenzoic acid                comprises reacting mesitylene and carbon dioxide                under a catalyst and obtaining                2,4,6-trimethylbenzoic acid, where the catalyst is                hydrogen chloride or concentrated hydrochloric                acid.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                2,4,6-trimethylbenzoic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The 2,4,6-trimethylbenzoic acid ensures high                purity of 98.1-99.1%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass concentration                  of the concentrated hydrochloric acid is 37-40%.                  The mass of the catalyst is 0.1-20% by mass of                  the mesitylene. The molar ratio of mesitylene to                  carbon dioxide is 1:0.3-0.5. Preferred Method:                  The reaction is carried out by continuously                  injecting mesitylene and carbon dioxide into the                  reactor, where the continuous injection flow rate                  of the mesitylene is from 50-100 ml/minute. The                  reaction temperature is 20-70 degrees C and the                  pressure of the reaction is 2-4 Mpa. The reaction                  time is 5-25 seconds. The reaction further                  comprises post-treatment of the reaction product,                  where the post-treatment comprises either cooling                  the reaction product and carrying out                  solid-liquid separation to obtain                  2,4,6-trimethylbenzoic acid when the catalyst is                  hydrogen chloride or sequentially subjecting the                  reaction product to stratification, organic layer                  cooling and solid-liquid separation to obtain                  2,4,6-trimethylbenzoic acid when the catalyst is                  concentrated hydrochloric acid.

PT P
PN CN113430547-A
TI Device useful for preparing potassium formate by            electrolyzing carbon dioxide, comprises anode chamber            and cathode chamber which are connected by cation            exchange membrane which is Nafion membrane.
AU WANG W
   LV W
   ZHANG R
AE YANCHENG INST TECHNOLOGY (YANH-C)
GA 2021B4219X
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises anode chamber and cathode                chamber that are connected by cation exchange                membrane (5). The cation exchanging membrane is                Nafion (sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene based                fluoropolymer-copolymer) membrane. The top of the                anode room is opened with oxygen outlet. The anode                chamber is filled with anode electrolyte (4). The                anode electrolytes are acidic electrolyte or                neutral electrolyte. The top end of the cathode                chamber has tail gas outlet and carbon dioxide                inlet. The cathode electrolyte (3) is alkaline                electrolyte, and anode electrode (2) is provided in                the anode chamber. The carbon dioxide access tube,                pH meter and cathode electrode (1) are arranged in                the cathode chamber. The anode electrode is                electrically connected to the cathode electrode,                and one end is connected with the carbon dioxide                entrance inlet, and the other end is inserted into                the cathode electrolyte.
   USE - The device is useful for preparing potassium                formate by electrolyzing carbon dioxide. The                potassium formate useful for oil field drilling                fluid, snow melting agent, anti-freezing agent,                carbon black surface modifying agent, crop foliar                fertilizer, cement early strength agent and leather                tanning agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The device provides potassium formate by                electrolyzing carbon dioxide and electrolytic                method, which is good for carbon dioxide emission                reduction and low cost for producing potassium                formates. The device obtains the potassium                hydroxide solution after finishing electrolysis,                distilling to obtain the potassium-formate product,                good for product separation, the pH observation                method can detect the reaction end point, avoids                the use of expensive device e.g. chromatography,                provides great convenience for industrial                production. The electrolytic reaction process can                completely realize unmanned pipe, no limit to the                production time, so it is better for using the                cheap power of night valley electricity or energy                waste that can not be removed in time to produce,                so as to reduce the production cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                electrolytic method of device for electrolyzing                carbon dioxide to prepare potassium formate.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of the                device for preparing potassium formate by                electrolyzing carbon dioxide (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
   Cathode electrode (1)
   Anode electrode (2)
   Cathode electrolyte (3)
   Anode electrolyte (4)
   Cation exchange membrane (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (1), the                  concentration of the potassium hydroxide solution                  is higher than 1 mole per liter, and the cathode                  catholyte in the cathode chamber is                  high-concentration potassium hydroxide solution                  saturated with carbon dioxide. In the step (2),                  the acidic potassium salt or acidic aqueous                  solution is potassium sulfate, potassium                  bisulfate, potassium perchlorate, potassium                  dihydrogen phosphate, phosphoric acid, perchloric                  acid or sulfuric acid aqueous solution. The anode                  anolyte of the anode chamber continuously                  provides hydrogen ions to the cathode                  compartment, consuming hydroxide ions or                  bicarbonate ions in the solution of the cathode                  compartment, and carbon dioxide dissolved in                  water will react with hydroxide ions to produce                  bicarbonate ions. The cathode electrode is                  tin-based, bismuth-based or cobalt-based                  high-efficiency catalyst for converting carbon                  dioxide into formate, and the anode electrode is                  iridium-ruthenium-titanium or                  iridium-tantalum-titanium high-efficiency oxygen                  evolution catalyst. The anode electrode and                  cathode electrode are connected by wires and                  power source is connected in between. The total                  reaction is reacting 2 moles of carbon dioxide                  and 2 moles of potassium hydroxide to obtain 2                  moles of potassium formate and oxygen.

PN CN108187677-A
TI Preparation of catalyst used for removing hydrogen cyanide by supercritical carbon dioxide/supercritical hydrothermal combination method includes dissolving active component precursor, washing carrier, heating, cooling and separating.
AU TIAN S
   ZHANG Y
   LI Y
   HUANG J
   HU X
   NING P
   GU J
   GUAN Q
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 201850867E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of catalyst comprises dissolving active component precursor in solvent A to obtain 0.1-10 mol/L active component impregnation solution, washing carrier, adding active component impregnation liquid to reaction vessel, heating carbon dioxide to supercritical state of carbon dioxide, immersing for 1-60 minutes, opening pressure relief valve, reducing pressure, collecting carbon dioxide, adding water to pretreatment reaction system, heating to supercritical state of water, reacting for 1-30 minutes, cooling, opening pressure relief valve, reducing pressure and separating gas and liquid. The active component precursor is transition metal acetate, transition metal nitrate, transition metal chloride salt or transition metal sulfate. The solvent A is water, ethanol or methanol.
   USE - Method of preparing catalyst used for removing hydrogen cyanide by supercritical carbon dioxide/supercritical hydrothermal combination method (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has short dipping time, improves dispersion degree and has high removing efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of catalyst comprises dissolving active component precursor in solvent A to obtain 0.1-10 mol/L active component impregnation solution, washing carrier, adding active component impregnation liquid to reaction vessel, heating carbon dioxide to supercritical state of carbon dioxide, immersing for 1-60 minutes, opening pressure relief valve, reducing pressure, collecting carbon dioxide, adding water to pretreatment reaction system, heating to supercritical state of water, reacting for 1-30 minutes, cooling, opening pressure relief valve, reducing pressure and separating gas and liquid. The active component precursor is transition metal acetate, transition metal nitrate, transition metal chloride salt or transition metal sulfate. The solvent A is water, ethanol or methanol. The carrier is gamma -aluminum oxide, cerium dioxide, zirconium dioxide, activated carbon, carbon nanotubes, activated carbon fiber, carbon black, TS-1 molecular sieve or Zeolite Socony Mobil-5 (ZSM-5) molecular sieve. The solid-liquid ratio of carrier and active component impregnation solution is 1:1-10. The temperature of supercritical carbon dioxide is 32-85 degrees C. The pressure is 7.3-25 MPa. The temperature of supercritical state of water is 374-600 degrees C. The pressure is 22.1-35 MPa.

PN US2022145008-A1
TI Polymer composition used in microporous carbon material for partially separating carbon dioxide gas from mixture of gases, comprises covalent triazine framework, and multiplicity of A unit and B unit, and portion of connection points are terminated by endcapping nitrile groups.
AU DAI S
   POPOVS I
   YANG Z
AE UNIV TENNESSEE RES FOUND (TENE-C)
   UT-BATTELLE LLC (UTBA-C)
GA 2022665384
AB    NOVELTY - Polymer composition comprises covalent triazine framework, and multiplicity of A unit (I) and B unit (II), and portion of connection points are terminated by endcapping nitrile groups.
   USE - Polymer composition used in microporous carbon material for partially separating carbon dioxide gas from carbon dioxide-containing mixture of gases
   ADVANTAGE - The polymer composition is directed to covalent triazine framework (CTF) polymer membranes that exhibit exceptional gas permeabilities and selectivities for separating gases, such as separating carbon dioxide (CO2) from other gas molecules, such as nitrogen (N2) and oxygen (O2) and used in polymer membranes having excellent potential for large-scale gas separations of commercial and environmental relevance. The membrane separation possesses many inherent advantages, such as high energy efficiency, low capital investments, operational simplicity and flexibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Polymer composition comprises covalent triazine framework, and multiplicity of A unit (I) and B unit (II), and a portion of connection points are terminated by endcapping nitrile groups, where asterisk (asterisk) in A units denotes a point of covalent bonding with an asterisk in B units, and each asterisk (asterisk) in B units denotes a point of covalent bonding with an asterisk in A units, and r is an integer between 1-3. R=fluorinated hydrocarbon containing two aromatic rings and a ether linkage between aromatic rings.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:

PN NL2025074-B1
TI Method of upgrading gas stream, involves separating residual gas stream in processing stage by condensing portion of carbon dioxide present in residual gas stream by bringing residual gas stream into gas-liquid phase at predetermined temperature.
AU EIJKMAN P F W
AE BASGAS BV (BASG-Non-standard)
GA 2021C4364L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves separating the first gas stream in a first processing phase (1) into an upgraded gas stream and a residual gas stream, which upgraded gas stream comprises methane and carbon dioxide in a second ratio. The residual gas stream is separated, in a second processing phase (2), into the reusable gas stream and a carbon dioxide stream, which recyclable gas stream comprises methane and carbon dioxide in a fourth ratio. The proportion of methane is greater than in the third ratio. The reusable gas flow obtained in the second processing phase is combined with the gas flow in the first processing phase. The residual gas stream is separated in the second processing stage by condensing portion of the carbon dioxide present in the residual gas stream by bringing the residual gas stream into a gas-liquid phase at a temperature greater than or equal to -50 degrees C, greater than or equal to -20 degrees C, greater than or equal to 0 degrees C.
   USE - Method of upgrading gas stream used as fuel for agricultural vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The installation suitable for application of the method is built and used more cheaply by not cryogenically cooling the residual gas stream for splitting off carbon dioxide, but splitting off the carbon dioxide at normally occurring ambient temperatures. The residual gas stream is only cooled to such an extent that sufficient carbon dioxide is separated to obtain the correct composition of the reusable gas stream, so that the requirement to separate as much carbon dioxide is avoided. The efficiency of the cycle is optimized. The requirement of the pure made reusable gas flow and the efficiency of the first machining phase not adversely affected are avoided by the lowering of the quality of the first gas stream. The method is added well to the first gas stream without affecting the efficiency of the subsequent steps. The good ratio in the reusable gas stream is achieved.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the steps of method of upgrading gas stream used as fuel.
   First processing phase (1)
   Second processing phase (2)
   Third step (3)

AU CHEN J
   LA X
   LA J
AE GUANGDONG QIANNENG TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A5526B
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling of electrolyte in waste lithium ion battery materials involves disassembling the waste lithium-ion battery, performing carbon dioxide extraction at 35-45 degrees C and 23-25 MPa for 45-50 minutes, adding an entrainer into the carbon dioxide extraction tank during extraction, reducing the temperature of the extraction tank to normal temperature to obtain a extraction product (A), performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of product (A) to obtain a extraction product (B), dehydrating the product (B) using molecular sieves, removing an inorganic acid in the product (B) using a weakly basic anionic surface-active exchange resin to obtain a base electrolyte, analyzing the substance content in the base electrolyte, adding an electrolyte organic solvent and an electrolyte to the base electrolyte under the protection of inert gas according to the lithium ion electrolyte substance content standard to obtain an electrolyte solution.
   USE - Recycling of electrolyte in waste lithium ion battery materials.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recycling of electrolyte in waste lithium ion battery materials involves disassembling the waste lithium-ion battery, placing the electrolyte in a supercritical carbon dioxide extraction tank under inert gas protection, switching on the critical carbon dioxide extraction unit, opening a gas valve to the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction tank, performing carbon dioxide extraction at 35-45 degrees C and 23-25 MPa for 45-50 minutes, adding an entrainer into the carbon dioxide extraction tank during extraction, closing the gas valve, reducing the temperature of the extraction tank to normal temperature to obtain a extraction product (A), performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of product (A) to obtain a extraction product (B), dehydrating the product (B) using molecular sieves, removing an inorganic acid in the product (B) using a weakly basic anionic surface-active exchange resin to obtain a base electrolyte, analyzing the substance content in the base electrolyte, adding an electrolyte organic solvent and an electrolyte to the base electrolyte under the protection of inert gas according to the lithium ion electrolyte substance content standard to obtain an electrolyte solution.

   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The entrainer is methanol-type entrainer or carbonate-type entrainer chosen from propylene carbonate, butylene carbonate and/or pentene carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The weakly basic anionic surface-active exchange resin is hydroxide-type surface active exchange resin.

The                article comprises a first sheet and a second sheet,                a first side of the first sheet faces a second side                of the second sheet, and the first sheet and the                second sheet are bonded together to form an                internal cavity in a space between the first side                of the first sheet and the second side of the                second sheet. The bond extends around at least a                portion of a perimeter of the internal cavity. The                bond is contiguous around only one or more portions                of the perimeter of the internal cavity, and                includes one or more apertures capable of admitting                a fluid to the internal cavity, forming an open                cavity; or bond is contiguous around an entire                perimeter of the internal cavity, forming a sealed                bladder capable of retaining a fluid in the                internal cavity at a pressure above or below                atmospheric pressure.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                article, which comprises first sheet comprising at                least one layer and a second sheet comprising at                least one layer, where a first side of the first                sheet faces a second side of the second sheet, and                the first sheet and the second sheet are bonded                together to form an internal cavity in a space                between the first side of the first sheet and the                second side of the second sheet, the bond extends                around at least a portion of a perimeter of the                internal cavity, the internal cavity comprises a                foamed material; the foamed material is a                physically-expanded foam formed of a thermoplastic                elastomeric material comprising one or more first                thermoplastic elastomers; and the foamed material                is a product of placing an unfoamed article                comprising a solid foamable material in liquid                carbon dioxide, infusing the solid foamable                material with the liquid carbon dioxide, and                expanding the infused solid foamable material by                phase transitioning the infused carbon dioxide into                a gas under conditions which do not soften the                solid foamable material, forming the foamed                material.

PN CN110918112-A
TI Preparation of carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst used in preparation of electrode, involves preparing nano metal-organic framework material ZIF-8 using dimethylimidazole and zinc nitrate hexahydrate, drying, and calcining.
AU TANG Z
   ZHENG Y
   YANG Z
AE NAT CENT NANOSCIENCE & TECHNOLOGY CHINA (NANI-C)
GA 202026002C
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst involves preparing nano metal-organic framework material ZIF-8 by hydrothermal reaction using dimethylimidazole, zinc nitrate hexahydrate and methanol as raw materials, drying, and calcining in inert gas atmosphere.
   USE - Preparation of carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst used in preparation of electrode (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables easy and economical preparation of carbon dioxide electrochemical reduction catalyst having excellent activity and Faraday efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of the catalyst in preparation of electrode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The inert gas is argon gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The nano metal-organic framework material is prepared by dissolving 1000-1500 mg dimethylimidazole in 10-20 mL methanol to prepare solution (A), dissolving 1000-1200 mg hexahydrate zinc nitrate in 20-40 mL methanol to prepare solution (B), mixing the solution (A) with the solution (B) at room temperature in reactor with polytetrafluoroethylene lining and stainless steel sleeve, subjecting to hydrothermal reaction at 100-10 degrees C for 2-5 hours, adding 20-40 mL methanol, washing, and cooling. The calcination is carried out in a tubular furnace at 800-1100 degrees C for 2-4 hours. The drying is carried out at 50-80 degrees C for 20- 30 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The electrode is prepared by dispersing 0.5-2 mg catalyst in 100 mu L ethanol solution, adding 2-8 wt.% Nafion (RTM: sulfonated tetrafluoroethylene-based fluoropolymer-copolymer) solution, stirring, coating resultant solution of on electrode, and drying.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRONICS - Preferred Components: The electrochemical cell further comprises a water supply unit for supplying water, a carbon dioxide supplying unit for supplying carbon dioxide, a polymer membrane (12) interposed in the electrolyte, and a gas diffusion layer provided on the first electrode side. The water supply unit supplies water to the first electrode. The carbon dioxide supply unit supplies carbon dioxide to the electrolyte, preferably second electrode electrolyte. The carbon dioxide supply unit supplies carbon dioxide to dissolve carbon dioxide in supersaturated state in the electrolyte.

The first electrode electrolyte comprises potassium hydroxide aqueous solution or potassium nitrate aqueous solution. The second electrode electrolyte comprises organic salt containing cations including quaternary ammonium, imidazolium, pyridinium or phosphonium ions, and anions including chloride, bromide, iodide, tetrafluoroborate, hexafluorophosphate, bistriflimide or anhydrides (RCO2-).

PN CN113236389-A; CN113236389-B
TI Compressed carbon dioxide energy storage system, has high-pressure gas storage module that contains multiple high-pressure gas storage tanks, and low-pressure gas storage module that contains multiple low-pressure gas storage tanks.
AU HUANG Q
   SUN Q
AE UNIV SHANDONG (USHA-C)
GA 202194782G
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a high-pressure gas storage module, a low-pressure gas storage module, a compressor (1) and a turbine (6). The high-pressure gas storage module contains multiple high-pressure gas storage tanks (11). The low-pressure gas storage module contains multiple low-pressure gas storage tanks (8). The gas pressure in each high-pressure gas storage tank is all different, and is evenly divided into the pressure range. The gas pressures in each low-pressure gas storage tank are all different, and are evenly distributed within the pressure range. The compressor has three stages of compressors, each stage of the compressor is connected by a carbon dioxide pipeline. The outlet of each stage of compressor is the inlet of the next stage of compressor.
   USE - Compressed carbon dioxide energy storage system for renewable energy sources such as photovoltaics and wind power.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can select high-pressure gas storage tanks and low-pressure gas storage tanks with appropriate pressures to meet the power requirements of the compressor or turbine, thus improving the adjustment capability of the compressed carbon dioxide energy storage system.

   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a compressed carbon dioxide energy storage system.

GA 202081884P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves entering a refinery dry gas (3) to a membrane separation unit (1) as a raw material gas. The gas phase enters into a compressor. The gas phase consists most of hydrogen and a portion of methane, and a small amount of other component which permeates through the membrane and accumulates the methane and hydrogen gas at the permeation side. A small amount of hydrogen and methane and other component which do not pass through the membrane is separated from the membrane. The non-permeable side is enriched with carbon dioxide, and is sent to a carbon dioxide recovery unit (2). A carbon rich gas (5) from the membrane separation unit enters the carbon dioxide recovery unit, after absorption and desorption. The carbon dioxide enriched gas and a small amount of fuel gas are obtained. The multiple dry gas comprises catalytic dry gas, coking dry gas, reformed dry gas, aromatized gas, dis-proportionated dry gas, pressure swing adsorption (PSA) tail gas or hydrogenation dry gas.

GA 2019A2474P
AB    NOVELTY - A system comprises a case comprising inlet for carbon dioxide and hydrogen on one side, an outlet for discharging the reaction product which is formed on other side, and catalyst for reverse water gas shift reaction arranged in the inner space of the case, charging simultaneously catalyst for Fischer-Tropsch reaction, such that performing both reactions which improves yield of carbon monoxide, and carbon monoxide generating unit (100) comprising a catalytic reactor, in which the heat generated in the reactions is to be used in water gas shift reaction.
   USE - System used for converting carbon dioxide using hydrogen (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system is simple by eliminating or minimizing heat exchanger to remove the heat generated from the Fischer-Tropsch reaction, and enables converting carbon dioxide with high yield while reducing energy required in conversion reaction and high-overall energy efficiency, and excellent overall process productivity, and reducing maintenance cost and operation cost. The heat generated from the Fischer-Tropsch reaction is utilized as the energy supplied to the reverse water gas shift reaction to maximize the efficiency, and also possible to operate the generation unit in an adiabatic state without supply of energy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for conversion of carbon dioxide using hydrogen, which involves generating carbon monoxide by introducing carbon dioxide and hydrogen into a reactor simultaneously filled with a catalyst for a reverse gas shift reaction and a catalyst for Fischer-Tropsch reaction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide conversion system.
   Carbon monoxide generating unit (100)
   Removing unit (110)
   Hydrocarbon generating unit (120)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The system further comprises a removal unit (110) for separating and removing hydrocarbons, and receiving the reaction product generated from carbon monoxide generating unit, which is arranged on rear end of the generating unit, and hydrocarbon generating unit (120) which is filled with the Fischer-Tropsch catalyst, receiving a gaseous material from the removal unit, and performing Fischer-Tropsch reaction, and arranged at the rear end of the removing unit. The catalytic reactor comprises a catalyst layer filled with the catalyst for reversible gas shift reaction, and a catalyst layer filled with the catalyst for Fischer-Tropsch reaction which are alternately stacked at least once or repeatedly, and are uniformly mixed, and divides an internal space into at least two or more reaction zones by a partition wall or a reaction tube, and each of the partitioned reaction zones comprising catalyst for reverse water gas shift reaction or catalyst for reverse gas reaction, or the adjacent reaction zones is filled with different catalysts. The catalyst layers are located at the same height in adjacent reaction zones. Preferred Process: The method further involves separating and removing hydrocarbons, and receiving the reaction product generated from carbon monoxide generating process, receiving the gaseous material after the removal step to generate hydrocarbons by Fischer-Tropsch catalyst, and converting process is carried out in reactor in which the catalyst layer is arranged so that the carbon dioxide and hydrogen introduced contacts with the catalyst layer. Preferred Conditions: The carbon monoxide production step is performed reaction temperature at 300-475 degrees C. The hydrocarbon production step is performed at reaction temperature at 200-350 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Fischer-Tropsch catalyst is based on iron, and further comprises copper, potassium, cobalt, zinc, and aluminum. The catalyst for the reverse water gas shift reaction comprises at least one metal chosen from copper, lithium, rhodium, molybdenum, platinum, cerium and palladium. Preferred Composition: The catalyst for Fischer-Tropsch reaction and catalyst for reverse water gas sift reaction is 1:99-99:1.

TI Emission reduction device of exhaust gas of ship, has ballast water treatment unit of second oxidant supply unit for supplying oxygen and carbon dioxide recovery unit for recovering carbon dioxide from exhaust gas discharged from engine.
AU KWON H
   MOON Y S
   KIM I W
   GANG G J
AE DAEWOO SHIPBUILDING & MARINE ENG CO LTD (DEWO-C)
GA 201861256H
AB    NOVELTY - The device has fuel supply line (120) for supplying fuel to the engine (111). An oxidant supply line (130) is provided for supplying an oxidant to the engine. A first oxidant supply unit is connected to the oxidant supply line to supply air as an oxidant of the fuel. A second oxidant supply unit is connected to the oxidant supply line for supplying oxygen and carbon dioxide as oxidizing agents of the fuel. The second oxidant supply unit has a ballast water treatment unit for supplying oxygen and a carbon dioxide recovery unit for recovering carbon dioxide from the exhaust gas discharged from the engine. The air supply by the first oxidizer supply unit and the supply of oxygen and carbon dioxide by the second oxidizer supply unit are selectively supplied to the oxidizer supply line.
   USE - Emission reduction device of exhaust gas of ship.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide and oxygen are supplied to the engine to remove nitrogen oxides which is very environmentally friendly. The fuel is efficiently burned while satisfying the exhaust gas nitrogen oxide emission regulation of the engine, and the operating costs are reduced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a operating method for emission reduction device of exhaust gas of ship.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the apparatus for reducing the emission of toxic substances in ship. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Engine (111)
   Fuel supply line (120)
   Oxidant supply line (130)
   First oxidizing agent feeding unit (140)
   Control unit (190)

TI Chemical additive comprises solubilizing agent and            viscosity reducing agent in ratio, and where            solubilizer is p-methoxy benzoate compound, which            increases carbon dioxide heavy oil mixing ability, and            has wide application prospect.
AU SUN Y
   O KWANG-HWAN
   XU J
   WANG S
   DENG H
   SUN B
   JIA L
   ZHANG Y
   ZHANG L
   YU C
   SUI Y
   LI P
   WU G
AE SINOPEC CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC HENAN OILFIELD XPLORATION & DEV (SNPC-C)
   EXPLORATION & DEV RES INST SINOPEC HENAN (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHENGLI OILFIELD CO EXPLORATION (SNPC-C)
GA 201829907X
AB    NOVELTY - Chemical additive for improving recovery of a                deep heavy oil carbon dioxide gas flooding process,                comprises solubilizing agent and a viscosity                reducing agent in weight ratio of 6:1, where                solubilizer is a p-methoxy benzoate compound which                is represented by (I). The viscosity reducing agent                for long-chain methacrylate-styrene-vinyl acetate                copolymer is represented by (II), and number of C                atoms is 12-20, and ratio of i:j:k is                (5-10):(2-8):(1-3).
   USE - Chemical additive used for improving recovery                of deep heavy oil carbon dioxide gas flooding                process.
   ADVANTAGE - The chemical additive which increases carbon                dioxide heavy oil mixing ability, reduces carbon                dioxide thick oil minimum phase pressure to form                carbon dioxide mixed phase displacement, reduces                viscosity of thick oil, improves thick oil flowing                property so as to improve the recovery ratio of                thick oil, has high efficiency, strong economical                efficiency, low energy consumption, and has wide                application prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Chemical additive for improving recovery of a                deep heavy oil carbon dioxide gas flooding process,                comprises solubilizing agent and a viscosity                reducing agent in weight ratio of 6:1, where                solubilizer is a p-methoxy benzoate compound which                is represented by (I). The viscosity reducing agent                for long-chain methacrylate-styrene-vinyl acetate                copolymer is represented by (II), and number of C                atoms is 12-20, and ratio of i:j:k is                (5-10):(2-8):(1-3).
   n=integer of 2-12.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The weight ratio of                  the solubilizer to the viscosity reducing agent                  is 4:1. The long chain ester methacrylic                  acid-styrene-vinyl acetate copolymer is prepared                  by passing methyl methacrylate, styrene and vinyl                  acetate as raw materials in the molar ratio of                  (5-10):(2-8):(1-3), with toluene as solvent,                  adding initiator azobisisobutyronitrile, under                  nitrogen atmosphere at 65-75 degrees C under                  refluxing reaction, stirring for 6-10 to obtain                  terpolymer. The temperature is raised to 80 plus                  minus 2 degrees C, adding concentrated sulfuric                  acid adding saturated alkyl monohydric alcohol in                  an equimolar amount to methyl methacrylate, and                  the saturated alkyl monohydric alcohol has 12-20                  carbon atoms, and subjecting ester to nitrogen                  gasification for exchange reaction for 4-6 hours                  to obtain crude product. The crude product is                  transferred to ethanol for purification to obtain                  final product. The concentrated sulfuric acid is                  concentrated sulfuric acid with a concentration                  of 98%, and volume of concentrated sulfuric acid                  is total weight of raw materials is 1:1 g:ml. The                  viscosity reducing agent dodecyl                  methacrylate-styrene-vinyl acetate copolymer is                  prepared by adding 80 ml of toluene as a solvent                  to a 150 ml three-necked flask equipped with a                  mechanical stirring and condensing tube,                  sequentially adding a total amount of 10 g of                  methyl methacrylate and styrene in a molar ratio                  of 10:8:1, adding 5% of the total weight of the                  initiator azobisisobutyronitrile, replaced by                  nitrogen bottle of air, after stirring at 70                  degrees C under reflux conditions for 8 hours to                  obtain a terpolymer, adding 10 ml of 98%                  concentrated sulfuric acid at 80 degrees C using                  a constant pressure dropping funnel, adding                  n-dodecanol n an equimolar amount to methyl                  methacrylate, subjecting to a transesterification                  reaction at 80 degrees C under a nitrogen                  atmosphere for 5 hours. The crude product is                  obtained by transferring the crude product to                  ethanol for purification. The total amount of                  chemical additives used for flooding the total                  amount of CO2 used is 1-10%, when the slug method                  is injected, the total amount of chemical                  additives used is 5-30% of the total amount of                  carbon dioxide in the pre-slug layer. Preferred                  Components: The solubilizing agent is propyl                  p-methoxy benzoate. The viscosity reducing agent                  is dodecyl methacrylate-styrene-vinyl acetate                  copolymer. Preferred Composition: The amount of                  azobisisobutyronitrile is 5% of the total weight                  of the raw material.
