PN CN109734606-A; CN109734606-B
TI Method for extending life of hydrogenation catalyst for preparing aromatic amine antioxidant, involves adding 4-aminodiphenylamine and water to autoclave, introducing carbon dioxide into air in kettle, and recovering carbon dioxide.
AU YU W
   DING J
   LIU F
   CHENG Q
   YU S
   CHAN Y
   SHAN Y
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 201945177A
AB    NOVELTY - The method for extending life of hydrogenation catalyst for preparing aromatic amine antioxidant, involves (1) adding 4-aminodiphenylamine and water to the autoclave, introducing carbon dioxide into the air in the kettle, and recovering the carbon dioxide under reduced pressure, allowing the mixed solution to stand for stratification, and transporting the organic phase to a hydrogenation reactor, (2) adding a ketone and a catalyst to the hydrogenation reactor, pressurizing with hydrogen, heating and stirring, and reacting, (3) adding the ketone and activated carbon into the activated carbon dispersion tank before the hydrogenation liquid is filtered, and introducing the suspended hydraulic pressure into the precision filter, forming a uniform thin carbon layer, sending hydrogenation liquid to a precision filter for filtration, and backflushing the catalyst into the hydrogenation reactor using a ketone for recycling after the filtration is completed.
   USE - Method for extending the life of hydrogenation catalyst for preparing aromatic amine antioxidant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has the characteristics of easy operation, recycling of raw materials, no three waste emissions, environmental protection and high efficiency. The number of hydrogenated catalysts used for the preparation of aromatic antioxidants is greatly increased thus reduces the consumption cost of the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The method for extending life of hydrogenation catalyst for preparing aromatic amine antioxidant, involves (1) adding 4-aminodiphenylamine and water to the autoclave, introducing carbon dioxide into the air in the kettle, pressing, heating, stirring and washing, recovering the carbon dioxide under reduced pressure, allowing the mixed solution to stand for stratification, and transporting the organic phase to a hydrogenation reactor which is previously replaced with hydrogen, and recycling the aqueous phase left in the autoclave, (2) adding a ketone and a catalyst to the hydrogenation reactor, pressurizing with hydrogen, heating and stirring, and reacting, (3) adding the ketone and activated carbon into the activated carbon dispersion tank before the hydrogenation liquid is filtered, and uniformly dispersing the suspension under ultrasonic conditions, and introducing the suspended hydraulic pressure into the precision filter to make the activated carbon filter the surface of the core, forming a uniform thin carbon layer, purging the piping system of the precision filter with nitrogen, sending hydrogenation liquid to a precision filter for filtration, and backflushing the catalyst into the hydrogenation reactor using a ketone for recycling after the filtration is completed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The mass ratio of 4-aminodiphenylamine to water is 1:(0.1-10), the pressure of carbon dioxide is 0.1-5 MPa, and the washing temperature is 70-100 degrees C. The molar ratio of 4-aminodiphenylamine to ketone is 1:(1-10), and the mass ratio of 4-aminodiphenylamine to dry catalyst is 1:(0.005-0.1). The mass ratio of the dry catalyst to the dry activated carbon is 1:(0.005-0.1), and the mass ratio of the ketone to the dry activated carbon is 1:(0.001-0.05). Preferred Components: The catalyst is platinum/carbon, palladium/carbon, modified platinum/carbon, and/or modified palladium/carbon. The ketone is acetone, methyl isobutyl ketone, and/or methyl isoamyl ketone. Preferred Condition: The hydrogen pressure in the hydrogenation reactor is 1-8 MPa, and the reaction temperature is 50-200 degrees C. The mesh of the activated carbon is 200-1000 mesh, and the isoelectric point is 0.5-6.5. Preferred Process: The hydrogenation reactor is equipped with a gas phase production outlet, the steam in the kettle is continuously extracted by steam stripping during the reaction, and the recovered raw material ketone is continuously added to the hydrogenation reactor after the steam is condensed, the evolved hydrogen is pressurized by a hydrogen circulation pump and continuously passed into the hydrogenation reactor, and a constant hydrogen pressure is maintained in the kettle until the reaction is completed.

PN CN109553540-A
TI Extracting choline in dandelion roots comprises e.g. soaking, sterilizing and washing picked dandelion roots in water, placing the washed dandelion root in oven for drying, and treating the surface of the dried dandelion root.
AU REN X
AE REN X (RENX-Individual)
GA 201932492A
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting choline in dandelion roots comprises soaking, sterilizing and washing picked dandelion roots in water, placing the washed dandelion root in oven for drying, treating the surface of the dried dandelion root, placing the surface treated dandelion root in crusher for crushing, crushing dandelion root into powder having particle diameter of 60-200 mu m, placing the dandelion root powder in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction vessel, carrying out the first extraction after introducing carbon dioxide flow rate of 10-60 l/hour in extraction environment having analytical temperature of 20-80 degrees C and analytical pressure of 5-15 MPa, taking the first extracted object, after allowing to stand the first extracted object at normal temperature for a period of time, where the extraction environment with an analytical temperature of 20-80 degrees C and an analytical pressure of 5-15 MPa, carrying out second extraction by introducing carbon dioxide having flow rate of 10-60 l/hour.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting choline in dandelion roots.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the extraction efficiency of choline.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Extracting choline in dandelion roots comprises soaking, sterilizing and washing picked dandelion roots in water, placing the washed dandelion root in oven for drying, treating the surface of the dried dandelion root, placing the surface treated dandelion root in crusher for crushing, crushing dandelion root into powder having particle diameter of 60-200 mu m, placing the dandelion root powder in supercritical carbon dioxide extraction vessel, carrying out the first extraction after introducing carbon dioxide flow rate of 10-60 l/hour in extraction environment having analytical temperature of 20-80 degrees C and analytical pressure of 5-15 MPa, taking the first extracted object, after allowing to stand the first extracted object at normal temperature for a period of time, where the extraction environment with an analytical temperature of 20-80 degrees C and an analytical pressure of 5-15 MPa, carrying out second extraction by introducing carbon dioxide having flow rate of 10-60 l/hour, after allowing to stand the second extracted object at normal temperature for a period of time, taking the second upper layer object, mixing the first upper layer object and the second upper layer object, adsorbing and analyzing the mixed object by resin, and drying to obtain extract of choline.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The soaking, sterilizing and washing of picked dandelion roots in water are processed by placing the picked dandelion roots in water for 5-10 hours, washing each dandelion root, and placing each dandelion root in ultraviolet disinfection cabinet for disinfection, the method comprises placing the surface-treated dandelion root in crusher for crushing, and crushing the dandelion root into powder having particle diameter of 120 mu m, carrying out the extraction in extraction environment having analytical temperature of 20-80 degrees C and analytical pressure of 5-15 MPa, carrying out the first flow after passing flow rate of 10-60 l/hour of carbon dioxide, carrying out the first extraction by introducing carbon dioxide having flow rate of 25 l/hour at analysis temperature of 40 degrees C and analytical pressure of 8 MPa, carrying out the extraction in extraction environment having analytical temperature of 20-80 degrees C and analytical pressure of 5-15 MPa, carrying out the first flow after passing flow rate of 10-60 l/hour of carbon dioxide, under the extraction environment of analytical temperature of 60 degrees C and analytical pressure of 15 MPa, carrying out second extraction by introducing carbon dioxide having a flow rate of 50 l/hour.

AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises carbon dioxide and                refrigeration working substance, where                refrigeration working substance comprises water,                oil base carrier liquid and hydrate accelerant and                slurry stabilizer, where the carbon dioxide is                dissolved in water, the hydrate accelerator and                slurry stabilizer dissolved in the oil base carrier                liquid and/or water.
   USE - The composition is useful in heat transfer                medium, and in a phase change refrigeration system                (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: provides a way for realizing                efficient and reasonable energy and practical                utilization of hydrate phase change refrigeration,                and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) use of a composition as a heat transfer                medium;
   (2) preparing a heat transfer medium,                comprising preparing a refrigerant, and then                uniformly mixing the refrigerant with carbon                dioxide, the refrigerant containing water, an                oil-based carrier liquid, a hydrate accelerator,                and a slurry stabilizer;
   (3) processing hydrate phase change                refrigeration comprises (i) applying pressure to a                composition and contacting with a low temperature                substance having a temperature lower than 20                degrees C for heat exchange causing a hydration                reaction of the composition to form a suspension of                carbon dioxide hydrate, (ii) further contacting the                suspension with a high temperature substance for                heat exchange to obtain a fluid containing carbon                dioxide, and the temperature of the high                temperature substance is higher than the                temperature of the low temperature substance;                and
   (4) hydrate phase-change refrigeration system                comprising a low temperature substance source, a                first heat exchanging device, a pressurizing                device, a second heat exchanger and a high                temperature substance source. first heat exchanger                and a second heat exchanger through a pipeline, the                first heat exchanger has a heat exchange function                of reactor, the composition according to any one of                claims and the low temperature material source                after pressurizing device of low temperature                material heat exchange in the first heat exchange                device, the composition is hydrating, high                temperature material suspending liquid suspension                form to hydrate, the hydrate by pipeline                transportation into the second heat exchange device                with high temperature provided by the material                source to contact the high temperature material                cooling, the temperature of hydrate rise, after the                increase of the temperature of hydrate phase-change                or not change the phase into the pressurizing                device after circulating in the first heat exchange                device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of hydrate phase-change refrigeration system.                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The content of carbon                  dioxide is 2-20 wt.%, the water content is 10-60                  wt.%, the content of the oil-based carrier liquid                  is 20-80 wt.%. The hydrate accelerator and slurry                  stabilizer are each added in an amount of 0.01-35                  wt.% and 0.01-4 wt.%. Preferred Components: The                  hydrate accelerator comprises 0-15 wt.%                  tetrahydrofuran (preferred), 0-15 wt.%                  cyclopentane (preferred), and 0-32 wt.%                  tetrabutylammonium bromide, the slurry stabilizer                  comprises 0.01-1 wt.% surfactant and 0.01-2 wt.%                  polymerization inhibitor, preferably the                  surfactant is sodium lauryl sulfate, dodecyl                  betaine, Span (RTM: Sorbitan monolaurate) and                  Tween (RTM: Polysorbate), preferably, the                  polymerization inhibitor is sorbitan tristearate,                  glyceryl monostearate and sorbitan oleate,                  preferably, the oil-based carrier liquid is                  mineral white oil, synthetic oil, gasoline and                  diesel. Preferred Method: The preparation method                  comprises adding 2-20 wt.% carbon dioxide, 10-60%                  water, and 20-80 wt.% oil-based carrier liquid,                  0.01-35 wt.% hydrate accelerator and 0.01-4 wt.%                  slurry stabilizer and hydrate promoter is 0-15                  wt.% tetrahydrofuran, 0-15 wt.% cyclopentane or                  0-32 wt.% tetrabutylammonium bromide, the slurry                  stabilizer comprises 0.01-1 wt.% surfactant and                  0.01-2 wt.% polymerization inhibitor, preferably,                  surfactant is sodium lauryl sulfate, dodecyl                  betaine, Span (RTM: Sorbitan monolaurate) and                  Tween (RTM: Polysorbate), preferably, the                  polymerization inhibitor is sorbitan tristearate                  and monostearic acid glyceride, sorbitan oleate,                  preferably, the oil-based carrier liquid is                  mineral white oil, synthetic oil, gasoline,                  and/or diesel. In thre step (i), the pressure                  applied is 0.5-15 MPa, preferably 1-10 MPa. The                  method further comprises circulating carbon                  dioxide-containing fluid as the composition to                  perform the above steps (i) and (ii). The                  temperature of the low temperature substance is                  lower than 15 degrees C, preferably sea water                  and/or ground water. The temperature of the high                  temperature substance is 5 degrees C higher than                  the temperature of the low temperature substance,                  preferably the high temperature substance is air                  having a temperature higher than 20 degrees C                  and/or water. The space distance between the high                  temperature substance and the low temperature                  substance is 5-600 m, preferably 20-200 m. The                  suspension is contacted with the high temperature                  material after transport for 0.2-15 minutes,                  preferably 1-10 minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The first heat                  exchange device and the second heat exchange                  device is provided with a pressure reducing                  valve, a pressurizing device and a first heat                  exchange device is set with a high pressure                  storage tank, preferably, the low-temperature                  substance source between the first heat exchange                  device, between the first heat exchange device                  and a reducing valve, a pressurizing device and a                  compressor are respectively provided with a                  throttle valve. The first heat exchanging device                  comprises a reaction chamber and a heat exchange                  component, the heat exchange component is set in                  the reaction chamber, the bottom of the reaction                  chamber, second heat exchange to the second heat                  feeding device of top part of the material                  compressed by the compressor enters the high                  pressure storage tank, and the high pressure                  storage tank to the top of the reactor. The heat                  exchange component of the first heat exchanger                  and the high pressure storage tank is to exchange                  heat with the material in the high pressure                  storage tank through low-temperature substance                  after heat exchange in the first heat exchanger                  after discharge. The system further comprises an                  additive tank and a carbon dioxide storage tank,                  is connected with the first heat exchange device,                  respectively used for supplementing the additive                  and carbon dioxide into the first heat exchanging                  device according to the need.

PN CN107858170-A; CN107858170-B
TI Preparing bio-oil by carbon dioxide atmosphere two-step hydrothermal liquefaction of lignocellulose comprises e. g. taking straw powder, crushing, sieving, grading and performing one-step pre-treatment and one-step hydrothermal liquefaction.
AU YANG T
   WANG J
   LI R
   LI B
   KAI X
AE UNIV SHENYANG AEROSPACE (UYAE-C)
GA 201829545G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bio-oil by carbon dioxide atmosphere two-step hydrothermal liquefaction of lignocellulose comprises e. g. taking straw powder, crushing, sieving, grading, performing one-step pre-treatment and one-step hydrothermal liquefaction in sequence, adding the straw powder into a batch type high temperature and high pressure reactor, stirring, adding solvent deionized water, stirring evenly with a glass rod, sealing the reactor, purging the air in the kettle with high purity carbon dioxide purge, removing heating jacket, cooling the reaction kettle and opening the exhaust valve.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing bio-oil by carbon dioxide atmosphere two-step hydrothermal liquefaction of lignocellulose.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces residue and gas yield and ensures the directional control of the biomass-to-liquid process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing bio-oil by carbon dioxide atmosphere two-step hydrothermal liquefaction of lignocellulose comprises (i) taking 30-50 mesh straw powder, crushing and sieving; (ii) grading, performing one-step pre-treatment and one-step hydrothermal liquefaction in sequence, adding the straw powder into a batch type high temperature and high pressure reactor, stirring with a magnetic stirrer, adding solvent deionized water, stirring evenly with a glass rod, sealing the reactor, purging the air in the kettle with high purity carbon dioxide purge, where the one-step hydrothermal liquefaction comprises heating at 200 degrees C for 10-30 minutes, increasing heating rate to 5-10 degrees C/minute and heating again at 270-345 degrees C for 15-120 minutes; (iii) removing the heating jacket, cooling the reaction kettle through the fan to 30 degrees C, opening the exhaust valve, collecting the gas, opening the reactor when the pressure in the kettle is close to the ambient pressure, pouring out the liquid phase product and residue, flushing the kettle wall and tubing successively with acetone to obtain solid-liquid mixture; and (iv) filtering the mixture using acetone, evaporating in a water bath at 82-85 degrees C to obtain oil-water mixture, separating liquid, obtaining heavy oil and light oil and water mixture, extracting the oil and water mixture with dichloromethane to obtain dichloromethane phase mixture, performing rotary evaporation at constant temperature of 42 degrees C in a water bath, drying the residue separated from the liquid phase at 105 degrees C to constant weight and obtaining the residue.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the solvent is ethanol and water, and the volume ratio of ethanol and water is 1:1. Preferred Method: In the step (iv), the separation is carried out by filtering the mixture through acetone and obtaining bio-oil by rotary evaporation at 82-85 degrees C in a water bath. The residue separated from the liquid phase was dried at 105 degrees C until constant weight, defined as residue. In the step (ii), the classification reaction includes a multi-step pretreatment stage and a multi-step hydrothermal liquefaction stage.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of gold-doped copper palladium                core-shell structure nanomaterial involves: (1)                preparing highly dispersed gold nanoparticle gel;                (2) adding a protective agent to the highly                dispersed gold nanoparticle gel, and adding                precursor of copper and palladium; and (3) adding a                reducing agent to the solution obtained in step (2)                for reduction, centrifuging the obtained product,                and dispersing in deionized water to obtain                catalyst with Raman enhancement ability.

The mass ratio of gold,                copper and palladium is 12:(0.6-1.8):1. The                particle size of gold is 45-150 nm. The thickness                of the shell is 2-10 nm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The precursor of                  copper and palladium is chloropalladate and                  copper chloride in a mass ratio of 1:(1-3),                  preferably 1:1. TI Heat transfer and phase change simulation method            for supercritical carbon dioxide pressure crack mesh            expansion, involves assigning attribute parameter of            fluid under phase condition, and realizing simulation            of fluid non-uniform attribute.
AU WU J
   HAO N
   LI C
   WANG Y
AE WANG Y (WANG-Individual)
GA 202225957F
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves introducing thermal                diffusion effect of fracturing fluid in a crack and                a rock body. A fluid thermal diffusion and flow                finite volume algorithm and a finite unit algorithm                in the crack fluid and the rock matrix are utilized                to perform unit dispersion. A dynamic temperature                change of the fracturing fluid and a reservoir rock                mass is calculated to obtain a result. A phase                condition of a fracturing fluid at a current point                is determined according to a temperature and                pressure value of the blasting fluid in any one                point in the step one. AU XU K

   ZHANG C
AE UNIV YANGZHOU (UYYZ-C)
GA 2021E1094W
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide gas sensing material comprises a carbon dioxide gas sensing material, where the material is a zinc stannate oxide nanoparticle cluster material.
   USE - The sensing material is useful for respiratory gas detection.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces band gap width of zinc stannate oxide nanoparticle, improves electron mobility, and enures simple and safe preparation method, low cost and high practicability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) A method for preparing the sensing material, involving (a) mixing tin salt, zinc salt, sodium hydroxide, polyvinylpyrrolidone, hydrogen peroxide solution and deionized water, and magnetically stirring to obtain a uniform mixed solution, (b) transferring the mixed solution into a stainless steel autoclave lined with PTFE, sealing, reacting at 180degrees Celsius, naturally cooling to room temperature after the reaction, centrifuging, washing and drying to obtain a precursor material, and (c) drying the precursor material in air at 80degrees Celsius for 12 hours, and naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain zinc stannate oxide nanoparticle cluster material; and
   (2) An application method of the sensing material, involves adding zinc stannate oxide nanoparticle cluster material into deionized water for grinding to obtain a paste, uniformly coating the paste on the outer surface of a gas sensor substrate, completely covering the gold electrode, and drying in 80degrees Celsius air for 12 hours to obtain a gas-sensitive coating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The diameter of the zinc stannate oxide nanoparticle cluster material is 0.5-1.7 microm. The zinc stannate oxide nanoparticle cluster material has carbon dioxide gas-sensing properties at room temperature and relative humidity of 0-80%, where the photosensitivity is improved under the illumination of a light source. The light source comprises blue light, violet light and/or UV light. The molecular weight of the polyvinylpyrrolidone is 8000-700000.Preferred Components: The tin salt comprises tin pentachloride hydroxide. The zinc salt comprises zinc acetate dihydrate. The mixed solution comprises 2.1 g tin tetrachloride pentahydrate, 0.44 g zinc acetate dihydrate, 1.5 g sodium hydroxide, 1.2 g polyvinylpyrrolidone, 5 ml hydrogen peroxide solution with a mass fraction of 30%, and the reminder of deionized water. The gas sensor substrate comprises a sensor platinum electrode sample piece.Preferred Composition: The concentration of the hydrogen peroxide solution is 30%.Preferred Method: The reaction is performed at 180degrees Celsius for 12-20 hours. The prepared zinc stannate oxide nanoparticle gas sensor can be directly tested.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nickel-based catalyst comprises (a) adding nickel source and catalytic auxiliary agent into a first solvent to obtain a mixed solution, (b) adding mesoporous silicon dioxide template into a precursor solution of inorganic metal salt, stirring, drying to obtain an inorganic metal salt-silicon dioxide compound, (c) roasting the inorganic metal salt-silicon dioxide compound to obtain a metal oxide-silicon dioxide composite carrier material, (d) adding the mixed solution in the step (a) into the metal oxide-silicon dioxide composite carrier material, stirring, drying to obtain a solid product, and (e) calcining the solid product in a mixed gas of hydrogen-argon gas or hydrogen-nitrogen mixed gas atmosphere to obtain the nickel-based catalyst.

PN US2021151202-A1
TI Method for automatically offsetting carbon dioxide emissions in real-time using blockchain technology and distributed ledger technology, involves generating carbon offsets from geographically distributed renewable energy installations.
AU JABBAR R
   JABBAR K
AE JABBAR R (JABB-Individual)
   JABBAR K (JABB-Individual)
GA 2021538118
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves generating carbon offsets from collocated as well as geographically distributed renewable energy installations by capturing data from multiple IoT devices, and databases. The carbon dioxide emission reduction associated with the data is calculated and issues digital representations of data of carbon offsets that embody the associated carbon dioxide emission reductions. The digital representations through software application plug-ins, relying on multiple IoT devices and databases which generate and settle a time-based sequence of purchase orders allows for automated and real-time offsetting of CO2 emissions. The digital representations are automatically retired from the ledger or registry when they are purchased by entities wishing to offset their CO2 emissions.
   USE - Method for automatically offsetting carbon dioxide emissions in real-time using blockchain technology and distributed ledger technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is able to transact a native digital currency freely and instantaneously across the world and ensures that double spending of units of currency is impossible.

GA 202034937K
AB    NOVELTY - Forest fire prevention monitoring device based on wireless sensor network comprises a multiple cluster comprising a cluster head node and a monitoring node. The cluster head node is used to collect environmental information collected by each monitoring node. The monitoring node is used to collect environmental information e.g. wind direction, soil moisture, air temperature and humidity and transmits the collected forest environment information to the cluster head nodes. The data information of the cluster head node is transmitted to a convergence node through a communication network for fusion, and finally transmitted to a remote monitoring center. The remote monitoring center receives the data and synchronizes the environmental information and location information of the forest fire prevention monitoring area. A carbon dioxide concentration sensor is used to collect carbon dioxide concentration information and transmits the information to the cluster head node in the cluster.

PT P
PN CN110732328-A
TI Multi-core-shell structure nickel-based catalyst used for carbon dioxide reforming reaction, is obtained by synthesizing porous core-shell structure carbon material, then adding to solution containing nickel and magnesium ions and soaking.
AU HAN J
   LIANG Y
   YU F
   ZHAO B
AE UNIV WUHAN SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UWSC-C)
GA 202011519D
AB    NOVELTY - A multi-core-shell structure nickel-based catalyst is obtained by removing bagasse from impurities and hydrothermally synthesizing a porous core-shell structure carbon material, then adding the carbon material to an equal volume of a solution containing nickel and magnesium ions, soaking for 10-18 hours, drying to obtain a nickel-based catalyst precursor, and then using carbothermal reduction method to reduce the nickel-based catalyst precursor under an inert gas or a reducing gas to obtain core-shell structure carbide doped nickel-magnesium oxide. The catalyst comprises 15 %mass nickel, 5-15 %mass magnesium oxide and the balance amount of carbon material carrier with a porous core-shell structure. The nickel metal has a particle diameter of 20-27 nm.
   USE - The multi-core-shell structure nickel-based catalyst is useful for carbon dioxide reforming reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst can be used to recover nickel and magnesium metals through simple calcination, thus achieving metal recycling, reducing the cost, saving resources and protecting the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of using the multi-core-shell structure nickel-based catalyst for carbon dioxide catalytic reforming, which involves methane-carbon dioxide reforming reaction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The catalyst is prepared by: (1) removing bagasse by dehydrating and crushing the bagasse, then mixing with an acid solution at a ratio of (100-180) g:1000 ml, soaking at room temperature for 10-20 hours, then washing the filtered mixture with deionized water until the supernatant has a pH of 7, and finally drying the filtered material at 100-110 degrees C for 12-24 hours to obtain a solid (B); (2) preparing a porous core-shell structure carbon material by placing the solid (B) obtained in step (1) in a hydrothermal reactor to undergo a hydrothermal carbonization reaction, then washing with deionized water and drying at 100-110 degrees C for 10-18 hours; (3) preparing a nickel-based catalyst precursor by dissolving a nickel-containing compound in deionized water and stirring until completely dissolved to obtain 0.52 mol/l nickel-containing solution (A), dissolving a magnesium-containing compound in deionized water and stirring until completely dissolved to obtain a 0.25-0.75 mol/l magnesium-containing solution (B), mixing the solution (B) with the solution (A), then adding the porous core-shell structure carbon material to the same volume of solution (B) as the porous core-shell structure carbon material, soaking at room temperature for 12-24 hours and drying at 100-110 degrees C for 12-24 hours to obtain a solid (C); and (4) preparing the multi-core-shell nickel-based catalyst by using the carbothermal reduction method to place the solid (C) in an inert or reducing gas, heating the mixture at 800-1000 degrees C, then subjecting to heat preservation for 1-3 hours, and cooling to room temperature. The step of methane-carbon dioxide reforming reaction involves subjecting the above-obtained catalyst to reduction treatment at 800-900 degrees C for 1 hour in the presence of an inert gas or reducing gas atmosphere, where the reaction pressure is 10-140 kPa and the reaction temperature is 750-850 degrees C after the reduction treatment is completed, and then carrying out catalytic reforming reaction with methane-dioxide feed gas at a space velocity of 10-20 l/(g.hour) to prepare a synthesis gas containing hydrogen and carbon monoxide. The conversion rates of methane and carbon dioxide are 85-89% and 86-92%, respectively. The gas volume ratio of hydrogen and carbon monoxide in the mixed gas is 0.89-0.97. Preferred Conditions: The reaction time of the catalyst in the carbon dioxide reforming reaction is 0-800 hours. The conditions for hydrothermal synthesis in step (1) are a temperature of 180-250 degrees C and a reaction time of 15-25 hours. Preferred Components: The concentration of the acid solution used in step (1) is 0.01-2 mol/l. The acid used is aqueous solutions of nitric acid, hydrochloric acid, sulfuric acid or phosphoric acid. The nickel-containing compound in step (3) is nickel nitrate hexahydrate, nickel sulfate hexahydrate, nickel sulfide, nickel sulfate, nickel hydroxy acid or nickel halide. The magnesium-containing compound in step (3) is magnesium nitrate hexahydrate, magnesium sulfate heptahydrate or magnesium hydroxide. In step (4), the inert gas is nitrogen, helium, argon and/or xenon, and the reducing gas is hydrogen, olefin and/or alkyne.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)
MC E11-W; E12-A05; E12-A13; E31-A01; E31-N03D; E34-B01; H04-E04; H04-F02E; H04-F03; H04-F05; H04-F06; J04-E01; J04-E03; J04-E04A; J04-E11; N01-B; N02-C01; N04-A; N06-C08; N06-E01; N06-E03; N07-J; N07-K01

PT P
PN CN110721686-A; CN110721686-B
TI Catalytic cracking catalyst for preparing clean            gas containing methane, is prepared by heating peat            carbon precursor, performing water-vapor activation and            carbon dioxide activation, and supporting iron(III)            chloride or nickel nitrate.
AU YUAN H
   WANG S
   GU J
   ZHANG J
   SHAN R
AE GUANGZHOU ENERGY INST CONVERSION CHINESE (CAEC-C)
GA 2020106105
AB    NOVELTY - A catalytic cracking catalyst using peat                carbon as a support is prepared by pyrolyzing (i) a                dried peat at 700-900 degrees C in an inert                atmosphere to obtain a peat carbon precursor,                heating (ii) the peat carbon precursor at 700-900                degrees C in an inert atmosphere, passing an                ultrapure water for high-temperature water-vapor                activation, converting the inert atmosphere into a                carbon dioxide atmosphere, maintaining the                temperature to activate the peat carbon precursor                using carbon dioxide, converting the carbon dioxide                atmosphere into the inert atmosphere, and naturally                cooling to room temperature to obtain a peat                carbon, and performing wet impregnation (iii) by                supporting iron(III) chloride or nickel nitrate on                the peat carbon to obtain a composite catalyst,                drying the composite catalyst, and calcining the                dried composite catalyst. The supported amount of                iron or nickel in the catalytic cracking catalyst                is 5-15%.
   USE - Catalytic cracking catalyst is used for                preparing clean gas comprising carbon monoxide,                hydrogen and methane (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalytic cracking catalyst has improved                specific surface area and pore structure. The                contact area between the catalyst and the reactant                in the preparation of clean gas is increased.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of                the peat carbon-based catalytic cracking catalyst                in preparation of clean gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: In the process (ii), the                  water-vapor activation time is 30-40 minutes, and                  the carbon dioxide activation time is 30-40                  minutes in the carbon dioxide gas atmosphere. The                  process (iii) involves dissolving iron(III)                  chloride hexahydrate or nickel nitrate                  hexahydrate in water to obtain a iron(III)                  chloride solution or a nickel nitrate hexahydrate                  solution, weighing the peat carbon, dissolving                  the peat carbon in the iron(III) chloride                  solution or nickel nitrate hexahydrate solution,                  and stirring, in which the mass ratio of iron or                  nickel with respect to peat carbon is 0.05:1 to                  0.15:1. In the process (iii), the calcination is                  performed at 700-900 degrees C for 1-2 hours in                  an argon atmosphere. Preparation of clean gas                  involves placing the catalytic cracking catalyst                  in a reaction vessel, heating at 800 degrees C in                  an argon atmosphere, simultaneously introducing                  phenol and ultrapure water, and catalyzing phenol                  and ultrapure water passed through the catalytic                  cracking catalyst in the argon atmosphere. The                  ratio of the flow rate of phenol with respect to                  flow rate of ultrapure water is 1:3.
DC H04 (Petroleum processing - including treating, cracking, reforming, gasoline preparation - biosynthesis based on hydrocarbon feedstocks is included (C10G).)
MC H04-B02; H04-F02B; H04-F03; H04-F05; H04-F06

PN IN201641043599-A
TI Integrated optic grating sensor array for detecting hazardous gasses such as carbon dioxide, methane and ammonia has first, second and third grating structures positioned at rib waveguide to sense multiple gasses at same time.
AU MALATHI S
   UGRA M R
   SHEETHAL D P
AE UNIV RAMAIAH APPLIED SCI M S (UYRA-Non-standard)
GA 201851603W
AB    NOVELTY - The integrated optic grating sensor array has a rib waveguide core with a longitudinal axis, a first grating structure with a refractive index of 1.45 and coupled to a first sensing layer, a second grating structure with a refractive index of 1.458 and coupled to a second sensing layer, and a third grating structure with a refractive index of 1.54 and coupled to a third sensing layer. The first, second and third grating structures are positioned at the rib waveguide core to sense multiple gasses at the same time. The first sensing layer contains polyaniline based polymer material to detect the methane gas. The second sensing layer contains polyhexamethylenebiguanide based polymer material to detect the carbon dioxide gas. The third sensing layer contains polymethylmethacrylate based polymer material to detect the ammonia gas.
   USE - Integrated optic grating sensor array for detecting hazardous gasses such as carbon dioxide, methane and ammonia.
   ADVANTAGE - Provides device that is small in size, passive, immune to electromagnetic interference, resistance to harsh environments and have capability to perform distributed sensing. Ensures hazardous target gasses such as methane, carbon dioxide and ammonia respectively are detected using integrated optic grating based gas sensors by observing the Bragg wavelength shift in all the cases.

   USE - A gas supply apparatus for supplying mixed gas to methanation reactor of methane production apparatus (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Occurrence of the difference from the 1st target gas ratio that occurs in the gas ratio in the reactor can be suppressed. Occurrence of a residual variation of the gas ratio resulting from the time-lag from the detection of a gas ratio to acquisition can be suppressed by using the estimation ratio which considers the lag time. The estimation ratio is closer to the gas ratio in the reactor, and the gas ratio in the reactor is stable, securing the quality of the methane produced by a reactor. Since the gas ratio in a reactor is stabilized, the size of a surge tank can be reduced. Since the methanation reaction which is an exothermic reaction occurs within each reactor, the temperature of each reactor rises, and decline in the conversion ratio of methane within each reactor and deactivation of a methanation reaction can be suppressed.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) methane production apparatus comprising methanation reactor which manufactures methane from carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and gas supply apparatus which supplies mixed gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen to methanation reactor; and
   (2) control method of gas supply apparatus which involves estimating gas ratio, which is a volume ratio of hydrogen with respect to carbon dioxide in the mixed gas containing carbon dioxide and hydrogen, and supplying hydrogen or carbon dioxide of quantity according to the difference of the estimated gas ratio and the preset 1st target gas ratio from the gas supply adjustment unit.

PN WO2022192866-A1
TI Oxidizing alkane comprises combining alkane and            ozone in liquid phase medium comprising protic additive            under conditions sufficient to oxidize alkane to            products comprising hydroxylate.
AU SUBRAMANIAM B
   ZHU H
   JACKSON T A
AE UNIV KANSAS (UNIV-C)
GA 2022B6688K
AB    NOVELTY - Oxidizing (P1) an alkane comprises (a)                combining an alkane and ozone in a liquid phase                medium comprising a protic additive under                conditions sufficient to oxidize the alkane to                products comprising a hydroxylate.
   USE - The process is useful for oxidizing an                alkane.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: has a selectivity for a                hydroxylate of at least 80%, a ozone utilization                value defined as moles of the hydroxylate produced                per moles of ozone consumed of at least 100%;                produces carbon dioxide at a selectivity of                &#8815; 10%; and do not require the use of                carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                oxidizing (p2) an alkane comprising the step (a) as                per se, where the liquid phase medium is free of                added carbon dioxide, and adding the protic                additive to the liquid phase medium prior to                oxidizing the alkane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further                  comprises adding the protic additive to the                  liquid phase medium prior to oxidizing the                  alkane. The combining is carried out at a                  temperature of at least 15&#176; C and a                  total pressure of a vapor phase present above the                  liquid phase medium of &#8815; 5 MPa.                  Preferred Components: The protic additive is                  water (preferred), and/or an alcohol. The liquid                  phase medium comprises: at least 1 mmol protic                  additive; &#8815; 1 mmol carbon dioxide; and                  0.2-5 vol.% protic additive and a balance of the                  alkane. The liquid phase medium is free of added                  carbon dioxide. The alkane is: a 2-20C alkane; a                  branched alkane; and isobutane. A partial                  pressure of non-condensable gases in a vapor                  phase present above the liquid phase medium is                  0.1-0.6 MPa. A mole fraction of ozone in a feed                  gas mixture providing the ozone is 1-5%. In the                  process (p2), the liquid phase medium comprises                  at least 1 mmol water; and 0.2-5 vol.% water and                  a balance of the alkane.
   EXAMPLE - 40 ml Isobutane at 17&#176; C (saturation                vapor pressure of 0.86 MPa) were added to the batch                reactor. A known amount of water was also added                initially to study its effect. The products were                analyzed by gas chromatography/flame ionization                detector and proton NMR methods. The liquid phase                products after 7 sequential cycles of                pressurization with the ozone/oxygen mixture and                depressurization to the isobutane vapor pressure at                17&#176; C. In each cycle, the ozone depleted                in the previous cycle was replenished to generate                additional tert-butyl alcohol-butyl alcohol (TBA)                from the excess isobutane. When water was added,                ozone utilization (moles of TBA formed/moles of                ozone fed) was enhanced, and the product                distribution was shifted towards predominantly TBA,                with the formation of only a small amount of                acetone. Strikingly, in the absence of added water,                nearly equimolar TBA and tert- butyl hydroperoxide                (TBHP) formation was observed at significantly                lower yields of these products. Therefore, the                remaining experiments were carried out with added                water. Designed experiments confirmed that the role                of water was not to aid TBHP decomposition as                initially suspected. Quite unexpectedly, water                appears to favor the hydrotrioxide pathway for TBA                formation. This was completely different from the                free radical pathway for TBHP formation via                conventional oxygen oxidation of isobutane.

PN CN113307295-A
TI Production of nano-calcium carbonate by adding additive 1 and additive 2 to calcium hydroxide suspension, adjusting temperature, mixing, blowing carbon dioxide gas to carry out carbonization, ventilating, and adding surface treatment agent.
AU CHEN T
   KONG F
   CHENG Y
   WU G
AE ANHUI QIANJIANG ULTRAFINE POWDER TECHNOL (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2021A2685B
AB    NOVELTY - Production of nano-calcium carbonate comprises adding additive 1 and additive 2 to 1-1.5 mol/L calcium hydroxide suspension, adjusting temperature, mixing, blowing 10-25 wt.% carbon dioxide gas to carry out carbonization reaction, ventilating for 15-30 minutes when pH of suspension drops to 6.5-7, stopping aeration, adding surface treatment agent to carry out surface treatment, filtering, drying, crushing, and depolymerizing.
   USE - The method is for production of nano-calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method obtains primary particle size of less than 100 nm of nano-calcium carbonate product whose various indicators meet the requirements of GB/T19590 2011 standard, overcomes the low oversaturation of low carbon dioxide concentration kiln gas during carbonization, and does not easily obtain defects of nano-calcium carbonate products.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The calcium hydroxide suspension is obtained by digesting and reacting calcium oxide and water. Preferred Components: The volumetric flow rate of carbon dioxide is 4-24 L/(hour.mol calcium hydroxide).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive 1 is a nano-calcium carbonate crystal form control agent; and has addition ratio of 0.1-2 wt.% of the mass of nano-calcium carbonate. The additive 1 includes sucrose, triethanolamine, EDTA and disodium EDTA, zinc sulfate, sodium hexametaphosphate and/or citric acid and its mixture.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The additive 2 is a carbon dioxide absorption accelerator; and has added amount of 0.01-0.1 wt.% of the mass of calcium carbonate. The additive 2 includes surfactant that can reduce the surface tension of water. The additive 2 includes fluorocarbon functional group-containing surfactant and silicone surfactant.

PN CN113136593-A
TI Synthesis of ibuprofen involves adding organic solvent to one-way electrolytic cell reactor, adding alkylimidazole dicyanamide salt as supporting electrolyte, adding 1-p-isobutylphenyl-1-chloroethane, and carrying out electrolysis with electricity.
AU LIN X
   SANG Y
AE UNIV CHIFENG (UYCH-Non-standard)
GA 202186324Y
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of ibuprofen involves adding organic solvent to the one-way electrolytic cell reactor, adding alkylimidazole dicyanamide salt as supporting electrolyte, then adding 1-p-isobutylphenyl-1-chloroethane, and passing in carbon dioxide with stirring until carbon dioxide is saturated, using the magnesium or aluminum electrode as the anode, and using the carbon or iron electrode as the cathode, and carrying out the electrolysis with electricity when the carbon dioxide is continuously passed in.
   USE - Synthesis of ibuprofen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables short synthetic route, high yield, low raw material price, no metal salt, mild conditions, and the solvent, supporting electrolyte and extractant used in the reaction is recycled and reused. The method provides simple operation, low cost and reduced environmental pollution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The amount of alkylimidazole dicyanamide salt is 0.2-1 times the volume of the organic solvent. The reaction pressure is maintained under normal pressure, and the current is 0.1-1 A. The reaction temperature is maintained at no more than 50 degrees C. Preferred Components: The organic solvent is dimethylformamide or acetonitrile. The solvent is recovered under reduced pressure, water was added and the pH was adjusted to 1 with mineral acid, and the ether is used as the solvent for fractional extraction, the ether layers is combined, and the ether is recovered under reduced pressure. The obtained solid is recrystallized with ethanol and water as solvents.

   USE - Process for converting hydrocarbons to                products used as component in production of                construction, chemical material, cement, paper,                polymer, rubber, coatings, food, personal care                products, and pharmaceuticals; or used as                cementitious material in geopolymer, cement and                hybrid cement (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process for converting hydrocarbons to                products is recyclable process, where additives                consumed are significantly less and reduces carbon                footprint of overall process; is cheaply and                efficiently produced; have high economic value; and                improves controlling at relatively low                temperatures.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide and                  at least one molecular energy carrier are formed                  through at least one of gasification process,                  preferably supercritical water gasification                  process, gas reforming process, partial                  oxidation, fermentative gas production process,                  and enzymatic gas production process. The carbon                  dioxide is in supercritical state or subcritical                  state. The solid divalent metal carbonate is at                  least one of calcium carbonate, iron carbonate or                  magnesium carbonate. The silicate formed is                  separated and dried to form fumed silica, nano-                  or micro-silica. The divalent metal contains                  silicate which is silicate mineral, or                  nesosilicates or orthosilicates, having                  orthosilicate ion, which constitute isolated                  silicon tetroxide anion (SiO4)4- which has                  tetrahedral shape and are connected by divalent                  metals. The divalent metal contains silicate                  which is at least one of olivine, wolstanite,                  serpentine, forsterite, and monticellite.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process for                  converting hydrocarbons to products involves                  leaching divalent metal contains silicate at pH                  from 1 to below neutral to form divalent metal                  ions and silicate in reaction mixture, preferably                  pH is less than 2-7; increasing pH to react                  divalent solution with carbon dioxide to form                  solid divalent metal salt or silicate; reacting                  solid divalent salt with carbon dioxide to form                  solid divalent metal carbonate. Preferred                  Conditions: The temperature for leaching is more                  than 25 to less than 100 degrees C, preferably                  25-50 degrees C. The neutralizing is conducted by                  addition of base to increase pH to 7 or above,                  preferably 25-50 degrees C, and pressure in range                  from 0.5-3 bar, preferably 1-2 bar. The reaction                  of solid divalent salt with carbon dioxide is                  conducted at pressure in range from 5-50 bar,                  preferably 10-20 bar. Preferred Components: The                  hydrocarbons are at least one of organic                  material, such as biomass, coal, oil, plastics,                  and natural gas. The molecular energy carrier is                  hydrogen or hydrocarbon. The carbonate formed is                  separated and dried.

PT P
PN CN111838303-A
TI Inactivating alliinase activity in garlic during controlled atmosphere storage comprises adjusting ratio of carbon dioxide, oxygen, and nitrogen to inhibit the activity of alliinase.
AU XU J
   GAO J
   HE G
   HAN X
   LI L
   SHI X
   DANG J
AE XINJIANG AGRIC VOCATIONAL TECH COLLEGE (XINJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020A9440N
AB    NOVELTY - Inactivating alliinase activity in garlic during controlled atmosphere storage comprises adjusting the ratio of carbon dioxide, oxygen, and nitrogen to inhibit the activity of alliinase, and the air conditioning parameters are: oxygen concentration is 3.5%, carbon dioxide concentration is 14%, nitrogen concentration is 82.5%, storage temperature is 3-5 degrees C, the air humidity is 65-70 wt.%, processing after 60 days of storage.
   USE - The method is useful for inactivating alliinase activity in garlic during controlled atmosphere storage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts air conditioning pre-treatment, no heating, no waste water or waste gas generation, no damage to other nutrients in the garlic; is suitable for batch treatment, and has good enzyme passivation effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating the method for inactivating alliinase activity in garlic during controlled atmosphere storage (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method of inactivating alliinase activity in garlic specifically comprises (1) preparing garlic raw materials and drying them, (2) removing the pseudo stems, classifying according to the standards, and , (3) transferring the packaged garlic to the air-conditioned cold storage, and stacking it completely, starting the air conditioning warehouse, setting the temperature to 3-5 degrees C, air humidity is 65-70%, and air conditioning parameters are oxygen concentration is 3.5%, carbon dioxide concentration is 14%, nitrogen concentration is 82.5%, and keeping the cold storage operating parameters stable, (4) checking the storage of garlic every week, (5) carrying out routine monitoring of garlic alliinase activity and recording in detail, (6) removing the garlic from the air conditioning warehouse, when the storage period is 60 days, processing, the alliinase activity in the garlic drops below 30 U/g, and the enzyme activity inhibition rate is more than 85%, and (7) mechanically peeling the garlic, directly beating to extract alliinase. In the process, preparing the garlic raw materials and drying, harvesting the garlic after natural maturity, placing in a cool and ventilated place for natural drying after harvest. In the step (3), controlling the gas composition by the air conditioning control system, and controlling the temperature by the refrigeration equipment.

PN CN110951107-A
TI Controllably preparing polyimide (PI) aerogel material used in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide by reacting 3,5-diaminobenzoic acid and biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride as precursor in presence of crosslinking agent.
AU ZHAO X
   YI X
   ZHANG J
   LIU B
   LIU X
   SHEN X
   WU J
AE NEW MATERIALS INST SHANDONG ACAD SCI (UYQQ-C)
GA 202028256U
AB    NOVELTY - A polyimide (PI) aerogel material is controllably prepared by taking 3,5-diaminobenzoic acid and biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride as precursor of reaction in presence of crosslinking agent using sol-gel process to prepare PI wet gel, aging and drying to obtain PI aerogel material.
   USE - A controllable preparation of polyimide (PI) aerogel material used in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares polyimide aerogel material which has high photocatalytic efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for screening PI aerogel materials for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (CO2) which involves using PI aerogels with different energy band structures in the process of photocatalytic reduction of CO2 according to test results and screening PI aerogel with optimal energy band structure for catalytic reduction of CO2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Concentration of precursor is 5-10 wt.%. Crosslinking agent is 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenoxy) benzene. Preferred Process: Acetic anhydride and pyridine are added during the preparation of PI wet gel by sol-gel method. Method includes aging for 12-14 hours after gelling the the volume of PI wet gel, soaking in 75% N-methylpyrrolidone in ethanol solution for 24-28 hours, soaking in 25 vol.% N-methylpyrrolidone in ethanol solution for 24-28 hours, aging for 24-28 hours, and repeating fluid change for 3 times. Drying is performed at temperature of 275 degrees C, and pressure of 8.5-9 MPa. Continuous flow system reactor is used for reduction experiment with 300 W xenon lamp as light source, analyzing product components by mass spectrometer and gas chromatograph.

AB    NOVELTY - Non-alcoholic (0.0 vol.%) pilsner beer has an alcohol content of less than 0.05 vol.%, a bitterness value of 20-25 European (EBC), an original wort concentration of 4.5-7.5 degrees P, a carbon dioxide content of 0.50-0.60 %mass, a total alcohol of greater than or equal to 15 mg/l, and a total ester of greater than or equal to 5 mg/l.
   USE - As non-alcoholic pilsner beer.
   ADVANTAGE - The beer has alcohol ester flavor of traditional beer, and has light taste and prominent acidity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the pilsner beer, involving sequentially subjecting the pilsner beer to preheating, degassing, dealcoholization, cooling, filling carbon dioxide, post-modification, filtering, stabilizing, and filling to obtain the pilsner beer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The beer is prepared by using barley malt and water as main raw materials, and performing pulverization, saccharification, filtration, boiling, swirling precipitation, cooling and oxygenation, addition of yeast, fermentation, and filtration steps to prepare beer. The pilsner beer preheated at 40-43 degrees C is degassed by vacuum to a vacuum of 80-90 mbar until the carbon dioxide in the pilsner beer is completely discharged. The degassed beer is vacuum distilled at 40-43 degrees C under a vacuum degree of 80-90 mbar, until the ethanol content drops below 0.05 vol.%. The post-modification step involves adding 0.05-0.2%, preferably 0.1% natural beer flavors and 0.5-4.5 g/100 ml, preferably 1 g/100 ml natural sweeteners to the de-alcoholized beer. Preferred Conditions: The natural beer flavor and natural sweetener are added in an online manner. The raw material of the beer comprises pilsner malt, with light-colored charcoal malt, without using auxiliary materials such as rice and corn. The saccharification involves a two-stage saccharification, where the first stage is carried out at 62-65 degrees C for 30-60 minutes, the second stage is carried out at 71-73 degrees C for 10-30 minutes, and no exogenous enzyme preparation is added during the saccharification process. During the boiling process, hops or hop products are added, where the hops are selected for varieties with a humulone content of less than or equal to 30%. In the fermentation process, the number of yeasts in the fermentation tank is controlled to 15-18 million/ml, the fermentation is carried out at 8-11 degrees C, the reduction is carried out at 8-13 degrees C, and the cold storage is carried out for greater than or equal to 15 days. In the filtering process, a diatomite filtering method is adopted. Preferred Components: The natural sweetener is chosen from fructose syrup, maltose syrup, sucrose syrup, glucose syrup, and white granulated sugar.

PN CN110540905-A
TI Separating and extracting Croton essential oil in preparing medicine for treating tumour, preferably lung cancer, involves taking croton fruit, peeling off, obtaining Croton seed kernel, crushing, and processing.
AU NIU Q
   LIU C
   GE F
   YUAN K
   LI J
AE SHANDONG INST POMOLOGY (SDAC-C)
GA 2019A43726
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and extracting Croton essential oil involves taking Croton fruit, peeling off, obtaining seed kernel, crushing, and processing, adding kernel powder to the supercritical extraction container, inputting the supercritical carbon dioxide fluid into the extraction container under 25 Mpa extraction pressure at 35 degrees C temperature for 1 hour. The extracted carbon dioxide fluid enters the separator for separation. The flow of carbon dioxide is 2 L/minute, and the essential oil is discharged from the separator.
   USE - Method for separating and extracting Croton essential oil in preparing medicine for treating tumour, preferably lung cancer (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method can ensure that the effective components of obtained essential oil are retained as much as possible, the medicinal material resources are fully utilized, and the toxin is deactivated and the toxicity is reduced.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Conditions: Separating and extracting Croton essential oil involves crushing the fruit into 20-50 mesh particle size powder. The extraction time 1 hour is 30 minutes of dynamic extraction and 30 minutes of static extraction. The processing method involves adding silver Albizia julibrissin extract into croton kernel powder and freeze-drying. The reaction takes place in a humidity of 20-25% and a temperature of 40-50 degrees C for 2-3 hours, followed by treating the kernel powder after the reaction with 150-180 Mpa pressure for 5-8 minutes. The mass ratio of the croton kernel powder and Albizia julibrissin is 10:1-1.5. The Albizia julibrissin and water in 1:20 ratio are mixed uniformly, extracted at 80-90 degrees C temperature for 1-2 hours, concentrated, added with ethanol to adjust the ethanol degree to 75-80, left it overnight, centrifuged, and freeze-dried the precipitate, and added with water in 1:5 ratio to obtain the extract. The gas chromatography mass spectrometry analysis contains 36.73 wt.% 17-octadecanoic acid, 8.49 wt.% tetradecanoic acid, 8.17 wt.% methyl 17-octadecanoate, 6.45 wt.% n-hexadecanoic acid, 5.28 wt.% n-decanoic acid, 4.37 wt.% ethyl linoleate, and 4.19 wt.% isoamyl 9-octadecenoate. The gas chromatography mass spectrometry analysis is performed on an Agilent 6890B GC and a 5977A mass selective detector (MSD, Agilent, SantaClara, CA, USA), HP-5MS capillary column (30m /asterisk 0.25mm, 0.25 microns film thickness, Restek Corporation, Bellefonte, PA, USA). Helium is used as a carrier gas at a rate of 1 mL/minute, with the temperature program such as 100 degrees C, initial temperature, rise to 200 degrees C at a rate of 6 degrees C/minute, and then rise to 300 degrees C at 4 degrees C/minute and hold for 10 minutes. The sample (1 micro-liter) is injected at a temperature of 280 degrees C and the split ratio is 1/70 for 1 minute. An electron impact ionization system with an ionization energy of 70eV and an electron ionization spectrum with a mass scanning range of 30-500 m/z.
   ACTIVITY - Cytostatic.
DC B07 (General - tablets, dispensers, catheters (excluding drainage and angioplasty), encapsulation etc, but not systems for administration of blood or saline or IV feeding etc.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN CN110404545-A; CN110404545-B
TI Preparation of methane-carbon dioxide reforming synthesis gas nickel-based catalyst by preparing nickel-based organic framework material, adding to tetraethyl orthosilicate alcohol solution, shaking, standing, calcining solid, and grinding.
AU REN H
   MA Q
   ZHAO Y
   LV Z
   XU J
AE UNIV XIJING (UNXJ-C)
   UNIV XIJING (UNXJ-C)
GA 201994851D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of methane-carbon dioxide reforming synthesis gas nickel-based catalyst comprises dissolving trimesic acid and nickel nitrate in ethanol solution at 80-180 degrees C to obtain nickel-based organic framework material, adding to tetraethyl orthosilicate alcohol solution, shaking, standing, calcining solid at 400-700 degrees C, grinding, and sieving through 40-60 mesh sieve. The molar ratio of trimesic acid and nickel nitrate is 1-3:1. The mass ratio of tetraethyl orthosilicate and nickel-based organic framework material is 0.40-2.34:1.
   USE - The method is for preparation of methane-carbon dioxide reforming synthesis gas nickel-based catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst can improve the activity of methane reforming reaction, and has high activity and high stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alcohol solution is ethanol. The ratio of tetraethyl orthosilicate and alcohol solution is 0.20-1.17 g:1 mL. Preferred Method: The reaction is done for 12-72 hours. The method further comprises cooling reaction solution, centrifuging to separate the solid, washing, drying to 80-120 degrees C and grinding to obtain nickel-based organic framework material. The shaking is done for 30 minutes. The standing is done for 10-48 hours, and drying to 60-120 degrees C. The calcining is done at 400-700 degrees C and at heating rate of 5 degrees C/minute for 3-6 hours.

PN CN110078702-A; CN110078702-B
TI Preparing cyclic carbonate involves carbonylating carbon dioxide with diol compound under catalysis of catalyst to obtain cyclic carbonate, where catalyst comprises imidazole-type polyionic liquid framework.
AU CHENG W
   LIU Y
   SHI Z
   SU Q
   DONG L
   ZHANG S
AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 201969226V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a cyclic carbonate involves carbonylating a carbon dioxide with a diol compound under the catalysis of a catalyst to obtain a cyclic carbonate. The catalyst comprises an imidazole-type polyionic liquid framework. The polyionic liquid frame is represented by (I).
   USE - Method for preparing cyclic carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare cyclic carbonate has double active components, multipleactive sites, high catalyzing efficiency, simplepreparation process, stable and not easy todecompose, separated from the liquid phase and soon, and has high industrial application value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing a cyclic carbonate involves carbonylating a carbon dioxide with a diol compound under the catalysis of a catalyst to obtain a cyclic carbonate. The catalyst comprises an imidazole-type polyionic liquid framework. The polyionic liquid frame is represented by formula (I)..
   X-=anion of the polyionic liquid framework and Br-, OH- or CO32-.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reaction temperature of the carbonylation reaction is preferably 80-180 degrees C. The reaction pressure of the carbonylation reaction is 2-10 mega Pascal. The reaction time of the carbonylation reaction is 6-24 hours. Preferred Compositions: The mass ratio of the diol reactant and the catalyst in the carbonylation reaction is 1:1-10. Preferred Components: The solvent for the carbonylation reaction is acetonitrile, methanol or N,N-dimethylformamide. The diol compound is ethylene glycol or 1,2-propanediol. Preferred Process: The preparation method further involves adding a certain amount of triimidazolyl benzene monomer and tribromomethyl monomer in a 25 milliliter Schlenck reaction flask, adding a certain amount of ethanol solvent, and protect with nitrogen and stirring at 80 degrees C for 24 hours, after the completion of the reaction, a large amount of a white solid. The solid product is repeatedly washed three times with an ethanol solvent, and then the resultant product was vacuum-dried at room temperature for 12 hours to obtain a white powdery substance which is a polyionic liquid frame. The glycol compound, a polyionic liquid framework catalyst, and a solvent are mixed in a closed high pressure reactor to uniformly mix. The reaction kettle is filled with carbon dioxide gas. The pressure in the reaction system in the autoclave is in the range of 2-10 mega Pascal, and the temperature of the reaction vessel is maintained in the range of 80-180 degrees C, and the carbonylation reaction is carried out for 6-24 hours to obtain a cyclic carbonate.

PT P
PN CN109579032-A; CN109579032-B
TI Catalyzing complete combustion of carbon monoxide by two-component copper-based catalyst comprises e.g. catalytic elimination of carbon monoxide by two-component copper-based catalyst in the form of spheres and oxidizing to carbon dioxide.
AU ZHANG H
   CHEN J
   SHEN Y
   XIONG K
   LI X
AE UNIV CHONGQING TECHNOLOGY & BUSINESS (UYSS-C)
   UNIV CHONGQING TECHNOLOGY & BUSINESS (UYSS-C)
GA 201933394G
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyzing complete combustion of carbon monoxide by two-component copper-based catalyst comprises catalytic elimination of carbon monoxide using two-component copper-based catalyst in the form of spheres containing both copper and manganese components, under the condition that oxygen as an oxidant accounts for 1% of the total amount of the mixed gas, allowing two-component copper-based catalyst carbon monoxide to oxidizing to carbon dioxide at 31 degrees C, converting to 50% at 75-80 degrees C and completely converting to carbon dioxide at 125-130 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for catalyzing complete combustion of carbon monoxide by two-component copper-based catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: eliminates carbon monoxide, and solves the problem of catalyzing the elimination of carbon monoxide by low concentration oxygen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The two-component copper-based catalyst catalyzes the gas in the carbon monoxide reaction including carbon monoxide, oxygen and inert gas, and the molar ratio of carbon monoxide to oxygen is 1:1. The total number of moles of carbon monoxide and oxygen is 2% of the total moles of the reaction gas, and the total mole of inert gas is 98% of the total moles of reaction gas. In the step (1), the molar ratio of copper to manganese is 2:1, and the catalyst exhibits a spherical morphology with a particle size of 50-100 nm. The copper-containing species containing in the obtained catalyst are all highly active species which can be reduced to 300-350 degrees C. Preferred Method: The copper-based catalyst is prepared by: (1) mixing copper(II) nitrate trihydrate, manganese(II) nitrate solution and distilled water to obtain mixed solution, where the copper and manganese molar ratio is 1.5-2:1, (2) adding water, cetyltrimethylammonium bromide and absolute ethanol to the mixed solution, stirring at room temperature for 15-30 minutes to obtain mixed liquid, where the stirring speed is 80-100 revolutions/minute, (3) adding aqueous ammonia to the mixed liquid, and then stirring at room temperature for 2 hours, where the stirring speed is 150-300 revolutions/minute, (4) transferring mixed liquid to a hydrothermal synthesis kettle, and treating at 120-125 degrees C for 24 hours, and (5) performing suction-filtration of mixed system after the hydrothermal treatment to obtain solid, washing solid with distilled water, then drying solid, placing dried solid in a muffle furnace and performing calcination at 550-560 degrees C for 4-5 hours to obtain a two-component copper-based catalyst. In the step (4), the treatment temperature is 120 degrees C.

TI Preparing chitosan-copper/titanium dioxide nano composite material useful in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide comprises e.g. stirring the chitosan, dispersing, then adding copper acetate and tetrabutyl titanate and stirring.
AU SHE H
   MA X
   BAI W
   SUN Y
   SU B
   WANG Q
   WANG L
   HUANG J
AE UNIV NORTHWEST NORMAL (UNWN-C)
   UNIV NORTHWEST NORMAL (UNWN-C)
GA 201895689U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing chitosan-copper/titanium dioxide nano composite material comprises stirring the chitosan, dispersing in ethanol, then adding copper acetate and tetrabutyl titanate, stirring for 1-2 hours, sonicating for 10-20 minutes, then adding glacial acetic acid, and reacting at 100-110 degrees C for 20-24 hours, cooling the reaction mixture to room temperature, washing it with alcohol and deionized water, drying and grinding.
   USE - The material is useful in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material utilizes hydrothermal method to successfully load the titanium-doped titanium dioxide onto the chitosan, chitosan and copper expands the range of visible light absorption of titanium dioxide, increases absorption intensity in the UV-visible range to 360-430 nm and yields higher methane in photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of chitosan to copper acetate is 1:0.01-1:0.025. The mass ratio of chitosan to tetrabutyl titanate is 1:0.25-1:0.75. The amount of glacial acetic acid added is 50-80% of the total mass of chitosan, copper acetate and tetrabutyl titanate. Preferred Method: The drying is carried out in an oven at 60-80 degrees C for 10-12 hours.

PN WO2018042452-A1; WO2018042452-A4; EP3507259-A1; PH12019550026-A1; IN201621029473-A; EP3507259-A4; ID201905719-A
TI Three-stage degassing process for removing dissolved carbon dioxide from biomethanated spent wash, involves preheating biomethanated spent wash in heat exchangers, and feeding products to low-temperature and high-temperature zones.
AU W OKE D
   OKE D W
AE MOJJ ENG SYSTEMS LTD (MOJJ-Non-standard)
   MOJJ ENG SYSTEMS LTD (MOJJ-Non-standard)
   MOJJ ENG SYSTEMS LTD (MOJJ-Non-standard)
GA 201819603L
AB    NOVELTY - Three-stage degassing process for removing dissolved carbon dioxide from biomethanated spent wash, involves (a) preheating the biomethanated spent wash in heat exchangers at 50-60 degrees C as in figure 1-4 of the patent specification, (b) feeding products of step (a) to low-temperature zone of column provided in figures 1-4 of the patent specification, and (c) feeding products of step (b) to high-temperature zone of column provided in figure 4 of the patent specification.
   USE - The three-stage degassing process is useful for removing dissolved carbon dioxide from the biomethanated spent wash.
   ADVANTAGE - The degassing process is simple, reliable, and cost-effective, environmentally-friendly, and reduces the energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a reactor with low and high temperature zones in one column.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The preheating of biomethanated spent wash is performed with heat of fresh raw spent wash, or optionally performed by other waste heat stream available in distillery. The temperature of low-temperature zone is 56-62 degrees C, and the pressure of low-temperature zone is 0.15-0.31 Bar. The temperature of high-temperature zone is 68-85 degrees C, and the pressure of high-temperature zone is 0.33-0.56 Bar. Preferred System: The degassing is performed in two stages in one column. The spent wash partly degasified in two stages can optionally be further flashed in a vessel with nozzles provided in figure 5 of the patent specification to generate a high surface area spray of the spent wash with a jet flow for complete removal of dissolved gases from biomethanated spent wash.
