PT P
PN CN112176137-A
TI Blast furnace iron-making method involves mixing steam and oxygen in blast furnace, preheating, replacing with humidification blast of waste heat steam of iron and steel plant, reacting steam and carbon, and reacting with ferric oxide.
AU CHEN D
   ZHANG J
   LI B
   LI X
   ZHANG X
   WANG S
   DU X
AE MA'ANSHAN IRON & STEEL CO LTD (MGJT-C)
GA 2021062772
AB    NOVELTY - Blast furnace iron-making method involves using a steam pipe network, an oxygen pipe network and a blower, mixing a steam and oxygen in blast furnace and preheating using flue gas of the hot blast stove to reduce condensation of the steam and contact with air to obtain a high-temperature hot blast, passing high-temperature hot blast into a blast furnace from tuyere, replacing the blast furnace with a humidification blast of waste heat steam of iron and steel plant, reacting steam and carbon in the blast furnace tuyere cyclone zone to absorb heat and to produce carbon monoxide and hydrogen, reacting oxygen in air with carbon to produce carbon monoxide, in which the amount of gas is 1.21 time that of air, reacting ferric oxide with carbon monoxide to produce iron(II,III) oxide and carbon dioxide, reacting iron(II,III) oxide with carbon monoxide to produce ferrous oxide and carbon dioxide, and reacting ferrous oxide with carbon monoxide to produce iron and carbon dioxide.
   USE - Blast furnace iron-making method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables economical blast furnace iron-making with improved waste heat steam utilization factor in short time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Blast furnace iron-making method involves using a steam pipe network, an oxygen pipe network and a blower, in which the steam pipe network and the oxygen pipe network are respectively connected to a flue gas heat exchanger through an automatic valve and an electronic flow meter, and the blower is connected to a hot blast stove through automatic valve and electronic flow meter, mixing a steam and oxygen in blast furnace and preheating using flue gas of the hot blast stove to reduce condensation of the steam and contact with air to obtain a high-temperature hot blast, passing high-temperature hot blast into a blast furnace from tuyere, replacing the blast furnace with a humidification blast of waste heat steam of iron and steel plant, which can effectively utilize heat of vaporization of waste heat steam, in which moisture of the humidification blast needs to absorb heat to become steam and the heat of vaporization is 2260 kJ/kg, using steam used to partially replace air and oxygen, which reduces the amount of hot air that needs to be heated in the hot blast stove and reduces the energy consumption of the hot air process per ton of iron, under the conditions of specific heat of steam is 1.1 time that of air, reacting steam and carbon in the blast furnace tuyere cyclone zone to absorb heat and to produce carbon monoxide and hydrogen, reacting oxygen in air with carbon to produce carbon monoxide, in which the amount of gas is 1.21 time that of air, reacting ferric oxide with carbon monoxide to produce iron(II,III) oxide and carbon dioxide, reacting iron(II,III) oxide with carbon monoxide to produce ferrous oxide and carbon dioxide, reacting ferrous oxide with carbon monoxide to produce iron and carbon dioxide, reacting ferric oxide with hydrogen to produce iron(II,III) oxide and water, reacting iron(II,III) oxide with hydrogen to produce ferrous oxide and water, and reacting ferrous oxide with hydrogen at more than 810 degrees C to produce iron and water. The steam injected into the blast furnace has a strong ability to react and absorb heat in the tuyere cyclone zone and to heat the gas with gas. In order to ensure the upper temperature of the furnace gas supply, the oxygen enrichment is required to be more than 3 times that of usual air. According to the furnace gas volume, the theoretical combustion temperature is increased to create desired conditions for the large oxygen-enriched injection of pulverized coal. The utilization rate of hydrogen is higher than utilization rate of carbon monoxide in the lower portion of the blast furnace. The fork curve temperature of hydrogen is higher than fork curve temperature of carbon monoxide in the lower portion of the blast furnace. After the air is added with steam and oxygen enrichment, the total amount of hot air per ton of iron is reduced, and the total amount of blast furnace dust carried by the top gas is reduced. At the same time, the steam in the top gas has a strong ability to bind dust, the blast furnace gravity dust removal ash volume increases, and the blast furnace bag dust removal ash volume decreases. After top-pressure recovery turbine residual pressure power generation, temperature and pressure drops to remove water than carbon monoxide and nitrogen, and the heating value of blast furnace gas increases, and the hot air temperature of hot blast stove heated by blast furnace gas increases. When the conversion rate is 0-0.5 at 1000 degrees C, the average reaction rate of hydrogen is 1.460x 10-4 mol/second, the average reaction rate of carbon monoxide is 2.944x 10-5 mol/second, and the reaction rate of hydrogen is more than 5 times that of carbon monoxide. When the conversion rate is 0-0.5 at 950 degrees C, the average reaction rate of hydrogen is 1.264x 10-4 mol/second, the average reaction rate of carbon monoxide is 2.851x 10-5 mol/second, and the reaction rate of hydrogen is more than 4.5 times that of carbon monoxide.
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).); Q77 (Furnaces, kilns, ovens, retorts (F27))
MC M24-A02; M24-A05D; M24-A05E1; M24-B01A; M24-D02; M24-D03; M24-D04; Q77-A01

TI Composition for capturing carbon dioxide comprises calcium aluminate and calcium ferrite, produced by forming clinker by mixing waste alumina powder, magnetite fine powder, and limestone sludge with solvent, molding, drying and calcining.
AU SONGMYONGSHIN
   LEE W G
   KIM K
AE KNU IND COOP FOUND (UKAN-C)
GA 202234560N
AB    NOVELTY - Composition comprises more than 50 wt.% calcium aluminate (CaO-Al2O3) and calcium ferrite (CaO-Fe2O3).
   USE - The composition is useful for capturing carbon dioxide (claimed), and in cement products e.g. bricks, interlocking blocks, and boundary stones.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition can efficiently capture carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide emitted through various routes by carbonating carbon dioxide in the atmosphere, can exhibit an effect of preventing global warming, is environmentally-friendly and economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing a composition, comprising (i) forming a clinker by mixing a mixture of 15-40 wt.% waste alumina powder, 30-50 wt.% magnetite fine powder, and 10-35 wt.% limestone sludge with a solvent, and molding; (ii) drying the clinker obtained in step (i) and then calcining at 800-1100 degrees C; and (iii) recovering the calcined product obtained in step (ii) by cooling.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The composition is in the form of a powder having a powder degree of 3000-4000 cm2/g. The waste alumina powder comprises 60-90 wt.% aluminum oxide based on the total weight of the waste alumina powder, and has a fineness of 1000-2000 cm2/g. The magnetite fine powder comprises 60-90 wt.% iron oxide based on the total weight of the magnetite fine powder, and has a fineness of 1000-2000 cm2/g. The limestone sludge comprises 30-50 wt.% calcium oxide based on the total weight of the limestone sludge. The size of clinker in step (i) is 5-20 mm. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), rate of temperature increase during firing is 1-30 degrees C/minute. In the step (iii), cooling is performed by naturally cooling to 600-700 degrees C, then rapidly cooling to atmospheric temperature within 3-30 minutes. The method comprises (iv) pulverizing the calcined product recovered in step (iii).
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of a composition preparation that do not support the claims.

   USE - Integrated method of self-generated                electricity for decarbonization, denitrification,                energy saving and emission reduction of carbon                dioxide and nitrogen oxide.

Preferred                  Composition: The mass of surfactant is 2-7 wt.%                  of mass of the filtrate.

PN CN112121805-A
TI Preparing catalyst used for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to synthesize methanol, involves adding alcohol solvent to copper, zinc, zirconium salt, stirring, performing solvothermal reaction, filtering, drying, calcining, and grinding.
AU LIANG Y
   MAO D
   GUO X
   YU J
   WU G
AE SHANGHAI INST TECHNOLOGY (SHGH-C)
GA 2021015146
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to synthesize methanol involves adding alcohol solvent to copper salt, zinc salt and zirconium salt, stirring and ultrasonically disperse, and then transfer to reactor; heating reaction kettle and solvothermal reaction is carried out under sealed conditions, filtering obtained reaction product, drying, calcining, and grinding to obtain target product of carbon dioxide hydrogenation catalyst for methanol synthesis.
   USE - Method for preparing catalyst used for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to synthesize methanol (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method for preparing catalyst has small Cu crystallites and large metal Cu surface area, has strong interaction of Cu, Zn and Zr, and shows high methanol yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to synthesize methanol, which is copper oxide (CuO)-zinc oxide (ZnO)-zirconium oxide (ZrO2) catalyst, which is specifically prepared by method for preparing catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The copper salt is hydrated copper nitrate (Cu(NO3)2x 3H2O), the zinc salt is hydrated zinc nitrate (Zn(NO3)2x 6H2O), and the zirconium salt is hydrous zirconium nitrate (Zr(NO3)4x 5H2O). Preferred Conditions: The added amount of copper (Cu) salt, zinc (Zn) salt and zirconium (Zr) salt satisfies molar ratio of 4-6:1-3:2-4. The stirring is 10-30 minutes at room temperature, and the ultrasonic dispersion time is 10-30 minutes. The three salts of Cu, Zn, and Zr are prepared with alcohol solvents to form solution with total ion concentration of 0.5-2 molar/L. The temperature of solvothermal reaction is 140-240 degrees C, and the time is 1-12 hours. The temperature of drying process is 110-130 degrees C, and the time is 4-24 hours. The temperature of calcination process is 350-550 degrees C, and the time is 2-12 hours. Preferred Method: The method for applying catalyst to reaction of carbon dioxide hydrogenation to synthesize methanol involves adding obtained catalyst to reactor, and then the volume ratio 1-3:10 of hydrogen and mixed gas of nitrogen is reduced at 250-350 degrees C for 2-10 hours before carbon dioxide hydrogenation reaction, and then mixed gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen with volume ratio of 1:3-5 is introduced, and carried out at 200-280 degrees C and 2-5 MegaPascal hydrogenation reaction, where space velocity of mixed gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen is 1000-10000 mL/gx hour.

TI Preparing molybdenum nanosphere co-catalyst useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to methane comprises e.g. adding molybdenum powder, and hydrogen peroxide, reacting, performing high temperature reaction and purification.
AU ZENG Y
   HUANG S
   LONG Y
   LIANG H
AE UNIV SHENZHEN (UYSZ-C)
GA 2020154863
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing molybdenum nanosphere co-catalyst comprises adding molybdenum powder to reaction kettle, adding hydrogen peroxide to reaction kettle, carrying out oxidation reaction, and then adding ethanol to obtain solution A, adding photocatalyst to solution A, stirring, performing high temperature reaction in reaction kettle for period of time, and performing purification process to obtain molybdenum nanosphere cocatalyst supported on photocatalyst surface.
   USE - The co-catalyst is useful for photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The co-catalyst: contains molybdenum nanosphere, which improves material's ability to adsorb carbon dioxide, promotes reaction between photo-generated electrons, holes, and carbon dioxide, improves lifetime of photo-generated electrons and holes, increases utilization rate of electrons and holes, broadens spectral response capability, and ensures efficiacy of photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide to methane.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for molybdenum nanosphere co-catalyst, prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The photocatalyst is a graphite phase carbon nitride photocatalyst. The mass ratio of molybdenum powder to photocatalyst is 1:1-1:30. Preferred Method: In the method, carrying out oxidation reaction at 20-30 degrees C for 20-40 minutes. In the method, performing high-temperature reaction in reaction kettle at 120-160 degrees C for 10-12 hours. In the method, purification specifically comprises natural cooling, centrifuging, removing precipitate after completing centrifugation, and placing precipitate in vacuum drying box to dry.

PT P
PN CN110540371-A
TI Performing carbon dioxide laser scanning method for atomic level defect repair of optical element film, involves scanning surface of clean optical element film by using carbon dioxide laser with critical energy density.
AU LI X
   LU G
   ZHI X
   WANG D
   WANG K
   YUAN S
   ZHOU Q
   PENG Y
AE UNIV NANJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNSC-C)
GA 2019A50392
AB    NOVELTY - Performing carbon dioxide (CO2) laser scanning method involves scanning the surface of a clean optical element film by using a CO2 laser with a critical energy density. The atomic-level defects are repaired inside the optical element film and its interface with the substrate under the action of local heat to improve the bonding force between the film and the substrate. The critical energy density is higher than the electron transition of the defect structure and lower than the surface topography deformation energy.
   USE - Method for performing carbon dioxide laser scanning method used for atomic level defect repair of optical element film.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform carbon dioxide laser scanning method, that uses low-power laser energy for repair, does not cause damage to the material, and does not cause changes to the original material surface morphology.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method for performing CO2 laser repair, which involves cleaning the optical glass in one direction with a dust-free cloth dipped in the cleaning solution in a class 10,000 clean room until the naked eye cannot find the dust. The hair dryer is used at normal temperature to blow the cleaned surface until it is dry. The vacuum adsorption is used to fix the dried optical glass on the CO2 laser processing platform to ensure that the surface to be repaired is facing the laser direction. The laser light source is turned on, adjusted the laser power, laser spot diameter and repetition frequency, import the trace pattern of the laser scan, locate the scan starting point, and started the CO2 laser irradiation repair. The laser power is reduced , the other parameters remain unchanged, the trajectory pattern of the laser scan does not change, the original scan start point is positioned as the new scan start point, and the second irradiation repair is performed. Preferred Components: The optical glass includes fused silica glass, K9 or BK7. The material of the optical glass deposition layer includes silicon oxide (SiO2), hafnium oxide (HfO2) or aluminum oxide (Al2O3), and the thickness of the deposition layer is 10-1000 nanometer. Preferred Conditions: The defects of the optical glass deposition layer refer to point defects generated during the deposition process of the optical glass deposition layer, and include an E 'center, an oxygen center, a hydroxyl group, or a peroxy group. The vacuum adsorption refers to the use of a table with a vacuum adsorption function to adsorb optical glass, the degree of vacuum is 20-60 kilopascal. The relevant parameters of the CO2 laser are a wavelength of 10.6 micrometer, a power of 4-20 watt, a frequency of 1-200 kilohertz, a pulse width of 2-1000 microsecond, and a spot diameter of 1-5 mm.
DC L01 (Glass - includes chemical compositions, batch treatment, furnaces, flat glass forming, hollow-ware forming, postforming and glass/ceramics, but not lens designs, bottling, bottle-washing, closures for containers, glazing designs, glass cutting, chamfering edges, printing on glass, disposing of used glass or the production of pure sodium silicate. Chemical aspects of optical fibres (C03).); P62 (Hand tools, cutting (B25, B26).); S06 (Electrophotography and Photography); V08 (Lasers and Masers)

PT P
PN US2019329076-A1; US11305135-B2
TI Method for converting post-explosion gases to breathable mixture, involves receiving flow of post-explosion gas with oxygen, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, nitrogen, and methane, followed by metering stored nitrogen and stored oxygen.
AU GIVENS R
   KLEIN J A
   MCDORMAN D L
   REUTHER J J
   SEIDT J D
AE GIVENS R (GIVE-Individual)
   KLEIN J A (KLEI-Individual)
   MCDORMAN D L (MCDO-Individual)
   REUTHER J J (REUT-Individual)
   SEIDT J D (SEID-Individual)
GA 2019911740
AB    NOVELTY - A post-explosion gases converting method involves receiving a flow of post-explosion gas with oxygen, carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide, nitrogen, and methane, and removing oxygen, carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide from the flow of post-explosion gas to obtain a mixture comprising oxygen, carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide, and a residual stream comprising nitrogen and methane, removing the oxygen from the mixture of oxygen, carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide, concentrating the oxygen in a primary oxygen storage canister, removing the nitrogen from the residual stream, storing the nitrogen in a nitrogen storage canister separate from the primary oxygen storage canister, venting the methane back to the inhabitable level, low-oxygen ambient environment, and metering the stored nitrogen and the stored oxygen to a user through a breathing mask at a habitable level of 19-21% oxygen.
   USE - Method for converting post-explosion gases to breathable mixture.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further involves before removing oxygen from the mixture of oxygen, carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide, contaminating gases poison or greatly reduce a viable operating time of the electrolytic sieve, supplying oxygen from the primary oxygen storage canister to a catalyst bed, preventing contents from the catalyst bed from entering the primary oxygen storage canister, and catalyzing carbon monoxide molecules to form carbon dioxide molecules via the catalyst bed to create a flow of gases, absorbing carbon dioxide from the flow of gases via ionic liquid cells, recycling exhaled oxygen from the breathing mask via a loop-back channel, supplementing oxygen to the breathing mask from second oxygen storage canister, changing a flow rate of the process based on a variable flow pump, measuring pressure of the breathing mask via a pressure sensor metering the stored nitrogen and the stored oxygen to a user through a breathing mask at a habitable level of 19-21% oxygen based on the pressure measured by the pressure sensor.

   M = trivalent cation of indium, vanadium or                aluminum,

   The material belongs to the hexagonal crystal                system and the space group is P6222.

   preparing (I); and
   carbon dioxide/nitrogen separation comprising                vacuum treating after the high-valence                metal-organic framework material reaches adsorption                saturation to make it desorb not higher than                100degrees Celsius and reusing the high-valence                metal-organic framework material after the                desorption.

   (2)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): (I) is prepared by                  (i) adding the metal salt corresponding to the M                  element and H TCPB into the organic solvent,                  adding the regulator acid after complete                  dissolution and mixing uniformly to obtain a                  reaction mixture solution, where, the regulator                  acid comprises hydrochloric acid, nitric acid,                  formic acid, acetic acid or trifluoro Acetic acid                  and the molar ratio of metal salt corresponding                  to M element, H4TCPB, regulator acid and organic                  solvent is 1:1:(0.01-0.05):2; and (ii) carrying                  out reaction mixture solution, and solvothermal                  synthesis reaction at 100-160 degrees Celsius for                  48-96 hours, adopting organic solvent to carry                  out cleaning, after the reaction finishes, drying                  n+and obtain described high valence state Metal                  organic framework material.Preferred Components: The metal salt                  corresponding to the M element is indium(III)                  nitrate tetrahydrate, aluminum nitrate tetra                  hydrate or vanadium chloride. In the step (i),                  the organic solvent is N,N'-dimethylformamide or                  N,N'-dimethylacetamide. In the step (ii), the                  organic solvent comprises mixed solvent of                  N,N'-dimethylformamide and methanol, the mixed                  solvent of N,N'-dimethylformamide and ethanol,                  N,N'-dimethylformamide, mixed solvent of amide                  and methanol or a mixed solvent of                  N,N'-dimethylacetamide and ethanol. In the mixed                  solvent, the mass percentage content of methanol                  or ethanol is 25-50%.

PN CN112798538-A; CN214400523-U
TI Method for judging presence of bacteria in blood,            involves measuring primary harmonic signal of carbon            dioxide, and judging whether blood in culture bottle            contains bacteria according to relationship between            peak value.
AU SHAO J
   LU J
   YAO M
   XU J
   CHEN X
AE RUIPU MEDICAL EQUIP DONGGUAN CO LTD (RUIP-Non-standard)
GA 202156607P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves measuring primary harmonic                signal of carbon dioxide with standard                concentration. The frequency at the primary                harmonic signal central point of the carbon dioxide                is locked and fed back to the laser controller so                that the laser emits laser with fixed frequency.                The laser of the fixed frequency passes through the                carbon dioxide gas above the culture liquid to                attenuate the light intensity. The second harmonic                peak value is obtained after attenuation. The                determination is made whether the blood in the                culture bottle contains bacteria according to the                relationship between the second harmonic peak value                and the carbon dioxide concentration.
   USE - The method is useful for judging whether the                bacteria in blood.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has shorter judging time, higher                detecting precision, reduces the detecting cost and                better protecting environment.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                method for judging presence of bacteria in                blood.

TI New molecular crystalline catalyst, useful for            cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxy            compounds under normal temperature and pressure.
AU ZOU J
   LI L
   YOU S
   CHEN K
AE JIANGXI ACAD SCI APPLIED CHEM INST (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020A6735K
AB    NOVELTY - Molecular crystalline catalyst comprising                ((Zn2(btca)2(ttzNH2)).3solvents)n, where the btca2-                is the divalent anion of 5-carboxylic acid                benzotriazole, ttz-NH2 is 5-aminotetrazolium,                3solvents are 3 solvent molecules, and n is natural                number from 1-positive infinity, is new.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for cycloaddition                reaction of carbon dioxide and epoxy compounds                under normal temperature and pressure                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: has simple preparation process,                high catalytic activity, good selectivity, good                stability, and has great potential application                value in the cycloaddition reaction of carbon                dioxide and epoxy compounds.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing the molecular crystalline                catalyst; and
   (2) cycloaddition reaction of carbon dioxide                and epoxy compounds comprising adding 20 mmol epoxy                compound to Schlenk reaction tube, then adding 5 mg                molecular crystalline catalyst and 0.2 mmol                tetrabutylamine bromide, passing in 1 atm carbon                dioxide gas and reacting at room temperature for 48                hours to obtain the product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The molecular                  crystalline catalyst is prepared by (a)                  dissolving the organic ligands H2btca, ttz-NH2                  and zinc nitrate hexahydrate into a mixed solvent                  of N,N-dimethylformamide, deionized water and                  ethanol to obtain a mixed solution, where the                  molar ratio of H2btca, ttz-NH2, zinc nitrate                  hexahydrate, N,N-dimethylformamide, water and                  ethanol is 1:1:2:322.6:1667:100, and (b) adding                  the above mixed solution in a closed hydrothermal                  reactor, reacting at a constant temperature of                  130 degrees C for 72 hours, taking out the                  product, separating the solid, and washing the                  solid with N,N-dimethylformamide for multiple                  times to obtain yellow block crystals. Preferred                  Components: The molecular crystalline catalyst                  belongs to the orthorhombic system, having a                  space group of Pnma, and unit cell parameters of                  alpha is 90 degrees , beta is 90 degrees , and                  gamma is 90 degrees , and the crystal volume is Z                  is equal to 4. The catalyst has 1 zinc (Zn2+)                  ion, 1 btca2-ligand, 0.5 ttz-NH2 in the smallest                  asymmetric structural unit, where the zinc (Zn2+)                  ion uses four-coordinated tetrahedral                  coordination mode, which respectively interacts                  with one oxygen atom and one oxygen atom from the                  three btca2- ligands, the two nitrogen atoms and                  one nitrogen atom of one ttz-NH2 are coordinated,                  and the adjacent zinc (Zn2+) ions first form a                  two-dimensional stepped structure through btca2-                  ligand, and then forming three-dimensional                  network structure through ttz-NH2 ligand, and the                  molecular crystalline catalyst presents a regular                  hexagonal pore structure in the direction, and                  its pore size is 16.42x 8.68 Angstrom .

AU DENG D

   CHENG K

   BAO X
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202018858L
AB    NOVELTY - Use of molybdenum disulfide catalyst in carbon dioxide hydrogenation for synthesizing methanol, is claimed, where the molybdenum disulfide catalyst is a supported or unsupported type.
   USE - The molybdenum disulfide catalyst is useful for synthesizing methanol from carbon dioxide hydrogenation.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst solves the problems of low activity and selectivity of the catalyst for carbon dioxide hydrogenation to methanol, is economical, easy to operate, high in carbon dioxide conversion rate, high in methanol selectivity in the product and excellent in stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the molybdenum disulfide catalyst, involving (i) directly mixing molybdenum source with sulfur source or mixing with carrier, dissolving in water, sealing in an autoclave under the protection of an inert gas and heating in a heating device at 180-800 degrees C for 1-15 hours to obtain product, and (ii) washing the obtained product with an alkaline solution to remove residual sulfur and unreacted raw material, washing with deionized water and drying to obtain the finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The reaction pressure of carbon dioxide hydrogenation is 1-100 bar, the reaction temperature is 60-300 degrees C, the volume ratio of hydrogen and carbon dioxide is 1:9-9:1, and the space velocity of the reaction gas is 100-100000 ml.hour-1.g-1. In step (i), the heating rate of the programmed heating is 0.5-30 degrees C/minute. The drying is carried out by vacuum-drying or atmospheric pressure drying at 30-120 degrees C for 6-12 hours. Preferred Components: In step (i), the molybdenum source comprises molybdenum trioxide, molybdenum dioxide, molybdenum blue, hexacarbonyl molybdenum, molybdenum acetate, ethanol molybdenum, molybdenum oxalate, molybdenum bromide, molybdenum acetylacetonate, sodium molybdate, potassium molybdate, ammonium molybdate, ammonium phosphomolybdate, phosphomolybdic acid and/or tetrathiomolybdate. In step (i), the sulfur source comprises sulfur, thiophene, thiourea, dimethyl sulfoxide, thioacetamide, butanethiol, ammonium thiocyanate, potassium thiocyanate, carbon disulfide, sodium thiocyanate, sodium sulfide, potassium sulfide, tetrathiomolybdate and hydrogen sulfide. The inert gas is nitrogen, helium or argon. In step (ii), the alkaline solution is potassium hydroxide solution, sodium hydroxide solution and aqueous ammonia. Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of the molybdenum atom and the sulfur atom in the molybdenum source and the sulfur source is 1:2-1:500.

PN CN110499144-A
TI Preparing energy-saving and high-effect air-conditioning refrigerant comprises e.g. taking carbon dioxide (R744), R404A, methane (R50), R-134a and ethylene, filling R404A into cleaned and dried steel bottle and filling carbon dioxide (R744).
AU MENG J
   SUN J
   CHENG Q
AE ZHUHAI KOMEG INTELLIGENT TECHNOLOGY CO (ZHUH-Non-standard)
GA 2019A4132E
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing energy-saving and high-effect air-conditioning refrigerant comprises (i) taking 30-50 pts. wt. carbon dioxide (R744), 20-30 pts. wt. R404A, 10-20 pts. wt. methane (R50), 10-15 pts. wt. R-134a and 2-5 pts. wt. ethylene (R13); and (ii) filling R404A into the cleaned and dried steel bottle, filling with carbon dioxide (R744), stop filling after filling half of the required amount, recharging with methane (R50), filling it completely as needed, filling with R-134a and completely according to the required amount, filling final amount of ethylene (R13) and filling finally remaining carbon dioxide (R744).
   USE - The method is useful for preparing energy-saving and high-effect air-conditioning refrigerant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes readily available raw materials; produces refrigerant which does not harm ozone and human body; and is suitable for large-scale promotion.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The weight of raw materials in step (i) preferably comprises 50 pts. wt. carbon dioxide (R744), 30 pts. wt. R404A, 20 pts. wt. methane (R50), 15 pts. wt. R-134a and 2 pts. wt. ethylene (R13). Preferred Method: The preparation of energy-saving and high-effect air-conditioning refrigerant comprises pouring steel bottle over after filling the raw materials, preferably placing mouth of the bottle facing down and bottom of the bottle facing up, and shaking steel bottle to mix the raw materials evenly. The method comprises cooling steel bottle and placing at room temperature at (25 degrees C) before filling the raw materials. The method comprises cleaning inner wall of the steel bottle using dry ice.

PT P
PN CN108655113-A
TI Oil film scrap cutting and crushing machine water-jet roller cleaning method, involves discharging liquid carbon dioxide into nozzle to rapidly and completely remove oil and waste material adhered on conveyor chain and cutting roller.
AU ZHANG J
   LIANG Y
   YU G
   LIU D
AE DONGYING JINDAO ENVIRONMENTAL ENG CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 201883711V
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves passing liquid carbon dioxide to gap between a conveying chain and a scraper and between a cutting roller and the scraper. The liquid carbon dioxide is discharged into a nozzle between the conveyor chain and the cutting roller to rapidly and completely remove oil and a waste material adhered on the conveyor chain and the cutting roller.
   USE - Oil film scrap cutting and crushing machine water-jet roller cleaning method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing liquid carbon dioxide with ultra-low viscosity, high fluidity and permeability to clean the conveyor chain and the cutting roller through a liquid carbon dioxide conveying pipe in a rapid manner so as to effectively maintain film scrap cutting and breaking function.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of an oil film scrap cutting and crushing machine water-jet roller.
DC P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)
MC P43-B99

PT P
PN WO2019093891-A1; US2019185422-A1; AU2018366077-A1; CA3082032-A1; IN202047018117-A; CN111295370-A; US10759745-B2; EP3707121-A1; US2020361858-A1; US10975022-B2; EP3707121-B1; CN111295370-B; AU2018366077-B2; CA3082032-C; MX386596-B; IN402932-B; IN202248043370-A; MY191504-A
TI Producing purified urea solution comprises e.g.            reacting carbon dioxide and ammonia under urea            synthesis conditions in urea synthesis reactor            operating at high pressure, to give urea synthesis            stream with urea, carbon dioxide and ammonia.
AU PUSTJENS R A M
   VAN DEN TILLAART J A
   VAN DEN TILLAART J A A
AE STAMICARBON BV (STAM-C)
   STAMICARBON BV (STAM-C)
   STAMICARBON BV (STAM-C)
   STAMICARBON BV (STAM-C)
GA 2019435332
AB    NOVELTY - Producing purified urea solution comprises:                e.g. (A) reacting carbon dioxide and ammonia under                urea synthesis conditions in urea synthesis reactor                operating at high pressure, to give urea synthesis                stream containing urea, carbon dioxide and ammonia,                and amount of carbamate; (B) expanding the urea                synthesis stream in recovery section thus reducing                the pressure, and heating at least part of the                expanded urea synthesis stream in at least one                dissociation unit at medium and/or low pressure, to                give an aqueous urea stream and a recovery section                vapor containing carbon dioxide and ammonia; and                (C) subjecting in a purification section at least                part of the aqueous urea stream to purification,                where the purification is stripping to remove                ammonia and/or the purification yields a urea                solution with an alkalinity as ammonia of less than                0.2 wt.% when at 32.5 wt.% urea, giving a purified                urea solution and a purification section off-gas                containing water and ammonia.
   USE - The process is useful for producing a purified                urea solution, which is useful in nitrogen oxide                (NOx) abatement, preferably as diesel exhaust fluid                (DEF) or dilution to DEF (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The process: improves purity of the urea                solution; does not involve stripping at high                pressure of the urea synthesis stream.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing a purified urea solution comprises:                (A) reacting carbon dioxide and ammonia under urea                synthesis conditions in a urea synthesis reactor                operating at high pressure, to give a urea                synthesis stream containing urea, carbon dioxide                and ammonia, and an amount of carbamate; (B)                expanding the urea synthesis stream in a recovery                section thus reducing the pressure, where the urea                synthesis stream that is expanded comprises at                least 90 wt.% of the amount of carbamate, and                heating at least part of the expanded urea                synthesis stream in at least one dissociation unit                at medium and/or low pressure, to give an aqueous                urea stream and a recovery section vapor containing                carbon dioxide and ammonia; (C) subjecting in a                purification section at least part of the aqueous                urea stream to purification, where the purification                is stripping to remove ammonia and/or the                purification yields a urea solution with an                alkalinity as ammonia of less than 0.2 wt.% when at                32.5 wt.% urea, giving a purified urea solution and                a purification section off-gas containing water and                ammonia; (D) optionally diluting at least part of                the purified urea solution and/or the aqueous urea                stream to be purified with water to obtain a target                concentration of urea; (E) where the purification                section off-gas is condensed to give purification                section condensate and the purification section                condensate is recycled to the urea synthesis                reactor. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                a plant for the production of a purified urea                solution which urea solution is preferably for use                in nitrogen oxide (NOx) abatement systems, as                diesel exhaust fluid (DEF) or dilution to DEF,                comprising (A1) a urea synthesis section comprising                a high pressure urea synthesis reactor having an                inlet for carbon dioxide and an inlet for ammonia,                where carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide are reacted                under urea synthesis conditions to form a urea                synthesis stream comprising urea, water, and                ammonium carbamate, (B1) a recovery section where                the urea synthesis stream is heated at reduced                pressure, giving an aqueous urea stream and a                recovery section vapor comprising carbon dioxide                and ammonia, and where the recovery section vapor                is condensed to give carbamate and ammonia,                preferably with separate condensers for ammonia and                for carbamate, (C1) a purification section where                the aqueous urea stream is stripped in a                purification section treatment unit to remove                ammonia, giving a purified urea stream and a                purification section off-gas, (D1) optionally a                urea dilution section, where the purified urea                stream and/or the aqueous urea stream is diluted                with water, and the plant comprises a recycle                conduit for carbamate from the recovery section to                the urea synthesis section and a separate second                recycle conduit for ammonia from the recovery                section to the urea synthesis section, the                purification section further comprises a                purification section condenser for condensing the                purification section off-gas to purification                section condensate, and where the plant comprises a                liquid flow connection for purification section                condensate from the purification section condenser                to the urea synthesis reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The purification                  section treatment unit comprises a low pressure                  steam stripper receiving the aqueous urea stream,                  where the steam stripper has an inlet for steam,                  and the purification section comprises an ejector                  for withdrawing gas from the purification                  section. The steam stripper comprises a cyclone                  separator at the top, a liquid distribution tray,                  and a packed bed, configured such that in                  operation a two phase fluid of expanded aqueous                  urea stream enters the cyclone separator, gas                  leaves at the top, liquid flows down from the                  cyclone separator on the liquid distribution                  tray, and the liquid distribution tray                  distributes the liquid over the packed bed. The                  recovery section further comprises a recovery                  section condenser for the recovery section vapor,                  where the plant comprises a liquid flow line from                  the purification section condenser to the                  recovery section condenser, and a liquid flow                  line from the recovery section condenser to the                  urea synthesis reactor. The urea synthesis                  reactor comprises a ferritic-austenitic duplex                  stainless steel. The plant does not include a                  waste water treatment section.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The purification section                  condensate is supplied by liquid flow to the urea                  synthesis reactor, and the purification is low                  pressure steam stripping and involves contacting                  the aqueous urea solution in counter current flow                  with steam at a pressure of less than 3 bar. The                  process comprises: (a) condensing the recovery                  section vapor to give a liquid carbamate recycle                  stream in a recovery section condenser, where the                  condenser operates at lower pressure than the                  urea synthesis reactor; (b) supplying the                  carbamate recycle stream to the urea synthesis                  reactor, preferably using a pump; (c) and                  supplying the purification section condensate to                  the recovery section condenser. The process                  further comprises a step of supplying                  non-condensed gases from the recovery section                  condenser, optionally through an additional                  condenser, to a scrubbing step with scrubbing                  liquid in a scrubber giving a scrubbed gas and a                  spent scrubbing liquid, where the process further                  comprises supplying a second off-gas from the                  purification section condenser to the scrubber,                  and preferably supplying part of the spent                  scrubbing liquid to the recovery section                  condenser, optionally through the additional                  condenser. The step (B) comprises (B1) expanding                  the urea synthesis stream to medium pressure,                  optionally gas/liquid separation, and heating at                  least part of the urea synthesis stream at medium                  pressure to give a medium pressure (MP) recovery                  section vapor and a MP aqueous urea solution, and                  (B2) expanding the MP aqueous urea solution to                  low pressure, and heating the expanded aqueous                  urea solution at low pressure to give an low                  pressure (LP) recovery section vapor and an LP                  aqueous urea solution, where at least 85% of the                  fresh carbon dioxide feed is added directly into                  the reactor. The step (C) comprises expanding the                  aqueous urea stream, preferably the second                  aqueous urea solution, to the operating pressure                  of the purification section, where the                  purification section operating pressure is                  preferably of 0.01-1.5 bar absolute, and the step                  (C) comprises steam stripping the expanded                  aqueous urea stream, the steam stripping involves                  direct injection of steam in the expanded aqueous                  urea stream. The steam stripping is carried out                  at 0.01-1.5 bar absolute in a stripping column                  with a cyclone separator at the top of the                  column, a liquid distribution tray and a packed                  bed, where gas is separated from the expanded                  aqueous urea solution stream in the cyclone                  separator, the gas exits through an outlet at the                  top, liquid flows down from the cyclone separator                  on the liquid distribution tray, and the liquid                  distribution tray distributes the liquid over the                  packed bed, and no passivation air is used and/or                  the carbon dioxide feed contains less than 0.05                  wt.% oxygen.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); E16 (Aliphatics - containing N and/or halogen.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

PN CN110105250-A; CN110105250-B
TI Producing isophorone diisocyanate, comprises carrying out coupling reaction of isophorone diamine and gaseous carbon dioxide and polyoxometallate catalyst.
AU YU H
   DAN D
   WU Z
   WEI Y
   HAN S
AE SHANGHAI INST TECHNOLOGY (SHGH-C)
GA 201970683J
AB    NOVELTY - Producing isophorone diisocyanate, comprises carrying out coupling reaction of isophorone diamine and gaseous carbon dioxide as raw material and polyoxometallate catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for producing isophorone diisocyanate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has low production cost; reduces production of three wastes; and is suitable for industrial production. The catalyst: has mild reaction conditions and high specific selectivity; and is environmentally friendly, recyclable and economical.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises adding isophorone diamine, polyoxometallate and solvent into a reaction vessel, mixing, then adding phenylsilane, acid-binding agent and dehydrating agent, uniformly mixing, and finally introducing gaseous carbon dioxide, reacting at 0-50 degrees C for 6-24 hours, then separating, and purifying to obtain final product. Preferred Components: The catalyst is iron, nickel, chromium, copper or zinc centered Anderson-type polyoxometallate, or iron, nickel, chromium, copper or zinc centered Anderson-type polyoxometallate modified with trialkoxy derivative as central metal. The acid-binding agent is pyridine or triethylamine. The dehydrating agent is dimethyl sulfoxide or phosphorus oxychloride. The solvent is 1,3-dimethyl-2-imidazolidinone or toluene. The molar ratio between polyoxometallate, isophorone diamine, phenylsilane, acid-binding agent and dehydrating agent is 0.02:2:4:1:2, and the weight ratio between the solvent and isophorone diamine is 3 ml/mmol. Preferred Method: The reaction temperature is 0-030 degrees C, preferably 0-30 degrees C, and the reaction time is 6-12 hours, preferably 12 hours. The method comprises recycling polyoxometallate catalyst using organic solvent.

PT P
PN FR3065453-A1; WO2018197573-A1
TI Preparing mesoporous silica from a hybrid silica/polyalkylene oxide material, comprises at least one step of supercritical carbon dioxide treatment of the material.
AU PROUZET E
   AYMONIER C
AE CENT NAT RECH SCI (CNRS-C)
GA 2018832668
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing mesoporous silica from a hybrid silica/polyalkylene oxide material, comprises at least one step of supercritical carbon dioxide treatment of the material.
   USE - The process is useful for preparing mesoporous silica from a hybrid silica/polyalkylene oxide material.
   ADVANTAGE - The process exhibits enhanced the mechanical and chemical resistance of the silica, to remove residual traces of organic compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The treatment with supercritical carbon dioxide is: carried out under the following conditions i.e. temperature of 20-200 degrees C, pressure of 1x 105-300x 105 Pa, and treatment duration of 5-100 minutes; and carried out under the following conditions i.e. temperature of 35-50 degrees C, pressure of 50x 105 Pa to 150x 105 Pa, or treatment duration of 10-60 minutes. In the process, before the supercritical carbon dioxide treatment, the hybrid silica/polyalkylene oxide material is placed in a solvent e.g. ethanol, and subjected to sonication. The process further comprises, after the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, a heat treatment step at a temperature of 80-500 degrees C. The step of synthesis of the silica/polyalkylene oxide hybrid material comprises: contacting at least one silica precursor including alkoxysilanes, with at least one alkylene polyoxide in an aqueous medium at pH of 1-3; and addition in the reaction medium of a fluoride salt. Preferred Components: The silica/polyalkyleneoxide hybrid material is in powder form. The polyalkylene oxide includes: ethylene polyoxides, propylene polyoxides, copolymers of ethylene oxide and propylene oxide comprising 2-500 alkylene oxide units, alkyl esters of 10-30C carboxylic acid and 2-3C alkylene oxide, 10-30C alkyl ethers or 2-3C alkylene polyoxides comprising 2-500 alkylene oxide units; or polyethylene oxide and propylene oxide block copolymers comprising 10-200 alkylene oxide units, or 10-24C alcohol ethers and polyethylene oxide comprising 4-10 alkylene oxide units.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Flow cell electrolyzer for electrochemically            reducing gas reactant, comprises carbon dioxide,            passing onto or through gas diffusion electrode, into            gaseous carbon monoxide with anodic compartment            comprising anode with current collector.

   ROBERT M
AE UNIV PARIS (UYPA-Non-standard)

GA 2021106630
AB    NOVELTY - Flow cell electrolyzer (1) comprises carbon                dioxide, passing onto or through a gas diffusion                electrode (9), into gaseous carbon monoxide with an                anodic compartment comprising anode (2) with                current collector, anodic electrolyte solution (3),                at a controlled flow rate. The current collector                comprises water as solvent, anodic electrolyte,                anodic electrolyte solution inlet (4) and an anodic                electrolyte solution outlet (5) connected to the                anodic compartment, to circulate the anodic                electrolyte solution. The electrolyzer also                comprises cathodic compartment comprising cathodic                electrolyte solution (6), at a controlled flow rate                comprising a solvent, and cathodic electrolyte; and                gas diffusion electrode. The cathodic electrolyte                solution inlet (7) and cathodic electrolyte                solution outlet (8) are connected to the cathodic                compartment, to circulate the cathodic electrolyte                solution, and the remaining reagent gas carbon                dioxide and the product gas carbon monoxide by the                outlet.
   USE - Flow cell electrolyzer for electrochemically                reducing gas reactant.
   ADVANTAGE - The flow cell electrolyzer leads to the                formation of a highly energetic radical anion                (CO2-), requires catalytic activation in order to                reduce the energy cost of processing, and increase                the selectivity of the species formed in the                reaction process, allow fine-tuning of the primary                and secondary coordination spheres by manipulating                the chelating environment and steric and electronic                effects of the ligands.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The gas diffusion electrode comprises an                electrochemically inert gas diffusion porous                current collector of surface (S), one molecular                catalyst is used to electrochemically reduce the                gas reactant comprising carbon dioxide into gaseous                carbo monoxide in the cathodic electrolyte solution                by production of gaseous hydrogen. The molecular                catalyst is selected from metal tetra phenyl                porphyrin with N(1-4C alkyl)3 groups, with the                metal selected from iron, or cobalt; other groups                are independently selected from H, OH, F, C(CH3)3,                or metal phthalocyanine, cobalt quarter pyridine,                anion exchange membrane (10), impermeable carbon                dioxide, carbon monoxide and hydrogen, between the                anodic compartment and the cathodic compartment. A                channel (11) is used for passing flow of the                reagent gas carbon dioxide, at a controlled flow                rate Qg, through the porous surface of the gas                diffusion electrode of the cathodic compartment on                which there is the molecular catalyst, while the                catholyte electrolyte solution circulates in                between the gas diffusion electrode and the anion                exchange membrane. A pumping unit (12) is set to                circulate by pumping the anodic electrolyte                solution in the anodic compartment and the cathodic                electrolyte solution in the cathodic compartment                between the inlet and outlet, and a control flow is                used by pumping the flow comprising gas carbon                dioxide in the channel passing through the gas                diffusion porous cathode. The pumping unit is                configured to control all flow rates of the                cathodic and anodic electrolytes as well as the                reagent gas carbon dioxide passing through the                surface of the gas diffusion porous cathode. A                power supply is set to provide the energy necessary                to trigger the electrochemical reactions involving                the reagent. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                method of reducing a gas reactant comprising carbon                dioxide into gazeous carbon monoxide (CO) in flow                cell electrolyzer, involves:
   (A) passing the flow of reagent gas carbon                dioxide, at a controlled flow rate Qg, onto or                through the surface of the gas diffusion porous                current diffusion electrode (GDE) of the cathodic                compartment on which there is the molecular                catalyst, while the cathodic electrolyte solution                circulates in between the Gas Diffusion Electrode                and the anion exchange membrane; and
   (B) applying a potential at the Gas Diffusion                Electrode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows operation of carbon dioxide                in flow cell electrolyzer

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The anodic and/or                  cathodic electrolyte solution has a neutral or                  basic pH of 9-14, particularly 11.5 to 14, more                  particularly from 13-14. The gas reactant flow is                  at atmospheric pressure. The anodic and the                  cathodic electrolytes are at ambient temperature.                  The tetra-phenyl iron porphyrin as molecular                  catalyst has pH between 11.5 to 14, more                  particularly from 13-14, gas reactant flow passes                  at atmospheric pressure through the porous                  surface S of the gas diffusion electrode (GDE),                  and anodic and the cathodic electrolytes are at                  ambient temperature. The cobalt phthalocyanine as                  a as molecular catalyst has pH is between 11.5 to                  14, more particularly from 13 to 14, gas reactant                  flow passes at atmospheric pressure through the                  porous surface of the gas diffusion electrode                  (GDE), and anodic and the cathodic electrolytes                  are at ambient temperature. The potential applied                  to the Gas Diffusion Electrode is between 0V and                  -1V versus RHE, when the molecular catalyst is a                  tetra-phenyl iron porphyrin for generating a                  current density for CO production of 150 mA.cnr2;                  between 0.48 V and -0.98 V versus RHE, when the                  molecular catalyst is a metal phthalocyanine for                  generating a current density for CO production of                  50 mA.cnr2; between -0.3 V and -1 V versus RHE,                  when the molecular catalyst is a cobalt                  phthalocyanine with N+(1-4C alkyl)3 groups for                  generating a current density for CO production of                  150 mA.cnr2; between -0.6 V and -1 V versus RHE,                  when the molecular catalyst is a cobalt quarter                  pyridine with N+(1-4C alkyl)3 groups for                  generating a current density for CO production of                  150 mA.cnr2. The molecular catalyst is a                  tetra-phenyl iron porphyrin, the selectivity of                  the electrochemical reaction is between 98% and                  99.9%, when the molecular catalyst a metal                  phthalocyanine, the selectivity of the                  electrochemical reaction is between 90% and 92%;                  when the molecular catalyst is a cobalt                  phthalocyanine with N+(1-4 alkyl)3 groups, and                  selectivity of the electrochemical reaction is                  between 92% and 96% or 86.1% and 98.8%. Preferred                  Components: The molecular catalyst is                  tetra-phenyl iron porphyrin of formula (I) or                  cobalt molecular catalyst of formula (II). The                  cathodic electrolyte comprises a phosphate buffer                  or potassium hydroxide, and anodic electrolyte                  comprises a phosphate buffer or potassium                  hydroxide, particularly cathodic electrolyte                  comprises sodium hydroxide (NaOH) or cesium                  hydroxide (CsOH) and/or anodic electrolyte                  comprises sodium hydroxide or cesium hydroxide.                  The porous cathode comprises on the current                  collector, an electrode film which contains                  polymers with the molecular catalysts, where the                  electrode film is deposited or grafted on the                  current collector..R1-R10=N+(1-4C alkyl)3 group, or selected                  from H, OH, F, or C(CH3)3 group, particularly H                  or F; andR1'-R16'=H, F C(CH3)3 or N+(1-4C alkyl)3,                  particularly H, more particularly R1, R4, R5, R8,                  R9, R12, R13, and R16 is N+(CI-C4 alkyl)3                  substituent.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN JP2019181367-A; JP7106954-B2
TI Carbon dioxide recovery device, has horizontal            multi-stage structure which comprises liquid supply            mechanism for supplying absorption liquid to gas-liquid            contact portion and sequentially circulates stages            along array.
AU NAKAMURA N
   OKUHARA H
   TAKANO K
   IKEDA R
AE IHI CORP (ISHI-C)
   IHI CORP (ISHI-C)
GA 201988383W
AB    NOVELTY - The device has absorption device (1) which                absorbs carbon dioxide contained in the gas into                the absorption liquid. A regenerator regenerates                the absorbing liquid by releasing carbon dioxide                contained in the absorbing liquid. A circulation                mechanism circulates an absorption liquid between                the absorption device and the regeneration device.                A gas-liquid contact portion (2) is assigned to                stages arranged in a lateral direction. Each stages                has vertical flat plates (P1,P2,P3) arranged in                parallel. A horizontal multi-stage structure has a                liquid supply mechanism for supplying an absorption                liquid to the gas-liquid contact portion and                sequentially circulates the stages along the array.                The recovery device has an elongated shape                extending in the lateral direction in which the                stages are arranged.
   USE - Carbon dioxide recovery device for recovering                carbon dioxide contained in exhaust gas such as                combustion gas in chemical processing plant,                thermal power station, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The device with good and efficient gas-liquid                contact, while suppressing pressure loss in                gas-liquid contact is provided. The carbon dioxide                recovery device having a gas-liquid contact                configuration is easily changed in design by                increasing or decreasing the number of stages, and                carbon dioxide contained in the gas is efficiently                recovered.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The container includes a gas introduction port                for supplying gas to the gas-liquid contact                portion, and a gas discharge port for discharging                gas from the gas-liquid contact portion. A                desulfurization section is mounted between the gas                inlet and the gas-liquid contact section and                removes sulfur oxide from the gas. A cleaning unit                is arranged between the gas-liquid contact unit and                the gas discharge port and cleans the gas provided                in the container. A liquid distributor supplies a                liquid to the vertical flat plates. The liquid                distributor of the desulfurization unit supplies an                alkaline liquid as the liquid. The absorption                device comprises an induction wall for guiding the                gas and the gas flows upward or downward at each                stage while the gas flows through the gas-liquid                contact portion. The carbon dioxide recovery device                has an elongated shape extending in the lateral                direction in which the stages are arranged. A                heating source supplies regeneration heat to the                absorption liquid. A cleaning section cleans the                released carbon dioxide, the heating source is                mounted at one end of the elongated container, the                gas outlet is provided at the other end of the                container, and the cleaning section is arranged                between the gas-liquid contact portion and the gas                discharge port. A liquid distributor supplies water                to the vertical plates, and by water supplied from                the liquid distributor. The component of the                absorption liquid accompanying the carbon dioxide                is removed. The liquid recovery ports are provided                in each of the stages for recovering the absorbing                liquid flowing down to the bottom. The liquid                recovery port of one stage is connected to the                liquid distributor of the stage where the absorbing                liquid flows next. The circulation mechanism                includes a supply flow path for supplying an                absorption liquid from the liquid supply mechanism                of the absorption device to the liquid supply                mechanism of the regeneration device. A reflux flow                path reflux the absorbent from the liquid supply                mechanism of the regeneration device to the liquid                supply mechanism of the absorption device. A heat                exchanger recovers heat from the absorption liquid                in the reflux flow path of the circulation                mechanism and supplies the heat to the regenerator.                The liquid supply mechanism includes heat exchanger                provided in the pipe for adjusting the temperature                of the absorbing liquid. The number of heat                exchanger provided in the pipe is a number capable                of adjusting the temperature of the absorbing                liquid sequentially supplied to the stages for each                stage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross sectional view of                the absorption device that constitutes carbon                dioxide recovery device.
   Absorption device (1)
   Gas-liquid contact portion (2)
   Desulfurization section (4)
   Cleaning section (5)
   Vertical flat plates (P1,P2,P3)

PN CN108516558-A
TI Producing superfine white carbon black comprises e.g. taking sodium silicate and silicon dioxide, pumping to reaction vessel, introducing the carbon dioxide, subjecting to solid-liquid separation, washing, beating and drying.
AU LI N
   LI Z
AE LI N (LINN-Individual)
GA 201873511B
AB    NOVELTY - Producing superfine white carbon black comprises (i) taking sodium silicate and silicon dioxide, (ii) adding a compounding liquid to the sodium silicate so that the concentration of the sodium silicate reaches a concentration silica, (iii) pumping the reaction liquid to the reaction vessel, (iv) introducing the carbon dioxide (v) introducing carbon dioxide gas to the set S1 time, (vi) introducing the carbon dioxide gas, (vii) subjecting to solid-liquid separation according to a conventional liquid-solid separation process, and the obtained solid matter is a filter cake and the obtained liquid is a mother liquid, (viii) washing by adding water and solid-liquid separation according to the product requirements, the conductivity of washing the filter cake to the washing water, and (ix) beating and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for producing superfine white carbon black.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical; and has stable quality of product and fine particle diameter.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing superfine white carbon black comprises (i) taking sodium silicate of modulus 2.6-3.5 and 50-380 g/l silicon dioxide, (ii) adding a compounding liquid to the sodium silicate so that the concentration of the sodium silicate reaches a concentration of 50-160 g/l silica to obtain a reaction liquid, (iii) pumping the reaction liquid obtained in the step (ii) to the reaction vessel, where the volume of the reaction liquid sent to the reaction vessel is Vz, and the reaction liquid is heated to a certain value T1 at a temperature of 73-95 degrees C, (iv) introducing the carbon dioxide gas having a weight concentration of 25-100 wt.% while maintaining T1 plus minus 3 degrees C, when the temperature reaches T1 and the concentration value Nc1 is set, where the time is 20-60 minutes and the time is set to S1, the flow rate of the carbon dioxide gas V1 is K1asterisk(VzasteriskNna)/(Nc1asterisk(S1+S2));,the unit is: V1-L/minutes, Vz-L, Nna-g/L, Nc1-% , S1-minutes , S2-minutes, K1 is a coefficient and the value is 0.1-0.5 according to the reaction condition, which is the first reaction, (v) introducing carbon dioxide gas to the set S1 time, where the carbon dioxide gas is stopped, and the temperature of the reaction liquid is adjusted within 5-60 minutes to reach a certain value T2 of the temperature of the third stage reaction of 75-95 degrees C for the second reaction, (vi) introducing the carbon dioxide gas having a weight concentration of 25 wt% to 100 wt% is further while maintaining T2 plus minus 3 degrees C after the temperature of the reaction solution reaches T2 and the concentration value Nc2 is set, and the time is 60-150 minutes, and the time is set For S2, the flow rate of carbon dioxide gas is V2 is K2asterisk(VzasteriskNna)/(Nc2asterisk(S1+S2)), where the unit is V2-l/minutes , Vz-l, Nna-g/l, Nc2-%, S1-minutes, S2-minutes, K2 is a coefficient, and the value is 0.08-0.4 according to the reaction condition, the condition for determining the end of the reaction is when the time reaches the time S2, the pH value of the reaction liquid is measured, and if the pH value of the reaction liquid is more than 9, the carbon dioxide gas is continuously supplied and the temperature is maintained until the pH of the reaction liquid is 9 to 8.5. When the reaction is stopped, the slurry is obtained for the third stage reaction, (vii) subjecting to solid-liquid separation according to a conventional liquid-solid separation process, and the obtained solid matter is a filter cake and the obtained liquid is a mother liquid, (viii) washing by adding water and solid-liquid separation according to the product requirements, the conductivity of washing the filter cake to the washing water is 1300-550 mu s/cm after the interval, the washing is over, and (ix) beating and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the mixing liquid is water, mother liquid or washing water of the step (vii). The liquid is water and the mixed solution of the sodium carbonate.

AU CHOI G
   KIM M H
   CHOI H J
AE CHOI G (CHOI-Individual)
GA 202241363N
AB    NOVELTY - A cleaning extinguishing agent for electronic devices comprises a carbon dioxide-releasing compound.
   USE - A cleaning extinguishing agent for electronic devices (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The clean fire extinguishing agent can prevent, delay or extinguish fires caused by high temperature or heat in airtight electronic devices, so it has high stability and economical efficiency. The agent is harmless and eco-friendly because there is almost no contamination, and it has cleanliness because it does not pollute electronic devices, and the manufacturing cost is low due to the low price.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for electronic device comprising airtight space in which electronic components are placed; and a clean extinguishing agent containing a carbon dioxide-releasing compound.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide-releasing compound is selected from lithium carbonate, sodium carbonate, sodium hydrogen carbonate, potassium carbonate, potassium hydrogen carbonate, magnesium carbonate, calcium carbonate, aluminum carbonate, ammonium carbonate, or hydrates, preferably sodium bicarbonate, potassium hydrogen carbonate, or hydrate. The carbon dioxide-releasing compound is in the form of a powder, granules, beads, tablets, rods, flakes, disks or films, or based on paper, non-woven and woven fabrics, or impregnated sheet. Preferred Process: The airtight space is formed as a housing or a case of the electronic device. The carbon dioxide-releasing compound or the clean extinguishing agent containing the same is packaged or filled in a heat-resistant bag or container and is fixedly or detachably disposed inside or outside the airtight space. The carbon dioxide-releasing compound is contained in an amount capable of releasing carbon dioxide capable of filling the internal volume of the hermetic space 1-10 times.

PN CN110540282-A
TI Adjusting pH of water comprises using pressure of water pump to mix carbon dioxide gas and water to form carbonic acid solution, and slowly adding the formed carbonic acid solution to the raw water to adjust the pH value of the raw water.
AU WU X
   HU T
   JIANG L
   NI B
   YUE Q
   ZHANG D
AE SHANGHAI NAT ENG RES CENT URBAN WATER (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019A5039F
AB    NOVELTY - Adjusting pH of water comprises (i) using pressure of water pump to mix carbon dioxide gas and water to form carbonic acid solution, and (ii) slowly adding the formed carbonic acid solution to the raw water to adjust the pH value of the raw water.
   USE - The method is useful for adjusting pH of water.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: increases the utilization rate of carbon dioxide; reduces its overflow rate; and maximizes its ability to adjust pH.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the mass ratio of the carbon dioxide gas and water is between 1:400 and 1:600. A microporous aeration head is provided at the outlet end of the carbonic acid solution pipe. Preferred Method: In the step (i), an SV type static mixer is used to mix carbon dioxide gas and water to form carbonic acid solution. The mixing pressure of the SV type static mixer is not less than 0.4 MPa. In the step (ii), the formed carbonic acid solution is added to the raw water by pipe feeding. The method further comprises obtaining the adjusted pH value in real time through a pH online monitor set in raw water, and adjusting the amount of carbon dioxide added according to the obtained pH value. The adjusted pH value is obtained after the carbonic acid solution reacts with the raw water for many times.

PN CN113552024-A
TI Method for calculating diffusion mass transfer of carbon dioxide in presence of oil and water at same time, involves calculating effective diffusion coefficient in oil phase and water phase.
AU YU D
   LI A
   LU N
   ZHANG Z
   WANG D
   ZHANG L
   JIA Q
   CHEN Y
   WANG Z
AE PETROLEUM ENG TECHNOLOGY RES INST JIANGS (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2021C3829S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves dividing (101) the carbon dioxide (CO2) in the oil-water two-phase diffusion stage as step 1. A mathematical model of different diffusion stages of CO2 diffusion is established (102) in the oil-water two phases as step 2. The CO2 diffusion coefficient relational expression is established (103) in the oil-water two-phase according to the material conservation law as step 3. The mathematical simulation is performed to obtain CO2 concentration distribution and pressure drop curve as step 4. A pressure drop model of the micro diffusion process is established as step 5. The diffusion experiment of CO2 is performed in oil-water phase system as step 6. The effective diffusion coefficient Do of CO2 is calculated in the oil phase and the effective diffusion coefficient Dw of the water phase as step 7.
   USE - Method for calculating diffusion mass transfer of carbon dioxide in presence of oil and water at same time in oil field development. Uses include but are not limited to oil, natural gas, and water.
   ADVANTAGE - The effective diffusion coefficient of the carbon dioxide obtained by the calculation method in the saturated crude rock core can accurately reflect the actual diffusion process. It can truly simulate the underground diffusion situation. It provides guidance for the field solution design. It determines the time of braising well in carbon dioxide energy-increasing fracturing.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of the method for calculating diffusion mass transfer of CO2 in the presence of oil and water at the same time.
   Step for dividing the carbon dioxide in the oil-water two-phase diffusion stage (101)
   Step for establishing mathematical model of different diffusion stages of carbon dioxide diffusion (102)
   Step for establishing carbon dioxide diffusion coefficient relation in the oil water phase (103)
   Step for taking the stratum water and crude oil sample (106)
   Step for combining experiment and geological parameter (107)

AB    NOVELTY - Reduction catalyst body (2) for carbon                dioxide, comprises: a metal layer; and a projection                comprising an aggregate of fine metal particles                provided on the metal layer, and possessing a                polyhedral structure having surfaces of three faces                or more of a polygon, where the projection has a                site of reducing carbon dioxide, as at least a part                of the surfaces.
   USE - The catalyst body is useful in reduction                electrode which is used in reduction reaction                device for carbon dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst body utilizes porous metal layer                have pore distribution peaks, that is increasing                surface area, improving diffusibility of ions and                reactant, improving conductivity, which enables to                increase the reduction reaction activity of carbon                dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) manufacturing the reduction catalyst body                for carbon dioxide, comprising immersing a metal                base material including the metal into an                electrolytic solution containing a compound                including a counter anion capable of forming a salt                or a complex with a metal forming the metal layer,                applying a first electric potential at which the                metal can be oxidized, to the metal base material                immersed into the electrolytic solution, and                applying a second electric potential at which the                metal can be reduced, to the metal base material                immersed into the electrolytic solution and after                subjected to the application of the first electric                potential;
   (2) a reduction electrode (1) for carbon                dioxide, comprising the reduction catalyst body and                a conductive member (3) connected to the reduction                catalyst body; and
   (3) a reduction reaction device for carbon                dioxide, comprising a reaction cell including the                reduction electrode and an oxidation electrode, and                a power supply connected to the reduction electrode                and the oxidation electrode.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates a sectional view of a                reduction electrode.
