PN CN115095399-A
TI Ultra-critical carbon dioxide split temperature            closed Brayton cycle power system, has compressor            connected with vortex tube nozzle according to flow            direction of cold fluid, where turbine drives power            output device to realize expansion power output.
AU JIANG J
   LI Z
   GONG X
   ZHU Y
   YUE P
   GUO C
   GUO Y
   XU X
   QIN S
   LIANG S
AE SHANGHAI PUDONG ADVANCED ENERGY & POWER (SHAN-Non-standard)
   INST ENG THERMOPHYSICS CHINESE (CAET-C)
GA 2022C54186
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a compressor (C) connected with                a vortex tube (VT) nozzle according to flow                direction of cold fluid. A cold end outlet (VT-C)                of the vortex tube is connected with A cold side                inlet of a low-temperature regenerator (LTR). The                cold side outlet of the low temperature regenerator                is connected to a cold end of a high temperature                regenerators (HTR), where the high temperature                re-generationator, a heater (H) and a turbine (T)                are sequentially connected together. The turbine                drives a power output device to realize an                expansion power output (POD) according to flow                direction. A water cooler and a compressor are                connected together to form a closed cycle                loop.
   USE - Ultra-critical carbon dioxide split                temperature closed Brayton cycle power                system.
   ADVANTAGE - The supercritical carbon dioxide split                temperature closed Brayton cycle power system                avoids the extra consumption caused by the                re-compressor is large, the system complexity is                increased. Using the vortex tube characteristics                realize cold and hot fluid split matching low                temperature regenerator heat exchange, avoiding                heat regenerator clamping point problem. The vortex                tube outlet cold fluid into the cold side inlet of                the low-temperature regenerator after absorbing                heat is changed into a single-phase ultra-critical                carbon dioxide , and the two-phase cold fluid is                changed in the carbon dioxide cooler, the specific                heat of the matched ultra-critical carbon dioxide                near critical area is increased sharply, improving                the heat exchange efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for an                ultra-critical carbon dioxide split-temperature                closed Brayton cycle method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view showing                the structure of the supercritical carbon dioxide                split temperature closed Brayton cycle power                system.
   CCompressor
   HHeater
   HTR High temperature regenerators
   LTR Low-temperature regenerator
   POD Expansion power output
   TTurbine
   VTVortex tube
   VT-CCold end outlet

GA 2022C15886
AB    NOVELTY - Producing potassium carbonate, comprises:                absorption and carbonization of potassium chloride                with carbon dioxide in the presence of                diethanolamine (DEA); obtaining a suspension and                cooling it; introducing unreacted DEA, carbon                dioxide and potassium chloride into the                carbonization column; filtering; and washing the                precipitate of potassium bicarbonate and its                calcification. The DEA solution is fed into the                collector of the aminated potassium chloride brine                with the molar ratio potassium chloride: DEA of                (1:1.09)-(1:1.07) at a temperature in the upper and                middle zone of the carbonization column of                &#8815; 30 and 40-70&#176; C, respectively                and the content of carbon dioxide in the                countercurrent supplied gases 65-85%.
   USE - The method is useful for producing potassium                carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the yield of the                product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing potassium carbonate, comprises:                absorption and carbonization of potassium chloride                with carbon dioxide in the presence of                diethanolamine (DEA); obtaining a suspension and                cooling it; introducing unreacted DEA, carbon                dioxide and potassium chloride into the                carbonization column; filtering; and washing the                precipitate of potassium bicarbonate and its                calcification. The DEA solution is fed into the                collector of the aminated potassium chloride brine                with the molar ratio potassium chloride: DEA of                (1:1.09)-(1:1.07) at a temperature in the upper and                middle zone of the carbonization column of                &#8815; 30 and 40-70&#176; C, respectively                and the content of carbon dioxide in the                countercurrent supplied gases 65-85%. The main                mother filtrate after separation of potassium                bicarbonate is evaporated to 40% of the total mass                of the solution with the release of unreacted DEA                and carbon dioxide into the gas phase, and                potassium chloride into the solid phase, where                off-gases from production are absorbed in an                absorption column with a saturated solution of                potassium chloride at a temperature not exceeding                30&#176; C.

TI Carbon dioxide catalytic reduction device used in            e.g. in raw pharmacy, biological sensing, thermal            chemical method, comprises photoelectric catalytic            composite film arranged in reactor which is divided            into anode chamber and cathode chamber, and light            source arranged above cathode chamber.
AU LI Z
   CAO J
   BAI Y
   CHEN H
   DONG J
   ZHAO T
   WEN B
   LIU X
AE UNIV TAIYUAN IND (UYTA-Non-standard)
GA 2022606495
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide catalytic reduction device                comprises reactor, photoelectric catalytic                composite film, cathode, anode, direct current (DC)                power supply and light source. The film is arranged                in reactor which is divided into anode chamber and                cathode chamber. The anode and cathode are arranged                in anode chamber and cathode chamber. The positive                and negative poles of DC power supply are connected                to anode and cathode, respectively. The light                source is arranged above cathode chamber. The film                comprises bipolar film and metal nickel net with a                surface-loaded photoelectric catalyst. The bipolar                film comprises anion exchange film and cation                exchange film. The metal nickel net is used as a                cathode and net with surface-loaded photoelectric                catalyst is attached to cation exchange film. The                anode chamber is aqueous electrolyte solution, and                cathode chamber is carbon dioxide and                phosphate-buffered saline aqueous solution                containing enzymes and coenzymes. The light source                is xenon lamp.
   USE - The device is useful for carbon dioxide                catalytic reduction reaction (claimed), and in raw                pharmacy, biological sensing, thermal chemical                method, photochemical method, electrochemical                method and bio-enzyme method.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: solves the problem that the                coenzyme is difficult to recycle in the existing                enzyme-catalyzed carbon dioxide reduction                technology, is difficult for the traditional                catalytic system to have the problems of mild and                controllable process; has high catalytic efficiency                and excellent selectivity; realizes carbon dioxide                reduction device that couples photoelectric                catalysis and enzyme catalysis for the recycling of                coenzymes; realizes photoelectric catalysis and                enzyme catalysis; and carries out carbon dioxide                catalytic reduction reaction under illumination of                xenon lamp light and externally applied                voltage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                carbon dioxide catalytic reduction device (Drawing                includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The preparation method of                  the photoelectric catalytic composite film is:                  (i) using mixture of polyvinyl alcohol,                  polyvinylpyrrolidone, polysulfone, and/or                  polyvinylbenzyl chloride as the support of the                  anion exchange film, using compounds containing                  primary amino group, secondary amino group,                  tertiary amino group and/or quaternary amino                  group as the fixing group of anion exchange film,                  adding glutaraldehyde solution as cross-linking                  agent, preparing anion exchange film liquid, and                  preparing anion exchange film by casting method,                  (ii) using mixtures of polyvinyl alcohol,                  polyvinylpyrrolidone, polyphenylene ether,                  polysulfone and/or styrene as support of the                  cation exchange film, using compounds containing                  sulfonic acid group, carboxylic acid group and/or                  phosphoric acid group as fixing group of cation                  exchange film, adding ferric chloride or calcium                  chloride solution as a cross-linking agent,                  preparing cation exchange film liquid, and                  casting on surface of anion exchange film                  prepared in the step (i) to obtain a cation                  exchange film, and (iii) attaching metal nickel                  net to surface of cation exchange film before the                  cation exchange film is not completely dried,                  then casting the ultrasonically dispersed                  photoelectric catalyst aqueous solution or                  anhydrous ethanol solution on the surface of the                  metal nickel net to obtain the finished                  product.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The photoelectric                  catalyst is cyanide nitrogen, titanium dioxide,                  molybdenum disulfide, cadmium sulfide, copper(I)                  oxide, ferric oxide or bismuth oxychloride. The                  electrolyte aqueous solution is sodium sulfate,                  sodium hydroxide or potassium hydroxide. The                  enzyme is formate dehydrogenase or a mixed enzyme                  comprising formate dehydrogenase, formaldehyde                  dehydrogenase and methanol dehydrogenase. The                  coenzyme is a reduced coenzyme comprising                  nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide and                  nicotinamide adenine dinucleotide phosphate. The                  anode is platinum sheet.

AU BAXTER L

AE HALL LABS LLC (HALL-Non-standard)

AB    NOVELTY - Separating (M1) liquids comprises: passing a                feed stream (103) into a separator, where the feed                stream comprises an organic compound and carbon                dioxide, and further comprising oxides comprising                sulfur oxides, nitrogen oxides and/or ozone; and                separating by gravity an organic-rich stream from                an inorganic-rich stream, where the organic-rich                stream comprises a portion of the organic compound                and a first portion of the carbon dioxide and the                inorganic-rich stream comprises a portion of the                oxides and a second portion of the carbon                dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for separating                liquids.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is pressure control method                allowing for a boost in efficiency; and utilizes                separator operates at cryogenic temperatures that                allowing the system to operate at relatively low                pressures as the oxides and ozone processed require                either high pressures at moderate temperatures or                cryogenic temperatures for relatively lower                pressures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                separating (M2) compounds comprising passing a feed                stream into a separator, where the feed stream                comprises an organic compound and carbon dioxide,                and further comprising oxides comprising sulfur                oxides, nitrogen oxides and/or ozone, the separator                comprises an auger, and a portion of the carbon                dioxide and the oxides are solids, heating the                auger to melt a portion of the portion of the                oxides that are the solids to form a liquid oxides                stream and separating by gravity an organic-rich                stream from an inorganic-rich stream, where the                organic-rich stream comprises a portion of the                organic compound and a first portion of the carbon                dioxide and the inorganic-rich stream comprises a                portion of the oxides and a second portion of the                carbon dioxide.

   Heating fluid (111)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method (M1) further                  comprises: heating the auger to melt a portion of                  the portion of the oxides that are the solids to                  form a liquid oxides stream; separating the                  liquid oxides stream from the solids and the                  organic phase; a quiescent settling chamber;                  separating the nitrogen oxide in the separator;                  the sulfur oxide in the melter of a separator;                  separating the first portion of the carbon                  dioxide from the organic phase; recycling a                  portion of the first portion of the carbon                  dioxide stream into the separator; and operating                  at cryogenic temperatures. The separating by                  gravity comprises decanting. The compounds that                  are solid particles are separated from the feed                  stream. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide                  and the oxides are solids and/or liquids. The                  separator further comprises: a heating element; a                  filter medium; and an auger and a portion of the                  carbon dioxide and the oxides are solids. The                  feed stream further comprises compounds                  comprising water, ammonia, hydrogen sulfide,                  mercury, soot, dust, minerals, microbes,                  precipitated salts, precious metals, base metals                  and/or particulates. The feed stream is a                  liquid.

PT P
PN WO2019092128-A1
TI Particulate activated carbon material in which particulate activated carbon is impregnated with at least one alkali carbonate salt e.g. disodium carbonate, used in carbon dioxide capture device for capturing carbon dioxide from air.
AU VARGAS A
   KUELUER A T
   WURZBACHER J A
   GEBALD C
AE CLIMEWORKS AG (CLIM-Non-standard)
GA 201943568P
AB    NOVELTY - Particulate activated carbon material in which particulate activated carbon is impregnated with at least one alkali carbonate salt selected from the group consisting of: dipotassium carbonate, dilithium carbonate or disodium carbonate as well as mixed salts, is disclosed. The impregnated particulate activated carbon has a pore volume of at least 0.1 cm3/g for pore sizes of at least 5 nm and a pore volume of at most 0.3 cm3/g for pore sizes of less than 5 nm, determined using nitrogen adsorption methods, as described in ISO 15901-2 and ISO 15901-3 and according to the quenched solid density functional theory (QSDFT) calculation scheme.
   USE - The material is useful in carbon dioxide capture device for capturing carbon dioxide from air in a cyclic process (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) carbon dioxide capture device comprising the material, preferably in the form of at least one air permeable container comprising the material in particulate form, or in the form of a multitude of layers of such containers arranged in a stack;
   (2) making (M1) the material, comprising dissolving alkali carbonate salt in a solvent, preferably deionized water, where preferably the concentration is 1-8 (preferably 1.5-4.5) mmol alkali carbonate salt per ml water, where particulate activated carbon, if need be after drying and/or purification, is added to the solution, under stirring, preferably at a 5-40 degrees C, preferably 20-30 degrees C and/or for a time span of 30 minutes to 100 hours, preferably 6-40 hours, and subsequently at least the solid fraction is isolated and/or dried by evaporation, preferably vacuum evaporation; and
   (3) capturing (M2) carbon dioxide from atmospheric air using the material, preferably the carbon dioxide capture device, where a temperature swing cycle or a temperature/vacuum swing cycle, with or without steam injected, is used for adsorption and desorption of the carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali carbonate salt impregnated particulate activated carbon has: a pore volume of at least 0.1 (preferably 0.1-2.2 or 0.2-1.5) cm3/g for pore sizes of 5 nm or 5-50 nm, determined using nitrogen adsorption methods as described in ISO 15901-2 and ISO 15901-3 and according to the QSDFT calculation scheme; a pore volume of at least 0.05 cm3/g for pore sizes of 50-1000 nm, as determined using mercury porosimetry analysis as described in ISO 15901-1; and/or a pore volume of at most 0.25 cm3/g or at most 0.2 cm3/g or 0.05- 0.2 cm3/g or 0.05-0.15 cm3/g, for pore sizes of less than 5 nm, determined using nitrogen adsorption methods as described in ISO 15901-2 and ISO 15901-3 and according to the QSDFT calculation scheme. The alkali carbonate salt impregnated particulate activated carbon has a pore surface of at least 20 (preferably 20-500 or 50-400) m2/g for pore sizes of above 5 nm or 5-50 nm, and/or a pore surface of at most 500 m2/g or 150-500 or 100-400 m2/g for pore sizes of less than 5 nm, determined using nitrogen adsorption methods as described in ISO 15901-2 according to the QSDFT calculation scheme. The impregnated particulate activated carbon has: a BET surface area measured according to the standard described in ISO 9277 of 100-800 m2/g, or less than 500 m2/g; a tapped density of 300-800 kg/m3, preferably 400-600 kg/m3; and a particle size of 0.1-8 (preferably 0.2-4.5) mm, and/or in the range of mesh (ASTM) of 3-140, preferably 4-50. The impregnated particulate activated carbon contains at least 10 (preferably 25-45) wt.% of alkali carbonate salt, preferably dipotassium carbonate. The alkali carbonate salt impregnated particulate activated carbon has an average carbon dioxide capacity, at 30 degrees C, 60% relative humidity and 450 ppmv carbon dioxide concentration after 1000 minutes adsorption of 0.5-5 (preferably 1-2.5) mmol/g and/or after 180 minutes adsorption of 0.25-2 (preferably 0.6-1.5) mmol/g. The particles of the alkali carbonate salt impregnated particulate activated carbon are essentially spherical, extruded rods, pellets. In the method (M2): at least a part of the desorption of carbon dioxide is performed at a pressure of 50-400 (preferably 100-300) (mbar)abs and at a temperature of 80-150 degrees C or at least a part of the desorption of carbon dioxide is performed at a pressure in the range of 50-400 (preferably 100-300) (mbar)abs and at a temperature of 35-80 degrees C, preferably 45-80 degrees C and another part of the desorption of carbon dioxide is performed at a temperature of 80-150 degrees C, preferably 90-135 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The device comprises a housing in which the air permeable container containing the particles of the alkali carbonate salt impregnated particulate activated carbon is located, where the housing has at least one opening for allowing in and/or allowing out atmospheric air for adsorption and closing lids for the opening to close the housing as well as means for applying a vacuum and/or temperature change for release of the adsorbed carbon dioxide as well as means for removal of the adsorbed carbon dioxide from the housing and for collecting and/or further concentrating and/or condensing the carbon dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

PN WO2019006301-A1; US2019032003-A1; WO2019006301-A8
TI Converting carbon dioxide into organic compounds involves culturing non-photosynthetic autotrophic cells in culture vessel having fermentation medium comprising carbon substrate, carbon dioxide and diatomic hydrogen.
AU PARK J
   LIU N
   STEPHANOPOULOS G
AE MASSACHUSETTS INST TECHNOLOGY (MASI-C)
   PARK J (PARK-Individual)
   LIU N (LIUN-Individual)
   STEPHANOPOULOS G (STEP-Individual)
   MASSACHUSETTS INST TECHNOLOGY (MASI-C)
GA 201901721J
AB    NOVELTY - Converting (M1) carbon dioxide into organic compounds comprises culturing non-photosynthetic autotrophic cells in a culture vessel containing a fermentation medium comprising a controlled, limiting amount of carbon substrate, a controlled amount of carbon dioxide and a controlled amount of diatomic hydrogen (H2).
   USE - The methods are useful for converting carbon dioxide into organic compounds, converting acetyl-CoA into lipids, converting carbon dioxide into lipids, producing bioproducts and culturing microbial cells (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The methods utilize the: microorganisms that enable faster, efficient and higher yield carbon conversion; and carbon substrate that is limited to avoid catabolite repression, which results in the preferential consumption by the cells of acetate, thus facilitating efficient and reliable production of acetate via the fixation of carbon dioxide, which is an abundant carbon feedstock that does not interfere with food supply, and thus consumption of acetate in the production of lipids is both an environmentally and economically ideal method of energy storage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) converting (M2) acetyl-Coenzyme A (acetyl-CoA) into lipids comprising culturing oleaginous cells in a culture vessel containing a fermentation medium comprising a controlled, limiting amount of carbon substrate and a controlled amount of acetate;
   (2) converting (M3) carbon dioxide into lipids and producing bioproducts, comprising (a) culturing non-photosynthetic autotrophic cells and (b) culturing oleaginous cells in the culture vessel comprising fermentation medium comprising a controlled, limiting amount of carbon substrate and the acetate produced in (a) to produce lipids and the bioproducts; and
   (3) a method comprising culturing microbial cells (M4) in a culture medium comprising many carbon substrates comprising at least one carbon substrate that is preferentially utilized by the microbial cells and at least one carbon substrate that is not preferentially utilized by the microbial cells, where the preferentially utilized carbon substrate is present in a limiting amount so that the not preferentially utilized carbon substrate provides the majority of the carbon for carbon-based molecules produced by the microbial cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: In method (M1) the non-photosynthetic autotrophic cells are acetogenic cells. The acetogenic cells comprise Moorella thermoacetica, Clostridium ljungdahlii or Acetobacterium woodii. In method (M2) the oleaginous cells are oleaginous yeast cells i.e. Yarrowia lipolytica cells. . The oleaginous cells overexpress a kinase that phosphorylates the carbon substrate, where the carbon substrate is gluconate and the kinase that phosphorylates the carbon substrate is gluconate kinase.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In method (M1) the proportion of carbon substrate to H2 is controlled by: changing the amount of carbon substrate in the fermentation medium; altering the relative rates at which the substrate and H2 are added into the culture vessel. The proportion of carbon substrate and/or H2 to the culture volume is controlled by altering the relative rates at which the substrate and/or H2 are added into the culture vessel. The rate at which the carbon substrate is added to the culture vessel is less than or equal to 0.3 grams of carbon substrate per gram of cells (dry weight)/hour. The amount of carbon substrate is insufficient to permit the cells to produce sufficient electrons. The limiting amount of carbon substrate provides less than 25%, preferably less than 1% of the quantity of electrons in the culture. The amount of H2 is sufficient to provide a quantity of electrons that is equal to or more than the electron deficit resulting from the decreased concentration of carbon substrate. The quantity of electrons provided by H2 is at least 75%, preferably at least 99% of the quantity of electrons in the culture. The rate of addition of H2 is at least as fast as the maximum gas transfer rate. The rate of addition of carbon dioxide is: unlimited; or one half of the rate of addition of H2. In method (M2) the amount of the carbon substrate is sufficient to generate reduced NADPH through the oxidative pentose phosphate pathway (oxPPP) and convert acetyl-CoA into lipids, but is insufficient to be incorporated into the lipids as a major component. The proportion of carbon substrate to acetate is controlled by: changing the amount of carbon substrate in the fermentation medium; altering the relative rate at which the fermentation medium containing the carbon substrate is added into the culture vessel. The rate at which the carbon substrate is added to the culture vessel is less than 0.03 grams of carbon substrate per gram of cells (dry weight) per hour, where the initial supplied concentration of acetate is at least 34 g/l. In method (M4) the concentration of the preferentially utilized carbon substrate: is maintained in the limiting amount during the culturing of the microbial cells, where catabolite repression is not induced; provides NADPH and/or another cofactor with reducing capabilities to the microbial cells, optionally a ferredoxin, NADH, and/or flavin adenine dinucleotide (FADH2); provides ATP and/or another cofactor with high-energy phosphate bond(s) to the microbial cells, preferably guanosine triphosphate and/or pyrophosphate Preferred Components: The carbon substrate is sufficient to permit the cells to ATP necessary for cellular maintenance, but insufficient to be incorporated into the organic compound as a major component. The carbon substrate comprises at least one sugar comprising glucose, fructose and/or xylose. The acetate provides the acetyl-CoA and ATP necessary for converting acetyl-CoA into lipids. The amount of acetate in the culture vessel is not depleted during lipid production. The bioproducts are oleochemicals, polyketides or mevalonate pathway derived natural products. The mevalonate pathway derived natural products are terpenoids or its derivatives. In method (M4) The concentration of the preferentially utilized carbon substrate: is maintained in the limiting amount during the culturing of the microbial cells, where catabolite repression is not induced; provides NADPH and/or another cofactor with reducing capabilities to the microbial cells, optionally a ferredoxin, NADH, and/or flavin adenine dinucleotide (FADH2); provides ATP and/or another cofactor with high-energy phosphate bond(s) to the microbial cells, preferably guanosine triphosphate and/or pyrophosphate; is present at less than 10%, preferably less than 1% of the total mixed carbon substrates; is a part of a multi-substrate mixture; becomes accessible to the microbial cells by controlled biological, chemical, or thermal decomposition of naturally occurring multisubstrate mixture such as biomass; and is maintained at low or negligible concentrations while being supplied continuously or semi-continuously to the reactor.The multi-substrate mixture is a formulated mixture, optionally a defined culture mixed-substrate media, or a naturally occurring mixture, optionally a hemicellulosic biomass.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The culture vessel is a semi-batch reactor or continuous reactor. The continuous reactor is a continuous flow stirred-tank reactor or a continuous bubble column reactor.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

PT P
PN CN111604069-A
TI Preparation of copper-based carbon dioxide            electrocatalytic material by mixing oxidant solution            and organic ligand solution, putting metallic copper,            oxidizing, cleaning, soaking in second mixed solution,            and heating.
AU LIN P
   CHEN C
   XU Z
   ZENG X
AE UNIV SHENZHEN (UYSZ-C)
GA 202087332V
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of copper-based carbon dioxide                electrocatalytic material comprises mixing oxidant                solution and organic ligand solution, putting                metallic copper until organic ligand is adsorbed on                specific crystal plane of metallic copper,                oxidizing, cleaning metallic copper to remove                organic ligands adsorbed on surface, soaking in                second mixed solution containing reducing agent and                halogen ion salt, and heating.
   USE - The method is for preparation of copper-based                carbon dioxide electrocatalytic material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the catalytic performance                of catalyst and selectivity to multi-carbon                products.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                copper-based carbon dioxide electrocatalytic                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The reducing agent is                  tartaric acid, nitrilotriacetic acid,                  hydroxylamine hydrochloride and/or sodium                  borohydride; and has concentration of 1 mM-1 M.                  The halogen ion salt is sodium chloride, sodium                  bromide, sodium iodide, potassium chloride,                  potassium bromide, potassium iodide, cesium                  chloride, cesium bromide and/or cesium iodide;                  and has concentration of 0.01 mM-2 M. The oxidant                  solution is hydrogen peroxide solution, potassium                  persulfate solution, sodium persulfate solution,                  ammonium persulfate solution and/or potassium                  persulfate solution. Preferred Method: The                  heating is done at 25-90 degrees C for 5-120                  minutes.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Components: The metallic copper is                  copper sheet, copper powder, copper block, copper                  mesh and/or copper wire.

AU CUI J
   WANG W
   ZHENG J
   YANG S
   LIU H
   WU Z
AE CANGZHOU ZHONGRUN CHEM ADDITIVES CO LTD (CANG-Non-standard)
GA 2022A61341
AB    NOVELTY - 1-acryloyloxy-2-pyrrolidone-acrylamide-methyl                4-vinylbenzoate ternary polymer based oil                displacement additive (I) is new. The oil                displacement additive is a ternary polymer obtained                by polymerizing three monomers,                1-acryloyloxy-2-pyrrolidone, acrylamide and methyl                4-vinylbenzoate.
   USE - (I) is useful for enhancing mutual solubility                of carbon dioxide and crude oil in heavy oil                reservoir flooding and exploitation, and reducing                viscosity of crude oil (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I): solves problems of high cost of                oil-soluble viscosity reducer and high cost of                oil-soluble viscosity reducer in method of using                oil-soluble viscosity reducer and carbon dioxide                composite huff and puff compared with existing                additive and solves problem is that the dosage is                large and solubility of carbon dioxide in heavy                oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - 1-acryloyloxy-2-pyrrolidone-acrylamide-methyl                4-vinylbenzoate ternary polymer based oil                displacement additive of formula (I) is new. The                oil displacement additive is a ternary polymer                obtained by polymerizing three monomers,                1-acryloyloxy-2-pyrrolidone, acrylamide and methyl                4-vinylbenzoate.
   x = 100-300;
   y = 20-100; and
   z = 100-300.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   1. preparation of (I); and
   2. use of (I) for enhancing mutual solubility                of carbon dioxide and crude oil in heavy oil                reservoir flooding and exploitation, and reducing                viscosity of crude oil.
                                                                                                                     
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of                  (I), comprises (i) adding                  1-acryloyloxy-2-pyrrolidone, acrylamide and                  methyl 4-vinylbenzoate into a reaction vessel,                  then adding N,N dimethylformamide, stirring and                  dissolving, and adding initiator for reaction to                  obtain mixture, and (ii) reacting obtained                  mixture at 60-80&#176;C for 1.5-2 hours to                  obtain oil displacement additive.Preferred Method: The method further                  includes post-processing after completing step                  (ii), subjecting obtained reaction solution to                  rotary evaporation to remove N,N                  dimethylformamide, then precipitating with                  n-hexane, filtering and drying to obtain                  oil-displacing additive.Preferred Components: The number-average                  molecular weight of oil displacement additive is                  30,000-100,000. The molar ratio between                  1-acryloyloxy-2-pyrrolidone, acrylamide and                  methyl 4-vinylbenzoate is 6-15:1-5:6-15. The                  initiator is benzoyl peroxide or                  azobisisobutyronitrile. Volume of the original saturated                petroleum is measured under the experimental                temperature and pressure conditions. Initial pore                structure characteristics and fluid distribution                characteristics of the plunger core sample are                obtained. Computed tomography (CT) scan is                performed after injecting carbon dioxide at the                preset pressure. Simmering process is performed                simultaneously. Different scanning times are set                according to the preset simmering time. Volume of                displaced oil is measured under standard                conditions. Replacement efficiency is                calculated.
   USE - Method for experimenting exchange law of                carbon dioxide after dry fracturing reservoir                fluid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables comparing and analyzing the                pore structure and the fluid distribution                characteristic before and after injecting carbon                dioxide to obtain the target area reservoir fluid                replacement rule in an effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Pore structure characteristics and fluid                distribution characteristics of the fractured core                are obtained to obtain fluid replacement law of a                reservoir in the target area. A fluid replacement                law of the reservoir fluid in the target area is                applied to petroleum development.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of a plunger core sample. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

TI Catalyst precursor used for producing detergents, drugs, fine chemicals, lubricants, plasticizers, and polymers in chemical industry, comprises iron species, alkali metal or salt, and complexing agent.
AU YAO B
   EDWARDS P P
   XIAO T
AE UNIV OXFORD INNOVATION LTD (UYOX-C)
   UNIV OXFORD INNOVATION LTD (UYOX-C)
   UNIV OXFORD INNOVATION LTD (UYOX-C)
GA 202098085R
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst precursor comprises iron species, alkali metal or salt, and complexing agent.
   USE - Catalyst precursor used for producing detergents, lubricants, plasticizers, drugs, fine chemicals and polymers in chemical industry.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst precursor enables to improve carbon dioxide, or carbon monoxide conversion, and improves the hydrocarbon yields such as olefins or jet fuels.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing catalyst precursor, which involves mixing iron species, alkali metal, or salt, complexing agent, and solvent; agitating mixture to provide homogenous mixture; heating obtained mixture to partially remove solvent; and forming slurry, or paste;
   (2) a catalyst, which is suitable for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, or carbon monoxide, which is obtained by activation of catalyst precursor, or method for preparing catalyst precursor;
   (3) a method for preparing catalyst, which involves providing catalyst precursor, subjecting catalyst precursor to calcination, and activating precursor;
   (4) a process for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, which involves contacting feedstock provided by hydrogen, and carbon dioxide with catalyst precursor, or catalyst at elevated temperature and pressure;
   (5) a process for hydrogenation of carbon monoxide, which involves contacting feedstock provided with hydrogen, and carbon monoxide with catalyst precursor, or catalyst at elevated temperature, and pressure; and
   (6) a process for production of olefins, which involves contacting feedstock, which is provided by hydrogen and carbon monoxide, or hydrogen and carbon dioxide with catalyst precursor, or catalyst at elevated temperature, and pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alkali metal comprises potassium, sodium, lithium and caesium. The iron species comprises iron nitrate salt, preferably iron (II) nitrate, or iron (III) nitrate, iron powder. The transition metals selected from manganese (Mn), zinc (Zn), cobalt (Co), copper (Cu), iron (Fe), potassium (K), or salts, oxides or hydroxides. The catalyst precursor comprises iron (III) nitrate, manganese (II) nitrate, potassium carbonate, cobalt nitrate, and sodium carbonate. Preferred Conditions: The molar ratio of Fe and alkali metal is approximately 20:1-4:1, preferably approximately 10:1. The molar ratio of Fe and Mn is approximately 100:1-4:1, preferably approximately 10:1. Preferred Method: The method for preparing catalyst precursor involves mixing Fe powder, alkali metal such as K, Na, or Li, complexing agent such as citric acid, or salt, and transition metal such as Mn, Co, or salt, oxide or hydroxide; and agitating obtained mixture to form homogenous mixture. The method for preparing catalyst involves reducing precursor in the exposure of carbon monoxide, and hydrogen.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The complexing agent comprises one or more functional groups, which is selected from carboxylic acids, hydroxyl groups, amide groups, amino groups, citric acid, tartaric acid, oxalic acid, ethylenediaminetetraacetic acid (EDTA), nitroilotiracetic acid (NTA), diethylenetriaminepentaacetic acid (DTPA), and N-(2-hydroxyethyl)ethylenediamine-N,N',N'-triacetic acid (HEDTA), or salt.

PT P
PN CN110229354-A; CN110229354-B
TI Dissolving cellulose, involves mixing cellulose, organic alkali and solvent, introducing carbon dioxide gas into solid-liquid mixture, contacting solid-liquid mixture, stirring, dissolving and obtaining cellulose solution.
AU HUANG J
   LIU F
   NA H
   WANG J
   ZHU J
AE NINGBO INST MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY & ENG C (CANM-C)
GA 201980523W
AB    NOVELTY - Method for dissolving cellulose, involves (i) uniformly mixing cellulose, organic alkali and solvent in a dissolving device with an open environment to obtain a solid-liquid mixture, (ii) continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas into the solid-liquid mixture, while continuously stirring the solid-liquid mixture, (iii) fully contacting the solid-liquid mixture with carbon dioxide, and (iv) stirring and dissolving at 20-80 degrees C for 0.1-4 hours to obtain a cellulose solution.
   USE - The method is useful for dissolving cellulose.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has less requirements on equipment and process, low temperature and pressure, high dissolution rate, high efficiency and sufficient dissolution for cellulose, and is safe and reliable. The obtained cellulose solution has high stability and is suitable for large-scale continuous molding processing, production application, storage and transportation. The device is simple and convenient to operate, does not need to adopt a closed environment and realizes rapid and complete dissolution of cellulose in the dissolving device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a system for dissolving cellulose, which comprises a dissolving device with an open environment comprising a solid-liquid mixture comprising uniformly mixed cellulose, organic alkali and solvent, a stirring mechanism for continuously stirring the solid-liquid mixture, a carbon dioxide supply mechanism for continuously introducing carbon dioxide gas into the solid-liquid mixture, and a condensing device arranged at an outlet end of the dissolving device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic alkali comprises a nitrogen-containing heterocyclic compound, preferably 1,8-diazabicyclo-bis(5.4.0)-7-undecene and/or 1,5-diazabicyclo(4.3.0)-5-nonene. The solvent comprises dimethyl sulfoxide, N-methylpyrrolidone, N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-diethylacetamide and/or toluene.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide gas is continuously introduced into the solid-liquid mixture from below the liquid level, and/or the solid-liquid mixture is stirred at 1-1000 rpm. The method further involves cooling the gas discharged from the dissolving device by adopting the condensing device. Preferred Condition: The stirring and dissolving step is carried out at 40-50 degrees C for 0.5-2 hours. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of cellulose to solvent is (1:100)-(60:100), preferably (8:100)-(20:100). The molar ratio of organic alkali to anhydroglucose unit in cellulose is (1:1)-(10:1), preferably (2.5:1)-(4:1). Preferred Component: The cellulose comprises cellulose, microcrystalline cellulose and/or alpha -cellulose extracted from herbal or woody plant fibers.

   WALTER J C

   UNIV WASHINGTON STATE (UNWS-C)
GA 201944813T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves heating a first volume of                supercritical carbon dioxide to a first                temperature. A second volume of supercritical                carbon dioxide is heated to a second temperature                such that the second temperature is greater than                the first temperature. A feedstock containing                impurity is provided. The impurity is extracted                from the feedstock using the first volume of                supercritical carbon dioxide at the first                temperature. A thermal decomposition operation is                performed on the feedstock, after extracting and                the thermal decomposition operation is performed                using the second volume of supercritical carbon                dioxide at the second temperature.
   USE - Method for pre treating feedstock prior to                thermal decomposition of feedstock.
   ADVANTAGE - The bio-oil or a hydrotreated product is                extracted from the volume of supercritical fluid by                reducing the pressure of the supercritical fluid,                thus causing the bio-oil or hydrotreated product to                fall out of the supercritical fluid. The ability to                co-utilize or switch between types of feedstock                improves the utilization of available resources and                the overall system economics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of drying feedstock prior to thermal decomposition                of the feedstock.

AB    NOVELTY - A granular dry ice manufacturing apparatus comprises a drum mixer (1) having an opening portion (2) at one side and having inner surface in the shape of polygon including hexagon or octagon, and a spray head (11) capable of ejecting liquefied carbon dioxide from the opening towards the interior of the drum mixer. The rotation speed is adjusted by the drum mixer. The opening portion is inclined obliquely upwards from the horizontal, and granular dry ice and gasified carbon dioxide gas are discharged from the opening.
   USE - Apparatus for manufacturing granular dry ice (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus enables manufacture of granular dry ice without using press machine, piston and screw cylinder for compaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for manufacture of granular dry ice, which involves spraying liquefied carbon dioxide gas from the spray head during rotation of the drum mixer, rotating the generated dry ice snow inside the drum mixer to form granules, adjusting the tilt and rotation speed of the device to adjust the grain to the desired size, and discharging granular dry ice from the opening.

PN CN107556190-A
TI Synthesizing diphenyl ethyl carbonate comprises reacting 2-bromoethyl benzene, cesium carbonate and organic solvent, introducing carbon dioxide, monitoring 2-bromoethyl benzene concentration, filtering and concentrating.
AU SUN J
   DONG Z
   LU J
AE UNIV LIAONING SHIHUA (ULNS-C)
GA 201804808E
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing diphenyl ethyl carbonate comprises (a) dissolving 2-bromoethyl benzene, cesium carbonate and an organic solvent in a reaction container (b) introducing carbon dioxide under normal pressure until the reaction container has carbon dioxide atmosphere (c) placing the reaction container, stirring, reacting, monitoring 2-bromoethyl benzene concentration in the reaction solution in the reaction process, monitoring 2-bromoethyl benzene concentration after completely reaction and (d) cooling the reaction liquid to room temperature, filtering and concentrating.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing diphenyl ethyl carbonate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: avoids high toxicity agents; is safe; uses low cost raw materials; improves the cleanness of the industrial synthetic reaction; reduces the environmental pollution; uses one-step raw material type; does not require catalyst; and improves the economical efficiency of the process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing diphenyl ethyl carbonate comprises (a) dissolving 2-bromoethyl benzene, cesium carbonate and an organic solvent in a ratio of 1 mol: 1-2 mol: 5-10 ml in a reaction container (b) introducing carbon dioxide under normal pressure until the reaction container has carbon dioxide atmosphere (c) placing the reaction container at 50-80 degrees C, stirring at 500-1000 revolutions/minute, reacting, monitoring 2-bromoethyl benzene concentration in the reaction solution in the reaction process, monitoring 2-bromoethyl benzene concentration after completely reaction and (d) cooling the reaction liquid to room temperature, filtering and concentrating.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is anhydrous acetonitrile. The stirring in the step (c) uses magnetic stirring process. The concentration of 2-bromoethyl benzene in the reaction process is monitored by thin-layer chromatography analysis.

TI Preparation of solvent-resistant myricetin            ultrafine particles involves dissolving myricetin            substance in organic solvent, passing carbon dioxide            into crystallizer, spraying myricetin solution,            releasing pressure, and collecting product.
AU WANG Z
   CHEN Z
   YAO Y
   WANG C
   ZHAN J
   DAI L
   HUANG D
   CHEN W
   TAO Y
AE UNIV CHINA PHARM (UYCP-C)
GA 2018A5445Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of myricetin ultrafine particles                comprises weighing myricetin drug substance and                dissolving in mixed organic solvent, where mixed                organic solvent is a mixture of acetone and                dimethyl sulfoxide at volume ratio of 19:1; passing                carbon dioxide into crystallizer at a certain flow                rate to adjust the temperature and pressure;                continuously passing carbon dioxide, keeping                temperature and pressure, and spraying myricetin                solution into the crystallizer; and continuously                passing carbon dioxide, releasing pressure, opening                crystallizer, and collecting product.
   USE - Method for preparing solvent-resistant                myricetin ultrafine particles.
   ADVANTAGE - The particles are fluffy and cotton-shaped and                improves elution rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The crystallization is                  done at 45 degrees C and 11 MPa.

PT P
PN WO2020114899-A1; AU2019393943-A1; CA3120939-A1; SG11202105556-A1; CN113165883-A; EP3891098-A1; US2022048776-A1; AU2019393943-B2
TI Converting carbon dioxide and hydrogen into a product stream comprising carbon monoxide, water and hydrogen, involves introducing carbon dioxide, hydrogen and oxygen into a reaction vessel, and performing a reverse water gas shift reaction.
AU BALAJI S P
   KLOKKENBURG M
   SCHOUWENAAR R
   QUEVEDO ENRIQUEZ J A
   QUEVEDO E J A
AE SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
   SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL INT RES MIJ BV (SHEL-C)
   SHELL OIL CO (SHEL-C)
GA 202050741P
AB    NOVELTY - Converting carbon dioxide and hydrogen into a product stream comprising carbon monoxide, water and hydrogen, involves introducing carbon dioxide, hydrogen and oxygen into a reaction vessel, and performing a reverse water gas shift reaction at elevated temperature, where no catalyst is present in the reaction vessel. A gas stream comprising carbon dioxide, a hydrogen rich gas stream and an oxygen rich gas stream are introduced into the reaction vessel in separate feed streams, where the hydrogen rich gas stream is introduced into the reaction vessel at a temperature between 15 and 450 degrees C. The hydrogen rich gas stream and oxygen rich gas stream being introduced in close vicinity of each other, where at least the hydrogen rich gas stream and the oxygen rich gas stream are introduced into the reaction vessel through a burner comprising coaxial channels for the separate introduction of the different gas streams.
   USE - Method for converting carbon dioxide and hydrogen into a product stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to convert carbon dioxide and hydrogen into a product stream, that is useful in reducing the carbon footprint of certain industrial technologies, and efficiently convert carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide at high temperatures at a large scale.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Converting carbon dioxide and hydrogen into a product stream comprising carbon monoxide, water and hydrogen, involves introducing carbon dioxide, hydrogen and oxygen into a reaction vessel, and performing a reverse water gas shift reaction at elevated temperature, where no catalyst is present in the reaction vessel. A gas stream comprising carbon dioxide, a hydrogen rich gas stream and an oxygen rich gas stream are introduced into the reaction vessel in separate feed streams, where the hydrogen rich gas stream is introduced into the reaction vessel at a temperature between 15 and 450 degrees C. The hydrogen rich gas stream and oxygen rich gas stream being introduced in close vicinity of each other, where at least the hydrogen rich gas stream and the oxygen rich gas stream are introduced into the reaction vessel through a burner comprising coaxial channels for the separate introduction of the different gas streams. The burner being located at the top of the reaction vessel, where the hydrogen and oxygen in the hydrogen rich gas stream and oxygen rich gas stream undergo a combustion reaction upon entering the reaction vessel, hence provided the heating energy required for the reverse water-gas shift reaction. The temperature in the reaction vessel is maintained in the range of 1000-1500 degrees C by varying the molar ratio of hydrogen to oxygen, which are introduced into the reaction vessel in the hydrogen rich gas stream and oxygen rich gas stream, respectively. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a reverse water-gas shift process unit, which comprises at least one reaction vessel operatively connected to a cooling unit, and further comprising a gas introduction line connected to a water splitter unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The hydrogen rich gas stream and oxygen rich gas stream are introduced into the reaction vessel in close vicinity of but not next to each other. The reaction vessel is preheated to a temperature ranging up to 1100 degrees C. The product stream leaving the hot reaction vessel is cooled with water to provide a cooled product mixture comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen, steam and optionally unconverted carbon dioxide. The hydrogen rich gas stream and/or at least portion of the oxygen rich gas stream in the feed is obtained from a water splitter. The oxygen rich gas stream from the water splitter is liquified and re gasified before use as feed. The steam is separated from the product mixture and then further cooled to the dew point to provide liquid water which is recycled to a water splitter to produce hydrogen and/or oxygen.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

TI Realizing sensible heat recovery and tail gas            utilization of sintering lime by methane reforming            comprises e.g. introducing methane and carbon dioxide            into kiln from bottom to the bottom of kiln cooling            zone.
AU WANG G
   WANG J
   XUE Q
   JIANG Z
AE UNIV BEIJING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UNBS-C)
GA 201924907T
AB    NOVELTY - Realizing sensible heat recovery and tail gas                utilization of sintering lime by methane reforming                comprises introducing methane and carbon dioxide                into the kiln from the bottom to the bottom of kiln                cooling zone, realizing the cooling and sensible                heat recovery of sintering lime by methane                reforming reaction, the obtained product is                high-quality quicklime and synthesis gas is rich in                carbon monoxide and hydrogen, the method                specifically comprises (i) preheating the carbon                dioxide and methane at room temperature,                introducing from the lower part of the kiln,                gradually heating the carbon dioxide and methane,                gradually cooling the temperature of the lime, the                temperature of the lime is higher than the                temperature of the gas introduced, the volumetric                flow of carbon dioxide and methane is the same, and                the total flow rate of the raw material gas is                40-80 m3/t lime, and (ii) when the raw material gas                temperature reaches 700 degrees C, the gas                interacts with the supported catalyst.
   USE - The method is useful for realizing sensible                heat recovery and tail gas utilization of sintering                lime by methane reforming.
   ADVANTAGE - The reforming product is rich in carbon                monoxide and hydrogen. The method realizes fast                cooling of burned lime and sensible heat recovery,                reducing carbon dioxide emission, is simple,                economical, ensures the goo production of lime kiln                and high quality of lime, has good economic benefit                and environmental protection benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Realizing sensible heat recovery and tail gas                utilization of sintering lime by methane reforming                comprises introducing methane and carbon dioxide                into the kiln from the bottom to the bottom of kiln                cooling zone, realizing the cooling and sensible                heat recovery of sintering lime by methane                reforming reaction, where the obtained product is                high-quality quicklime and synthesis gas is rich in                carbon monoxide and hydrogen, the method                specifically comprises (i) preheating the carbon                dioxide and methane at room temperature,                introducing from the lower part of the kiln,                gradually heating the carbon dioxide and methane,                gradually cooling the temperature of the lime, the                temperature of the lime is higher than the                temperature of the gas introduced, the volumetric                flow of carbon dioxide and methane is the same, and                the total flow rate of the raw material gas is                40-80 m3/t lime, and (ii) when the raw material gas                temperature reaches 700 degrees C, the gas                interacts with the supported catalyst, where the                carbon dioxide and methane is converted into carbon                monoxide and hydrogen, each cubic meter of raw                material gas absorbs 5,000-6,000 kJ heat at the                same time, rapidly reducing the temperature of lime                corresponding, forming high quality product with                activity of not less than 320.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic flow chart of a                method for realizing sensible heat recovery and                tail gas utilization of sintering lime by methane                reforming (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method, the carbon                  dioxide comes from the lime kiln tail gas, the                  mixed feed gas is composed of carbon dioxide and                  methane enters the kiln for heat exchange from                  the lower part of the lime kiln through the heat                  exchange pipe, adjusting the heat exchange rate                  by adjusting the diameter and shape of the heat                  exchange tube, the pipeline is located in the                  area of 700-1150 degrees C is filled with alumina                  honeycomb porous material, and reforming catalyst                  is supported in the porous material. The gas                  obtained after reforming can be used in chemical                  synthesis, combustion power generation, iron ore                  reduction process, and H2/CO can be improved by                  mixing with coke oven gas to improve its                  efficiency for chemical synthesis and iron ore                  reduction.

PN CN114436703-A
TI Capturing carbon dioxide and co-producing granular            multi-element sulfur-carbon-based composite nitrogen            fertilizer, comprises preparing powdery multi-element            sulfur-carbon-based composite nitrogen fertilizer,            adding stabilizer composed of plant nutrients, and            mixing uniformly.
AU WANG J
AE NANJING WOYE CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202272767F
AB    NOVELTY - Capturing carbon dioxide and co-producing                granular multi-element sulfur-carbon-based                composite nitrogen fertilizer, comprises preparing                aqueous ammonia, carrying out gas-liquid reaction                with aqueous ammonia, absorbing and neutralizing                carbon dioxide, sulfur dioxide, sulfur trioxide and                nitrogen dioxide in the flue gas to generate                ammonium carbonate, ammonium sulfite, ammonium                sulfate and nitric acid respectively, reacting                nitric acid with ammonia to form ammonium nitrate,                centrifuging to obtain a mixture of powdered                ammonium bicarbonate, ammonium sulfate and ammonium                nitrate with ammonium bicarbonate as main                ingredient i.e. powdery multi-element                sulfur-carbon-based composite nitrogen fertilizer,                adding stabilizer composed of plant nutrients that                can improve the stability of ammonium ions in                powdery multi-element sulfur-carbon-based compound                nitrogen fertilizer as main raw material, and                mixing uniformly.
   USE - The method is capturing carbon dioxide and                co-producing granular multi-element                sulfur-carbon-based composite nitrogen                fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts simple equipment; has                reliable technology and low treatment cost; can                directly realize the recycling and utilization of                carbon dioxide resources; ensures the products are                directly used in agriculture, forestry, and                grassland production, and are suitable for various                soils and crops, which can greatly increase crop                yield, improve the quality of agricultural                products, and realize the recycling of carbon                dioxide, sulfur oxides and nitrogen oxides.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Capturing carbon dioxide and co-producing                granular multi-element sulfur-carbon-based                composite nitrogen fertilizer, comprises making                liquid ammonia and water into a certain                concentration of aqueous ammonia based on a certain                proportion, and placing it in a carbonization                neutralization absorption tower, de-dusting the                flue gas discharged from the chimney and purifying,                fully oxidizing the sulfur oxide (SOX) and nitrogen                oxide (NOX) in the flue gas by ventilation, and                performing countercurrent into the carbonization                absorption tower, carrying out gas-liquid reaction                with aqueous ammonia, absorbing and neutralizing                carbon dioxide, sulfur dioxide, sulfur trioxide and                nitrogen dioxide in the flue gas to generate                ammonium carbonate, ammonium sulfite, ammonium                sulfate and nitric acid respectively, where the                ammonium carbonate further absorbs carbon dioxide                to form ammonium bicarbonate, further forcing                ammonium sulfite to oxidize through ventilation to                form ammonium sulfate, and further reacting nitric                acid with ammonia to form ammonium nitrate, placing                it out from the bottom of the tower to carry out                solid-liquid separation to obtain mixed material                having high water content after the solution                reaches supersaturation to form a certain amount of                crystals, placing the mixture to a centrifuge for                centrifugation to obtain a mixture of powdered                ammonium bicarbonate, ammonium sulfate and ammonium                nitrate with ammonium bicarbonate as the main                ingredient and a water content of less than 5%,                i.e. powdery multi-element sulfur-carbon-based                compound nitrogen fertilizer, adding certain amount                of compound stabilizer composed of plant nutrients                that can improve the stability of ammonium ions in                powdery multi-element sulfur-carbon-based compound                nitrogen fertilizer as the main raw material,                mixing uniformly, granulating through a non-drying                counter-roll extrusion granulator under normal                temperature and pressure to obtain granular                multi-element sulfur-carbon-based compound nitrogen                fertilizer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The ammonium ion composite                  stabilizer is prepared by separately crushing and                  pre-processing raw materials including magnesium                  sulfate, calcined dolomite powder, monoammonium                  phosphate, humate, and alkyl sulfonate,                  preferably (1) subjecting the raw materials to                  pulverization and pre-treating, where the                  particle size of the raw materials is 60-120 mesh                  after pulverization and pretreatment, and (2)                  pulverizing the pretreated magnesium sulfate,                  calcined dolomite powder, monoammonium phosphate,                  humate, and alkyl sulfonate in a mass ratio of                  10-20:8-15:10-20:8-20:10-30 and mixing well.                  Preferred Components: The ammonia solution                  contains 15-50 wt.% ammonia; the pressure in the                  carbonization absorption tower is 0.25-2.0 MPa,                  and the temperature of carbonization absorption                  tower is 15-45&#8451;; it is pumped out from                  the bottom of the tower with a pump after the                  solution reaches supersaturation and forms 25-48%                  solid suspension; the powdery multi-element                  sulfur-carbon-based compound nitrogen fertilizer                  and the ammonium ion compound stabilizer are                  mixed in a mass ratio of 60-98:2-30. The ammonium                  ion composite stabilizer is composed of magnesium                  sulfate, calcined dolomite powder, monoammonium                  phosphate, humate and/or alkyl sulfonate; the                  humate is composed of potassium humate, magnesium                  humate and/or calcium humate; and the alkyl                  sulfonate is dodecyl sulfonate or pentadecyl                  sulfonate; and the alkyl sulfonate is a mixture                  of potassium alkyl sulfonate, calcium alkyl                  sulfonate and/or magnesium alkyl sulfonate. The                  dimple diameter of pair of roller extrusion                  granulator is &#966;1.5-15 mm; the products                  having diameter of &#966;1.5-2.5 mm are used                  for flower fertilization; the products having                  diameter of &#966;2.5-8 mm are used for                  fertilizing crops and cash crops; and the                  products having a diameter of &#966;8-15 mm                  are used for fertilization of fruit trees and                  forestry.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer
DC C04 (Fertilisers - including urea and phosphoric acid production. Also soil modifiers and plant growth media. Chemical aspects of compost production.); C07 (Apparatus, formulation, general. including veterinary syringes, general formulations where the active compound is not central to the invention (e.g. wettable powders) and analysis.)

PT P
PN CN113030043-A; CN113030043-B
TI Preparation of aggregation-induced emission-type polymer gold cluster material for detecting carbon dioxide, involves reacting tris(aminoethyl)amine and diacryloyl cystamine, and introducing tetraphenylethylene epoxide into polyamide-amine.
AU WANG Y
   LI C
   XIAO Y
   CHEN Y
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 202176421X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of aggregation-induced emission (AIE)-type hyperbranched polymer gold cluster material involves mixing benzophenone and 4-hydroxy benzophenone, adding tetrahydrofuran, stirring, performing coupling reaction to prepare tetraphenylethylene-alcohol, mixing tetraphenylethylene-alcohol and epoxy chloropropane, adding dibutanone, by substitution reaction to generate tetraphenylethylene epoxy compound, using tris(2-aminoethyl) amine and diacryloyl cystamine, reacting by Michael addition to obtain disulfide bond-containing hyperbranched polyamide-amine, introducing tetraphenylethylene epoxy compound into the hyperbranched polyamide-amine to obtain disulfide bond-containing hyperbranched polyamide-amine with AIE effect, dripping acetic anhydride into hyperbranched polyamide-amine with AIE effect, reacting at room temperature to obtain acetylated hyperbranched polyamide-amine, and using acylated hyperbranched polyamide-amine as template and reducing agent to reduce chloroauric acid.
   USE - Preparation of AIE-type hyperbranched polymer gold cluster material used in quantitative detection of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses simple and fast fluorescent response, which can realize the proportion type detection of carbon dioxide gas, so as to avoid complex complicated operation steps, long detection time and high-cost device and loss of experimental material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of AIE-type hyperbranched polymer gold cluster material involves mixing benzophenone and 4-hydroxy benzophenone, in the molar ratio of 1:1, adding tetrahydrofuran solvent, stirring and dissolving, performing coupling reaction to prepare tetraphenylethylene-alcohol, mixing tetraphenylethylene-alcohol and epoxy chloropropane, in the molar ratio of 4.3:5, adding dibutanone solvent, by substitution reaction to generate tetraphenylethylene epoxy compound, using tris(2-aminoethyl) amine as third monomer and diacryloyl cystamine as second monomer, in the molar ratio of 1.1:1, continuously reacting under nitrogen atmosphere for 96 hours, by Michael addition to obtain disulfide bond-containing hyperbranched polyamide-amine, introducing tetraphenylethylene epoxy compound into the hyperbranched polyamide-amine to obtain disulfide bond-containing hyperbranched polyamide-amine with AIE effect, dripping acetic anhydride into the hyperbranched polyamide-amine with AIE effect, reacting for 24 hours at room temperature to obtain acetylated hyperbranched polyamide-amine, and using acylated hyperbranched polyamide-amine as template and reducing agent to reduce chloroauric acid. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for applying AIE-type hyperbranched polymer gold cluster material in quantitative detection of carbon dioxide gas, which involves:
   (A) continuously introducing pure carbon dioxide gas into 30 mL secondary distilled water under standard atmospheric pressure for 0.5 hour, where the solution after introducing is taken as saturated solution of carbon dioxide gas;
   (B) adding the saturated solution of different volume to 1 mL solution of AIE-type hyperbranched polymer gold cluster material;
   (C) using secondary distilled water to ensure the volume of the solution is 2 mL;
   (D) obtaining aqueous solution with carbon dioxide concentration gradient change;
   (E) using fluorescence spectrophotometer to detect the fluorescence of series sample; and
   (F) finally obtaining linear rule of the carbon dioxide concentration and double fluorescent material fluorescence ratio, where the linear rule reflects the linear relation of the AIE-type hyperbranched polymer gold cluster material fluorescence ratio and carbon dioxide concentration change.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the AIE type hyperbranched polymer gold cluster material. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The preparation method further involves testing different reaction temperature, fluorescence ratio of the AIE-type hyperbranched polymer gold cluster material under different reaction time and different reaction pH.
