PN US2019291088-A1; US11065608-B2
TI New multidentate ligand having substituted imidazol-yl-methyl-propane-diyl-bis-imidazole- compounds, used to prepare metal complex i.e. used in catalyst e.g. to catalyze e.g. hydrosilylation of styrenes and hydroboration of styrenes.
AU PARKIN G
   RAUCH M
   RUCCOLO S
AE UNIV COLUMBIA NEW YORK (UYCO-C)
   UNIV COLUMBIA NEW YORK (UYCO-C)
GA 201982126C
AB    NOVELTY - Multidentate ligand having substituted 2,2'-(2-((1H-imidazol-2-yl)methyl)propane-1,3-diyl)bis(1H-imidazole) compounds (I) is new.
   USE - The ligand is useful for preparing metal complex, where the metal complex is useful in a catalyst for catalyzing hydrosilylation of styrenes, catalyzing hydroboration of styrenes, catalyzing hydrosilylation of carbon dioxide, preparing a formaldehyde equivalent from carbon dioxide, reducing carbon monoxide, catalyzing hydrogenation of alkenes or alkynes, catalyzing polymerization of alkenes, catalyzing production of hydrogen-on-demand from alcohols or amines, catalyzing hydrosilylation of ketones or aldehydes, catalyzing Tishchenko reaction, catalyzing hydrogenation of carbon dioxide and catalyzing hydrogenation of carbon monoxide (all claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Multidentate ligand having substituted 2,2'-(2-((1H-imidazol-2-yl)methyl)propane-1,3-diyl)bis(1H-imidazole) compounds of formula (I) is new.
   R1 = H, halide, alkyl, aryl, aralkyl, heteroalkyl, heteroaryl, alkoxy, OH, heteroalkoxy, NH2, alkylamino, arylamino, CN, NO2, sulfonyl or heterocyclic group; either
   R2, R3 = H, halide, alkyl, aryl, aralkyl, heteroalkyl, heteroaryl, alkoxy, OH, heteroalkoxy, NH2, alkylamino, arylamino, CN, NO2, sulfonyl or heterocyclic group; or
   R2R3 = optionally saturated 5-7C homocyclic or heterocyclic ring, where the ring is optionally substituted by H, halide, alkyl, aryl, aralkyl, heteroalkyl, heteroaryl, alkoxy, OH, heteroalkoxy, NH2, alkylamino, arylamino, CN, NO2, sulfonyl or heterocyclic group; and
   Z = linker group.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing (M1) a metal complex, comprising coordinating the ligand to a metal center via a combination of up to 3 nitrogen donors and the carbon atom;
   (2) a metal complex prepared by the method, where the metal complex has substituted compounds of formula (II);
   (3) catalyzing (M2) hydrosilylation of styrenes, comprising providing the catalyst to a hydrosilylation reaction of a styrene;
   (4) catalyzing (M3) hydroboration of styrenes, comprising providing the catalyst to a hydroboration reaction of a styrene;
   (5) catalyzing (M4) hydrosilylation of carbon dioxide, comprising providing the catalyst to a hydrosilylation reaction of carbon dioxide;
   (6) preparing (M5) a formaldehyde equivalent from carbon dioxide, comprising contacting a reaction mixture comprising carbon dioxide and a silane with a compound prepared from the ligand of claim 1, where the silane comprises substituted silanes (R3SiH) (IA);
   (7) reducing (M6) carbon monoxide, comprising contacting the reaction mixture comprising carbon monoxide with the compound prepared from the ligand;
   (8) catalyzing (M7) hydrogenation of alkenes or alkynes, comprising providing the catalyst to a hydrogenation reaction of an alkene or an alkyne;
   (9) catalyzing (M8) polymerization of alkenes, comprising providing the catalyst to a polymerization reaction of alkenes;
   (10) catalyzing (M9) production of hydrogen-on-demand from alcohols or amines, comprising providing the catalyst to the reaction;
   (11) catalyzing (M10) hydrosilylation of ketones or aldehydes, comprising providing the catalyst to a hydrosilylation reaction of a ketone or an aldehyde;
   (12) catalyzing (M11) Tishchenko reaction, comprising providing the catalyst to the reaction;
   (13) catalyzing (M12) hydrogenation of carbon dioxide, comprising providing the catalyst to a hydrogenation reaction of carbon dioxide; and
   (14) catalyzing (M13) hydrogenation of carbon monoxide, comprising providing the catalyst to a hydrogenation reaction of carbon monoxide.
   M = atom comprising Li, Mg, Ca, Fe, Ni, Cu, Zn, Zr or Cd;
   X = no atom, H, Me, halo, O2CH, S2CH, SH, N(H)Ph, CH(Me)Ph, O2CMe or S2CMe; and
   R = H, alkyl or aryl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal center comprises an atom comprising main group metals, transition metals or lanthanoids. The metal center comprises an atom comprising lithium, magnesium, calcium, iron, nickel, copper, zinc, zirconium or cadmium. The compound comprises the metal complex.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation: No preparation method is given. Preferred Components: Z comprises a silicon-containing group. The metal complex has 17 complexes e.g. lithium containing complex of formula (III), zinc containing complex of formula (IV), zinc containing complex of formula (V), magnesium containing complex of formula (VI) and copper containing complex of formula (VII).
   Preferred Definitions:
   Z = Si(R4)2; and
   R4 = H, halide, alkyl, aryl, aralkyl, heteroalkyl, heteroaryl, alkoxy, OH, heteroalkoxy, NH2, alkylamino, arylamino, CN or heterocyclic group.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 1 Compound (I) are specifically claimed i.e. 2-(((dimethyl(1-(propan-2-yl)-1,5-dihydro-1,3-benzodiazol-2-yl)silyl)((dimethyl(1-(propan-2-yl)-3a,4,5,6,7,7a-hexahydro-1H-1,3-benzodiazol-2-yl)silyl))methyl)dimethylsilyl)-1-(propan-2-yl)-1H-1,3-benzodiazole of formula (Ia).
   EXAMPLE - 40 ml Water was added to a solution of 640 mg of lithium containing complex (II) in 25 ml of benzene. Mixture was stirred for 1 hour at room temperature, during which period a precipitate formed. Volatile components were removed in vacuum and the residue obtained was extracted into 25 ml of benzene. Solvent was removed from the extract in vacuum to obtain 2-(((dimethyl(1-(propan-2-yl)-1,5-dihydro-1,3-benzodiazol-2-yl)silyl)((dimethyl(1-(propan-2-yl)-3a,4,5,6,7,7a-hexahydro-1H-1,3-benzodiazol-2-yl)silyl))methyl)dimethylsilyl)-1-(propan-2-yl)-1H-1,3-benzodiazole (Ia) with a yield of 96%.

PN WO2019158983-A1; AU2018408879-A1; EP3752172-A1; US2021077412-A1; EP3752172-A4
TI Delayed release oral dosage form useful for reducing energy intake by human, comprises hops extract.
AU INGRAM J R
   WALKER E G
AE NEW ZEALAND INST PLANT & FOOD RES LTD (NZPL-Non-standard)
   NEW ZEALAND INST PLANT & FOOD RES LTD (NZPL-Non-standard)
   NEW ZEALAND PLANT & FOOD RES INST LTD (NZPL-Non-standard)
   NEW ZEALAND INST PLANT & FOOD RES LTD (NZPL-Non-standard)
GA 201972477X
AB    NOVELTY - Delayed release oral dosage form comprises a hops extract, where the acid component of the hops extract is not isomerized or a single active component, where the active component comprises cohumulone.
   USE - The dosage form is useful for (a) reducing energy intake by a subject, or (b) reducing appetite during period of fasting, (c) increasing satiety hormones, (d) weight management, (e) appetite control, (f) decreasing food intake, (g) inducing satiety and/or (h) treating obesity in a subject, or (i) extending a period of fasting (all claimed). The subject is human.
   ADVANTAGE - The oral dosage form exhibits delayed release of active ingredient, and synergistic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: (1) an oral dosage form comprising (A) a hops extract, where the hops extract comprises 25-100 weight/weight% alpha -acids, (B) a hops extract, where the alpha -acid component of the hops extract comprises 10-45% cohumulone, (C) (a) 100 mg supercritical carbon dioxide hops extract from the cultivar Pacific Gem, (b) approximately 492 mg canola oil, (c) 1 mg rosemary oil and (d) optionally silicon dioxide, where the oral dosage form is a Capsugel DRcaps (RTM: Not defined) capsule, (D) (a) 250 mg of supercritical carbon dioxide hops extract from the cultivar Pacific Gem, (b) approximately 342 mg canola oil, (c) 1 mg rosemary oil and (d) optionally silicon dioxide, (E) (a) 100 mg of a supercritical carbon dioxide hops extract from the cultivar Pacific Gem, (b) approximately 492 mg canola oil, (c) 1 mg rosemary oil and (d) optionally silicon dioxide, (F) (a) 250 mg of supercritical carbon dioxide hops extract from the cultivar Pacific Gem, (b) approximately 342 mg canola oil, (c) 1 mg rosemary oil and (d) optionally silicon dioxide, (G) (a) 100 mg of supercritical carbon dioxide hops extract from the cultivar Pacific Gem, (b) approximately 400 mg canola oil, (c) 1 mg rosemary oil and (d) optionally silicon dioxide, (H) (a) 250 mg of supercritical carbon dioxide hops extract from the cultivar Pacific Gem, (b) approximately 250 mg canola oil, (c) 1 mg rosemary oil and (d) optionally silicon dioxide, (I) (a) 100 mg supercritical carbon dioxide hops extract from the cultivar Pacific Gem, (b) approximately 400 mg canola oil, (c) 1 mg rosemary oil and (d) optionally silicon dioxide, (J) (a) 250 mg supercritical carbon dioxide hops extract from the cultivar Pacific Gem, (b) approximately 250 mg canola oil, (c) 1 mg rosemary oil and (d) optionally silicon dioxide; (2) method of reducing energy intake by a subject, or (a) reducing appetite, (b) increasing satiety hormones, (c) weight management, (d) appetite control, (e) decreasing food intake, (f) inducing satiety and/or (g) treating obesity, involves administering the above-mentioned oral dosage form to the subject, or administering an agent capable of binding taste receptor type 2 member 1 (TAS2R1), TAS2R14 and TAS2R40 in the gastrointestinal tract of the subject or administering a hops extract to the subject, where the acid component of the hops extract is not isomerized; and (3) use of supercritical carbon dioxide hops extract from the cultivar Pacific Gem, edible oil, an antioxidant/preservative and optionally silicon dioxide in preparing a medicament for (a) reducing energy intake, (b) reducing appetite, (c) increasing satiety hormones, (d) weight management, (e) appetite control, (f) decreasing food intake, (g) inducing satiety, and/or (h) treating obesity, where the medicament is in the form of a Capsugel DRcaps (RTM: Not defined) capsule.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Components: The hops extract comprises 30-65 weight/weight% alpha -acids. The hops extract comprises greater than 50 weight/weight% alpha - acids. The alpha -acid component of the hops extract comprises 20% cohumulone. The form comprises 5-200 mg cohumulone and 5-1000 mg hops extract. The hops extract is from the cultivar Pacific Gem. The hops extract is a carbon dioxide supercritical extract. The form further comprises edible oil, where the edible oil is canola oil. The ratio of edible oil to hops extract is 10:1-1:10. The ratio of edible oil to hops extract is 1:1 or 4:1. The form further comprises an antioxidant, where the antioxidant is second edible oil. The second edible oil is rosemary oil. The form further comprises a viscosity modifier, where the viscosity modifier is silicon dioxide. The form is a delayed release capsule. The delayed release capsule is a Capsugel Licaps (RTM: Not defined) capsule. The contents of the oral dosage form have a viscosity of 250 cP-8000 P.
   ACTIVITY - Anabolic; Endocrine-Gen.; Anorectic. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   ADMINISTRATION - The dosage form in the form of capsule is administered by oral route (all claimed). No dosage details given.

PN CN109279604-A; CN209242689-U; CN109279604-B
TI High-density carbon dioxide forming device comprises e.g. turntable mounted on support plate set in frame body comprising upper frame and lower frame, liquid inlet unit, pressing unit, demolding unit, and cavity comprising chamber.
AU CHEN S
   CHEN J
   QIU Y
   HONG Z
   WU Y
   FENG C
AE UNIV XIAMEN TECHNOLOGY (UXLG-C)
   UNIV XIAMEN TECHNOLOGY (UXLG-C)
GA 201915893B
AB    NOVELTY - High-density carbon dioxide forming device comprises e.g. a frame body (1), a turntable (2) set in the frame body, a liquid inlet unit (3), a pressing unit (4) and a demolding unit (5). The frame body comprises an upper frame and a lower frame, and the upper frame is provided with a support plate. The turntable is horizontally rotatably mounted on the support plate, and is provided with multiple circumferentially distributed cavities. The cavity comprises a chamber penetrating up and down along the turntable. The circumferential direction of the turntable is provided with a liquid inlet station, a pressing station and a demolding station. When the turntable is rotated, the chamber can be filled with liquid. The station, the pressing station and the demolding station are in turn aligned. The liquid inlet unit comprises a heat exchanger and an inlet electromagnetic valve that are sequentially connected. The inlet electromagnetic valve is provided with an expansion throttle port.
   USE - Used as high-density carbon dioxide forming device.
   ADVANTAGE - The device improves the working efficiency, and saves the equipment cost by designing the rotary type multi-station.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High-density carbon dioxide forming device comprises a frame body (1), a turntable (2) set in the frame body, a liquid inlet unit (3), a pressing unit (4) and a demolding unit (5). The frame body comprises an upper frame and a lower frame, and the upper frame is provided with a support plate. The turntable is horizontally rotatably mounted on the support plate, and is provided with multiple circumferentially distributed cavities. The cavity comprises a chamber penetrating up and down along the turntable. The circumferential direction of the turntable is provided with a liquid inlet station, a pressing station and a demolding station. When the turntable is rotated, the chamber can be filled with liquid. The station, the pressing station and the demolding station are in turn aligned. The liquid inlet unit comprises a heat exchanger and an inlet electromagnetic valve that are sequentially connected. The inlet electromagnetic valve is provided with an expansion throttle port, where the expansion throttle comprising an outlet of the mouth is set at the liquid inlet station to fill the cavity with snowflake-shaped carbon dioxide. The pressing unit is set at the pressing station and comprises a pressing block and a first power device that drives the pressing block into the cavity to suppress snowflake shaped carbon dioxide. The demolding unit is set at the demolding station, and comprises a second power unit and a third power unit. The second power unit is set on the bottom portion of the chamber, where the chamber is reciprocable in a vertical direction to eject the bulk carbon dioxide from the chamber. The third power unit is set at the top portion of the chamber for extruding carbon dioxide pushed away from the turntable.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic representation of the high-density carbon dioxide forming device.
   Frame body (1)
   Turntable (2)
   Liquid inlet unit (3)
   Pressing unit (4)
   Demolding unit (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The turntable comprises an upper support plate, a lower support plate and multiple pressing cavities set between the upper support plate and the lower support plate. The upper support plate and the lower support plate are each provided with at least three material ports. The material ports of the upper support plate and the lower support plate are oppositely set in the cavity of the extrusion cavity. The extrusion cavity comprises a hollow cavity, an air flow cap and an outer cavity surrounding the cavity. The cavity comprises multiple cavity plate connected to each other to form the cavity, and each of the cavity plates is provided with a honeycomb through hole. An airflow cap is set on the through hole, and an outer wall of the cavity plate is provided with a groove. The groove is located at a position corresponding to the through hole. An exhaust chamber is formed between the outer chamber and the groove. The first power unit comprises a hydraulic cylinder and a clamp connecting rod. The hydraulic cylinder drives the clamp connecting rod to reciprocate in a vertical direction to drive. The compact moves back and forth within the cavity of the cavity to squeeze the snowflake carbon dioxide. The turntable is provided with detecting device for detecting the position of the pressing block. The heat exchanger is set at a middle portion of the support plate, and comprises a heat exchange barrel and a wound heat exchange tube set in the heat exchange barrel. The bottom of the heat exchange barrel is connected with the exhaust chamber. An exhaust duct for discharging gas to the outside atmosphere is connected at the top portion. The heat exchange tube is connected to an inlet conduit. An outlet is connected to the inlet electromagnetic valve. The liquid inlet unit comprises at least two inlet electromagnetic valves, and the two inlet electromagnetic valves are connected with the chamber of the cavity to form an inlet liquid work station. The device further comprises a conveyor belt fixed to the support plate. The conveying plane of the conveyor belt is flush with the top surface of the turntable for conveying the bulk carbon dioxide and demolding. The device further comprises a limit support plate and a guide member. The limit support plate is set at the demolding station, comprising a top plate and fixed to the top plate and multiple limiting rods extending downwardly. The third power unit is fixed to the top plate. The guiding member is fixed to the limiting rod connected to limit the entry of bulk carbon dioxide into the conveyor belt. The liquid inlet station, the pressing station and the demolding station are each provided with at least two in numbers, and the two stations are symmetrically set on the turntable.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Gas separation device comprises MDEA regeneration tower for oil field associated gas from desulfurization/decarbonization, separator for gas from MDEA regeneration tower to carry out gas-liquid separation, and sewage buffer tank for receiving from separator.
AU CHANG X
   WEN J
   ZHAO H
   YAO B
   YAO L
   ZHAO D
   LI Z
   YE F
   GUO J
   TENG J
   LI P
   YANG S
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC NORTHWEST OILFIELD CO (SNPC-C)
GA 202055872P
AB    (CN210736677-U)   NOVELTY - The invention claims a gas separating device, the separating process comprises the following steps: the gas to be processed to carry out gas-liquid separation, wherein the liquid is sent to the sewage buffer tank, gas to the pressurizing compressor; liquid generated after pressurizing is sent to the sewage buffer tank, the gas after pressurizing to the dewatering device; the liquid removed is sent to the sewage buffer tank, sending the gas to a level in the electric heater heating, then sending in the hollow fibre membrane module to separate, the H2S output to the H2S booster compressor boost. mixed with CO2 of H2S re-separated by the hollow fibre membrane module, H2S output to the H2S booster compressor in pressurizing and CO2 output to the CO2 booster compressor, H2S booster in the booster compressor H2S reaches the supercritical state, sends it to the formation sealing; the pressure and adjusting the pressure in the booster compressor CO2 at CO2, then sends it to the formation, or delivered to the driver well to drive oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for separating acid gas, involves (a) performing gas-liquid separation on acid gas to be treated, and passing liquid to sewage buffer tank, and passing gas to primary booster compressor, (b) pressurizing sent gas to primary booster compressor, and passing pressurized liquid to sewage buffer tank, and passing pressurized gas to dewatering device, (c) removing gas sent to liquid removal device, and passing removed liquid to sewage buffer tank, and passing gas to primary electric heater for heating, (d) passing heated gas to first-stage hollow fiber membrane group for separation, where hydrogen sulfide is output from high-pressure side to pressure-increasing side of hydrogen sulfide booster compressor, and uniformly mixing carbon dioxide with hydrogen sulfide of output from permeate side to secondary booster compressor, (e) passing small amount of hydrogen sulfide mixed in secondary booster compressor for heating in secondary heater, (f) passing heated mixture with small amount of hydrogen sulfide to second-stage hollow fiber membrane group for separation, where hydrogen sulfide is output from high-pressure side to the pressure-increasing side of hydrogen sulfide booster compressor, and the carbon dioxide is output to carbon dioxide booster compressor from permeate side, (g) when the pressurized hydrogen sulfide in hydrogen sulfide booster compressor reaches the supercritical state, passing hydrogen sulfide to stratum for sealing, and (h) after pressurizing carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide booster compressor, regulating pressure, passing to formation to be sealed, or delivering to oil displacement well for oil displacement.
EA    NOVELTY - Method for separating acid gas, involves (a) performing gas-liquid separation on acid gas to be treated, and passing liquid to sewage buffer tank, and passing gas to primary booster compressor, (b) pressurizing sent gas to primary booster compressor, (c) removing gas sent to liquid removal device, and passing removed liquid to sewage buffer tank, (d) passing heated gas to first-stage hollow fiber membrane group for separation, (e) passing hydrogen sulfide mixed in secondary booster compressor for heating in secondary heater, (f) passing heated mixture with hydrogen sulfide to second-stage hollow fiber membrane group for separation, (g) when the pressurized hydrogen sulfide in hydrogen sulfide booster compressor reaches the supercritical state, passing hydrogen sulfide to stratum for sealing, and (h) after pressurizing carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide booster compressor, regulating pressure, passing to formation to be sealed, or delivering to oil displacement well for oil displacement.
   USE - Method for separating acid gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Devices: The hollow fiber membrane group has pressure-resistant metal shell and hollow fiber membrane filled with pressure-resistant metal shell, where the shell is provided with air inlet, and air outlets are provided on both sides of the cavity. The step (a) involves performing gas-liquid separation on acid gas by gas-liquid separator. The liquid removal device in step (b) is molecular sieve liquid removal device.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: In separation method, the pressure increased by first-stage booster compressor and pressure increased by second-stage compressor are carried out under 1-2 MPa. In separation method, the pressure increased by hydrogen sulfide booster compressor and pressure increased by carbon dioxide booster compressor are carried out under 10-30 MPa. The step (g) involves boosting hydrogen sulfide by hydrogen sulfide booster compressor and passing flow meter, and passing to pressure regulating device for pressure adjustment to make hydrogen sulfide reach supercritical state, and/or the step (h) involves boosting carbon dioxide by carbon dioxide booster compressor and passing to flow meter, and passing to pressure regulating device for pressure adjustment. In separation method, the acid gas to be treated comes from methyldiethanolamine regeneration tower, and passing the liquid to sewage buffer tank for passing back to methyldiethanolamine regeneration tower. Preferred Method: The method involves passing crude oil extracted from flooding well by pumping pump to three-phase separator by pipeline, pressurizing separated produced gas with high carbon dioxide content to 10-30 MPa by carbon dioxide reinjection compressor and injecting to flooding well for oil displacement.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The fibers in hollow fiber membrane group comprises amino polyimide, amino cellulose acetate and/or amino polysulfone.

PN KR2022028494-A
TI Manufacturing biochar comprises pyrolyzing biomass material including pine sawdust and paper sludge and treating pyrolyzed biomass with steam.
AU OK Y
   LEE G B
   GALABITANA A D S
   WAN C
   KEPABANIDULANZA D
   HWAN K J
   JOE Y L
AE UNIV KOREA RES & BUSINESS FOUND (UYKR-C)
GA 2022370828
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing biochar comprises pyrolyzing biomass material including pine sawdust and/or paper sludge and treating the pyrolyzed biomass with steam.
   USE - The biochar is useful for adsorption of carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The biochar reduces the cost of waste treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for carbon dioxide adsorption method, comprising contacting the biochar with gas containing carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The pyrolyzing of biomass is carried out at 300-600 degrees C. The steam treatment is carried out at 400-600 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - Paper sludge and pine sawdust were dried at 60 degrees C, then crushed to less than 2 mm, placed in a ceramic crucible with a cover, and heated in a muffle furnace at a heating rate of 7 degrees C/minute for 2 hours under no air condition. The pyrolysis peak temperature was set to 300-600 degrees C and 550 degrees C for paper sludge and pine sawdust, respectively for 2 hours. The biochar was activated by steaming at 550 degrees C for 45 minutes at a flow rate of 5 ml/minute to obtain four types of biochar (PMS300, PMS600, PS550 and PSS550).

PN RU2021138248-A
TI Expanding human natural killer (NK) cells using            feeder cells comprises e.g. isolating mononuclear cells            from blood by gradient centrifugation, and cultivating            obtained NK cells in nutrient medium with interleukin-2            and fetal bovine serum.
AU GELM YU V
   GRIVTSOVA L YU
   PASOVA I A
   KONSTANTINOVA T V
   IVANOV S A
   KAPRIN A D
AE FEDERAL STATE BUDGETARY INST (BUDG-Non-standard)
GA 2022B3862N
AB    NOVELTY - Expanding human natural killer (NK) cells                using feeder cells, comprises isolating mononuclear                cells (MNCs) from human peripheral blood by                gradient centrifugation, carrying out stimulation                of proliferation of NK cells using feeder cells,                inoculating into a Petri dish or culture flask in                pure Roswell park memorial institute (RPMI) medium                and incubating for 60 minutes in carbon dioxide                incubator at 37&#176; C and 5% carbon dioxide,                after which non-attached NK cells are collected,                centrifuged at 1000 rpm for 5 minutes and adjusted                to the desired concentration, cultivating the                obtained NK cells with feeder cells under carbon                dioxide incubator in RPMI-1640 nutrient medium with                300 U/ml interleukin (IL)-2 and 10% fetal bovine                serum (FBS) and adding feeder cells to NK cells 3                times, renewing the nutrient medium every 48 hours                by adding 300 U/ml IL-2, and using feeder cells                immediately after receipt or storage in liquid                nitrogen with a cryoprotectant comprising 10%                dimethyl sulfoxide and 90% FBS.
   USE - The method is useful for expanding human NK                cells using feeder cells.
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Expanding human natural killer (NK) cells                using feeder cells, comprises isolating mononuclear                cells (MNCs) from human peripheral blood by                gradient centrifugation, carrying out stimulation                of proliferation of NK cells using feeder cells                previously obtained by irradiating the K562 cell                line with a dose of 100 Gy, in full Roswell park                memorial institute-1640 (RPMI-1640) growth medium                containing interleukin (IL)-2 under carbon dioxide                incubator at 37&#176; C and 5% carbon dioxide,                and the duration of expansion is 21 days, where                MNCs are adjusted to a concentration of 5                million/ml, inoculating into a Petri dish or                culture flask in pure RPMI medium and incubating                for 60 minutes in carbon dioxide incubator at                37&#176; C and 5% carbon dioxide, after which                non-attached NK cells are collected, centrifuged at                1000 rpm for 5 minutes and adjusted to the desired                concentration, cultivating the obtained NK cells                together with feeder cells under carbon dioxide                incubator in RPMI-1640 nutrient medium with 300                U/ml IL-2 and 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS) and                adding feeder cells to NK cells 3 times, which is                on the 0th day of cultivation in a ratio of 1:1, on                the 7th day in a ratio of 2:1 and on the 14th day                in a ratio of 5-10:1, renewing the nutrient medium                every 48 hours with the addition of 300 U/ml IL-2,                and using feeder cells immediately after receipt or                storage in liquid nitrogen with a cryoprotectant                comprising 10% dimethyl sulfoxide and 90%                FBS.

TI Modifying basalt fiber surface layer to aluminosilicate using device comprises e.g. chemically reacting surface layer of basalt fiber with aluminum alkyl to form aluminosilicate material and oxidizing and removing residual aluminum alkyl.
AU YANG X
AE JIANGSU HANPTON NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018883172
AB    NOVELTY - Modifying basalt fiber surface layer to aluminosilicate using device comprises chemically reacting surface layer of basalt fiber with aluminum alkyl to form aluminosilicate material and oxidizing and removing residual aluminum alkyl of the fiber surface layer with carbon dioxide gas containing a small amount of oxygen, where modified device comprises a spray tank, air drying chamber, aluminum alkyl tank, carbon dioxide tank and basalt fiber moving reel, steering shaft and a rewinding shaft. The modification method comprises e.g. pulling out basalt fiber head on at least one reels, passing steering shaft control direction and sequentially entering spray tank, air drying chamber, aluminum alkyl tank, carbon dioxide tank and connecting to the reel, injecting aluminum alkyl tank into an inert gas having a greater specific gravity than the air and injecting aluminum alkyl liquid into the aluminum alkyl tank and maintaining inert gas in tank covered with aluminum liquid surface.
   USE - The method is useful for modifying basalt fiber surface layer to aluminosilicate using device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Modifying basalt fiber surface layer to aluminosilicate using device comprises chemically reacting surface layer of basalt fiber with aluminum alkyl to form aluminosilicate material and oxidizing and removing residual aluminum alkyl of the fiber surface layer with carbon dioxide gas containing a small amount of oxygen, where modified device comprises a spray tank, air drying chamber, aluminum alkyl tank, carbon dioxide tank and basalt fiber moving reel, steering shaft and a rewinding shaft. The modification method comprises (i) pulling out basalt fiber head on at least one reels, passing steering shaft control direction and sequentially entering spray tank, air drying chamber, aluminum alkyl tank, carbon dioxide tank and connecting to the reel; (ii) injecting aluminum alkyl tank into an inert gas having a greater specific gravity than the air and injecting aluminum alkyl liquid into the aluminum alkyl tank, maintaining inert gas in tank covered with aluminum liquid surface and not in contact with the air and injecting carbon dioxide gas containing small amount of oxygen into the carbon dioxide tank; (iii) starting spraying water from spray pipe of the spray tank, starting blower of the fan in air-drying room, and starting rewinding shaft to move fiber filament to move the fiber, washing with spray water, drying with hot air and performing alkyl aluminum reaction and residual alkyl aluminum oxidation sequentially; and (iv) winding basalt fiber of reel completely by rewinding shaft, taking rewinding shaft and placing in sealed package and storing in isolation from the air.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the device utilized in the above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The aluminum alkyl tank lining is a metal titanium, platinum, rhodium, gold material or non-metal carbon material which does not react with the aluminum alkyl. The aluminum alloy tank, steering shaft in carbon dioxide tank is a metal titanium, platinum, rhodium, gold material or non-metal carbon material that does not react with the aluminum alkyl. The oxygen content of carbon dioxide in whole process is 4-8%.

GA 2018682027
AB    NOVELTY - Producing double alkali chlorohydrin epoxide comprises setting a hypochlorous acid reaction unit (1), dehydrochlorination reaction unit (2), chlorination reaction unit (3), saponification reaction unit (4), epoxide separation unit (5), saponification residual liquid sedimentation unit, and carbon dioxide recovery unit, entering raw chlorine and water in hypochlorous acid reaction unit to form hypochlorous acid and hydrogen chloride, entering mixture of hypochlorous acid and hydrogen chloride in the hydrogen chloride reaction unit and calcium carbonate in dehydrochlorination reaction unit, reacting calcium carbonate with hydrogen chloride to form calcium chloride, water and carbon dioxide, reacting non-hydrogen chloride hypochlorous acid aqueous solution of the dehydrochlorination reaction unit and chlorohydrinated raw material in chlorohydrination reaction unit with hypochlorous acid to form intermediate product chlorohydrins.
   USE - The method is useful for producing double alkali chlorohydrin epoxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is economically beneficially and environmentally friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing double alkali chlorohydrin epoxide comprises setting a hypochlorous acid reaction unit (1), dehydrochlorination reaction unit (2), chlorination reaction unit (3), saponification reaction unit (4), epoxide separation unit (5), saponification residual liquid sedimentation unit, and carbon dioxide recovery unit, entering raw chlorine and water in hypochlorous acid reaction unit to form hypochlorous acid and hydrogen chloride, entering mixture of hypochlorous acid and hydrogen chloride in the hydrogen chloride reaction unit and calcium carbonate in dehydrochlorination reaction unit, reacting calcium carbonate with hydrogen chloride to form calcium chloride, water and carbon dioxide, reacting non-hydrogen chloride hypochlorous acid aqueous solution of the dehydrochlorination reaction unit and chlorohydrinated raw material in chlorohydrination reaction unit with hypochlorous acid to form intermediate product chlorohydrins, introducing liquid phase material containing the intermediate product and calcium chloride in the chlorohydrination reactor and the saponifying agent sodium hydroxide into the saponification reaction unit, reacting intermediate product with sodium hydroxide to form an epoxide, reacting calcium chloride with sodium hydroxide to form sodium chloride and calcium hydroxide, introducing liquid phase material of the saponification reaction unit into the epoxide separation unit, separating epoxide from saponification residue, introducing saponification residual liquid of the epoxide separation unit into the saponification residue sedimentation unit, removing separated precipitate calcium hydroxide to carbon dioxide recovery unit, feeding carbon dioxide and the calcium hydroxide in the dehydrogenation reaction unit into the carbon dioxide recovery unit, reacting carbon dioxide with calcium hydroxide to form calcium carbonate, and subjecting calcium carbonate into dehydrochlorination reaction unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows flow diagram of the producing double alkali chlorohydrin epoxide (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Hypochlorous acid reaction unit (1)
   Dehydrochlorination reaction unit (2)
   Chlorination reaction unit (3)
   Saponification reaction unit (4)
   Epoxide separation unit (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hypochlorous acid reaction unit and the dehydrochlorination reaction unit are combined and water, chlorine gas, and calcium carbonate are added into the same unit. PN CN113213422-A
TI Improved process for producing synthetic ammonia feed gas from sodium cyanide tail gas, involves taking sodium cyanide tail gas comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, oxygen, methane, total chlorine and nitrogen, subjecting to conversion towers and performing Fischer-Tropsch reaction.
AU YANG H
   YANG P
   WANG W
   TIAN S
AE YANG H (YANG-Individual)
GA 202195947N
AB    NOVELTY - Improved process for producing synthetic ammonia feed gas from sodium cyanide tail gas involves taking sodium cyanide tail gas comprising hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, oxygen, methane, total sulfur, total chlorine and nitrogen, subjecting the gas to organic sulfur conversion tower equipped with dual-function conversion catalyst, converting organic sulfur into hydrogen sulfide and converting oxygen into water to obtain crude conversion gas, subjecting to hydrogen sulfide absorption tower to reduce, subjecting to trace oxygen conversion tower for oxygen conversion to obtain post-conversion gas, subjecting to a heat recovery device, dehydrating by adsorption device with total chlorine, subjecting to Fischer-Tropsch reactor, performing Fischer-Tropsch reaction of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide to obtain Fischer-Tropsch gas, and obtaining hydrogen nitrogen by adsorption of non-hydrogen nitrogen component by Fischer-Tropsch gas transmission pressure swing adsorption device.
   USE - Improved process for producing synthetic ammonia feed gas from sodium cyanide tail gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Improved process for producing synthetic ammonia feed gas from sodium cyanide tail gas involves taking sodium cyanide tail gas comprising 80-90 wt.% hydrogen, 0.1-0.5 wt.% carbon monoxide, 0.01-0.1 wt.% carbon dioxide, 0.2-0.8 wt.% oxygen, 0.01-0.3 wt.% methane, 2-100 parts per million total sulfur, 2-100 parts per million total chlorine and remaining of nitrogen, subjecting the gas to organic sulfur conversion tower equipped with a dual-function conversion catalyst, converting all organic sulfur higher than 0.01 part per million into hydrogen sulfide and converting oxygen higher than 100 parts per million into water to obtain crude conversion gas, subjecting to a hydrogen sulfide absorption tower to reduce to 0.01 part per million, subjecting to a trace oxygen conversion tower for oxygen conversion of less than 1 part per million to obtain post-conversion gas, subjecting to a heat recovery device to recover the heat, cooling to 20-50 degrees C, dehydrating to 2-100 parts per million by adsorption device with total chlorine of less than or equal to 0.1 parts per million, subjecting to Fischer-Tropsch reactor, performing Fischer-Tropsch reaction of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in the gas at 0.8-2.5 MPa to obtain Fischer-Tropsch gas, and obtaining hydrogen nitrogen by the adsorption of non-hydrogen nitrogen component by Fischer-Tropsch gas transmission pressure swing adsorption device to make synthetic ammonia feed gas, where the total concentration of carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide in the outlet gas is less than or equal to 20 parts per million, the concentration of hydrogen and nitrogen is greater than or equal to 99.95 wt.%, the total yield is greater than or equal to 96 wt.%, and the total concentration of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, oxygen and water is less than or equal to 20 parts per million.

TI Preparing glycol by efficiently catalyzing            ethylene oxide hydration reaction involves using ion            liquid and organic alkali as catalyst, carbon dioxide            is introduced into the reaction system, and ethylene            oxide is hydrated to obtain glycol.
AU GAO G
   DING T
AE UNIV EAST CHINA NORMAL (UYEN-C)
GA 202167049X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing glycol by efficiently catalyzing                ethylene oxide hydration reaction involves using                ion liquid and organic alkali as catalyst, the                carbon dioxide is introduced into the reaction                system, and ethylene oxide is hydrated to obtain                glycol, specifically as follows: the ethylene                oxide, water, ion liquid and organic alkali are                placed in the reaction kettle, and carbon dioxide                is introduced in to the reaction system after it is                sealed, where temperature is 60 to 120 degrees C,                the pressure of the carbon dioxide is 0.1 to                2.0MPa, the reaction is carried for 0.5 to 12 hours                to obtain the glycol, and the ion liquid and                organic alkali are present in the reaction system                at the same time. The molar ratio of ethylene oxide                to water is 1: 1 to 5; the molar dosage of the ion                liquid is 0.1 to 5 % of the molar dosage of the                ethylene oxide, and the molar dosage of the organic                alkali is 0.1 to 5 % of the molar dosage of                ethylene oxide.
   USE - Method for preparing glycol by efficiently                catalyzing ethylene oxide hydration reaction.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can solve the problem of high                hydration ratio in the existing direct hydration                reaction process, can effectively reduce the energy                consumption and cost in post-processing; and the                selected catalyst is simple and easy to obtain; the                activity is high, so it has potential application                in the production of ethylene glycol by ethylene                oxide hydration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing glycol by efficiently catalyzing                ethylene oxide hydration reaction involves using                ion liquid and organic alkali as catalyst, the                carbon dioxide is introduced into the reaction                system, and ethylene oxide is hydrated to obtain                glycol, specifically as follows: the ethylene                oxide, water, ion liquid organic alkali are placed                in the reaction kettle, and carbon dioxide is                introduced in to the reaction system after it is                sealed, where temperature is 60 to 120 degrees C,                the pressure of the carbon dioxide is 0.1 to                2.0MPa, the reaction is carried for 0.5 to 12 hours                to obtain the glycol, and the ion liquid and                organic alkali are present in the reaction system                at the same time. The molar ratio of ethylene oxide                to water is 1: 1 to 5; the molar dosage of the ion                liquid is 0.1 to 5 % of the molar dosage of the                ethylene oxide, and the molar dosage of the organic                alkali is 0.1 to 5 % of the molar dosage of                ethylene oxide. The chemical structure of the ion                liquid is (I) or (II). The chemical structure of                organic alkali is (III) to (VII).
   R=H, 1-6C alkyl, benzyl or vinyl;
   X=Cl, Br or I anion; and
   R1=H, 1-6C alkyl, benzyl, vinyl or                allyl.

PN CN108641806-A
TI Preparing grape seed oil by ultrasonic assisted supercritical carbon dioxide technology used in an anti-oxidant anti-aging whitening product comprises adding primary grape seed oil into molecular distiller, and separating grape seed oil.
AU HE D
   WANG J
   JIN C
   ZHANG L
AE NAT ENG RES CENT NANOTECHNOLOGY (NERC-C)
GA 201881288N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing grape seed oil by ultrasonic assisted supercritical carbon dioxide technology comprises (a) washing the grape seeds to remove impurities and dirt, draining and drying to obtain dried grape seeds; crushing the dried grape seeds to obtain grape seed pieces; (b) placing the grape seed granules in a supercritical reaction vessel, opening the carbon dioxide cylinder, cooling the carbon dioxide gas into a liquid, pressurizing, opening the ultrasonic device; opening the pressure relief port to reduce the pressure in the supercritical reactor to the normal pressure to obtain primary grape seed oil; (c) adding the primary grape seed oil into the molecular distiller, separating the grape seed oil until the primary grape seed oil is completely collected by the heavy and light phase tank; and (d) adding the grape seed oil into the molecular distiller to further separate the grape seed oil, and collecting the prepared grape seed oil in a heavy phase collection tank.
   USE - The grape seed oil is used in an anti-oxidant anti-aging whitening product.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has short extracting time, reduces the cost and improves the efficiency of extraction and separation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing grape seed oil by ultrasonic assisted supercritical carbon dioxide technology comprises (a) washing the grape seeds with water to remove impurities and dirt, draining the water and drying in an oven to obtain dried grape seeds; then, crushing the dried grape seeds to 60-80 meshes to obtain grape seed pieces; (b) placing the grape seed granules in a supercritical reaction vessel, opening the carbon dioxide cylinder, cooling the carbon dioxide gas into a liquid by a cold machine, and then pressurizing into the reaction kettle by the autoclave, opening the ultrasonic device for extraction, where the pressure is 10-40 MPa, the temperature is 50-100 degrees C and the dwell time is 60-120 minutes; after the end of the extraction, opening the pressure relief port to reduce the pressure in the supercritical reactor to the normal pressure to obtain primary grape seed oil; (c) adding the primary grape seed oil into the molecular distiller, separating the grape seed oil at a vacuum of 1-2 Pa until the primary grape seed oil is completely collected by the heavy and light phase tank, and using the grape seed oil collected in the heavy phase collection tank; and (d) adding the grape seed oil into the molecular distiller, controlling a certain degree of vacuum, temperature and rotational speed of the rotating brush to further separate the grape seed oil until the grape seed oil is completely collected by the heavy phase collection tank and the light phase collection tank, and collecting the prepared grape seed oil in a heavy phase collection tank. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for grape seed oil, produced by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The drying treatment temperature is 70-90 degrees C, and the time is 3-5 hours. The ultrasonic power is 300-500 W. The temperature in the molecular distiller is 60-80 degrees C, and the rotational speed of the rotating brush in the molecular distiller is 100 revolutions per minute.

   USE - The method is useful for preparing N-(4-tert-butylbenzyl)-N-methyl-1-(naphthalen-1-yl)methanamine (I) with the participation of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method involves one-pot two-step method of reductive amination and reductive methylation. The method is simple and easy to operate, has a wide range of raw material sources and low cost, and simultaneously does not require the participation of catalysts e.g. transition metals and precious metals in the synthesis, avoids metal residues in the medicine, is safe, environmentally friendly, and meets the requirements of environmentally friendly chemistry.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing N-(4-tert-butylbenzyl)-N-methyl-1-(naphthalen-1-yl)methanamine of formula (I) with the participation of carbon dioxide, comprises reacting 1-naphthaldehyde of formula (II) and (4-tert-butylphenyl)methanamine of formula (III) to obtain final product.
   tBu = tert-butyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises adding (III), (II) and sodium borohydride into reaction kettle, replacing vacuum by carbon dioxide for 2-4 times, adding dioxane as the reaction solvent, stirring at room temperature for 12-18 hours; subsequently, adding sodium borohydride under the flow of carbon dioxide, and pressurizing the reaction kettle was to 1 MPa, heating to 80-100degrees Celsius, and continuously stirring for 12-24 hours; adding water to quench the reaction and extracting with ethyl acetate; combining the organic phases, drying over anhydrous sodium sulfate, concentrating and separating by column chromatography to obtain the target compound.Preferred Components: The molar ratio between 4-tert-butylbenzylamine, 1-naphthalenecarboxaldehyde and sodium borohydride is 1:1:3-4.

PN CN113213512-A
TI Producing light magnesium carbonate by reacting organic amine solution, magnesium carboxylate aqueous solution and carbon dioxide, separating phases to obtain aqueous phase containing magnesium bicarbonate, and pyrolyzing it to obtain aqueous phase containing precipitated light magnesium carbonate.
AU ZHONG J
   WU M
   HUANG L
   ZHONG Y
   RAO X
AE LONGYAN ZIYUN CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LONG-Non-standard)
GA 202194098M
AB    NOVELTY - Method for producing light magnesium carbonate, involves (a) reacting the organic amine solution, the magnesium carboxylate aqueous solution and carbon dioxide, and separating the phases to obtain an aqueous phase containing magnesium bicarbonate and an organic phase containing carboxylic acid amine salt, and (b) pyrolyzing the aqueous phase obtained in step (a) to obtain a gas phase containing carbon dioxide and an aqueous phase containing precipitated light magnesium carbonate.
   USE - Method for producing light magnesium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes non-water-soluble weakly basic organic amines to enhance the dissolution of carbon dioxide to obtain high-concentration bicarbonate instead of carbonate, and simultaneously prevent impurity ions and magnesium ions from being precipitated in the form of carbonate, so that other carbonates in the magnesium carbonate product will affect the purity of the product. The purity of the light magnesium carbonate obtained by the method is above 98.5%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: reacting and extracting the organic phase containing the carboxylic acid amine salt obtained in step (a) with magnesium hydroxide; and returning the gas phase containing carbon dioxide obtained in step (b) to step (a) to continue the reaction. The method further includes at least one of: (a1) the organic amine chosen from at least one of diisooctylamine, di-n-hexylamine, trioctylamine, dodecylamine, octadecylamine, N,N-dimethyldodecylamine and dodecylammonium bromide; (b1) the organic solvent which is an organic solvent insoluble in water and soluble in the carboxylic acid amine salt; (c1) the organic solvent chosen from at least one of alcohols, ethers, esters, ketones and amides organic solvents; and (d1) when the organic amine solution is an organic solvent containing organic amine, the organic amine accounts for 5-100% of the mass of the organic amine solution. The step (b) further includes: filtering and drying the aqueous phase containing the precipitated light magnesium carbonate. The step (a) further includes at least one of: (a2) the concentration of the magnesium carboxylate aqueous solution which is 5-25wt.%; (b2) the pH of the aqueous magnesium carboxylate solution which is 7.5-13; (c2) the molar ratio of the organic amine in the organic amine solution to the magnesium carboxylate in the magnesium carboxylate aqueous solution which is (2-3):1; (d2) the molar ratio of organic amine to carbon dioxide in the organic amine solution which is (1.8-2.5):1; (e2) the reaction temperature at 5-100 degrees C; and (f2) the reaction time of 0.5-5 hours. Preferred Conditions: In step (b), the pyrolysis treatment is performed at 40-100 degrees C for 0.5-8 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (a), the organic amine solution is an organic amine or an organic solvent containing an organic amine. In step (a), the carboxylic acid in the magnesium carboxylate aqueous solution is chosen from at least one of formic acid, acetic acid, propionic acid and butyric acid.

PN VN75250-A
TI Producing activated carbon from Gleicheniaceae plants by activation with water vapor and carbon dioxide or by chemical catalysis involves e.g. preparing and preliminary processing raw materials from Gleicheniaceae biomass and pyrolysis.
AU MAI T N
   NGUYEN N M
AE MAI T N (MAIT-Individual)
   NGUYEN N M (NGUY-Individual)
GA 202164777H
AB    NOVELTY - Producing activated carbon from Gleicheniaceae plants by activation with water vapor and carbon dioxide or by chemical catalysis, involves: (i) preparing and preliminary processing raw materials from Gleicheniaceae biomass; (ii) pyrolysis at 300-1200 degrees C and activating with steam and carbon dioxide in different proportions (1-99%) to produce activated carbon; (iii) physical treatment to homogenize the activated carbon material obtained from pyrolysis, and crushing the carbon after activation and washing the crushed carbon to convert the pH of carbon to neutral pH to obtain activated carbon material more pure; and (iv) drying the activated carbon material to obtain a finished product. The catalytic activation method has basically the same steps as the above method where that step (ii) is replaced by the addition of chemical catalyst before or after the pyrolysis period.
   USE - The method is useful for producing activated carbon from Gleicheniaceae plants by activation with water vapor and carbon dioxide or by chemical catalysis.

   USE - The membrane is useful for carbon                dioxide/nitrogen separation (claimed) and flue gas                capturing.
   ADVANTAGE - The membrane has simple and controllable                preparation method and 80.6% carbon dioxide                permeation rate and 118.8% carbon dioxide/nitrogen                selectivity.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The preparation of the                  polyethyleneimine functionalized ZIF-8                  nanoparticle sol comprises separately dissolving                  polyethyleneimine, 2-methylimidazole and zinc                  nitrate hexahydrate in a solvent, adding the                  polyethyleneimine solution to the                  2-methylimidazole solution, stirring, mixing at                  room temperature for 0.1-10 hour, adding zinc                  nitrate hexahydrate solution to obtain a mixed                  solution, stirring, centrifuging to obtain a                  white precipitate and adding solvent to the white                  precipitate and sonicating for 0.5-60 minutes to                  obtain a polyethyleneimine functionalized ZIF-8                  particle nanoparticle sol. Preferred Components:                  The block polyetheramide is Pebax-1657 (RTM:                  Polymer material), Pebax-2533 (RTM: Polymer                  material), Pebax-1074 (RTM: Polymer material) and                  Pebax-4011 (RTM: Polymer material). The polymer                  support layer is a polysulfone flat membrane,                  polyethersulfone flat membrane, polyacrylonitrile                  flat membrane or polyvinylidene fluoride hollow                  fiber membrane. The solvent is ethanol and/or                  N,N-dimethylformamide. The solvent for dissolving                  the block polyetheramide is water, ethanol,                  n-butanol, N,N-dimethylformamide, and/or                  N-methylpyrrolidone.

PN CN110449187-A; CN110449187-B
TI Preparation of perovskite-metal complex composite            material used for, e.g. photocatalytic carbon dioxide            reduction by dissolving metal complex in organic            solvent, dropping to perovskite material organic            solvent solution, and stirring.
AU CHEN H
   ZHANG H
   KONG Z
   DONG Y
   JIANG Y
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 2019980848
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of perovskite-metal complex                composite material comprises dissolving 20-60 wt.%                metal complex in organic solvent, dissolving                perovskite material in same organic solvent,                dropping 1-10 wt.% metal complex organic solvent                solution, stirring at normal temperature for 5-15                hours, centrifuging, and washing with organic                solvent for 2-4 times. The organic solvent is less                polar organic solvent such as ethyl acetate, or                more polar organic solvent such as acetonitrile,                dimethylformamide or dimethylsulfoxide.
   USE - The method is for preparation of                perovskite-metal complex composite material used                for photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction,                photocatalytic hydrogen production, organic matter                degradation, and photo/photoelectrocatalysis (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is convenient, reproducible and                operable at room temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for carbon                dioxide reduction reaction comprising adding                organic solvent or saturated aqueous solution to                sealed glass bottle, adding small amount of reagent                and catalyst, sealing, vacuumizing to remove air,                filling with sufficient amount of carbon dioxide                gas, and photocatalytically testing through gas                chromatograph.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The perovskite                  material is a commercially available product or                  prepared by anti-solvent method or synthetic                  processes such as hot injection and cooling                  crystallization. Preferred Method: The                  preparation of composite material is done by                  dissolving 1-5 wt.% metal complex in organic                  solvent; dispersing perovskite material in 0-500                  mu L oleic acid and 0-100 mu L oleylamine,                  dropping 1-5 wt.% metal complex organic solvent                  solution, stirring at room temperature, adding                  poor solvent at volume ratio of 1:10-80, and                  centrifuging. The anti-solvent preparation method                  is done by preparing precursor solution A and                  precursor solution B separately, adding precursor                  solution A to good solvent of perovskite                  material, adding precursor solution B while                  stirring to obtain perovskite material ABX3,                  centrifuging, and purifying with organic solvent.                  The A of precursor solution is cesium, masurium,                  francium, rubidium or potassium. The precursor                  solution is carbonate solution, nitrate solution                  and/or sulfate solution. The B in precursor                  solution is lead, tin and/or palladium. The X is                  chlorine, bromine and/or iodine.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal complex                  includes but not limited to carboxyl group,                  phosphate group, amino-functionalized complex,                  polypyridine complex, thiol complex and/or                  azacyclic carbene complex.

   SOTACARBO-SOC TECHNOLOGIE AVANZATE LOW (SOTA-Non-standard)
GA 2019836725
AB    NOVELTY - A catalyst comprises copper and zinc oxide or                copper, zinc oxide and zirconium dioxide which is                supported by mesoporous silica.
   USE - Catalyst for preparation of methanol by                catalytic hydrogenation of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst is efficient at less favorable                conditions and has high performance and need not to                be activated in a reducing atmosphere, produces                methanol with high productivity and selectivity.                The catalyst improves dispersion of active phase in                the hydrogenation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) synthesis of catalyst for (a) preparation                of methanol by catalytic hydrogenation of carbon                dioxide, which involves preparing aqueous solution                containing complex agent, a copper salt and one of                zinc or a copper salt, zinc and zirconium, (b)                mixing of the support of mesoporous silica with the                obtained solution, optionally ultrasonically                processing treatment of the mixture, (c) forming                the gel and (d) triggering the self-combustive                process of the gel; and
   (2) preparation of methanol by catalytic                hydrogenation of the carbon dioxide, which involves                employing the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The catalyst                  composition of formula: (Cu)x(ZnO)y(ZrO2)z, where                  x is 0.3-0.8, preferably 0.4-0.5, y is 0.1-0.6,                  preferably 0.2-0.3, z is 0.0-0.4, preferably 0.3                  and x+y+z is 1.0. The mesoporous silica is                  amorphous silica. The complexing agent is glycine                  and copper and zinc salts or copper and zinc and                  zirconium salts are nitrate salts. Preferred                  Property: The mesoporous silica having hexagonal                  geometry of 6 nm and pores diameter of 9 nm. The                  catalyst has surface area (SBET) of 300-500 m2/g,                  preferably 400-480 m2/g, copper surface area of                  100 m2/g with respect to the copper (0),                  preferably 150-350 m2/g with respect to the                  copper (0), copper dispersion of 25-50%,                  preferably 40-50%, pore volume of 0.5-0.9 cm3/g                  and pore diameter of 6.2-6.5 nm. Preferred                  Composition: The ratio of sum of the weight of                  copper and zinc oxide and weight of the copper,                  zinc oxide and zirconium dioxide and weight of                  the mesoporous silica of 15-25 wt.%. The ratio of                  mol of the complexing agent and sum of the moles                  of the copper and zinc or copper, zinc and                  zirconium of 1.1-1.7. Preferred Condition: The                  formation of gel is carried out at 100-150                  degrees C. The triggering the self-combustive                  process is carried out at 270-330 degrees                  C.

PT P
PN CN110227465-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide methanation mesoporous            catalyst by ultrasonically dissolving materials            comprising aluminum salt and active metal comprising            chromium in ethanol, dripping propylene oxide, aging,            centrifuging, drying and calcining.
AU DING M
   LIN J
   XU Y
   MA G
   WANG J
AE UNIV WUHAN (UYWU-C)
GA 201980565P
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing carbon dioxide                methanation mesoporous catalyst involves (i)                dissolving aluminum salt, zirconium salt, nickel                salt and second active metal in ethanol and                ultrasonically dissolving to form a solution (A),                where the second active metal is any one of iron,                cobalt, manganese and chromium, (ii) stirring the                solution (A) and dripping propylene oxide into the                solution (A) while stirring to form a sol, (iii)                aging the sol at room temperature to form a gel and                (iv) washing the obtained gel with ethanol,                centrifuging, drying and finally calcining at                500-600 degrees C for 4.5-5.5 hours.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing carbon                dioxide methanation mesoporous catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts simple and easy operation                and cost-effective and conveniently and easily                available raw materials and has no expensive                template or surfactant. The catalyst exhibits                excellent low-temperature catalytic activity and                stability in a carbon dioxide methanation                reaction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                catalyst prepared by the method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method involves                  loading 1-30 wt.% nickel based on the total mass                  of the catalyst. Preferred Composition: The                  aluminum salt and the zirconium salt have                  aluminum element and zirconium element in a molar                  ratio of (0.4-10):1. The molar ratio of nickel                  element to the second active metal element is                  (1-6):1.

TI Method for improving carbon dioxide concentration of sleeve kiln flue gas, involves extracting part of exhaust gas, and performing dust removal and temperature reduction, followed by discharging burned exhaust gas into sleeve kiln chimney.
AU JI L
   ZHANG B
   ZENG W
   ZHAO C
   HUANG C
   GUAN S
   DING J
   WANG S
   LIU Q
AE SHOUGANG JINGTANG UNITED IRON & STEEL (SHGG-C)
GA 201945101L
AB    NOVELTY - A sleeve kiln flue gas carbon dioxide concentration improving method involves extracting part of the exhaust gas after dust removal in the sleeve kiln from the chimney, performing fine dust removal and temperature reduction on the exhaust gas extracted, removing sulfur dioxide, nitrogen oxides and water from the exhaust gas after cooling, mixing the exhaust gas treated with air, introducing into the sleeve kiln driving fan inlet, and increasing the mixed gas pressure by the fan, preheating the mixed gas obtained, introducing into the sleeve kiln combustion chamber and burning the gas, and discharging the burned exhaust gas into the sleeve kiln chimney together with the limestone decomposition gas.
   USE - Method for improving carbon dioxide concentration of sleeve kiln flue gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables improving carbon dioxide concentration of sleeve kiln flue gas with strong practicability, and reduced difficulty, and can reduce the oxygen content in the exhaust gas of the sleeve kiln and further reduce the pollutant discharge conversion value.

PN CN109675545-A; CN109675545-B
TI Preparation of multilayer structure tin oxide catalyst for electrocatalytic selective reduction carbon dioxide in aqueous solution to form formate, involves mixing divalent metal tin salt, organic solvent, water and amine ligand.
AU ZHANG Y
   PAN H
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 201941973V
AB    NOVELTY - A multilayer structure tin oxide catalyst (I) is prepared by taking divalent metal tin salt, organic solvent, and water and amine ligand mixed solution, carrying out hydrothermal reaction, washing, and drying.
   USE - Method for preparing multilayer structure tin oxide catalyst for electrocatalytic selective reduction carbon dioxide in aqueous solution to form formate (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has excellent catalytic activity, and has good stability and durability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A multilayer structure tin oxide catalyst of formula (SnOx) (I) is prepared by taking divalent metal tin salt, organic solvent, and water and amine ligand mixed solution, carrying out hydrothermal reaction, washing, and drying.
   x=1-2.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for application of multilayer structure tin oxide catalyst comprising dissolving multilayer structure tin oxide catalyst to carbon dioxide saturated 0.1-0.5 mol/L sodium bicarbonate electrolyte solution, applying - 2.0 to - 1.2 V potential with respect to the saturated calomel electrode, and performing selective carbon dioxide reduction.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Methods: The multilayer structure tin oxide catalyst is prepared by taking 1-5 g/L divalent metal tin salt comprising tin(II) chloride dihydrate, tin(II) nitrate undecahydrate and tin(II) sulfate, adding 20:4.25:0.75-20:0.75:4.25 volume ratio of N-N-dimethylformamide, and water and amine ligand mixed solution, carrying out hydrothermal reaction at 50-220 degrees C, preferably 180-200 degrees C for 1-6 hours, preferably 2-4 hours, washing, and drying at 40-80 degrees C. The potential is - 1.9 to - 1.4 V.

PN CN109621647-A; CN109621647-B
TI Method for separating and enriching mixed gas containing carbon dioxide, involves using ionic liquid/two-dimensional sheet material gas separation membrane.
AU PENG X
   YING W
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2019387406
AB    NOVELTY - A mixed gas containing carbon dioxide separating and enriching method involves using an ionic liquid/two-dimensional sheet material gas separation membrane under an electric field of 20-100 degrees C, a pressure of 0.01-0.2 MPa and an intensity of 7.5-7.5 V/mm, where the ionic liquid/two-dimensional sheet material gas separation membrane is prepared by dispersing a two-dimensional sheet material in deionized water to obtain a two-dimensional sheet material dispersion, and vacuum-filtering the two-dimensional sheet material dispersion onto a porous substrate to obtain a two-dimensional sheet material film. The surface of the two-dimensional sheet material film is applied with an ionic liquid and maintained it under a negative pressure of not less than 50 KPa for 1-8 hours to obtain an ionic liquid/two-dimensional sheet material gas separation membrane.
   USE - Method for separating and enriching mixed gas containing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables separating and enriching mixed gas containing carbon dioxide with excellent gas separation ability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porous substrate is porous polycarbonate film or inorganic alumina porous film. The mixed gas containing carbon dioxide is a mixture of carbon dioxide, hydrogen, methane and nitrogen. The two-dimensional sheet material is graphene oxide, molybdenum disulfide and tungsten disulfide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ionic liquid is 1-butyl-3-methylimidazolium tetrafluoroborate.

PN WO2020214285-A1
TI Production of ethylene used for e.g. packaging materials, involves separating obtained hydrogenation product into aprotic polar solvent recycle stream, and separating carbon dioxide lean gas stream into ethylene stream comprising ethylene.
AU LIU Z
   PENG K Y B
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   LIU Z (LIUZ-Individual)
   PENG K Y B (PENG-Individual)
GA 2020A27694
AB    NOVELTY - Production of ethylene involves introducing a hydrocarbon stream and an oxidant gas to a combustion pyrolysis unit (100) to obtain a combustion pyrolysis product, contacting the pyrolysis product with a cooled aprotic polar solvent in an acetylene absorption unit (300) to produce an acetylene solution and a gas stream (S1), contacting the acetylene solution with the stream (S1) in a liquid phase hydrogenation reactor to produce a hydrogenation product, separating hydrogenation product into an aprotic polar solvent recycle stream, a gas stream (S2), and an aprotic polar solvent purge stream, cooling the aprotic polar solvent recycle stream to yield the cooled aprotic polar solvent, recycling the cooled aprotic polar solvent to the absorption unit, introducing the gas stream (S2) to a carbon dioxide removal unit to produce carbon dioxide rich stream, and separating the carbon dioxide lean gas stream into an ethylene stream, an unconverted hydrocarbons stream and a syngas stream (SS1).
   USE - Production of ethylene used for break-resistant containers and packaging materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses less NMP for the production of ethylene and translates into lower heating and cooling duties, and has low air cooling duty, water cooling duty and propane refrigeration cooling duty.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of ethylene involves (a) introducing a hydrocarbon stream comprising natural gas and/or higher hydrocarbons and an oxidant gas to a combustion pyrolysis unit to obtain a combustion pyrolysis product comprising unconverted hydrocarbon, acetylene, ethylene, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, water and carbon dioxide via pyrolysis of at least a portion of the hydrocarbon stream, (b) contacting at least a portion of the combustion pyrolysis product with a cooled aprotic polar solvent in an acetylene absorption unit to produce an acetylene solution comprising at least a portion of the acetylene and optionally ethylene of the combustion pyrolysis product and aprotic polar solvent and a gas stream (S1) comprising unconverted hydrocarbons, ethylene, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide, (c) contacting at least a portion of the acetylene solution with a portion (P1) of the gas stream (S1) in a liquid phase hydrogenation reactor to produce a hydrogenation product comprising aprotic polar solvent, unconverted hydrocarbon, ethylene, carbon monoxide, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide, (d) separating at least a portion of the hydrogenation product into an aprotic polar solvent recycle stream, a gas stream (S2), and optionally an aprotic polar solvent purge stream comprising aprotic polar solvent and ethylene, (e) cooling at least a portion of the aprotic polar solvent recycle stream to yield the cooled aprotic polar solvent, (f) recycling at least a portion of the cooled aprotic polar solvent to the acetylene absorption unit in step (b), (g) introducing at least a portion of the gas stream (S2) to a carbon dioxide removal unit to produce a carbon dioxide rich stream comprising carbon dioxide and a carbon dioxide lean gas stream comprising unconverted hydrocarbons, ethylene, carbon monoxide, and hydrogen, and (h) separating at least a portion of the carbon dioxide lean gas stream into an ethylene stream comprising at least a portion of the ethylene of the carbon dioxide lean gas stream, an unconverted hydrocarbons stream comprising unconverted hydrocarbons, and a syngas stream (SS1) comprising carbon monoxide and hydrogen. The unconverted hydrocarbons comprise natural gas and/or higher hydrocarbons. The cooled aprotic polar solvent withstands a temperature of greater than the melting point of the aprotic polar solvent to less than 0 degrees F, preferably (-11)-(0) degrees F. The cooled aprotic polar solvent absorbs at least a portion of the acetylene of the combustion pyrolysis product to produce the acetylene solution. The gas stream (S1) hydrogenates at least a portion of the acetylene of the acetylene solution to produce ethylene. The amount of ethylene in the hydrogenation product is greater than the amount of ethylene in the gas stream (S1). The aprotic polar solvent recycle stream comprises at least a portion of the aprotic polar solvent of the hydrogenation product. The aprotic polar solvent recycle stream withstands temperature greater than the temperature of the cooled aprotic polar solvent. The gas stream (S2) comprises unconverted hydrocarbons, ethylene, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the ethylene production system.
   Combustion pyrolysis unit (100)
