TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The aprotic polar solvent comprises N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP), acetone, tetrahydrofuran (THF), dimethylsulfoxide (DMSO) solutions, monomethylamine (MMA) and/or dimethylformamide (DMF), preferably NMP. The hydrocarbon stream and/or the fuel gas stream comprise methane, natural gas, natural gas liquid, associated gas, well head gas, enriched gas, higher hydrocarbon, paraffin, olefin, alcohol, oxygenate, 1-6C compound and/or light naphtha. Preferred Process: The method involves cooling the aprotic polar solvent recycle stream to yield the cooled aprotic polar solvent by contacting the aprotic polar solvent recycle stream with supplemental aprotic polar solvent having ambient temperature, cooling the aprotic polar solvent recycle stream to yield the cooled aprotic polar solvent by introducing at least a portion of the aprotic polar solvent recycle stream to an air cooler to yield an air cooled aprotic polar solvent recycle stream, in which the temperature of the air cooled aprotic polar solvent recycle stream is lower than the temperature of the aprotic polar solvent recycle stream, cooling the aprotic polar solvent recycle stream to yield the cooled aprotic polar solvent by introducing at least a portion of the aprotic polar solvent recycle stream and/or at least a portion of the air cooled aprotic polar solvent recycle stream to a water cooler to yield a water cooled aprotic polar solvent recycle stream, in which the temperature of the water cooled aprotic polar solvent recycle stream is lower than the temperature of the aprotic polar solvent recycle stream and/or the temperature of the air cooled aprotic polar solvent recycle stream, respectively. The method further involves cooling the aprotic polar solvent recycle stream to yield the cooled aprotic polar solvent by introducing at least a portion of the aprotic polar solvent recycle stream, at least a portion of the air cooled aprotic polar solvent recycle stream and/or at least a portion of the water cooled aprotic polar solvent recycle stream to a propane refrigeration cooler to yield the cooled aprotic polar solvent, in which the temperature of the cooled aprotic polar solvent is lower than the temperature of the aprotic polar solvent recycle stream, the temperature of the air cooled aprotic polar solvent recycle stream and/or the temperature of the water cooled aprotic polar solvent recycle stream, respectively. The step (a) involves (i) introducing a fuel gas stream and an oxidant gas to a combustion zone to produce a combustion product, (ii) introducing a pyrolysis reactant mixture comprising the hydrocarbon stream and at least a portion of the combustion product to a pyrolysis reaction zone, in which the combustion product heats the hydrocarbon stream to a temperature effective for a pyrolysis reaction, and (iii) allowing at least a portion of the pyrolysis reactant mixture to react via the pyrolysis reaction and produce the combustion pyrolysis product. At least a portion of the unconverted hydrocarbons stream is recycled to the combustion zone as the fuel gas stream, recycled to the pyrolysis reaction zone as the hydrocarbon stream, and used as a fuel stream other than the fuel gas stream. The method further involves compressing at least a portion of the combustion pyrolysis product to a pressure of 150-350 psig prior to the step (b) of contacting at least a portion of the combustion pyrolysis product with a cooled aprotic polar solvent in an acetylene absorption unit, contacting a portion (P2) of the gas stream (S1) with at least a portion of the syngas stream (S1) to yield a syngas stream (SS2). The method involves decreasing the amount of ethylene in the aprotic polar solvent purge stream of 35% or more when compared to the amount of ethylene in an aprotic polar solvent purge stream in an otherwise similar process introducing aprotic polar solvent having a temperature of 0 degrees F or more to an acetylene absorption unit. The production of ethylene further involves contacting at least a portion of the combustion pyrolysis product and/or at least a portion of the compressed combustion pyrolysis product with a cooled N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone (NMP) in an acetylene absorption unit to produce an acetylene solution and a gas stream (S1), in which the cooled NMP has a temperature of (-11)-(0) degrees F, preferably (-4)-(-2) degrees F and the cooled NMP absorbs at least a portion of the acetylene of the combustion pyrolysis product to produce the acetylene solution, contacting at least a portion of the acetylene solution with a portion (P1) of the gas stream (S1) in a liquid phase hydrogenation reactor to produce a hydrogenation product, separating at least a portion of the hydrogenation product into an NMP recycle stream, a gas stream (S2), and optionally an NMP purge stream comprising at least a portion of the NMP of the hydrogenation product, in which the NMP recycle stream has a temperature of more than the temperature of the cooled NMP, cooling at least a portion of the NMP recycle stream to yield the cooled NMP, recycling at least a portion of the cooled NMP to the acetylene absorption unit, introducing at least a portion of the gas stream (S2) to a carbon dioxide removal unit to produce a carbon dioxide rich stream and a carbon dioxide lean gas stream, separating at least a portion of the carbon dioxide lean gas stream into an ethylene stream, an unconverted hydrocarbons stream, and a syngas stream (SS1), optionally recycling at least a portion of the unconverted hydrocarbons stream to the combustion zone as the fuel gas stream and/or to the pyrolysis reaction zone as the hydrocarbon stream. The method involves decreasing the amount of ethylene in the NMP purge stream to 45% or more when compared to the amount of ethylene in an NMP purge stream in an otherwise similar process introducing NMP having a temperature 0 degrees F or ore to an acetylene absorption unit, and decreasing the amount of cooled NMP used in the acetylene absorption unit to 35% or more when compared to the amount of NMP having a temperature of 0 degrees F or more used in an acetylene absorption unit in an otherwise similar process to produce the same amount of ethylene. The cooling of the NMP recycle stream to yield the cooled NMP involves (i) optionally contacting the NMP recycle stream with supplemental NMP to yield a combined NMP stream, in which the supplemental NMP stream has ambient temperature and the temperature of the combined NMP stream is lower than the temperature of the NMP recycle stream, (ii) introducing at least a portion of the NMP recycle stream and/or at least a portion of the combined NMP stream to an air cooler to yield an air cooled NMP recycle stream, in which the temperature of the air cooled NMP recycle stream is lower than the temperature of the NMP recycle stream and/or the temperature of the combined NMP stream, respectively, (iii) introducing at least a portion of the air cooled NMP recycle stream to a water cooler to yield a water cooled NMP recycle stream, in which the temperature of the water cooled NMP recycle stream is lower than the temperature of the air cooled NMP recycle stream, and (iv) introducing at least a portion of the water cooled NMP recycle stream to a propane refrigeration cooler to yield the cooled NMP, wherein the temperature of the cooled NMP is lower than the temperature of the water cooled NMP recycle stream. Preferred Composition: The amount of cooled aprotic polar solvent used in the acetylene absorption unit is decreased by 25% or more when compared to the amount of aprotic polar solvent having a temperature of 0 degrees F or more used in an acetylene absorption unit in an otherwise similar process to produce the same amount of ethylene. The molar ratio of hydrogen and carbon monoxide in the portion (P2) of the gas stream (S1), the syngas stream (SS1) and/or the syngas stream (SS2) is 0.5:1-3.0:1. The mass ratio of produced ethylene to cool NMP used in the acetylene absorption unit is increased by 25% or more when compared to the mass ratio of produced ethylene to NMP having a temperature of 0 degrees F or more used in an acetylene absorption unit in an otherwise similar process. Preferred Properties: The pyrolysis reactant mixture has temperature of 700 degrees C or more. The pyrolysis reaction zone has a residence time of 0.1-100 milliseconds. The NMP recycle stream and/or the combined NMP stream has a temperature of 160 degrees F or more, and the air cooled NMP recycle stream has a temperature of 140 degrees F or more. The water cooled NMP recycle stream has a temperature of 110 degrees F or more.

PN CN108731180-A
TI Smart home entry security protection system comprises a control module, a human body detection device, a detection module and an execution module.
AU HU C
AE HU C (HUCC-Individual)
GA 201887175S
AB    NOVELTY - Smart home entry security protection system comprises a control module, a human body detection device, a detection module and an execution module, where the control module is configured with preset reference oxygen content and reference carbon dioxide content. The detection module includes a temperature detecting unit, an oxygen content detecting unit, and a carbon dioxide content detecting unit. The temperature detecting unit is configured to detect the basement temperature and output a sampling temperature value. The oxygen content detecting unit is configured to detect the oxygen content in the basement and output a sample oxygen content, and the carbon dioxide content detecting unit is configured to detect the carbon dioxide content in the basement and output a sampled carbon dioxide content.
   USE - Used as smart home entry security protection system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Smart home entry security protection system comprises a control module, a human body detection device, a detection module and an execution module, where the control module is configured with preset reference oxygen content and reference carbon dioxide content. The detection module includes a temperature detecting unit, an oxygen content detecting unit, and a carbon dioxide content detecting unit. The temperature detecting unit is configured to detect the basement temperature and output a sampling temperature value. The oxygen content detecting unit is configured to detect the oxygen content in the basement and output a sample oxygen content, and the carbon dioxide content detecting unit is configured to detect the carbon dioxide content in the basement and output a sampled carbon dioxide content. The execution module includes a fresh air subsystem and an adjustment subsystem, and the human detection device is configured to detect a biothermal source in the basement. The fresh air subsystem includes a breathing plate body, an air inlet pipe, an air outlet pipe, an exhaust air pump, a compensation pump, a return air pump, a return air pipe and a compensation pipe. The breathing plate body is laid on the basement floor. The breathing plate body includes a base layer, a waterproof layer, a composite layer and a fresh air structure from bottom to top. The base layer is laid on the ground; the waterproof layer is disposed above the base layer. The composite layer includes a bonding member, a heat conducting member, and a heat insulating member. The heat conducting member is configured as a thermal conductive silicone, and the bonding member is provided as a cement adhesive. The heat insulating member is disposed as an aerogel felt, and the heat conducting member and the heat insulating member are spaced apart by the bonding member. The fresh air structure has an opening facing the composite layer, and the fresh air structure is fixed to the composite layer by an adhesive layer. The fresh air structure is hollowly disposed to form an air outlet passage, and the air outlet passage is connected to the air duct. The air inlet gap is disposed between the fresh air structure, and the fresh air structure is provided with a plurality of air inlet holes facing the air inlet gap, and the heat insulation member is below the air inlet gap, and the air inlet channel is Below the heat conductive member. The fresh air structures are disposed in parallel with each other; the air inlet pipe is connected to the air outlet pipe through an exhaust air pump, and the air outlet pipe is connected to the underground outdoor boundary, and the air inlet pipe is connected to the underground air through a return air pump a return air duct, where the return air duct is provided with a carbon dioxide compensator, and the return air duct forms a return air outlet on the side wall of the basement, and the return air outlet is disposed at a height ranging from 0.4 to 0.7 meters. The one end of the compensation tube is connected to the adjustment subsystem through a compensation pump, and the other end of the compensation tube forms a compensation air inlet at the top of the basement. The conditioning subsystem includes an oxygen generator for generating oxygen, an intake fan for introducing outside air, and a mixing chamber for receiving external air and generated oxygen. The control module is provided with a ventilation policy, and when the human body detecting device detects that the bio heat source executes the ventilation policy, the ventilation strategy includes repeatedly performing the breathing sub-policy for a first preset time. The breathing sub-policy includes a parameter acquisition step, a model comparison step, a parameter output step, and an execution step. The parameter acquisition step includes obtaining sample oxygen content, a sampling temperature value, and sampling a carbon dioxide content to establish a parameter model. The model comparison step includes comparing the parameter model with the environmental model, and calculating the oxygen compensation amount, the exhaust amount, and the return air amount. The parameter output step includes inputting the exhaust volume and the return air volume to the fresh air subsystem, and inputting the oxygen compensation amount to the regulation subsystem. The executing step includes: the fresh air subsystem controls the operation of the exhaust pump according to the exhaust amount, the fresh air subsystem controls the operation of the return air pump according to the amount of return air, and the compensation subsystem outputs the air in the basement according to the oxygen content.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the smart home entry security protection system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The width of the inlet gap is 20-50 mm. The model comparison step satisfies a first preset relationship, the first preset relationship being V1=V, where the V is the volume of the exhaust gas discharged by the exhaust pump and V1 is the volume of the air input to the basement by the compensation subsystem. The model matching step satisfies a second preset relationship, where the second preset relationship is AxV1=VSx(A1-A2)+(VS-V)2xax(D2-DY)2, where the A is the oxygen content, the VS is the total volume of the equivalent air in the basement, the A1 is the reference oxygen content, the A2 is the sample oxygen content, the a is the preset oxygen content adjustment parameter, the D2 is the sampling temperature value and the DY is the pre- Set the baseline stratification temperature value. The model comparison step satisfies a third preset relationship, and the third preset relationship is V2=b1/((VS-V)2xb2x(D-DY)2+VSx(B1-B2)) , where the b1 is the preset first exhaust parameter, the b2 is the preset second exhaust parameter, the V2 is the exhaust volume, the VS is the total volume of the basement equivalent air, the D is the sampling temperature value, the DY is the preset Baseline stratification temperature value, the B1 is the sampled carbon dioxide content at the top of the basement and the B2 is the sampled carbon dioxide content at the bottom of the basement. The model comparison step satisfies a fourth preset relationship, and the fourth preset relationship is V3=casteriskB2, where c is a preset return air parameter. The return air outlet is inclined downward. The ventilation strategy further includes a return air strategy, and when the respiratory sub-policy is completed, executing the return air strategy, the return air strategy includes controlling the return air pump to work with a preset first power Work for the second preset time.

PT P
PN WO2022038317-A1; FI202005806-A
TI Method of selectively catalytically reducing dinitrogen oxide present in a gaseous sample involves providing a catalyst capable of reducing dinitrogen oxide, and bringing gaseous sample into contact with catalyst to reduce dinitrogen oxide.
AU GENOUD G
   REINIKAINEN M
AE VALTION TEKNILLINEN TUTKIMUSKESKUS (VALW-C)
GA 2022276535
AB    NOVELTY - A method of selectively catalytically reducing dinitrogen oxide present in a gaseous sample involves providing a catalyst capable of reducing dinitrogen oxide; bringing the gaseous sample into contact with the catalyst to reduce dinitrogen oxide in gaseous sample in the presence of the catalyst; where as a result of the reduction step, the gaseous sample is adapted for determination of the amount of an isotopic form of carbon dioxide (CO2) in the gaseous sample.
   USE - A method of selectively catalytically reducing dinitrogen oxide present in a gaseous sample.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the applicability of various carbon dioxide extraction and trapping methods, and can reduce the amount of N2O without further diluting the sample. The system enables automated onsite and online monitoring of fugitive radiocarbon emissions in nuclear facilities. Conversion of N2O by reducing catalyst provides a significant improvement in the sensitivity of a subsequent carbon isotope determination by infrared spectroscopy.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) extracting carbon dioxide from a gaseous sample which involves concentrating the gaseous sample to increase the concentration of carbon dioxide, to obtain a concentrated gaseous sample; and bringing the concentrated gaseous sample into contact with a catalyst capable of reducing dinitrogen oxide to remove any dinitrogen oxide in the concentrated gaseous sample, to form nitrogen (N2) and oxygen (O2) as reaction products of the reduction reaction;
   (2) detecting carbon dioxide in a gaseous sample comprising at least carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxide (N2O), which involves catalytically reducing the dinitrogen oxide present in the gaseous sample; and determining the amount of an isotopic form of carbon dioxide in the gaseous sample by infrared absorption spectroscopy;
   (3) apparatus comprising in a cascade, first unit for catalytically reducing dinitrogen oxide present in a gaseous sample; and second unit for determining the amount of an isotopic form of CO2 present in the gaseous sample by infrared absorption spectroscopy; and
   (4) use of copper catalyst for selectively catalyzing reduction of dinitrogen oxide in a gaseous sample containing 14-carbon dioxide, before detecting the 14-carbon dioxide by infrared absorption spectroscopy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the apparatus for selectively catalytically reducing dinitrogen oxide.
   Air sample (21)
   Particle filter (22)
   Multiport valves (24a, 24b)
   Heaters (25a, 25b, 25c)
   Concentrated sample (26)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The gaseous sample also comprises carbon dioxide and the catalyst is not capable of reducing the carbon dioxide. The catalyst is a heterogeneous copper (Cu) catalyst or a heterogeneous calcined hydrotalcite catalyst, and the catalyst is heated to a temperature of greater than or equal to 200 degrees C, preferably 200-500 degrees C. The untreated gaseous sample comprises greater than or equal to 90 vol.% carbon dioxide, and the catalytic reaction is carried out without any addition or feed of external gases. Preferred Process: The method further comprises before the reduction step concentrating the gaseous sample to increase the concentration of isotopic form of carbon dioxide to be determined, to obtain a concentrated gaseous sample, where the concentrated gaseous sample comprises greater than or equal to 90 vol.% carbon dioxide. Before the determining step, the gaseous sample is led to a cryogenic trap which has been cooled to a temperature of less than or equal to 195 K, where the carbon dioxide present in the sample solidifies and becomes trapped; releasing the trapped carbon dioxide by heating the cryogenic trap to a temperature greater than or equal to 195 K; where as a result a concentrated gaseous sample is obtained. Before the determining step, method includes flowing the gaseous sample through an anion exchange resin that is capable of selectively adsorbing CO2 present in the gaseous sample; releasing the adsorbed CO2 from the resin, preferably by heating the resin; where as a result a concentrated gaseous sample is obtained. The reduction step is carried out on concentrated gaseous sample, and the isotopic form is 14-carbon dioxide. Preferred Devices: The first unit for catalytically reducing dinitrogen oxide present in a gaseous sample comprises a first catalyst bed comprising a copper (Cu) catalyst. The apparatus further comprises upstream of first unit, extraction unit for concentrating the gaseous sample to increase the concentration of carbon dioxide containing the isotopic compounds to be determined. The extraction unit comprises an anion exchange resin that is capable of selectively adsorbing CO2 present in the gaseous sample, and the anion exchange resin features primary, secondary, and/or tertiary amino groups.

TI Device for directly capturing carbon dioxide from air, has cooler which is connected with adsorbent discharging port in bottom of desorber, while connecting top portion of desorber with fast separating head of high level tank through lifting pipe.
AU LIU Y
   QIU F
AE BEIJING DERUNCHEN ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTI (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 202206752E
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a thin moving bed (7) that is composed of a filter grid shell (6) and a filter grid inner cylinder (5). The high level tank (1) and several draught fans (2) are set on top of bed. The lower portion of the high level tank is provided with a discharging pipe (8). The top portion of high level tank is provided with an air outlet (15). The multiple draught fan is connected with the filter grid inner cylinder. The upper portion of tank is provided with a fast separating head (17). A desorber (10) is connected with the feed opening of bed. The desorber is internally provided with a heating coil pipe (11), a CO2 distribution pipe (12) and a filter cylinder (16). The top of desorber is provided with a CO2 outlet, while providing adsorbent discharging port (13) in bottom. A cooler (20) is connected with the adsorbent discharge port. The top portion of desorber is connected with the fast separating head of the high level tank through the lifting pipe (18).
   USE - Device for directly capturing carbon dioxide from air, used in environment protection, atmospheric treatment, and resource utilization field. Can also be used in research and development work.
   ADVANTAGE - The device uses spherical solid amine thin layer moving bed to directly collect carbon dioxide from the air, realizing high efficiency, large scale of carbon dioxide capture, and can continuously produce high purity carbon dioxide product. The device has compact arrangement, small floor area, no waste solid, waste liquid discharge, and is capable of reducing the device investment and collecting cost of the carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for directly capturing carbon dioxide from air.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the device for directly capturing carbon dioxide from air.

TI Producing ethylene and syngas involves reacting,            via oxidative coupling of methane (OCM) reaction,            reactant mixture in reaction zone to produce product            mixture, introducing another reactant mixture,            recovering and recycling stream.
AU MAMEDOV A
   WEST D
   SARSANI V S R
   LIANG W
AE SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
   MAMEDOV A (MAME-Individual)

   LIANG W (LIAN-Individual)
   SARSANI V S R (SARS-Individual)
   WEST D (WEST-Individual)
   MAMEDOV A (MAME-Individual)
   SABIC GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES BV (SABI-C)
GA 2018619833
AB    NOVELTY - Ethylene and syngas are produced by (a)                reacting, via oxidative coupling of methane (OCM)                reaction, 1st reactant mixture in 1st reaction zone                to produce 1st product mixture where 1st reaction                zone includes OCM catalyst, 1st reactant mixture                includes methane and oxygen, and 1st product                mixture includes ethylene, ethane, hydrogen, carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide, and unreacted methane;                (b) introducing 2nd reactant mixture containing                portion of 1st product mixture to 2nd reaction zone                to produce 2nd product mixture; (c) recovering                methane stream, ethane stream, carbon dioxide                stream, ethylene stream, and syngas stream from                portion of 2nd product mixture where syngas stream                contains carbon monoxide and hydrogen; and (d)                recycling portion of ethane stream and portion of                carbon dioxide stream to 2nd reaction zone.
   USE - Production of ethylene and syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - Overall efficiency of the process is                increased. Ethylene cracking and carbon dioxide                hydrogenation can increase ethylene and syngas                production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Ethylene and syngas are produced by (a)                reacting, via oxidative coupling of methane (OCM)                reaction, 1st reactant mixture in 1st reaction zone                to produce 1st product mixture where 1st reaction                zone includes OCM catalyst, 1st reactant mixture                includes methane and oxygen, and 1st product                mixture includes ethylene, ethane, hydrogen, carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide, and unreacted methane;                (b) introducing 2nd reactant mixture containing                portion of 1st product mixture to 2nd reaction zone                to produce 2nd product mixture where 2nd reaction                zone excludes catalyst, common reactor (10)                includes both 1st reaction zone (11) and 2nd                reaction zone (12), portion of ethane of 2nd                reactant mixture undergoes cracking reaction to                produce ethylene, portion of carbon dioxide of 2nd                reactant mixture undergoes hydrogenation reaction                to carbon monoxide, 2nd product mixture includes                ethylene, ethane, hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon                dioxide, and unreacted methane, and amount of                ethylene in 2nd product mixture is greater than                amount of ethylene in 1st product mixture; (c)                recovering methane stream, ethane stream, carbon                dioxide stream, ethylene stream, and syngas stream                from portion of 2nd product mixture where syngas                stream contains carbon monoxide and hydrogen; and                (d) recycling portion of ethane stream and portion                of carbon dioxide stream to 2nd reaction                zone.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                ethylene and syngas production system.
   Reactor (10)
   1st reaction zone (11)
   2nd reaction zone (12)
   Separation unit (20)
   Ethylene and syngas production system                (100)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Processes: Portion of ethane                  stream and portion of carbon dioxide stream are                  recycled via common recycle stream. Portion of                  ethane stream and portion of carbon dioxide                  stream are recycled to 2nd reaction zone at                  carbon dioxide to ethane molar ratio of                  0.8:1-4:1. Carbon dioxide stream is separated                  from 2nd product mixture by amine absorption.                  Portion of water is separated from 2nd product                  mixture before recovering methane stream, ethane                  stream, carbon dioxide stream, ethylene stream,                  and syngas stream from portion of 2nd product                  mixture. Method includes cryogenic distillation                  to recover methane stream, ethane stream,                  ethylene stream, and syngas stream. Portion of                  methane stream is recycled to 1st reaction zone.                  Portion of syngas stream is contacted with                  methanol production catalyst to produce methanol.                  Portion of ethylene stream is contacted with                  acetylene hydrogenation catalyst. Portion of                  acetylene of ethylene stream is selectively                  hydrogenated to produce ethylene. Recycled ethane                  stream and/or recycled carbon dioxide stream are                  contacted with additional ethane and/or                  additional carbon dioxide. Supplemental ethane                  and supplemental carbon dioxide are introduced                  into 2nd reaction zone at molar ratio of                  0.8:1-4:1. Preferred Components: Second product                  mixture contains water. Hydrogen to carbon                  monoxide is molar ratio of 0.5:1-1:1. Ethylene                  stream contains ethylene and acetylene. Ethane to                  methane molar ratio in second reactant mixture is                  0.03:1-0.07:1. Carbon dioxide to methane molar                  ratio in 2nd reactant mixture is 0.1:1-0.2:1.                  Preferred Devices: Common reactor includes                  adiabatic reactor, autothermal reactor, tubular                  reactor, and/or continuous flow reactor.                  Preferred Parameters: First reaction zone has                  temperature of 750-900 degrees C and 2d reaction                  zone has temperature of 750-900 degrees C. First                  reaction zone has residence time of 10-750 ms and                  gas hourly space velocity of 3000-36000/hour.                  Second reaction zone has residence time of 100                  ms-2.5 s and has hourly space velocity of                  500-30000/hour.

TI Dispensing apparatus for dispensing carbonated            beverage liquid, comprises beverage source, dispensing            tap, dispense tube, infusion chamber to receive            carbonated beverage liquid, sensing unit to measure            carbon dioxide level and control unit.
AU VANDEKERCKHOVE S
   PENN A
   PAYNE A
AE ANHEUSER BUSCH INBEV SA (ANHE-C)
   ANHEUSER BUSCH INBEV SA (ANHE-C)
   ANHEUSER-BUSCH INBEV NV (ANHE-C)
   ANHEUSER BUSCH INBEV SA (ANHE-C)
   ANHEUSER BUSCH INBEV SA (ANHE-C)
GA 201808699T
AB    NOVELTY - Dispensing apparatus comprises (i) a beverage                source containing a carbonated beverage liquid,                (ii) a dispensing tap, (iii) a dispense tube for                disposing the beverage source in fluid                communication with the dispensing tap, (iv) an                infusion chamber arranged in connection with the                dispense tube between the beverage source and the                dispensing tap to receive the carbonated beverage                liquid, (v) a sensing unit configured to determine                a level of carbon dioxide in the carbonated                beverage liquid, (vi) a carbon dioxide source                coupled to the dispense tube, and (vii) a control                unit.
   USE - The dispensing apparatus is useful for                dispensing a carbonated beverage liquid. The                carbonated beverage liquid is a malt-based                carbonated beverage (all claimed). The dispensing                apparatus is useful for infusing solid ingredients                with a carbonated beverage liquid, and preparing                beverage products. The beverage liquid is beer,                wine, cider, hard liquor (e.g. distilled beverage,                spirit, liquor, and hard alcohol), soft drinks                (e.g. cola, soda, pop, tonic, and seltzer), iced                tea, soda water and other types of carbonated                beverages.
   ADVANTAGE - The dispensing apparatus ensures excellent                taste characteristic and/or visual appeal of a                carbonated beverage liquid.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Dispensing apparatus (100) comprises (i) a                beverage source (102) containing a carbonated                beverage liquid, (ii) a dispensing tap (114), (iii)                a dispense tube for disposing the beverage source                in fluid communication with the dispensing tap,                (iv) an infusion chamber (118) arranged in                connection with the dispense tube between the                beverage source and the dispensing tap to receive                the carbonated beverage liquid, where the infusion                chamber is configured to infuse an infusing                ingredient with the carbonated beverage liquid, (v)                a sensing unit configured to determine a level of                carbon dioxide in the carbonated beverage liquid                downstream of the infusion chamber, (vi) a carbon                dioxide source coupled to the dispense tube through                a valve, and (vii) a control unit configured to                operate the valve to allow carbon dioxide gas, from                the carbon dioxide source, to the carbonated                beverage liquid based on the determined level of                carbon dioxide in the carbonated beverage liquid,                so as to bring the level of carbon dioxide to a                predefined level. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included                for a method for dispensing a carbonated beverage                liquid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                dispensing apparatus.
   Dispensing apparatus (100)
   Beverage source (102)
   Dispensing tap (114)
   Infusion chamber (118)
   Infusion chamber outlet (122)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Apparatus: The control unit is                  configured to at least partially restore the                  level of carbon dioxide in the carbonated                  beverage liquid to a level before the infusion,                  where the infusion chamber is arranged in-line                  with the dispense tube. The apparatus further                  comprises a carbonation chamber arranged                  downstream of the infusion chamber, where the                  carbonation chamber comprises a beverage inlet in                  fluid communication with the infusion chamber, a                  beverage outlet in fluid communication with the                  dispensing tap, and a carbon dioxide inlet in                  fluid communication with the carbon dioxide                  source. The carbonation chamber comprises (a) a                  sparging device coupled to the carbon dioxide                  inlet and configured to bubble the carbon dioxide                  gas through the carbonated beverage liquid in the                  carbonation chamber, and (b) at least one                  transparent wall. The sensing unit is further                  configured to determine a level of carbon dioxide                  in the carbonated beverage liquid in the                  carbonation chamber. The sensing unit comprises a                  manometer, or one of an ultrasonic based meter, a                  capacitance based meter and a resistance based                  meter. The valve is a check valve.

PT P
PN CN114558440-A
TI High-efficacy zinc extraction coupled with pulp            method flue gas desulfurization and carbon-fixing            technology useful in steel, comprises e.g. reacting            zinc sulfate and aqueous ammonia in desulfurization            mother liquor with carbon dioxide in flue gas and            preparing zinc-ammonia complex solution.
AU LI C
   WANG D
   LIN L
   TIAN S
   ZHAO Q
   NING P
   JIA L
AE UNIV KUNMING SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UKST-C)
GA 2022772345
AB    NOVELTY - High-efficacy zinc extraction coupled with                pulp method flue gas desulfurization and                carbon-fixing technology comprises e.g. (i) adding                aqueous ammonia into the high-chloride zinc ash to                be treated, carrying out ammonia leaching                dechlorination, removing the chlorine and carrying                out solid-liquid separation; (ii) adding aqueous                ammonia and ammonium sulfate solution into the                filter residue, stirring, leaching, generating                zinc-ammonium sulfate complex and leaching; (iii)                reacting the obtained zinc-ammonia complex solution                with sulfur dioxide flue gas and obtaining                desulfurization mother liquor and flue gas; and                (iv) reacting in the gained desulfurization mother                liquor, zinc sulfate and aqueous ammonia                continuously with carbon dioxide in the flue gas,                separating solid-liquid, obtaining basic zinc                carbonate and ammonium sulfate solution and                preparing zinc-ammonia complex solution, and                processing part as a by-product.
   USE - The method is useful for high-efficacy zinc                extraction coupled with pulp method flue gas                desulfurization and carbon-fixing technology is                useful in steel and colored related                industries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides zinc-ammonia complex                liquid with high desulfurization activity; utilizes                zinc ammonia complex in complex liquid, (hydrogen)                zinc oxide and aqueous ammonia to synergistically                enhance removing sulfur dioxide in the flue gas;                utilizes the zinc sulfurate and aqueous ammonia in                the desulfurizedization mother liquor that continue                to react with the carbon dioxide in flue gases thus                realizes the decarburization of the gas, and                obtains the basic zinc carbonate and ammonium                sulfate, where the separated ammonium sulfurate can                be partially recycled for preparing zinc ammonium                complex liquid; is economical; utilizes the fresh                way of smelting smoke dust; and separates and                recycles valuable component in the zinc ash.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - High-efficacy zinc extraction coupled with                pulp method flue gas desulfurization and                carbon-fixing technology comprises (i) adding                aqueous ammonia into the high-chloride zinc ash to                be treated, mixing fully to carry out ammonia                leaching dechlorination, removing the chlorine in                the zinc ash in the form of ammonium chloride,                carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain                filter residue and filtrate, and obtaining filtrate                as a by-product; (ii) adding aqueous ammonia and                ammonium sulfate solution into the filter residue,                stirring the obtained mixed solution fully,                leaching, adding aqueous ammonia and ammonium                sulfate to react with zinc oxide and/or zinc                hydroxide in the filter residue to generate                zinc-ammonium sulfate complex and leaching zinc                selectively to obtain a zinc-ammonia complex                solution with desulfurization activity; (iii)                reacting the obtained zinc-ammonia complex solution                with sulfur dioxide flue gas, utilizing the                zinc-ammonia complex, zinc oxide and/or zinc                hydroxide and aqueous ammonia in the system and                strengthening synergistically the removal of sulfur                dioxide in flue gas to obtain desulfurization                mother liquor and flue gas after desulfurization;                and (iv) reacting in the gained desulfurization                mother liquor, zinc sulfate and aqueous ammonia                continuously with carbon dioxide in the flue gas                after desulfurization, realizing flue gas                decarbonization, separating solid-liquid, obtaining                basic zinc carbonate and ammonium sulfate solution,                returning part of the obtained ammonium sulfate                solution for recycling to prepare zinc-ammonia                complex solution, and processing part as a                by-product.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a technical view of a                high-efficacy zinc extraction coupled with pulp                method flue gas desulfurization and carbon-fixing                technology. (Drawing includes non-English language                text).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The high chloride zinc ash                  described in step (1) is the product obtained by                  reducing and volatilizing blast furnace gas ash                  and gas mud by heat method. The leaching is                  carried out for 30-120 minutes at                  20-40&#8451;, and the stirring intensity is                  300-700 revolutions/minute.Preferred Components: The high chloride zinc                  ash comprises &#8805;10 wt.% zinc and                  &#8805;0.1 wt.% chlorine. The aqueous ammonia                  in the step (i) is 3-28% aqueous ammonia which is                  added in a solid-to-liquid ratio of 1 (g): 2-6                  (ml), and the ammonia immersion temperature is                  20-50&#8451;. The aqueous ammonia in the step                  (ii) is 20-28 wt.% aqueous ammonia and the                  additions of the aqueous ammonia and the ammonium                  sulfate solution are such that the molar                  concentration ratio of ammonia, ammonium sulfate                  and zinc in the obtained mixed solution is                  28-38:4-10:1. The ammoniacal liquor described in                  the step (ii) is 20% ammoniacal liquor, and the                  concentration of the ammonium sulfate solution is                  2mol/liter and the added amount is such that the                  ratio of high chloride zinc ash to the added                  aqueous ammonia and ammonium sulfate solution is                  1 (g): 2.5 (ml): 2.5 (ml). The oxygen content in                  the sulfur dioxide flue gas is 9-25%, the sulfur                  dioxide concentration is 4000-6000 mg/m3, the gas                  flow rate is 300-600 ml/min, and the treatment                  temperature is 15-45&#8451;.

PN CN112913573-A
TI Promoting formation of large number of Morchella            primordia by plowing soil, spraying quicklime, crushing            and spreading Morchella strains, inoculating in            nutrition bag, spraying water and cultivating Morchella            primordium.
AU CHEN H
   ZHANG J
   FENG Z
AE SHANGHAI AGRIC SCI ACAD (SHNY-C)
GA 2021658084
AB    NOVELTY - Method for promoting formation of large number                of Morchella primordia involves (a) removing weeds                and sundries in the soil of the cultivation field,                plowing the soil, spraying quicklime, drying and                evenly tilling the soil, (b) selecting Morchella                strains, crushing and spreading the strains on the                surface of the soil, (c) adding wheat kernels,                husks and filling materials into a bag, then adding                water and sterilizing, (d) placing a nutrient bag                on the surface of the soil, (e) installing                sprinkler irrigation belt and spraying water to                keep the soil moist, (f) watering to make the                conidia on the soil surface disappear, maintaining                the soil surface water content and soil temperature                and controlling light intensity and light time,                relative humidity in the air and carbon dioxide                concentration and (g) when the Morchella primordium                appears, adjusting the light intensity, soil                temperature, air temperature, relative humidity of                the air and carbon dioxide concentration.
   USE - The method is useful for promoting large                number of Morchella primordia.
   ADVANTAGE - The method promotes production of Morchella                fruiting body and provide key parameters and                specific implementation directions for the                development of Morchella industrialized cultivation                technology.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for promoting formation of large number                of Morchella primordia involves (a) removing weeds                and sundries in the soil of the cultivation field,                plowing the loose soil at a depth of 15-20 cm, then                evenly spraying quicklime on the soil surface of                the cultivation field for sterilization, drying for                3-7 days and then using the rotary tiller to evenly                till the soil, (b) selecting Morchella strains with                a age of 25-30 days, crushing and spreading the                strains evenly on the surface of the soil, where                the sowing amount is 200-350 kg/acre and cover with                soil layer having a thickness of 2-5 cm, (c) adding                wheat kernels, husks and filling materials in a dry                weight ratio of 70-85:10-20:5-20 into a bag to                obtain the nutrition bag, then adding water to                adjust the moisture content to 58-63%, making each                nutrition bag weigh 250-300 g and sterilizing,                where the filling material comprises wood pellets                or corn cob pellets, (d) when the mycelium grows to                the surface of the soil, placing a nutrient bag on                the surface of the soil at a rate of 3-8 bags/m2                and when the fruiting is over or the nutrient bag                is contaminated, removing the nutrient bag, (e)                installing the sprinkler irrigation belt evenly                along the length of the ridge and spraying water                from the sprinkler irrigation belt to keep the soil                at the depth of 15-20 cm in the cultivation                compartment moist, (f) when the soil temperature is                greater than or equal to 9 degrees C 45-60 days                after sowing, starting the mushroom invigoration                operation, and watering to make the conidia on the                soil surface disappear, maintaining the soil                surface water content at 18-25% and the soil                temperature at 9-13 degrees C and controlling light                intensity and light time at 2880-3840 lx/hour,                relative humidity in the air at 60-70% and carbon                dioxide concentration within 1000 ppm and (g) when                the Morchella primordium appears, adjusting the                light intensity at 100-200 lx, soil temperature at                11-18 degrees C, air temperature at less than or                equal to 20 degrees C, relative humidity of the air                at 70-80%, and carbon dioxide concentration within                1000 ppm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - AGRICULTURE - Preferred Method: In step (e), the sprinkler                  irrigation belt is a 5-hole sprinkler irrigation                  belt with a diameter of 2.5 cm. The step (c)                  involves sterilizing at 121 degrees C and 0.1 MPa                  for 2 hours.

   UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)

PN CN109716997-A
TI Method for cultivating mushroom, involves evenly            distributing material on mushroom bed, leveling,            compacting and spreading remaining strains on surface            and covering surface of mushroom bed with agricultural            film.
AU MA J
   HUANG C
   SHAO H
AE JIANGSU HONGSHENG MUSHROOM CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201942963V
AB    NOVELTY - A mushroom cultivating method involves evenly                distributing a material on the mushroom bed, where                the strain of the wheat grain is 1 liter/m2 and the                strain of 3/5, after mixing with the base material,                leveling and compacting and spreading the remaining                2/5 strains on the surface. The surface of the                mushroom bed is covered with agricultural film to                prevent the base material from losing water,                properly puncture the hole, and moisturize                breathable and the relative humidity of the indoor                air of the mushroom is kept above 80%, the                concentration of carbon dioxide is 0.5-1.1% and the                mushroom hyphae is filled with material layer                between 13-15 days. The material is covered by peat                soil and germinated until the hyphae appear on the                surface of the soil, the water is sprayed                thoroughly and carry out the bacteria on the next                day. The soil is broken and formed into a pellet                structure at 24-27 degrees C for one day and the                concentration of carbon dioxide is reduced to less                than 0.1%.
   USE - Method for cultivating mushroom.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables cultivating mushroom with                low lignin content, high yield, better quality and                strong operability, in simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A mushroom cultivating method involves evenly                distributing a material on the mushroom bed, where                the second fermentation material of the mushroom                bed is about 100 kg/m2, the material temperature is                at 25-28 degrees C and the strain of the wheat                grain is 1 liter/m2 and the strain of 3/5, after                mixing with the base material, leveling and                compacting and spreading the remaining 2/5 strains                on the surface. The surface of the mushroom bed is                covered with agricultural film to prevent the base                material from losing water, properly puncture the                hole, and moisturize breathable and the relative                humidity of the indoor air of the mushroom is kept                above 80%, the concentration of carbon dioxide is                0.5-1.1% and the mushroom hyphae is filled with                material layer between 13-15 days. The material is                covered by peat soil and germinated until the                hyphae appear on the surface of the soil, the water                is sprayed thoroughly and carry out the bacteria on                the next day. The soil is broken and formed into a                pellet structure at 24-27 degrees C for one day and                the concentration of carbon dioxide is reduced to                less than 0.1%. The low temperature treatment and                ventilation are used to promote the mushroom, when                the mushroom body grows to a large size, the room                temperature is adjusted at 15-17 degrees C                according to the amount of occurrence, the mushroom                is collected from the 17-20th day after the soil is                covered, and a large amount of water needs during                the harvesting period, gently pinch the lid and                screw into the head, pay attention to protect the                young mushroom, remove the mushroom root after                picking the mushroom, level the soil and make the                mushroom tidy. The water spray is light, but not                too wet, after the formation of the needle                mushroom, is necessary to stop spraying water, grow                to a large soybean grain and increase the amount of                water spray. The three tide mushrooms collected in                the air-conditioned mushroom house, pass the hot                steam and sterilized at 75 degrees C for more than                3 hours to completely eliminate the bacteria or                pests, the door is completely opened, and the net                is placed, pull out the base material and                transferred to the truck with conveyor belt. The                sterilized mushroom is rinsed with water, wash the                mesh cloth and use pentachlorophenol and                formalin.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The base material is                  composed of 72.62 wt.% sand willow wood chips,                  11.55 wt.% bran, 5.22 wt.% maize flour, 8.61 wt.%                  soybean meal and 2 wt.% raw gypsum. The organic                  fertilizer comprises 40 wt.% straw, 30 wt.%                  Chinese medicine waste residue and 30 wt.% grass                  coal.

PN WO2020055527-A1; US10603654-B1
TI Pelletized sorbent used for separation of carbon            dioxide from a gaseous mixture, comprises first            component having basic immobilized amine sorbent,            second component having inorganic strength additive and            third component having polymer binder.
AU WILFONG W C
   MCMAHAN L G
   YEE S
   KAIL B W
   GRAY M L
   SOONG Y
AE US DEPT ENERGY (USAT-C)
   WILFONG W C (WILF-Individual)
   MCMAHAN L G (MCMA-Individual)
   YEE S (YEES-Individual)
   KAIL B W (KAIL-Individual)
GA 2020219277
AB    NOVELTY - Pelletized sorbent comprises: a first                component comprising a basic immobilized amine                sorbent; a second component comprising an inorganic                strength additive; and a third component comprising                a polymer binder, where the basic immobilized amine                sorbent and solid inorganic strength additive are                interconnected by the polymer binder.
   USE - The sorbents are useful for separation of                carbon dioxide from a gaseous mixture (all claimed)                e.g. post combustion gas stream.
   ADVANTAGE - The sorbents: utilize the polymer binder that                interconnects the basic immobilized amine sorbent                particles and inorganic strength additive to form                an agglomerated pellet capable of carbon dioxide                sorption, thus interconnection leading to both                physical binding of the constituents and chemical                bonding of the polymer binder to the basic                immobilized amine sorbent and inorganic strength                additive, thus providing added strength to the                pelletized sorbent; exhibits high crush strength,                high attrition resistance, good carbon dioxide                capture, and hydrophobicity in gas separation                operations; and primarily absorb the carbon dioxide                by first external diffusion of carbon dioxide from                the bulk gas stream to the surface of the pellet,                followed by diffusion of carbon dioxide into the                bulk pellet structure, then pore diffusion into the                BIAS particles comprising the pellet, and                subsequent reaction of carbon dioxide with BIAS                amine groups; and utilize the inorganic strength                additive that provides a single or many solid                substrates which primarily serve to provide                structural support to the pellet and facilitates                the structural integrity of the basic immobilized                amine sorbent based pellet, thus avoiding                structural degradation and material loss.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                pelletized sorbent comprising a first component                comprising 68-85 wt.% basic immobilized amine                sorbent, where the basic immobilized amine sorbent                is a class 1, class 2, or hybrid class 1 /class 2                BIAS, a second component comprising 5-37 wt.%                inorganic strength additive, where the inorganic                strength additive is fly ash, and a third component                comprising a polymer binder, where the basic                immobilized amine sorbent and the strength additive                are interconnected by the polymer binder, a carbon                dioxide sorbent capacity of greater than 1.7                mmol/g, a crush strength of greater than or equal                to 1 MPa or attrition value of less than 1% after                24 hours and a PCR value of greater than 50%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inorganic strength                  additive comprises fly ash, bottom ash, clay,                  activated carbon, silicon dioxide, aluminum oxide                  or calcium oxide, preferably fly ash. The sorbent                  comprises 63-92 wt.% basic immobilized amine                  sorbent. The sorbent is hydrophobic. The basic                  immobilized amine sorbent comprises an                  epoxy-based crosslinker (preferred) or an                  antioxidant, where the antioxidant is an                  inorganic antioxidant. The sorbent further                  comprises: a crush strength of greater than or                  equal to 0.3 MPa, preferably greater than or                  equal to 1.3 MPa; a carbon dioxide sorbent                  capacity of greater than or equal to 1 mmol/g,                  preferably greater than or equal to 2 mmol/g; a                  PCR value of greater than or equal to 20%,                  preferably greater than or equal to 50%; and a                  carbon dioxide sorbent capacity of greater than                  or equal to 1.7 mmol/g, preferably greater than 2                  mmol/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer binder                  comprises poly(vinyl chloride), poly(chloroprene)                  latex, poly(chloroprene), poly(vinyl alcohol),                  poly(vinyl acetate) or polystyrene, preferably                  poly(chloroprene) latex, poly(vinyl chloride) or                  poly(chloroprene). The BIAS comprises an                  antioxidant further comprises a fourth component                  comprising an epoxy-based crosslinker.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN109287288-A
TI Method for storing wheat, involves spreading wheat evenly on bamboo mat with rice paper, performing initial screening, performing carbon dioxide treatment, secondary screening, manual drying and packaging.
AU LI H
AE HUBEI TIANMEN QINGLONG RICE CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 201914846T
AB    NOVELTY - A wheat storing method involves spreading the wheat evenly on the bamboo mat with the rice paper, and let it dry. The wheat is combed and turned for every 2-3 hours, where daily sun exposure time is 5-8 hours, and continuous sun exposure is 3-5 days. Initial screening is performed by mechanically removing broken, dead leaves and grass seed impurities, and selecting wheat of quality and maturity that meets the prescribed standards. Carbon dioxide treatment is performed by placing wheat treated in a closed container, injecting carbon dioxide into the container, where the concentration of carbon dioxide in the container is 85-95%, and the insecticide is sterilized by UV irradiation for 12-14 hours. Secondary screening is performed by picking the wheat treated by the previous step, followed by manual drying by placing wheat in a drying room, drying until the moisture content of the wheat is less than 6%, drying is stopped, and the wheat is wrapped with a ventilated sack, and packaging.
   USE - Method for storing wheat.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables storing wheat with better anti-insect and moisture-proof effect, so that the wheat maintains a better quality.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A wheat storing method involves spreading the wheat evenly on the bamboo mat with the rice paper, and let it dry. The wheat is combed and turned for every 2-3 hours, where daily sun exposure time is 5-8 hours, and continuous sun exposure is 3-5 days. Initial screening is performed by mechanically removing broken, dead leaves and grass seed impurities, and selecting wheat of quality and maturity that meets the prescribed standards. Carbon dioxide treatment is performed by placing wheat treated in a closed container, injecting carbon dioxide into the container, where the concentration of carbon dioxide in the container is 85-95%, and the insecticide is sterilized by UV irradiation for 12-14 hours. Secondary screening is performed by picking the wheat treated by the previous step, followed by manual drying by placing wheat in a drying room, drying until the moisture content of the wheat is less than 6%, drying is stopped, and the wheat is wrapped with a ventilated sack, and packaging by pumping the air in each bag filled with carbon dioxide in a ratio of 10-12 kg of carbon dioxide per 10000 kg wheat, and sealing bag.

PN CN109908907-A; CN109908907-B
TI Methane carbon dioxide reforming synthesis gas catalyst comprises active component, auxiliary agent and carrier, active component is selected from nickel or coablt, preferably nickel, auxiliary is silicon, and carrier is activated carbon.
AU SUN X
   ZHANG S
   ZHANG Q
   SONG Y
   LIU J
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM RES (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC DALIAN PETROLEUM & PETROCHEM (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2019577362
AB    NOVELTY - Methane carbon dioxide reforming synthesis gas catalyst comprises an active component, an auxiliary agent and a carrier, the active component is selected from nickel or coablt, preferably nickel, auxiliary is silicon. The carrier is an alkaline earth metal modified petroleum coke-based activated carbon, and the alkaline earth metal is preferably calcium, one or two of magnesium, where active component is 5-30 wt.%, preferably 10-20 wt.%. The alkaline earth metal is 1-10 wt.%, preferably 3-5 wt.%, and carrier is contained in an amount of 50-90 wt.%, preferably 70-8 5wt.%.
   USE - Methane carbon dioxide reforming synthesis gas catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The methane carbon dioxide reforming synthesis gas catalyst has good dispersibility and high activity, and it is not easy to accumulate carbon.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing methane carbon dioxide reforming synthesis gas catalyst, which involves:
   (A) mixing petroleum coke, alkaline earth metal compound and activator, mixing and activating;
   (B) mixing the sample with water for low temperature treatment, then washing and drying;
   (C) introducing the auxiliary silicon into solid sample, followed by drying and baking; and
   (D) introducing active component into the solid sample, after drying and calcination to obtain methane carbon dioxide reforming catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkaline earth metal-containing compound is an alkaline earth metal salt or a hydroxide, preferably a hydroxide. The activator is one or more of potassium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydrogencarbonate or sodium hydrogencarbonate, preferably potassium hydroxide. The monosilicic acid solution is prepared from a silicon-containing compound, which is a silica sol, water glass or ethyl orthosilicate, preferably tetraethyl orthosilicate. Preferred Conditions: The specific surface of the catalyst is from 300-1,500 square meter per gram, preferably 500-1000 square meter per gram. The pore size distribution of the catalyst, less than 2 nm of micropores is greater than 60 wt.%, preferably greater than 80 wt.%. The activation temperature is 600-1000 degrees C, preferably 700-900 degrees C, and the activation time is 5-240 minutes, preferably 10-120 minutes. The activation process is carried out under microwave irradiation conditions of 2450 mega hertz or 915 mega hertz. The microwave power is 1-10 kilowatt. The calcination temperature is 300 to 700 degrees C, preferably the calcination temperature is 400-600 degrees C, the calcination time is 2 to 10 h, and the calcination time is preferably 4 to 8h. Preferred Compositions: The mass ratio of the petroleum coke, the alkaline earth metal compound, and the activator is 1:0.01-0.1:1-5, preferably 1:0.02-0.07:2-4. Preferred Process: The petroleum coke is ground to a powder form, and then uniformly mixed with the alkaline earth metal-containing compound and the activator, and the temperature is raised to the activation temperature, and after the activation is completed, the temperature is lowered to room temperature for subsequent treatment. The obtained solid sample is impregnated the solid with a soluble salt solution containing the active component cation. The solubility of the active component cation salt is a hydrochloride, nitrate or sulfate, preferably a nitrate.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of cumin oil involves (S1) adding 100 pts. wt. cumin and 25-30 pts. wt. absolute ethanol, mixing, sealing, soaking at room temperature for 1-3 hours, (S2) adding 20-40 pts. wt. mushroom water, heating at 40-50 degrees C for 0.5-1.5 hours, ultrasonically treating, drying at 100-105 degrees C, (S3) pulverizing, extracting using supercritical carbon dioxide equipment at 25-30 degrees C, 10-15 MPa, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 10-20 L/hour for 5-10 minutes, (S4) depressurizing to atmospheric pressure, extracting at 40-55 degrees C, 20-30 MPa, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 20-30 L/hour for 10-15 minutes, (S5) depressurizing to atmospheric pressure, extracting at 60-70 degrees C, 50-60 MPa, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 50-55 L/hour for 20-30 minutes, (S6) depressurizing to atmospheric pressure, and extracting at 75 degrees C, 65 MPa, and carbon dioxide flow rate of 60 L/hour for 5 minutes.
   USE - Preparation of cumin oil for food.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively retains the nutrient component in the cumin extract, and provides cumin oil with high extraction rate of 35%, which has excellent antibacterial effect and antiseptic effect on food.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The mushroom water is prepared by adding dried shiitake mushrooms to 20 times water, boiling at 100 degrees C for 3-4 hours, cooling to room temperature, and filtering the filtrate. Preferred Conditions: The ultrasonic treatment is carried out at frequency of 40 KHz and input power of 200 W for 5-30 minutes. In step (S2), drying is carried out until a moisture content reaches to 5-8%. In step (S3), the product is pulverized into 100 mesh sieve.

PT P
PN CN113401929-A
TI Preparing nanometer calcium magnesium carbonate material comprises e.g. clarifying seawater, pumping into nanofiltration filter by water pump, injecting carbon dioxide flue gas, centrifuging to dehydrate seawater and drying.
AU WANG R
   XIONG L
   LIU Y
AE HUBEI FORBON TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 2021B07464
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nanometer calcium magnesium carbonate material by using nanofiltration seawater to capture carbon dioxide comprises (i) introducing seawater into the clarification tank, and part of the sediment in the seawater will naturally settle in the clarification tank, and clarifying, and pumping into the nanofiltration filter through a water pump, (ii) processing clear seawater by the nanofiltration filter, (iii) pumping high salinity seawater into flue gas absorption device, injecting carbon dioxide flue gas and adding sodium hydroxide solution, and (iv) using a plate and frame filter press or centrifuge to dehydrate the seawater containing calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate, drying by a tube bundle dryer and obtaining nano-scale calcium magnesium carbonate material.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nanometer calcium magnesium carbonate material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces the emission of carbon dioxide in the flue gas.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing nanometer calcium magnesium carbonate material by using nanofiltration seawater to capture carbon dioxide comprises (i) introducing seawater into the clarification tank, and part of the sediment in the seawater will naturally settle in the clarification tank, and clarifying, and pumping into the nanofiltration filter through a water pump, (ii) processing clear seawater by the nanofiltration filter, obtaining desalinated water and high-salinity seawater, and the salinity of the high-salinity seawater is 52.5-105 wt.%, (iii) pumping the high salinity seawater into the flue gas absorption device (carbonization tower), injecting carbon dioxide flue gas from the bottom of the device, controlling the amount of flue gas to control the seawater temperature to 20-45 degrees C, and adding sodium hydroxide solution, ethanolamine, and/or diethanolamine to control the pH of the seawater at 8-8.5, under weak alkaline conditions, reacting the carbon dioxide in the flue gas reacts with the calcium and magnesium ions in the seawater to form the precipitation of calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate, and (iv) using a plate and frame filter press or centrifuge to dehydrate the seawater containing calcium carbonate and magnesium carbonate obtained in step (iii), and controlling the water content at 30-55 wt.%, drying by a tube bundle dryer or a drum dryer to dry the moisture to less than 0.5 wt.%, and obtaining nano-scale calcium magnesium carbonate material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (iii), the carbon dioxide flue gas comes from the burning of coal, oil, natural gas, straw, garbage, or steelmaking, or cement industry production. The carbon dioxide flue gas needs to pass through the spray tower for dust removal in advance, until the concentration of carbon dioxide flue gas particulate matter drops below 20 ppm.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)
MC E31-U01; E34-B04; E34-D03B
IP C01F-011/18; C01F-005/24; B82Y-030/00; B82Y-040/00

AD CN113401929-A    CN10698376    23 Jun 2021

PT P
PN WO2021042212-A1; CA3150121-A1
TI Synthesis of zirconium-stabilized calcium oxide nanoparticle sorbent by dissolving calcium alkoxide in mixed aromatic and alcoholic solvent, adding, drying calcium hydroxide alcogel, and admixing zirconium compound with calcium compounds.
AU KARAMI D
   MAHINPEY N
AE UTI LP (UTIU-C)
GA 202124013E
AB    NOVELTY - A zirconium-stabilized calcium oxide nanoparticle sorbent is synthesized by (a) forming calcium oxide nanoparticle by treating calcium compounds in a calcium compound treatment by (i) dissolving calcium alkoxide in a mixed aromatic and alcoholic solvent to form calcium alkoxide solution, (ii) adding water to calcium alkoxide solution to form liquid calcium hydroxide alcogel, and (iii) drying calcium hydroxide alcogel to provide calcium oxide nanoparticle composition; and (b) admixing zirconium compound with greater than or equal to 1 calcium compounds in the calcium compound treatment, to form the zirconium-stabilized calcium oxide nanoparticle sorbent. The sorbent has a Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) surface area of greater than or equal to 100 m2/g, average particle size of 100-500 nm, and carbon dioxide capture capacity of greater than or equal to 10 mole/kg sorbent.
   USE - Synthesis of zirconium-stabilized calcium oxide nanoparticle sorbent or pelletized zirconium-stabilized calcium oxide nanoparticle sorbent for use as carbon dioxide sorbent in a fixed-bed calcium looping process (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared sorbent of calcium oxide nanoparticles stabilized with zirconia exhibits an almost 100% carbon-dioxide-capture efficiency. The loss of carbon dioxide capacity of the zirconia-stabilized sorbents is shown to be near 10-20% during multi-cycle operations.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) use of zirconium-stabilized calcium oxide nanoparticle composition as a carbon dioxide sorbent, where composition comprises intermixed zirconium and calcium oxide nanoparticles in a solid zirconium-calcium oxide sorbent having a BET surface area of greater than or equal to 100 m2/g with average particle size of 100-500 nm;
   (2) adsorption of carbon dioxide which involves exposing carbon dioxide gas to zirconium-stabilized calcium oxide nanoparticle composition, where composition comprises intermixed zirconium and calcium oxide nanoparticles in a solid zirconium-calcium oxide sorbent having a BET surface area of greater than or equal to 100 m2/g with average particle size of 100-500 nm; and
   (3) zirconium-stabilized calcium oxide nanoparticle composition comprising intermixed zirconium and calcium oxide nanoparticles in a solid zirconium-calcium oxide sorbent having a BET surface area of greater than or equal to 100 m2/g with average particle size of 100-500 nm.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: Zirconium-stabilized calcium oxide nanoparticle sorbent has a carbon dioxide capture capacity of greater than or equal to 5, preferably greater than or equal to 15 mole/kg sorbent. Zirconium-stabilized calcium oxide nanoparticle sorbent has a carbon dioxide capture capacity that loses no not greater than 10%, preferably not greater than 2% carbon dioxide capture capacity after 20 cycles. Active calcium oxide conversion by carbon dioxide capture of the zirconium-stabilized calcium oxide nanoparticle sorbent is greater than or equal to 90%, preferably greater than or equal to 96% in a first carbon capture cycle. Zirconium-stabilized calcium oxide nanoparticle sorbent has BET surface area of greater than or equal to 140 m2/g, preferably greater than or equal to 190 m2/g. Zirconium-stabilized calcium oxide nanoparticle sorbent has an average particle size of less than or equal to 500 nm, preferably less than or equal to 90 nm. Sorbent reversibly adsorbs carbon dioxide at 650-700 degrees C to form calcium carbonate (CaCO3). Sorbent is regenerated from CaCO3 at 850-900 degrees C with the release of carbon dioxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Calcium alkoxide is of formula (RO)3Ca. Zirconium compound is a zirconium alkoxide selected from zirconium(IV) ethoxide (Zr(ethoxide)4), zirconium(IV) propoxide (Zr(propoxide)4), or zirconium(IV) tert.-butoxide (Zr(tert.-butoxide)4). Zirconium stabilizer precursor is a zirconium alkoxide selected from Zr(ethoxide)4, Zr(propoxide)4, or Zr(tert.-butoxide)4. Core-shell surfactant is Pluronic 123(P123) or trimethylamine (TMA). Calcium alkoxide solution comprises zirconium in an opaque slurry. Liquid calcium hydroxide alcogel comprises zirconium and is clear and colorless.R=straight chain alkyl.Preferred Processes: Admixing the zirconium compound comprises mixing zirconium stabilizer precursor with calcium alkoxide in steps (i) or (ii), so that liquid calcium hydroxide alcogel comprises zirconium; or (2) incipient wetness impregnation (IWI) of calcium oxide nanoparticle composition with zirconium stabilizer precursor solution; or (3) shelling calcium oxide nanoparticle composition surface, before or after calcining the calcium oxide nanoparticle composition, in a core-shell treatment with a core-shell surfactant. Mixing zirconium stabilizer precursor with calcium alkoxide in step (i) or (ii), comprises dissolving zirconium stabilizer precursor in mixed aromatic and alcoholic solvent. Shelling comprises adding mesoporous zirconia to calcined calcium oxide nanoparticles by core-shell treatment using the core-shell surfactant. Admixing zirconium compound comprises adding zirconium tert.-butoxide to calcium methoxide in mixed aromatic and alcoholic solvent. Zirconium tert.-butoxide and calcium methoxide are added to mixed aromatic and alcoholic solvent in a ratio of 0.05-0.1 moles of Zr(tert.-butoxide)4 per mole of Ca(CH3O)2. Adding water comprises adding 2-5 moles of water per mole of calcium alkoxide. Synthesis method further comprises mixing calcium hydroxide alcogel for alcogel aging period to provide aged alcogel, and drying calcium hydroxide alcogel comprises drying the aged alcogel. Drying the calcium hydroxide alcogel comprises supercritical drying and vacuum dehydration. Calcium hydroxide alcogel comprises zirconium, and drying the calcium hydroxide alcogel comprises a thermal dehydration of zirconium-calcium hydroxide nanoparticles to provide dehydrated zirconium-calcium hydroxide nanoparticles. Thermal dehydration is carried out at least partially under a dehydrating vacuum pressure. Synthesis method further comprises calcining the dehydrated zirconium-calcium hydroxide nanoparticles to provide a calcined mixed zirconia-calcium oxide sorbent. Synthesis method further comprises pelletizing the zirconium-stabilized calcium oxide nanoparticle sorbent, to provide a pelletized zirconium-stabilized calcium oxide nanoparticle sorbent. Preferred Conditions: Thermal dehydration is carried out at a dehydration temperature of greater than or equal to 450 degrees C. Calcining is carried out at greater than or equal to 850 degrees C.
   Preferred Definitions:
   R=Me or Et.
DC E37 (Mixtures of many components; inorganic reactions and processes of general applicability.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. The air duct, fresh air ducts (8) and exhaust air ducts (11) are guided through a heat exchange device (4) for heat recovery. Multiple exhaust air intake inserts (9) are provided at the respective suction end of the exhaust air ducts for the extraction of the carbon dioxide contaminated exhaust air (19). A control unit (20) is configured for regulating the carbon dioxide content in the interior.
   USE - Ventilation device for interiors of residential and commercial buildings.

The ventilation device with the rearrangement of the exhaust air routing near the floor in cooperation with the fresh air preheated through the heat exchanger. The heat losses are reduced through a ventilation device and pleasant indoor atmosphere is maintained in living and commercial buildings with a consistently high air quality. The operation of the ventilation system is clean and energy-saving.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for operating a ventilation device.
