TI Oxygen carrier used in separate reaction step of reaction of producing gas containing carbon monoxide by reduction of carbon dioxide and reaction of reducing the oxidized oxygen carrier with reducing gas containing hydrogen, comprises metal oxide.
AU KOJI T
   KENICHI S
   EISUKE B
AE SEKISUI CHEM CO LTD (SEKI-C)
GA 2022476291
AB    NOVELTY - Oxygen carrier comprises a metal oxide (I).
   USE - The oxygen carrier is useful in separate reaction step of reaction of producing gas containing carbon monoxide by reduction of carbon dioxide and reaction of reducing the oxidized oxygen carrier with reducing gas containing hydrogen (claimed), and used in chemical looping method.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxygen carrier increases conversion efficiency of carbon dioxide into carbon monoxide, and converts carbon dioxide into valuable substance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Oxygen carrier comprises a metal oxide of formula: Cu1-x(M)xOy (I).
   M = element having an electronegativity of polling of 1.3-2.3;
   x = a positive real number;and
   y = 1-4.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for producing the oxygen carrier, involving dripping a raw material aqueous solution containing the copper salt and M salt and an alkaline aqueous solution into water to obtain a mixed solution, recovering the precipitate generated in the mixed solution, and obtaining the metal oxide by calcining the recovered precipitate at 500-800 degrees C; and
   (2) a method for producing a gas, involving contacting the oxygen carrier with a raw material gas containing carbon dioxide, reducing the carbon dioxide to produce a produced gas containing carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The electronegativity of M is 1.3-1.9, and when the metal oxide is measured by an X-ray diffraction method, at least one diffraction peak having a half width of 0.7 degrees or less is observed in the X-ray diffraction profile. The diffraction peak is observed in the X-ray diffraction profile with 2 theta of 60-65 degrees . The heat-preserving time of the calcining temperature is 1-24 hours, and the heating rate to the calcining temperature is 0.5-20 degrees C/minute. Preferred Components: The oxygen carrier comprises copper and a metal oxide containing zinc as M and containing a second element comprising zinc, aluminum, gadolinium, silicon, manganese, cobalt, yttrium, cerium and/or magnesium. The oxygen carrier comprises a binder comprising zeolite, aluminum oxide, titanium dioxide, silicon dioxide, zirconium dioxide and/or magnesium oxide, for binding the metal oxide. Preferred Composition: In the metal oxide, the molar ratio of the second element to the total of the copper and the second element is 10-90 mol.%. The amount of the binder is 70 parts mass or less.
   Preferred Definitions:
   M = one of the elements belonging to 3rd to 6th periods or elements belonging to the 2nd to 14th groups, Mg, Al, Si, Ti, V, Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, Zn, Ga, Zr, Nb, Ag, In, Hf or Ta;and
   x = 0.1-0.9.

PT P
PN CN112174183-A
TI Preparing nano-calcium carbonate useful in light calcium carbonate, by removing dust on discharge tail gas of rotary kiln, drying, adding crystal form control agent and surfactant to absorption liquid, filtering carbonized slurry and drying.
AU TIAN J
   HU P
   GU W
AE HUBEI HUDA TIANMU NEW ENERGY MATERIALIND (HUBE-Non-standard)
GA 202106161G
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing nano-calcium carbonate by purification and concentration of flue gas from rotary kiln, involves (a) removing dust on discharge tail gas of rotary kiln using spray tower until concentration of particulate matter is reduced to below 20 ppm, (b) drying dust removed flue gas using drying tower until the moisture is reduced to below 0.1 wt.%, (c) passing obtained flue gas to carbon dioxide enrichment device until the concentration of carbon dioxide in carbon dioxide enrichment device is greater than or equal to 20%, (d) introducing obtained gas to carbonization tower apparatus, adding carbon dioxide capture agent, crystal form control agent and surfactant to absorption liquid for carbonization, and (e) filtering obtained carbonized slurry and sequentially performing dehydration, drying and crushing to obtain nano-calcium carbonate having particle size of less than 100 nm.
   USE - The nano-calcium carbonate is useful in light calcium carbonate application.
   ADVANTAGE - The method meets the requirements for preparing nano-calcium carbonate carbonized gas, improves the utilization rate of carbon dioxide, ensures large amount of carbon dioxide in exhaust gas of rotary kiln to concentration of more than 20%, realizes high conversion rate, and exhibits high-yield of nano-calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Device: The step (c) involves using mesoporous fiber permeable membrane device by carbon dioxide enrichment for filtering flue gas. Preferred Composition: In step (d), the addition amount of carbon dioxide capture agent is 0-0.05 wt.% based on absorption liquid, the addition amount of crystal form control agent is 2-5 wt.% based on absorption liquid, and the addition amount of surfactant is 2-5 wt.% based on absorption liquid. Preferred Components: The crystal form control agent comprises magnesium chloride, magnesium sulfate, barium chloride, sodium pyrophosphate, ethanol and/or sucrose. Preferred Conditions: The step (e) involves using plate and frame filter press or centrifugal dehydrator for performing dehydration until moisture content is less than or equal to 30-40 wt.%. The step (e) involves sequentially drying dehydrated materials using fluidized bed and drum to moisture content of less than or equal to 1-5 wt.%. The step (e) involves crushing using jet mill to obtain nano-calcium carbonate product.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (d), the carbon dioxide capture agent comprises ethanolamine, diethanolamine and/or N-methyldiethanolamine. The surfactant comprises sodium stearate and/or cetyldimethylallylammonium chloride.

The repeating unit includes: a hydrotalcite                nanosheet layer, a polyacrylic resin layer, a                polyethyleneimine layer and a polyacrylic resin                layer arranged in sequence from the inside out; or                polyacrylic resin layer, polyethyleneimine layer,                polyacrylic resin layer and hydrotalcite nanosheet                layer; or polyethyleneimine layer, polyacrylic                resin layer, hydrotalcite nanosheet layer and                polyacrylic resin layer; or polyacrylic resin                layer, hydrotalcite nanosheet layer, polyacrylic                resin layer and polyethyleneimine layer.

The sulfate-reducing bacteria are                Shewanella oneidensisMR-1 strain, Pandoraeasp. B-6                strain or Desulfotomaculum nigrificans. The sulfur                source is elemental sulfur, thiosulfate, sulfate or                sulphite. The porous complex is a porous complex                material e.g. ZIF-67, MIL-101 or HKUST-1. AU BI Z
   LV Y
   WANG Y

   CHINA NAT ENVIRONMENTAL MONITORING CENT (CHNA-Non-standard)
GA 202204071Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves carrying out first gas chromatography separation treatment on the sample to be tested containing a standard sample. A first gas phase chromatography separating treatment is performed to generate a cutting gas. The volatile organic compound is converted in the cutting gas to carbon dioxide by oxidation reaction. The carbon dioxide is converted in the cut gas into carbon monoxide through reduction reaction. The FID detection on the reduced gas is performed after the reduction reaction to produce a sample chromatogram. The amount of carbon mononucleic acid (CH4) is calculated in cut gas according to the sample chromatography to calculate the content of volatile organosides.
   USE - Method for measuring volatile organic compound (VOC) used for quantitative analysis of VOCs in ambient air.

TI Method for deciding enterprise internal packet carbon pricing, involves traversing grouping condition, finding out packet with comprehensive efficiency of optimal packet process, and taking carbon price in packet as final carbon price.
AU YIN Y
   WANG Y
   WU P
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2021E0989L
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves evaluating an initial comprehensive efficiency of an enterprise based on an investment and output index of the enterprise and obtaining a target value of carbon dioxide emission index. An evaluation unit and an initial carbon price corresponding to the evaluation unit are constructed into a data packet. The data packet is sorted and grouped. A carbon price in each packet is adjusted. The carbon price adjusted by each evaluated unit is brought into a carbon discharge amount and a carbon pricing function relationship formula. A Carbon dioxide emission index in an output index is changed to the carbon emission calculated. An analysis result of a slack-based measure (SBM)-data envelopment analysis (DEA) model is stored. A grouping condition is traversed. The evaluation unit is found with a comprehensive efficiency in each grouping process. The comprehensive efficiency adopted by the evaluating unit is taken as an optimal grouping process.
   USE - Method for deciding enterprise internal packet carbon pricing such as Microsoft (RTM: Company Name), Nedbank Limited, Infosys, etc.

PN CN113369285-A
TI Carbonation method for stably solidifying heavy metals in waste incineration fly ash, involves washing waste incineration fly ash, and drying fly ash to remove water, where dried fly ash is added to ethanol solution.
AU LIN X
   CHEN J
   LI X
   YAN J
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2021A8339T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves washing waste incineration fly ash, and drying the fly ash to remove water. The dried fly ash is added to ethanol solution, and triethylamine is added. Carbon dioxide is introduced into the suspension under stirring condition, and carbonation reaction is performed. The reaction mixture is fully stirred, and solid-liquid separation operation is performed to obtain fly ash-calcium carbonate gel solid. The fly ash-calcium carbonate gel solid is dried, and is rotated to a muffle furnace for constant temperature heat treatment. The fly ash is naturally cooled.
   USE - Carbonation method for stably solidifying heavy metals in waste incineration fly ash.
   ADVANTAGE - The method can directly use the calcium source of the waste flying ash to solidify the heavy metal of itself. The generated calcium carbonate is calcite to fly ash adsorption coating effect is obvious, effectively improving the curing efficiency of heavy metal. The fly ash solidified body has a certain strength and is substantially not increased, which is good for landfill or utilization. The processing cost is low, and it can effectively fix the carbon dioxide, and really realizing to treat waste by waste.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of a carbonation method for stably solidifying heavy metals in waste incineration fly ash. (Drawing includes non-English language text).

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bud tea extract, comprises crushing bud tea to bud tea powder, then extracting and separating bud tea powder using carbon dioxide fluid supercritical method to obtain bud tea extract.
   USE - The bud tea extract is useful in bud tea flavor for electronic cigarette oil (claimed) and in various beverages and foods.
   ADVANTAGE - The bud tea extract: does not contain caffeine; and has unique fragrance and certain effect of reducing irritation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) bud tea extract obtained by above method, the bud tea extract have linalool, linalool oxide and small molecular organic acids are 11.21-12.86 wt.%, 3.12-3.89 wt.% and 1.28-11.5 wt.% as main active ingredients;
   (2) bud tea flavor, comprising bud tea extract and 5-40 times the edible alcohol, which is extracted by adding 5-40 times edible alcohol to bud tea extract, stirring at room temperature until the ingredients are evenly dissolved, and centrifuging at a speed of 2000-10000 revolutions/minute under a temperature of 0 to -5) degrees C to obtain resulting supernatant i.e. bud tea flavor; and
   (3) electronic cigarette oil, comprising bud tea extract and 5-40 times polyol mixture, and the electronic cigarette oil is obtained by adding 5-40 times the polyol mixture to the bud tea extract, stirring at 25 degrees C until the ingredients are uniformly dissolved, and centrifuging or filtering the supernatant or filtrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises crushing bud tea to bud tea powder, adding bud tea powder to the supercritical fluid extraction tank with a carbon dioxide flow rate of 1-5 l/hour and extracting at 35-55 degrees C under a pressure of 10-25 MPa and the carrying out separation at a temperature of 35-55 degrees C and a separation pressure of 6-10 MPa for 0.5-6.0 hours to obtain bud tea extract. The extraction conditions are: temperature of 40-50 degrees C and pressure of 15-20 MPa. The separation conditions are: temperature of 35-45 degrees C and pressure of 7-8 MPa. The method further includes adding 60%-95% ethanol aqueous solution before the extraction and separation using the carbon dioxide fluid supercritical method and the weight ratio of the ethanol aqueous solution to bud tea powder is 0-30:100. Preferred Components: The bud tea is Puerh bud tea (sic) or Zijuan tea bud (sic).

   (3) preparation of the copolymer, which                involves synthesizing rigid polyamide blocks from                polyamide precursors, adding flexible blocks, and                performing condensation of rigid polyamide blocks                and flexible blocks; and
   (4) manufacture of the membrane, which                involves supplying the copolymer, dissolving the                copolymer in a solvent, deposition of the polymer                dissolved in the solvent on a substrate, and                evaporating the solvent.

PT P
PN CN112117206-A
TI Integrated circuit board pre-embedded type oxygen-free packaging detection method, involves removing packaging mold after package body is cooled, trigerring detection work of detecting rod, and packaging package body without oxygen.
AU WANG M
AE WANG M (WANG-Individual)
GA 2020D01052
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves mounting a to-be-packaged integrated circuit board on a carrier substrate (2). A pre-embedded detecting rod is mounted on the integrated circuit board. An electrode on the integrated circuit board is lead through the pre-embedded detecting rod. A pre-manufactured packaging mold (1) is closed. Prepared plastic package material is pre-heated. The plastic package material is slowly injected from an injection port on the package mold. The plastic package material is heated and solidified to form a package body. The packaging mold is removed after the package body is cooled. Detection work of the pre-embedded detecting rod is triggered. The package body is packaged successfully without oxygen.
   USE - Integrated circuit board pre-embedded type oxygen-free packaging detection method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables releasing stable carbon dioxide gas so as to remove remaining oxygen and reduce residual oxygen. The method enables isolating outside air according to filling property of carbon dioxide so as to provide certain time for technicians to overhaul and improve packaging quality of an integrated circuit boards. The method enables realizing integrated circuit board pre-embedded type oxygen-free packaging detection operation in an effective manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of an integrated circuit board.
   Pre-manufactured packaging mold (1)
   Carrier substrate (2)
   Lead wire node (3)
   Detection hemisphere (4)
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); U11 (Semiconductor Materials and Processes); V04 (Printed Circuits and Connectors)

The utility model through the first fan and the first vent pipe to the waste gas into the spraying chamber; the sodium hydroxide solution in the liquid storage tank is pumped out by the centrifugal pump and sprayed by the nozzle; the sodium hydroxide solution and the carbon dioxide and carbon disulfide in the waste gas react, so as to remove the carbon dioxide and carbon disulfide; and it can restrain the dust in the waste gas.

PT P
PN CN111889104-A; CN111889104-B
TI Preparing specific composite calcium oxide metal oxide nano catalytic material, comprises e.g. drying egg shells, placing in corundum crucible, calcining, filling nitrogen, adding nickel chloride hexahydrate and ultrasonically treating.
AU ZHUANG Z
   XIANG J
   CHEN J
   YU Y
AE UNIV FUZHOU (UFZU-C)
GA 2020B2984T
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing 0D/2D composite calcium oxide metal oxide nano catalytic material, comprises (i) cleaning egg shells, drying in oven, taking out, placing in corundum crucible, calcining in muffle furnace, and cooling to obtain white calcium oxide powder precursor, (ii) adding white calcium oxide powder precursor into three-necked flask filled with absolute ethanol, filling nitrogen, heating in water bath, stirring, and maintaining constant temperature, (iii) dissolving nickel chloride hexahydrate in absolute ethanol, performing ultrasonic treatment until dissolves completely to obtain yellow-green solution, pouring into above three-necked flask, stirring uniformly, centrifuging, washing, and drying until moisture evaporates completely to obtain green powder three-dimensional nickel-calcium composite material, and (iv) placing obtained three-dimensional nickel-calcium composite material in strong acid solution, removing calcium oxide template by dissolving acid, washing and centrifuging.
   USE - The 0D/2D composite calcium oxide metal oxide nano catalytic material is useful in photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has high efficiency, high selectivity and high stability during photocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction, and universality in different types of shell materials; and is environmentally friendly. The method: has simple process, and low production cost; and is one-step process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for 0D/2D composite calcium oxide metal oxide nano catalytic material prepared by above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), the drying is carried out at 60 degrees C for 3 hours; the calcination process specifically comprises heating at rate of 5 degrees C/minute, and then calcining at 1050 degrees C for 1 hour; and the cooling process specifically comprises cooling to 100 degrees C with the furnace. In step (ii), the constant temperature treatment conditions are: temperature is 30 degrees C, and stirring time is 30 minutes. In step (iii), the stirring time is 24 hours; the washing solvent used in washing is absolute ethanol; and the washing times is 4 times. In step (iii), the drying method is vacuum freeze drying at -53 degrees C, and the drying time is 12 hours. In step (iv), the strong acid solution is 0.1 M hydrochloric acid solution, and the acid dissolution time is 10 minutes; and the washing solvent used in washing is deionized water, and the washing times is 4 times. Preferred Components: In step (ii), the amount of calcium oxide powder precursor is 1.4 g; and the amount of absolute ethanol is 100 ml. In step (iii), the amount of nickel chloride hexahydrate is 5.94 g; and the amount of absolute ethanol is 150 ml.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Detergent soap useful for laundry purpose or killing insect, comprises diatomaceous earth, olive oil, borax, coconut oil, palm oil, Shea butter, sodium hydroxide, water, sodium bicarbonate, and carbon dioxide.
AU THOMAS M A
AE THOMAS M A (THOM-Individual)
GA 202074978V
AB    NOVELTY - Odor free detergent soap comprises (a) predetermined amounts of diatomaceous earth, olive oil, borax, coconut oil, palm oil, Shea butter, sodium hydroxide, water, sodium bicarbonate, and carbon dioxide, or (b) one bar of grated bar soap, 2 quarts of water, a clear solution of the bar soap, 4.5 gallons of hot water, and 2 cups of diatomaceous earth.
   USE - The detergent soap is useful for laundry purpose (claimed) or killing insect.
   ADVANTAGE - The detergent soap is odor free, bio-degradable, non-toxic insect killer, pet-friendly and excellent for washing clothes of people having sensitive skin, and prepared in a simple and easy manner with an improvement to sanitary cleaning elements for purpose of washing clothes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of preparing an odor free detergent soap for a laundry purpose, which involves (a) selecting one bar of a bar soap of a type used for bathing and i.e. odorless, (b) grating the one bar of the bar soap, (c) adding two quarts of water to the grated one bar of the bar soap to form a soap solution, (d) heating and stirring the soap solution until the grated one bar of the bar soap is dissolved, (e) adding 4.5 gallons of hot water to soap solution, and (f) adding two cups of food grade diatomaceous earth to the soap solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Component: The soap comprises 2 cups of diatomaceous earth per 5 gallons of water, 18.5 ounces of olive oil, 12.5 ounces of coconut oil, 9 ounces palm oil, 1 ounce of Shea butter, 5.8 ounces of sodium hydroxide, and 13.5 ounces of water. The diatomaceous earth includes particles having a particle size of 3 mu m-1 mm. The diatomaceous earth includes particles having a particle size of 10-200 mu m. The borax is taken from sodium borate, sodium tetraborate, and disodium tetraborate. The sodium bicarbonate comprising sodium carbonate and carbon dioxide and water when heated becomes a washing soda. The sodium carbonate is a detergent having an alkaline pH. The soap comprises an aqueous solution having insect killing properties. The bar soap is odorless bar soap. Preferred Method: The method further involves adding a predetermined amount of olive oil to the soap solution. The method further involves adding a predetermined amount of coconut oil to the soap solution. The method further involves adding a predetermined amount of palm oil to the soap solution. The method further involves adding a predetermined amount of Shea butter to the soap solution.
   ACTIVITY - Insecticide. No biological data given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN111359626-A
TI Ruthenium-nickel supported methanation catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to prepare methane, comprises ruthenium-nickel alloy active component and titanium dioxide carrier.
AU GUO Y
   XU C
   WANG J
   NIU J
AE LANZHOU CHEM & PHYSICS INST CHINESE ACAD (CLCP-C)
GA 202063802L
AB    NOVELTY - A ruthenium-nickel supported methanation catalyst comprises ruthenium-nickel alloy active component and titanium dioxide carrier. The loading amount of ruthenium-nickel alloy active component on carrier is 0.1-30 wt.%. The molar ratio of ruthenium and nickel in ruthenium-nickel alloy active component is 0.1:1-10:1.
   USE - Ruthenium-nickel supported methanation catalyst for hydrogenation of carbon dioxide to prepare methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The ruthenium-nickel supported methanation catalyst has excellent catalytic performance and is prepared by easy preparation process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the ruthenium-nickel supported methanation catalyst, which involves mixing 0.05-5 mol/L aqueous solution of ruthenium chloride and 0.05-5 mol/L aqueous solution of nickel nitrate hexahydrate, stirring at room temperature for 0.5-4 hours to obtain mixed solution, adding titanium dioxide powder to mixed solution, stirring at room temperature for 0.5-4 hours, adjusting pH to 8.5, raising temperature to 40 degrees C, dripping precipitation agent to co-precipitate the two metal salts, stirring for 1-12 hours, raising temperature to 80 degrees C, stirring for 1-24 hours, filtering, washing and drying to obtain precipitate where the addition amount of the precipitating agent is 1.1 times the theoretical value, reducing precipitate at 400-700 degrees C under reducing atmosphere and heating rate of 10-20 degrees C/minute, further reducing at high temperature and cooling to room temperature.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The precipitating agent is one of sodium carbonate, sodium hydroxide, potassium carbonate and potassium hydroxide. Preferred Condition: The drying is carried out at 60 degrees C under vacuum condition or at 120 degrees C without vacuum condition for 12-24 hours. The reducing atmosphere is high-purity hydrogen or standard hydrogen gas atmosphere.

PN DE102018214972-A1; EP3620427-A1; US2020071149-A1; CN110877885-A; CN110877885-B
TI Method for displacing air from e.g. beer bottles, involves penetrating sound waves through bottle mouths into beverages and/or vibrating sidewalls of bottles, so that carbon-dioxide is expelled from beverages to displace air through mouths.
AU GEBENDORFER S
AE KRONES AG (KROE-C)
   KRONES AG (KROE-C)
   KRONES AG (KROE-C)
GA 202019031S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves emitting sound waves (4a, 5a) from a sound source (4, 5), and propagating the sound waves through ambient air. The sound waves are penetrated through mouths (1c) of bottles (1) into beverages (2) and/or sidewalls (1b) of the bottles are set in vibration (6) with the sound waves, so that carbon-dioxide is expelled from the beverages and beverage foams in headspaces (1a) of the bottles such that air contained is displaced through the mouths. The first sound waves are directed onto the sidewalls of the bottles and an output frequency is adapted to a natural resonance frequency of the bottles that are filled with the beverages.
   USE - Method for displacing air from bottles of carbonated beverages e.g. beer and lemonade.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables arranging the sound sources along a direction of transport with individually adapted sound amplitudes, so as to allow precise control of the formation of foam in bottles in order to expel the air present above beverage and to avoid foam from overflowing.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a device for displacing air from bottles of carbonated beverages.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a bottle when CO2 is expelled.
   Bottles (1)
   Headspaces (1a)
   Sidewalls (1b)
   Mouths (1c)
   Beverages (2)
   Sound sources (4, 5)
   Sound waves (4a, 5a)
   Wall vibration (6)

PN EP3539880-A1; US2019283896-A1; US10442546-B2; EP3539880-B1
TI Inert gas generation system for fuel tank in aircraft has gas separator unit that receives second portion of fuel containing dissolved gas from fuel tank and removes dissolved gas from second portion of fuel.
AU RHEAUME J
   CORDATOS H
AE HAMILTON SUNDSTRAND CORP (SUNH-C)
   HAMILTON SUNDSTRAND CORP (SUNH-C)
GA 201980172C
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10) has a catalytic oxidation unit (32) that receives and combusts a first portion of fuel from a fuel tank (12) to produce inert gas, and an inert gas line (34) that receives the inert gas from catalytic oxidation unit and directs the inert gas to fuel tank in which a portion of the inert gas dissolves in fuel inside the fuel tank. A gas separator unit (52) receives a second portion of fuel containing dissolved gas from the fuel tank, and removes dissolved gas.
   USE - Inert gas generation system for fuel tank in aircraft.
   ADVANTAGE - The use of gas separator device prevents later cavitation of carbon dioxide and associated vapor lock. The fuel is separated from the vacuum source or sweep gas by a membrane, thus preventing fuel vapor from evolving and transporting across the oleophobic membrane with the dissolved gases. The catalytic fuel tank inerting system coupled with a gas separator allows for removal of carbon dioxide dissolved in fuel prior to fuel being used for thermal regulation throughout the aircraft, preventing cavitation and vapor lock.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method of inerting fuel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of a catalytic oxidation fuel tank inerting system with a gas separator component.
   Inert gas generation system (10)
   Fuel tank (12)
   Catalytic oxidation unit (32)
   Inert gas line (34)
   Gas separator unit (52)

TI Photocatalytic composite material comprises            two-dimensional layered structure comprising titanium            carbide dispersed around flower-like bismuth oxyhalide            microspheres formed by self-assembly of            nanosheets.
AU LIU X
   NI M
   ZHUGE Z
   CHEN T
   GONG Y
   LI C
   NIU L
AE UNIV CHINA JILIANG (UYJA-C)
GA 201968166F
AB    NOVELTY - A photocatalytic composite material comprises                two-dimensional layered structure comprising                titanium carbide dispersed around flower-like                bismuth oxyhalide microspheres formed by                self-assembly of nanosheets.
   USE - Composite material used in photocatalytic                reduction of carbon dioxide and heavy metal ions,                degradation of organic benzene and antibiotic                organic pollutants, and degradation of dyes (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The photocatalytic composite material                effectively reduces carbon dioxide and heavy metal                ions under visible light irradiation. The                preparation of composite material is simple and                economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A photocatalytic composite material comprises                two-dimensional layered structure comprising                titanium carbide dispersed around flower-like                bismuth oxyhalide microspheres (BiOClxI1-x, where x                is 0.1-0.5) formed by self-assembly of nanosheets.                An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of                photocatalytic composite material, which involves                dispersing titanium aluminum carbide powder in                hydrofluoric acid, magnetically stirring at room                temperature to obtain titanium carbide, dispersing                titanium carbide in organic solvent or ionic                solution, ultrasonically synthesizing                two-dimensional layered titanium carbide material,                dissolving lanthanum source and titanium carbide in                ethylene glycol solvent and ultrasonically                dispersing to obtain uniform mixture A, dissolving                chlorine source and iodine source in ethylene                glycol solvent, and ultrasonically dispersing to                obtain solution (B), adding solution (B) dropwise                to solution (A), stirring to obtain mixed solution                (C), carrying out reaction at constant temperature                of 120-180 degrees C for 12-24 hours, washing and                drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of                  titanium carbide and flower-like bismuth                  oxyhalide microspheres is 0.25-2%. The ionic                  solution comprises lithium ion, sodium ion,                  potassium ion, aluminum ion and tetravalent tin                  ion. The source of bismuth comprises at least one                  of bismuth nitrate pentahydrate and bismuth                  chloride. The solution concentration of bismuth                  is 0.05-0.1 mol/L. The chlorine source comprises                  at least one of potassium chloride and sodium                  chloride. The iodine source comprises at least                  one of potassium iodide and sodium iodide. The                  concentration of chlorine source and iodine                  source in solution (B) is 0.01-0.05 mol/L. The                  molar ratio of chlorine to iodine source is                  1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The organic solvent                  comprises at least one of dimethyl sulfoxide,                  tetrabutylammonium hydroxide, choline hydroxide,                  n-butylamine, ammonia, urea, and                  dimethylformamide.

TI Preparing cosmetic for e.g. repairing facial skin, by adding trypsin, EDTA and type I collagenase to processed ear or eyelid skin, enzymatically hydrolyzing, filtering, culturing in carbon dioxide incubator and collecting supernatant.

   HE X
   AI X
AE XIAN LUOWEITA BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201963553K
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a cosmetic from a fibroblast supernatant involves (1) taking ear or eyelid skin under aseptic conditions, removing subcutaneous tissue blood vessels, adding 0.5% trypsin and 0.2% EDTA in a volume ratio of 10:1, enzymatically hydrolyzing at 37 degrees C for 2 hours, adding 1 mg/ml type I collagenase, enzymatically hydrolyzing at 37 degrees C for 1 hour, passing the enzymatic hydrolyzate through a 200 mesh sieve and pipetting and filtering to obtain fibroblasts, (2) adding Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium/F12 culture medium containing 50 U/ml keratinocyte growth factor, 100 U/ml platelet-derived growth factor and 10% platelet-rich plasma to the cell suspension and culturing in a 5% carbon dioxide incubator at 37 degrees C for 48 hours, and (3) collecting the supernatant obtained in step (2) when the fibroblasts are amplified and preparing the supernatant into a base liquid, an essence and a facial mask.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing a cosmetic from a fibroblast supernatant, which is useful for repairing facial skin (claimed), promoting tissue regeneration and preventing scarring.
   ADVANTAGE - The cosmetic contains a large amount of collagen, elastic fiber, sodium hyaluronate and various growth factors, and has excellent effect on depressed scars and tissue loss, and strong skin repair function.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Process: The base liquid is prepared by adding 5-20 wt.% glycerol to the supernatant. The essence is prepared by adding 3-10 wt.% 1,4-butanediol and 5-10 wt.% trehalose to the supernatant. The facial mask is prepared by soaking the facial mask paper in 10-20 ml supernatant.

PN CN109399684-A
TI Preparing calcium carbonate involves adding calcite,limestone and white coal for blending, using hammer crusher for crushing, using ball mill for grinding, and drying after using permanent magnetic separator for magnetically selecting.
AU YANG F
AE CILI LIANXING MINING CO LTD (CILI-Non-standard)
GA 2019231376
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing calcium carbonate involves adding calcite,limestone and white coal for blending, using hammer crusher for crushing, using ball mill for grinding until granularity is 10 meshes, and drying at 65 degrees C after using permanent magnetic separator for magnetically selecting and removing impurities to obtain mixed material A. The mixed material A is used to calcine and water of 4 times weight is added for pulping of mixed material A to obtain mixed material B. The carbon dioxide is inletted in mixed material B to carbonize at 30 degrees C. The modifier is added to stir with speed of 600 revolutions per minute for 4 hours to obtain mixed material C. The mixed material C is added into microwave reactor for microwave processing to obtain mixture D. The material D inletting carbon dioxide is used for secondary carbonization at 60 degrees C, stirred and stearic acid is added. The ultrasonic oscillation is performed for 20 minutes at 85 degrees C as activator to obtain mixed material E.
   USE - Method for preparing calcium carbonate used in preparing calcium carbonate products (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare calcium carbonate that improves effect for removing the impurity, and reduce the viscosity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The mixing material E is passed through centrifugal dewatered, dried, cooled, crushed and sieved to obtain finished product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The ratio of calcite, limestone and white coal is 40:71:1. The magnetic field intensity of permanent magnetic separator is 4000GS. The cutting speed of pulping is 4000 revolutions per minute. The amount of modifying agent is 0.01 times of the weight of the mixed material B. The microwave processing of radiation time is 5 minutes. The power of microwave treatment is 120 watts. The ultrasonic frequency of ultrasonic vibration is 120 kilohertz. Preferred Process: The modified agent is prepared by adding 80-100 pts. wt. deionized water, 6-8 pts. wt. polyvinylpyrrolidone, and 2-4 pts. wt. polyacrylamide to stir uniformly to obtain modified agent.

GA 201894620X
AB    NOVELTY - The method for storing and preserving eggplant involves (1) selecting fresh eggplants without disease spots and mechanical damage and using a dry cloth to gently wipe dirt on skin of the eggplant fruit, (2) performing anti-falling treatment by uniformly spraying anti-falling solution having concentration of 0.01-0.025% on the eggplant stalks, (3) pre-cooling the eggplants at 9-12 degrees C for 6-8 hours, and fumigating the pre-cooled eggplant using 1-methylcyclopropene, (4) packing and storing the eggplants in a fresh-keeping bag, and ensuring that the oxygen content in each fresh-keeping bag is 10-20%, and the carbon dioxide content is 0.8-1.2%, and (5) putting the fresh-keeping bags with eggplants into a fresh-keeping storehouse and controlling the temperature of the storehouse at 10-14 degrees C and indoor air humidity to 90-95%.
   USE - Method for storing and preserving eggplant such as long eggplant or round eggplant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effective storage and preservation of eggplant which avoids the damage of wax layer on the surface of the eggplant due to cleaning the surface of eggplant, controls risk of the eggplant stem falling off, kills and inhibits the bacteria on the surface of the eggplant, and prolongs shelf life of the eggplants.

AU LAN D

   WU S
AE HUNAN INST ENG (HNIE-C)
   HUNAN INST ENG (HNIE-C)
GA 2018814031
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polyionic liquid composite material involves ultrasonically dispersing a surface-rich hydroxy-rich carrier in an organic solvent at room temperature, adding silane coupling reagent, reacting at 60-120 degrees C for 12-48 hours to obtain product (A), cooling, centrifugally washing to remove unreacted silane coupling reagent, adding organic solvent and nitrogen-rich organic amine, reacting at 60-120 degrees C for 12-48 hours to obtain product (B), cooling, adding dihalogenated hydrocarbon, reacting at 30-100 degrees C for 12-48 hours to obtain product (C), cooling to room temperature, centrifuging and washing using organic solvent for 5 times, drying in a vacuum oven at 60 degrees C for 24 hours, and grinding.
   USE - Preparation of polyionic liquid composite material for preparing cyclic carbonate (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared composite material has excellent recyclability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for usage method of polyionic liquid composite material in the preparation of cyclic carbonate, which involves adding catalyst polyionic liquid composite powder and liquid epoxide to high-pressure reactor at high temperature, introducing carbon dioxide until the pressure of the system rises to 0.1-3 MPa, heating the reactor 30-120 degrees C, reacting for 2-48 hours, cooling to room temperature, releasing unreacted carbon dioxide, filtering to remove the catalyst in the reaction liquid, and distilling to remove unreacted epoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The surface-rich hydroxy-rich carrier comprises graphene oxide, cellulose, silica sol, MCM-41 (mesoporous silica) and/or SBA-15 (mesoporous silica).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent comprises toluene, dichloromethane, acetonitrile, N,N-dimethylformamide and/or tetrahydrofuran. The silane coupling reagent comprises ( gamma )-chloropropyltrimethoxysilane, ( gamma )-chloromethyltrimethoxysilane, ( gamma )-chloropropyltriethoxysilane and/or ( gamma )-bromopropyltrimethoxysilane. The nitrogen-rich organic amine comprises triethylenetetramine, tetramethylguanidine, metformin, hexamethylenetetramine and/or dithiourea. The dihalogenated hydrocarbon comprises 1,2-dibromoethane, 1,4-dibromobutane, p-dibenzyl bromide, p-dibenzyl chloride, 1,6-dibromohexane and/or 1,8-dibromooctane. The epoxide comprises ethylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, propylene oxide, butylene oxide, cyclohexene oxide, cyclopentene oxide and/or styrene oxide. Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of the carrier to the silane coupling reagent is 1:(1-30). The mass ratio of the nitrogen-rich organic amine to the silane coupling reagent is 1:(0.2-10). The molar ratio of the dihalogenated hydrocarbon to the nitrogen-rich organic amine is 1:(0.1-10). The mass ratio of the polyionic liquid composite material to the epoxide is (0.01-0.2):1.

TI Method for forming high-nickel ternary lithium battery involves side-sealing and capping lithium battery casing with platinum-doped aluminum-plastic composite film, injecting e.g. sodium fluoride electrolyte, and charging and discharging.
AU CHEN Q
   ZHAO H
AE CHENGDU NEW KELI CHEM SCI CO LTD (XKLI-C)
GA 201880297V
AB    NOVELTY - Method for forming high-nickel ternary lithium battery involves side-sealing and capping lithium battery casing with platinum-doped aluminum-plastic composite film in which 60-120% composite film is reserved on one side of the casing, providing liquid injection port on the outer casing of lithium battery, injecting electrolyte containing sodium fluoride under carbon dioxide atmosphere, through the injection port, introducing mixed gas of carbon dioxide, fluorine gas and sulfur dioxide, sealing the injection port, charging and discharging the sealed lithium battery under ambient temperature of 3-50 degrees C, for three times, in which first charging is carried at constant current of 0.01-0.45 C, voltage of 4-4.8 V and ambient temperature of 3-10 degrees C, second charging is carried out at 0.06-0.8 C, 3-4 V, and 11-30 degrees C, and third charging is carried out at 0.9-1.2 C, 2-3 V, and 31-50 degrees C, vacuum-sealing lithium battery, removing gas generated, and cutting off the reserved 60-120% composite film.
   USE - Method for forming high-nickel ternary lithium battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The method introduces diluted fluorine gas and sulfur dioxide, and provides stable and compact solid electrolyte mask with improved lithium fluoride and lithium sulfite content, during the formation process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The outer casing is aluminum casing, or steel casing, preferably aluminum casing. Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of carbon dioxide, fluorine gas and sulfur dioxide, is 20-40:1-3:1-2, preferably 30:1.8:1.3.

GA 2018665418

PT P
PN CN108047022-A
TI Preparing succinic acid involves collecting waste gas and purified carbon dioxide, producing by microbial fermentation of succinate fermentation liquor, filtering through aperture of micro-filtration membrane, and removing impurities.
AU WANG L
AE HEBEI CLAVI BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HEBE-Non-standard)
GA 201841375D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing succinic acid involves collecting waste gas and purified carbon dioxide, producing by microbial fermentation of succinate fermentation liquor, filtering through aperture of 0.1-0.5 mm of micro-filtration membrane at 0.1-0.15 megapascal pressure. The operation temperature is 10-30 degrees C under cross-flow filtration for 15-25 times. The 10-50% of the fermenting liquid is used. The large particle impurity particles are removed. The solution is filtered through nano-filtration membrane with cut-off molecular weight of 500-2000 when the pressure is 1-5 megapascal. The temperature is 20-60 degrees C under the condition of crossflow filtration. The concentration multiple is 20-40 times. The residual protein and pigment are removed in the fermentation liquid to obtain clear sodium succinate solution. The clear dosium solution is treated at 5-40 degrees C by bipolar membrane electrodialysis to obtain succinate containing butanedioic acid solution.
   USE - Method for preparing succinic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare succinic acid has low device requirement and high purity, and in simple manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The amount of succinate butanedioic acid solution is subjected to hydrogen type strong acid type cation exchange resin, residual sodium ion is removed to obtain pure diacid solution and sodium hydroxide solution. The solution is decompressed and evaporated the purified succinic acid solution, cooled and crystallized to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The cation exchange resin is 001x 4, 001x 7, 001x 8, 001x 10, D001 or SQD-65.

TI Method for cleaning carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixture gas, involves washing undesired components out of gas, expelling undesired components from washing medium, and cooling nitrogen before supplied to stripping unit.
AU GOLOUBEV D
   KERESTECIOGLU U
   RIESCH P
   TRAN A T
   GOLUBEV D
AE LINDE AG (LINM-C)
GA 2018312011
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves washing undesired components out of gas in a gas washing unit (200) using a washing medium (h). The undesired components are expelled from the washing medium partially using nitrogen as a stripping gas in a stripping unit (210). The nitrogen is cooled using an expansion turbine before supplied to the stripping unit. Energy recovered from the nitrogen is recovered by the expansion turbine and feed into an energy system. Additional nitrogen, which is not supplied to the gas washing unit is cooled by the expansion turbine.
   USE - Method for cleaning a gas i.e. synthesis gas such as carbon monoxide, hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixture gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method washes the undesired components out of gas in the gas washing unit using the washing medium, expels the undesired components from the washing medium partially using the nitrogen as the stripping gas in the stripping unit, and cools the nitrogen using the expansion turbine before supplied to the stripping unit, thus cleaning the gas in an energy-efficient manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for cleaning a gas comprising a gas washing unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a rectisol-laundry for cleaning a gas using a washing medium such as methanol.
   Washing medium (h)
   Gas washing unit (200)
   Heat exchanger (201)
   Absorber (203)
   Cooling unit (204)
   Stripping unit (210)

TI Switch for high or medium voltage direct current network, has direct current circuit breaker water tight enclosure containing gas fluid comprising seventy percentage of carbon dioxide at filling pressure measured at specific temperature.
AU CREUSOT C
AE SUPERGRID INST (SUPE-Non-standard)
   SUPERGRID INST SAS (SUPE-Non-standard)
   SUPERGRID INST (SUPE-Non-standard)
   SUPERGRID INST (SUPE-Non-standard)
   SUPERGRID INST SAS (SUPE-Non-standard)
GA 201829801N
AB    NOVELTY - The switch has a branch with a direct current circuit breaker (5) comprising active cut elements in a watertight enclosure containing a gas fluid. The branch is inserted in a line of high or medium voltage direct current network. An energy absorption element (7) and a resonant circuit (6) having a capacitor (C) and a inductor (L) are mounted in parallel to the network. The enclosure contains a gas fluid comprising 70% of carbon dioxide at filling pressure ranging between 0.65 MPa and 1.1 MPa measured at a temperature of 20C. The inductor is less than 2700c-0.84 for a value of capacitor.
   USE - Switch for a high or medium voltage direct current network (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The switch is simple and compact, while providing high switching performances in direct current value for a range of operating temperature of the switch. The resonant circuit of the direct current circuit breaker is dimensioned to achieve switching performances and completely benefiting from the performance of the circuit breaker. The capacitor of the resonance circuit is controlled so as to reduce cost of the switch and increase current switching performance.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a circuit diagram of a switch.
   Capacitor (C)
   Inductor (L)
   Direct current circuit breaker (5)
   Resonant circuit (6)
   Energy absorption element (7)

AB    NOVELTY - Method of treating paint-sprayed sewage involves pretreating paint wastewater using Fenton reagent, oxidizing organics, decomposing, removing chemical oxygen demand, adding polyacrylamide and polyaluminum chloride, carrying out coagulation precipitation, entering effluent to biochemical treatment system, catalytically decomposing water, carrying out partial oxidation of refractory organics in wastewater, decomposing carbon-carbon bond of organic matter in wastewater into water and carbon dioxide, reducing chemical oxygen demand and further processing.
   USE - Method of treating paint-sprayed sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method efficiently treats paint-sprayed sewage without affecting the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method of treating paint-sprayed sewage involves pretreating paint wastewater using Fenton reagent, oxidizing organics, decomposing, removing chemical oxygen demand at the removal rate of 30%, adding polyacrylamide and polyaluminum chloride, carrying out coagulation precipitation, removing chemical oxygen demand at the rate of 60-80% from 3000-20000 mg/L to 1200-1400 mg/L, entering effluent to biochemical treatment system, catalytically decomposing water, forming hydroxyl groups with extremely strong oxidizing power, carrying out partial oxidation of refractory organics in wastewater, decomposing carbon-carbon bond of the organic matter in the wastewater into water and carbon dioxide, reducing chemical oxygen demand, changing the density and structure of its electron cloud to form intermediate products that are not too large in molecular weight, oxidizing divalent iron ion to ferric hydroxide, removing suspended solids and impurities in water, and removing pollutants through subsequent coagulation and sedimentation and biochemical treatment.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E31 (Compounds of V, Nb, Ta, Cr, Mo, W, Mn, Tc, Re, Fe, Ru, Os, Co, Rh, Ir, Ni, Pd, Pt, Pa, U and subsequent actinides.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)
